# 1 Stata basics

```{julia}
function group_variable(dfr,variable)
    res = Chain.@chain dfr begin
        DataFrames.groupby(
            [Symbol(variable)])
        DataFrames.combine(
            Symbol(variable) => length => :N)
    end
    return res
end

mean_skip_missing(x) = mean(skipmissing(x))
function means_variables(dfr,variable)
    vars = Chain.@chain dfr begin
        DataFrames.groupby(
        [Symbol(variable),])
        DataFrames.combine(
        Symbol(vars) => mean_skip_missing => Symbol(vars))
    end
    return vars
end

function StrToInt(x)
	x = string.(x)
    try
        Base.parse.(Int64, x)
    catch
        return missing
    end
end

_rawval(x) = hasproperty(x, :value) ? x.value : x  # unwrap ReadStatTables.LabeledValue

function _numeric_vec(col)
    vals = collect(skipmissing(col))
    isempty(vals) && return Float64[]
    T = Base.nonmissingtype(eltype(col))
    T <: Real && return convert(Vector{Float64}, vals)
    try
        return Float64[Float64(_rawval(v)) for v in vals]
    catch
        return nothing  # non-numeric (strings, etc.)
    end
end

"""
Expand a Stata-style varlist pattern against DataFrame columns. Supports:
  * `t*`, `*t`, `*mpg*`, `t?`   — glob wildcards
  * `price-weight`              — range of columns (inclusive, by column order)
  * exact names, Symbols, vectors, and the literal "_all"
"""
function _expand_varlist(df, vars)
    cols = string.(DataFrames.names(df))
    patterns = vars isa Union{Symbol,AbstractString} ? [string(vars)] :
               vars isa AbstractVector ? [string(v) for v in vars] :
               [string(v) for v in collect(vars)]

    out = String[]
    for p in patterns
        if p == "_all"
            append!(out, cols)
        elseif occursin('*', p) || occursin('?', p)
            rx = Regex("^" * replace(p, "*" => ".*", "?" => ".") * "\$")
            for c in cols
                occursin(rx, c) && push!(out, c)
            end
        elseif occursin('-', p) && !startswith(p, "-") && !(p in cols)
            parts = split(p, '-', limit=2)
            i = findfirst(==(String(parts[1])), cols)
            j = findfirst(==(String(parts[2])), cols)
            (i === nothing || j === nothing) && error("Unknown variable in range '$p'")
            i, j = minmax(i, j)
            append!(out, cols[i:j])
        else
            push!(out, p)
        end
    end

    seen = Set{String}(); uniq = String[]
    for v in out
        v in seen || (push!(seen, v); push!(uniq, v))
    end
    return Symbol.(uniq)
end

"""
    correlate_stata(df, vars=nothing)

Stata-style `correlate <varlist>` — pairwise (listwise-dropmissing)
correlation matrix of the listed variables, printed in Stata's
lower-triangular layout with abbreviated column headers.

  - `vars` can be `nothing` (use all numeric columns) or a Vector/Tuple of
    column names (Symbol/String).
  - Prints `(obs=N)` then a triangular table with 4-decimal correlations.
  - Variable names longer than 8 chars are abbreviated to `XXXXXX~Y` in the
    column headers, mirroring Stata's wrapping.
  - Returns the full symmetric correlation matrix (`Statistics.cor`) so you
    can keep using it programmatically.
"""
function correlate_stata(df::DataFrames.AbstractDataFrame, vars=nothing)
    varlist = vars === nothing ? Symbol[] : Symbol.(vars)
    if isempty(varlist)
        for c in DataFrames.names(df)
            elT = nonmissingtype(eltype(df[!, c]))
            elT <: Real && push!(varlist, Symbol(c))
        end
    end
    sub = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, varlist])
    n   = DataFrames.nrow(sub)
    M   = hcat([Float64.(collect(sub[!, v])) for v in varlist]...)
    C   = Statistics.cor(M)

    abbrev(s::AbstractString, w::Int=8) =
        length(s) <= w ? s : (s[1:w-2] * "~" * s[end:end])
    commafmt(num::Integer) = begin
        s = string(abs(num)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1; push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3; end
        (num < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    println()
    Printf.@printf("(obs=%s)\n\n", commafmt(n))
    Printf.@printf("%12s |", "")
    for v in varlist
        Printf.@printf(" %8s", abbrev(string(v)))
    end
    println()
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^(9 * length(varlist)))
    for (i, v) in enumerate(varlist)
        Printf.@printf("%12s |", string(v))
        for j in 1:i
            Printf.@printf(" %8.4f", C[i, j])
        end
        println()
    end
    return C
end

"""
    rmcoll_stata(df, vars; tol=1e-9, quiet=false) -> Vector{Symbol}

Stata-style `_rmcoll`: scan `vars` in order and drop any variable whose
multiple-correlation R² with the previously kept set exceeds `1 − tol`.
Stata's default tolerance is `1e-9`. Returns the surviving varlist; with
`quiet=false` (the default) prints `note: <var> omitted because of
collinearity` for each dropped variable, matching Stata's wording.
"""
function rmcoll_stata(df::DataFrames.AbstractDataFrame, vars;
                     tol::Float64 = 1e-9, quiet::Bool = false)
    varlist = Symbol.(vars)
    sub = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, varlist])
    M   = hcat([Float64.(collect(sub[!, v])) for v in varlist]...)
    n   = size(M, 1)
    keep = trues(length(varlist))
    keep[1] = true
    for j in 2:length(varlist)
        Xj = M[:, findall(keep[1:j-1])]
        yj = M[:, j]
        # Regression with intercept: residual SS / total SS
        Xa = hcat(ones(n), Xj)
        β  = Xa \ yj
        ŷ  = Xa * β
        rss = sum((yj .- ŷ).^2)
        tss = sum((yj .- Statistics.mean(yj)).^2)
        r2  = tss > 0 ? 1 - rss / tss : 1.0
        if 1 - r2 < tol
            keep[j] = false
            quiet || println("note: ", varlist[j], " omitted because of collinearity")
        end
    end
    return varlist[keep]
end

function summarize_stata(df::DataFrames.AbstractDataFrame, vars=nothing; detail::Bool=false)
    if vars === nothing
        varlist = Symbol[]
        for n in DataFrames.names(df)
            _numeric_vec(df[!, n]) === nothing || push!(varlist, Symbol(n))
        end
    else
        varlist = _expand_varlist(df, vars)
    end

    rows = []
    for v in varlist
        xraw = _numeric_vec(df[!, v])
        x = xraw === nothing ? Float64[] : xraw
        n = length(x)
        if n == 0
            push!(rows, (; Variable=string(v), Obs=0, Mean=missing,
                           Std=missing, Min=missing, Max=missing))
            continue
        end
        μ  = Statistics.mean(x)
        σ  = n > 1 ? Statistics.std(x) : missing
        base = (; Variable=string(v), Obs=n, Mean=μ, Std=σ,
                  Min=minimum(x), Max=maximum(x))
        if detail
            qs = Statistics.quantile(x, [0.01,0.05,0.10,0.25,0.50,0.75,0.90,0.95,0.99])
            # Stata uses biased moments for skewness/kurtosis
            m2 = Statistics.mean((x .- μ).^2)
            m3 = Statistics.mean((x .- μ).^3)
            m4 = Statistics.mean((x .- μ).^4)
            skew = m2 > 0 ? m3 / m2^(3/2) : missing
            kurt = m2 > 0 ? m4 / m2^2     : missing
            push!(rows, merge(base,
                (; p1=qs[1], p5=qs[2], p10=qs[3], p25=qs[4], p50=qs[5],
                   p75=qs[6], p90=qs[7], p95=qs[8], p99=qs[9],
                   Variance = ismissing(σ) ? missing : σ^2,
                   Skewness = skew, Kurtosis = kurt)))
        else
            push!(rows, base)
        end
    end

    # Stata's detail format is multi-section per variable; we fall back to
    # PrettyTables for that. Default (non-detail) matches Stata's `summarize`.
    if detail
        PrettyTables.pretty_table(DataFrames.DataFrame(rows))
        return
    end

    # Stata %9.0g: up to 7 sig figs, capped at 9 chars total (so leading "0."
    # for |x|<1 fits), then leading "0" stripped from |x|<1.
    _g9(x; w::Int=9, mx::Int=7) = begin
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return "."
        sig = mx
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        while length(s) > w && sig > 1
            sig -= 1
            s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        end
        0 < abs(x) < 1 ? replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.") : s
    end
    _comma(num::Integer) = begin
        s = string(abs(num)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1; push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3; end
        (num < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    println("    Variable |        Obs        Mean    Std. dev.       Min        Max")
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^57)
    for r in rows
        Printf.@printf("%12s |  %9s   %9s   %9s  %9s  %9s\n",
                       r.Variable, _comma(r.Obs),
                       _g9(r.Mean), _g9(r.Std),
                       _g9(r.Min),  _g9(r.Max))
    end
end

"""
    ereturn_list(m)

Stata-style `ereturn list` for a regression object `m` (FixedEffectModels /
GLM / StatsBase-compatible). Prints scalars, macros, and matrices, and
returns them in a NamedTuple so they can be retrieved programmatically.
"""
function ereturn_list(m)
    _try(f, m) = try f(m) catch; missing end

    scalars = (;
        N          = _try(StatsBase.nobs, m),
        df_m       = _try(StatsBase.dof, m),
        df_r       = _try(StatsBase.dof_residual, m),
        r2         = _try(StatsBase.r2, m),
        r2_a       = _try(StatsBase.adjr2, m),
        rss        = _try(StatsBase.deviance, m),
        rmse       = (rss = _try(StatsBase.deviance, m); dfr = _try(StatsBase.dof_residual, m);
                      (ismissing(rss) || ismissing(dfr) || dfr == 0) ? missing : sqrt(rss / dfr)),
        F          = hasproperty(m, :F) ? m.F : missing,
        p          = hasproperty(m, :p) ? m.p : missing,
    )
    macros = (;
        depvar  = hasproperty(m, :yname)   ? string(m.yname)   : missing,
        indeps  = try join(string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m)), " ") catch; missing end,
        cmd     = string(nameof(typeof(m))),
    )
    b = _try(StatsBase.coef, m)
    V = _try(StatsBase.vcov, m)

    println("scalars:")
    for (k, v) in pairs(scalars)
        ismissing(v) && continue
        println("  e($k) = ", v isa Real ? round(v; sigdigits=8) : v)
    end
    println("\nmacros:")
    for (k, v) in pairs(macros)
        ismissing(v) && continue
        println("  e($k) : \"", v, "\"")
    end
    println("\nmatrices:")
    if !ismissing(b)
        names_ = try StatsBase.coefnames(m) catch; ["x$i" for i in eachindex(b)] end
        println("  e(b)  : 1 x $(length(b))")
        PrettyTables.pretty_table(reshape(b, 1, :); header = names_)
    end
    if !ismissing(V)
        println("  e(V)  : $(size(V,1)) x $(size(V,2))")
        names_ = try StatsBase.coefnames(m) catch; ["x$i" for i in 1:size(V,1)] end
        PrettyTables.pretty_table(V; header = names_, row_labels = names_)
    end
    return (; scalars, macros, b, V)
end

"""
    ttest_by(df, yvar, byvar; level=0.95, unequal=false)

Stata-style `ttest <yvar>, by(<byvar>)`. Defaults to equal-variance (pooled),
like Stata; pass `unequal=true` for Welch. Prints the two-group summary table,
the difference with its CI, and one- / two-sided p-values, then returns the
underlying HypothesisTests object.
"""
function ttest_by(df, yvar::Symbol, byvar::Symbol; level::Float64=0.95, unequal::Bool=false)
    mask = .!ismissing.(df[!, yvar]) .& .!ismissing.(df[!, byvar])
    y   = df[mask, yvar]
    bv  = [_rawval(v) for v in df[mask, byvar]]
    levs = sort(unique(bv))
    length(levs) == 2 || error("by() must have exactly 2 non-missing levels, got $(length(levs))")
    g0 = Float64.(y[bv .== levs[1]])
    g1 = Float64.(y[bv .== levs[2]])

    tt = unequal ? HypothesisTests.UnequalVarianceTTest(g0, g1) :
                   HypothesisTests.EqualVarianceTTest(g0, g1)

    function grow(x, lbl)
        n  = length(x); μ = Statistics.mean(x); σ = n > 1 ? Statistics.std(x) : NaN
        se = σ / sqrt(n)
        tc = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(max(n-1, 1)), 1 - (1-level)/2)
        (; Group=string(lbl), Obs=n, Mean=μ, StdErr=se, StdDev=σ,
           CI_lo=μ - tc*se, CI_hi=μ + tc*se)
    end

    ci = HypothesisTests.confint(tt; level=level)
    rows = [grow(g0, levs[1]),
            grow(g1, levs[2]),
            grow(vcat(g0, g1), "combined"),
            (; Group="diff", Obs=length(g0)+length(g1),
                Mean=tt.xbar, StdErr=tt.stderr, StdDev=NaN,
                CI_lo=ci[1], CI_hi=ci[2])]

    println(unequal ? "Two-sample t test with unequal variances" :
                      "Two-sample t test with equal variances")
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(DataFrames.DataFrame(rows))

    Printf.@printf("\ndiff = mean(%s) - mean(%s)    t  = %.4f\n",
                   levs[1], levs[2], tt.t)
    Printf.@printf("Ho: diff = 0                   df = %g\n\n", tt.df)
    println("   Ha: diff < 0          Ha: diff != 0         Ha: diff > 0")
    Printf.@printf("Pr(T < t) = %.4f    Pr(|T| > |t|) = %.4f    Pr(T > t) = %.4f\n",
                   HypothesisTests.pvalue(tt; tail=:left),
                   HypothesisTests.pvalue(tt),
                   HypothesisTests.pvalue(tt; tail=:right))
    return tt
end

"""
    wald_test(model, vars; values=zeros(length(vars)))

Stata-style `test <varlist>` after a regression — joint Wald test of
H0: βⱼ = value[j] for each j in `vars`. Uses the model's vcov (so it
respects robust/cluster SE if fitted that way). Reports the F statistic
F(q, dof_resid) and p-value, like Stata's `test` after `regress`.
"""
function wald_test(model, vars::AbstractVector; values=zeros(length(vars)))
    β     = StatsBase.coef(model)
    V     = StatsBase.vcov(model)
    names = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(model))
    vs    = string.(vars)
    idx   = [findfirst(==(v), names) for v in vs]
    miss  = findall(x -> x === nothing, idx)
    isempty(miss) || error("variable(s) not found in model: $(vs[miss])")

    # Drop coefficients with non-finite β or vcov row (collinear / dropped),
    # mirroring Stata's `test`/`testparm` behaviour.
    vals = collect(values)
    bad  = findall(k -> !isfinite(β[idx[k]]) || any(!isfinite, V[idx[k], idx]),
                   eachindex(idx))
    if !isempty(bad)
        @warn "Dropping collinear / non-finite coefficient(s): $(vs[bad])"
        keep = setdiff(eachindex(idx), bad)
        vs, idx, vals = vs[keep], idx[keep], vals[keep]
    end
    isempty(vs) && error("No testable coefficients remain.")

    q     = length(vs)
    Rβ    = β[idx] .- vals
    W     = Rβ' * (V[idx, idx] \ Rβ)
    dfr   = StatsBase.dof_residual(model)
    Fstat = W / q
    p     = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(q, dfr), Fstat)

    println("Wald F test (respects model's vcov):")
    for (k, v) in enumerate(vs)
        Printf.@printf("  ( %d)  %s = %g\n", k, v, values[k])
    end
    Printf.@printf("\n       F( %d, %g) = %.4f\n", q, dfr, Fstat)
    Printf.@printf("            Prob > F = %.4f\n", p)
    return (; F=Fstat, df1=q, df2=dfr, p=p, chi2=W)
end

"""
    margins_stata(β, V, X; at=nothing, at_labels=nothing,
                  over=nothing, over_levels=nothing,
                  mean_fn=:exp, vce_label="Robust", level=0.95,
                  expression="Predicted number of events, predict()",
                  name="_cons")

Stata-style `margins` with delta-method SE. Three modes:

  - `at = nothing, over = nothing` → **Predictive margins** (sample average of
    `predict` over X). Header: "Predictive margins".

  - `at = c` (length-k vector incl. the constant slot) → **Adjusted predictions**
    at the single point. Pass `at_labels = [:var => v, ...]` to populate the
    "At:" block. Header: "Adjusted predictions".

  - `over = "var" => idx` (factor name and its column index in X) →
    **Predictive margins by factor level**: for each level k, set
    X[:, idx] .= k for ALL observations and compute the sample-average
    margin. Default levels = `sort(unique(X[:, idx]))`; override with
    `over_levels = [...]`. The output is a multi-row table with a sub-header
    for the factor name and one row per level.

For mean_fn=:exp, gradient = (1/N) Σ μ_i x_i (averaged) or μ·c (at-point);
SE = sqrt(g' V g). Pass V_rob (n/(n-1)-corrected) to match Stata's margins
after `poisson, vce(robust)`.
"""
function margins_stata(β::AbstractVector, V::AbstractMatrix, X::AbstractMatrix;
                       at::Union{Nothing,AbstractVector}=nothing,
                       at_labels::Union{Nothing,AbstractVector}=nothing,
                       over::Union{Nothing,Pair}=nothing,
                       over_levels::Union{Nothing,AbstractVector}=nothing,
                       mean_fn::Symbol=:exp, vce_label::String="Robust",
                       level::Float64=0.95, name::String="_cons",
                       expression::String="Predicted number of events, predict()")
    n_sample, _ = size(X)

    g9 = function(x; w_::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w_)
        sig_use = sig
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        # Stata's %9.0g effective rule: cap pre-strip at 9 chars EXCEPT for
        # negative |x|<1 where cap=10 (the strip will save 1 char). Without
        # this, negative |x|<1 values lose a digit vs Stata's output.
        cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 10 : 9
        while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
            sig_use -= 1
            s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        end
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        lpad(s, w_)
    end
    commafmt(num::Integer) = begin
        s = string(abs(num)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1; push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3; end
        (num < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end
    function _summary_row(margin, se)
        z      = margin / se
        p      = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z)))
        crit   = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
        (margin, se, z, p, margin - crit*se, margin + crit*se)
    end
    function _compute_margin_at_X(Xm)
        if mean_fn == :exp
            μ    = exp.(Xm * β)
            gvec = vec((μ' * Xm) ./ size(Xm, 1))
            margin = Statistics.mean(μ)
        elseif mean_fn == :identity
            μ    = Xm * β
            gvec = vec(Statistics.mean(Xm, dims = 1))
            margin = Statistics.mean(μ)
        else
            error("mean_fn=$mean_fn not supported (use :exp or :identity)")
        end
        margin, sqrt(gvec' * V * gvec)
    end

    # Determine title and rows to print
    if over !== nothing
        title = "Predictive margins"
        factor_name, factor_idx = over
        levels_to_use = over_levels === nothing ?
                        sort(unique(X[:, factor_idx])) : collect(over_levels)
        rows = Tuple[]
        for lev in levels_to_use
            Xm = copy(X)
            Xm[:, factor_idx] .= lev
            m, s = _compute_margin_at_X(Xm)
            push!(rows, (lev, _summary_row(m, s)...))
        end
    elseif at === nothing
        title = "Predictive margins"
        m, s  = _compute_margin_at_X(X)
        rows  = [(name, _summary_row(m, s)...)]
    else
        title = "Adjusted predictions"
        c = collect(at)
        length(c) == length(β) ||
            error("at must have length(β)=$(length(β)), got $(length(c))")
        if mean_fn == :exp
            margin = exp(LinearAlgebra.dot(c, β))
            gvec   = margin .* c
        elseif mean_fn == :identity
            margin = LinearAlgebra.dot(c, β)
            gvec   = c
        else
            error("mean_fn=$mean_fn not supported (use :exp or :identity)")
        end
        rows = [(name, _summary_row(margin, sqrt(gvec' * V * gvec))...)]
    end

    println()
    Printf.@printf("%-57s%-13s = %5s\n",
                   title, "Number of obs", commafmt(n_sample))
    Printf.@printf("Model VCE: %s\n", vce_label)
    println()
    Printf.@printf("Expression: %s\n", expression)
    if at_labels !== nothing && !isempty(at_labels)
        max_name_w = maximum(length(string(p_.first)) for p_ in at_labels)
        val_strs   = [Printf.@sprintf("%g", float(p_.second)) for p_ in at_labels]
        max_val_w  = maximum(length, val_strs)
        for (i, p_) in enumerate(at_labels)
            prefix = i == 1 ? "At: " : "    "
            Printf.@printf("%s%-*s = %s\n", prefix, max_name_w,
                           string(p_.first), lpad(val_strs[i], max_val_w))
        end
    end
    println()
    println("-"^78)
    println("             |            Delta-method")
    println("             |     Margin   std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95% conf. interval]")
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    if over !== nothing
        Printf.@printf("%12s |\n", first(over))   # factor sub-header row
        for (lev, m, s, z, p, lo, hi) in rows
            lev_str = string(lev isa AbstractFloat && lev == floor(lev) ?
                             Int(lev) : lev)
            Printf.@printf("%11s  | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                           lev_str, g9(m; w_=10), g9(s; w_=9),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p),
                           g9(lo; w_=9), g9(hi; w_=10))
        end
    else
        for (lab, m, s, z, p, lo, hi) in rows
            Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                           lab, g9(m; w_=10), g9(s; w_=9),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z),
                           Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p),
                           g9(lo; w_=9), g9(hi; w_=10))
        end
    end
    println("-"^78)
    return rows
end

"""
    setup_mus10(df10; quiet=true)

Convenience helper for chapter 10: fits the standard mus10 Poisson model
(docvis ~ private + chronic + female + income, vce(robust)) once and returns
a NamedTuple with everything downstream chunks need:

  - `.df` — cleaned subset of df10 with non-missing docvis/private/chronic/
    female/income/age (so panels with `age` for cluster SE also work)
  - `.fit` — full poisson_stata return (model, β_glm, V_glm, X_glm, etc.)
  - `.β`, `.V`, `.X`, `.n`, `.k` — convenience aliases in Stata-display order
    (slopes first, _cons last) for direct use with margins_stata,
    margins_dydx_stata, nlcom_stata, lincom_stata, etc.

Re-running any §10.5 / §10.6 chunk after a chunk-1 helper change picks up
the new helpers automatically — every downstream chunk starts with this
helper, so there's no stale β_v / V_rob / X_v hiding in some other cell.
"""
function setup_mus10(df10; quiet::Bool=true)
    df10c = DataFrames.dropmissing(df10[:,
        [:docvis, :private, :chronic, :female, :income, :age]])
    fit = poisson_stata(df10c,
        GLM.@formula(docvis ~ private + chronic + female + income);
        vce = :robust, quiet = quiet)
    return (; df = df10c, fit,
              β = fit.β, V = fit.V, X = fit.X, n = fit.n, k = fit.k)
end

"""
    margins_dydx_stata(β, V, X; factors, continuous,
                       atmean=false, at=nothing, at_labels=nothing,
                       noatlegend=false,
                       level=0.95, vce_label="Robust",
                       expression="Predicted number of events, predict()")

Unified Stata-style `margins, dydx(*)` — three modes via keyword args:

  - default (`atmean=false, at=nothing`) → **AME** (Stata: `margins, dydx(*)`).
    Title: "Average marginal effects". For each obs, compute ∂ŷ_i/∂x_j or the
    discrete change, then average over the sample.
  - `atmean=true` → **MEM** (Stata: `margins, dydx(*) atmean`). Title:
    "Conditional marginal effects". Evaluated at the sample-mean vector x̄.
  - `at=c` (length-k vector) → **MER** (Stata: `margins, dydx(*) at(...)`).
    Title: "Conditional marginal effects". Evaluated at the user-specified
    point c. Pass `at_labels = [:var=>v, ...]` for the "At:" block, or set
    `noatlegend=true` to suppress it (mirrors Stata's `noatlegend` option).

For each variable:
  - **Factor** (binary 0/1): discrete change ME = μ(x with j=1) − μ(x with j=0).
    Gradient: μ_1·x_1 − μ_0·x_0. For AME, this is averaged across observations.
  - **Continuous**: ME = β_j · μ(x). Gradient: e_j · μ + β_j · μ · x.

`factors` and `continuous` are vectors of `(name, col_idx::Int)` tuples giving
the variable name (no "1." prefix) and its column index in X.
"""
function margins_dydx_stata(β::AbstractVector, V::AbstractMatrix,
                            X::AbstractMatrix;
                            factors::AbstractVector,
                            continuous::AbstractVector,
                            kind::Symbol=:dydx,
                            atmean::Bool=false,
                            at::Union{Nothing,AbstractVector}=nothing,
                            at_labels::Union{Nothing,AbstractVector}=nothing,
                            noatlegend::Bool=false,
                            interactions::AbstractVector=NamedTuple[],
                            level::Float64=0.95,
                            vce_label::String="Robust",
                            expression::String="Predicted number of events, predict()")
    # `interactions` lets the helper handle product interaction columns
    # correctly. Each entry is a NamedTuple `(col=k, base=[i, j, ...])`
    # meaning column k of X is the elementwise product of columns
    # `base`. When a factor variable in `base` flips 0↔1 the helper
    # rebuilds col k from the (possibly modified) base columns; for a
    # continuous variable in `base`, the chain rule for ∂μ/∂x_j picks
    # up an extra `β_k · prod(other base cols)` term.
    # `kind` selects the column header AND the formula for *continuous*
    # variables (factor variables always use the discrete change, since
    # ey/ex of a 0/1 dummy is ill-defined). For Poisson with log link:
    #   :dydx → β_j · μ          (gradient e_j·μ + β_j·μ·x)
    #   :eyex → β_j · x_j        (gradient e_j·x_j;  ε doesn't depend on μ)
    #   :eydx → β_j              (gradient e_j;     semi-elasticity)
    #   :dyex → β_j · μ · x_j    (gradient depends on μ)
    kind in (:dydx, :eyex, :eydx, :dyex) ||
        error("kind must be :dydx, :eyex, :eydx, or :dyex; got $kind")
    kind_label = kind == :dydx ? "dy/dx" :
                 kind == :eyex ? "ey/ex" :
                 kind == :eydx ? "ey/dx" : "dy/ex"
    n, k = size(X)

    g9 = function(x; w_::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w_)
        sig_use = sig
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        # Stata's %9.0g effective rule: cap pre-strip at 9 chars EXCEPT for
        # negative |x|<1 where cap=10 (the strip will save 1 char). Without
        # this, negative |x|<1 values lose a digit vs Stata's output.
        cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 10 : 9
        while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
            sig_use -= 1
            s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        end
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        lpad(s, w_)
    end
    g_atval = function(x; sig::Int=7)
        sig_use = sig
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        # Stata's %9.0g effective rule: cap pre-strip at 9 chars EXCEPT for
        # negative |x|<1 where cap=10 (the strip will save 1 char). Without
        # this, negative |x|<1 values lose a digit vs Stata's output.
        cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 10 : 9
        while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
            sig_use -= 1
            s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        end
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        s
    end
    commafmt(num::Integer) = begin
        s = string(abs(num)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1; push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3; end
        (num < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    _summary(dydx, se) = begin
        z = dydx / se
        p = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z)))
        (z, p, dydx - crit*se, dydx + crit*se)
    end

    # Determine evaluation mode
    if at !== nothing
        mode  = :atpoint
        x_eval = collect(at)
        length(x_eval) == k ||
            error("at must have length(β)=$k, got $(length(x_eval))")
    elseif atmean
        mode  = :atmean
        x_eval = vec(Statistics.mean(X, dims = 1))
    else
        mode  = :average
        x_eval = nothing
    end

    title = mode == :average ? "Average marginal effects" :
                               "Conditional marginal effects"

    # _refresh_interactions! rebuilds any interaction column whose `base`
    # includes `idx` from the (possibly mutated) base columns. Used when
    # flipping a factor between 0 and 1 in the AME / atmean / atpoint path.
    function _refresh_interactions!(Xm::AbstractMatrix, idx::Integer)
        for intr in interactions
            if idx in intr.base
                col = ones(size(Xm, 1))
                for b in intr.base
                    col .*= Xm[:, b]
                end
                Xm[:, intr.col] .= col
            end
        end
    end
    function _refresh_interactions_vec!(xv::AbstractVector, idx::Integer)
        for intr in interactions
            if idx in intr.base
                v = 1.0
                for b in intr.base
                    v *= xv[b]
                end
                xv[intr.col] = v
            end
        end
    end

    # Compute each variable's dy/dx and its delta-method SE
    rows = Tuple[]
    for (nm, idx) in factors
        if mode == :average
            X0 = copy(X); X0[:, idx] .= 0.0; _refresh_interactions!(X0, idx)
            X1 = copy(X); X1[:, idx] .= 1.0; _refresh_interactions!(X1, idx)
            μ0 = exp.(X0 * β); μ1 = exp.(X1 * β)
            dydx = Statistics.mean(μ1 .- μ0)
            gvec = vec((μ1' * X1 .- μ0' * X0) ./ n)
        else
            x0 = copy(x_eval); x0[idx] = 0.0; _refresh_interactions_vec!(x0, idx)
            x1 = copy(x_eval); x1[idx] = 1.0; _refresh_interactions_vec!(x1, idx)
            μ0 = exp(LinearAlgebra.dot(x0, β))
            μ1 = exp(LinearAlgebra.dot(x1, β))
            dydx = μ1 - μ0
            gvec = μ1 .* x1 .- μ0 .* x0
        end
        se = sqrt(gvec' * V * gvec)
        push!(rows, ("1.$nm", dydx, se, _summary(dydx, se)...))
    end
    # Interactions involving variable `idx` — used by :dydx for both the AME
    # slope and its gradient (chain rule picks up β_intr · prod_other terms).
    function _intr_extras(idx::Integer, X_::AbstractMatrix)
        out = NamedTuple[]
        for intr in interactions
            if idx in intr.base
                po = ones(size(X_, 1))
                for b in intr.base
                    b == idx && continue
                    po .*= X_[:, b]
                end
                push!(out, (col = intr.col, prod_other = po))
            end
        end
        out
    end
    function _intr_extras_vec(idx::Integer, xv::AbstractVector)
        out = NamedTuple[]
        for intr in interactions
            if idx in intr.base
                v = 1.0
                for b in intr.base
                    b == idx && continue
                    v *= xv[b]
                end
                push!(out, (col = intr.col, prod_other_at = v))
            end
        end
        out
    end

    for (nm, idx) in continuous
        if mode == :average
            μ_all = exp.(X * β)
            μ̄    = Statistics.mean(μ_all)
            x̄_j  = Statistics.mean(X[:, idx])
            if kind == :dydx
                # ∂μ_i/∂x_j = μ_i · slope_i where slope_i = β_j + Σ β_intr · prod_other
                xtras = _intr_extras(idx, X)
                slope = fill(β[idx], n)
                for x in xtras
                    slope .+= β[x.col] .* x.prod_other
                end
                dydx = Statistics.mean(μ_all .* slope)
                gvec = vec((μ_all .* slope)' * X) ./ n
                gvec[idx] += sum(μ_all) / n
                for x in xtras
                    gvec[x.col] += sum(μ_all .* x.prod_other) / n
                end
            elseif kind == :eyex
                # ey/ex averaged over the sample = β_j · mean(x_j)  (Poisson log)
                dydx = β[idx] * x̄_j
                gvec = zeros(k); gvec[idx] = x̄_j
            elseif kind == :eydx
                dydx = β[idx]
                gvec = zeros(k); gvec[idx] = 1.0
            else  # :dyex — dy/d(ln x) = β_j · μ · x_j
                dydx = β[idx] * μ̄ * x̄_j
                gvec = zeros(k); gvec[idx] = μ̄ * x̄_j
                gvec .+= β[idx] * x̄_j .* vec((μ_all' * X) ./ n)
            end
        else
            μ_at = exp(LinearAlgebra.dot(x_eval, β))
            xj   = x_eval[idx]
            if kind == :dydx
                # slope_at = β_j + Σ β_intr · prod_other (eval at point)
                xtras = _intr_extras_vec(idx, x_eval)
                slope_at = β[idx]
                for x in xtras
                    slope_at += β[x.col] * x.prod_other_at
                end
                dydx = μ_at * slope_at
                gvec = μ_at * slope_at .* x_eval
                gvec[idx] += μ_at
                for x in xtras
                    gvec[x.col] += μ_at * x.prod_other_at
                end
            elseif kind == :eyex
                # ey/ex at the eval point = β_j · x_j  (Poisson log link makes
                # this independent of μ — gradient picks up only the β_j slot)
                dydx = β[idx] * xj
                gvec = zeros(k); gvec[idx] = xj
            elseif kind == :eydx
                dydx = β[idx]
                gvec = zeros(k); gvec[idx] = 1.0
            else  # :dyex
                dydx = β[idx] * μ_at * xj
                gvec = zeros(k); gvec[idx] = μ_at * xj
                gvec .+= β[idx] * μ_at * xj .* x_eval
            end
        end
        se = sqrt(gvec' * V * gvec)
        push!(rows, (string(nm), dydx, se, _summary(dydx, se)...))
    end

    dydx_names = vcat(["1.$nm" for (nm, _) in factors],
                      [string(nm) for (nm, _) in continuous])

    println()
    Printf.@printf("%-57s%-13s = %5s\n",
                   title, "Number of obs", commafmt(n))
    Printf.@printf("Model VCE: %s\n", vce_label)
    println()
    Printf.@printf("Expression: %s\n", expression)
    Printf.@printf("%s wrt:  %s\n", kind_label, join(dydx_names, " "))

    # "At:" block (only for atmean / atpoint, and only if not suppressed)
    if !noatlegend && mode != :average
        if mode == :atmean
            at_lines = Tuple[]
            for (nm, idx) in factors
                m1 = Statistics.mean(X[:, idx])
                push!(at_lines, ("0.$nm", 1.0 - m1))
                push!(at_lines, ("1.$nm", m1))
            end
            for (nm, idx) in continuous
                push!(at_lines, (string(nm), x_eval[idx]))
            end
            max_name_w = maximum(length(t[1]) for t in at_lines)
            for (i, (nm, val)) in enumerate(at_lines)
                prefix = i == 1 ? "At: " : "    "
                Printf.@printf("%s%-*s = %s (mean)\n",
                               prefix, max_name_w, nm, g_atval(val))
            end
        elseif at_labels !== nothing && !isempty(at_labels)
            max_name_w = maximum(length(string(p_.first)) for p_ in at_labels)
            val_strs   = [Printf.@sprintf("%g", float(p_.second)) for p_ in at_labels]
            max_val_w  = maximum(length, val_strs)
            for (i, p_) in enumerate(at_labels)
                prefix = i == 1 ? "At: " : "    "
                Printf.@printf("%s%-*s = %s\n",
                               prefix, max_name_w, string(p_.first),
                               lpad(val_strs[i], max_val_w))
            end
        end
    end

    println()
    println("-"^78)
    println("             |            Delta-method")
    Printf.@printf("             |      %s   std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95%% conf. interval]\n", kind_label)
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    for (lab, dydx, se, z, p, lo, hi) in rows
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       lab, g9(dydx; w_=10), g9(se; w_=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p),
                       g9(lo; w_=9), g9(hi; w_=10))
    end
    println("-"^78)
    !isempty(factors) && println("Note: dy/dx for factor levels is the discrete change from the base level.")
    return rows
end

# Back-compat alias for the prior name (deprecated — prefer margins_dydx_stata).
margins_dydx_atmean_stata(args...; kwargs...) =
    margins_dydx_stata(args...; atmean=true, kwargs...)

"""
    mfx_stata(β, V, X; factors, continuous, model_name="poisson",
              expression="Predicted number of events (predict)",
              level=0.95, evaluate=:atmean, at=nothing)

Stata-style legacy `mfx, dydx varlist(...)` output. Same dy/dx + delta-method
SE math as `margins_dydx_stata` but in the older `mfx` table layout:

  - Header line `Marginal effects after <model_name>`
  - `y = <expression>` and `=  <μ at eval point>`
  - Columns: variable | dy/dx | Std. err. | z | P>|z| | [95% C.I.] | X

The trailing `X` column shows the value of each regressor at the evaluation
point (sample mean by default; explicit point if `at=...`). Use this when
matching outputs from Stata 11/12 textbooks; for newer code, prefer
`margins_dydx_stata`.
"""
function mfx_stata(β::AbstractVector, V::AbstractMatrix, X::AbstractMatrix;
                   factors::AbstractVector, continuous::AbstractVector,
                   kind::Symbol=:dydx,
                   model_name::String="poisson",
                   expression::String="Predicted number of events (predict)",
                   level::Float64=0.95,
                   evaluate::Symbol=:atmean,
                   at::Union{Nothing,AbstractVector}=nothing)
    kind in (:dydx, :eyex, :eydx, :dyex) ||
        error("mfx_stata kind must be :dydx, :eyex, :eydx, or :dyex; got $kind")
    # Stata mfx: :dydx → "Marginal effects"; :eyex/:eydx/:dyex → "Elasticities"
    title_prefix = kind == :dydx ? "Marginal effects" : "Elasticities"
    kind_label   = kind == :dydx ? "dy/dx" :
                   kind == :eyex ? "ey/ex" :
                   kind == :eydx ? "ey/dx" : "dy/ex"

    n, k = size(X)
    x_eval = at !== nothing ? collect(at) :
             evaluate == :atmean ? vec(Statistics.mean(X, dims=1)) :
             error("mfx_stata supports evaluate=:atmean or `at=<vector>` only")
    μ_at = exp(LinearAlgebra.dot(x_eval, β))
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)

    # Per-column width caps matched to Stata's legacy mfx output. Each column
    # gets up to `sig` sig figs but reduces precision to fit `cap` chars
    # (counting the leading "0." for |x|<1, which is then stripped on output).
    _g(x; sig::Int=7, cap::Int=0) = begin
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return "."
        sig_use = sig
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        if cap > 0
            while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
                sig_use -= 1
                s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
            end
        end
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        s
    end

    rows = Tuple[]
    for (nm, idx) in factors
        # Discrete change for both kinds (mfx convention; `*` flags it)
        x0 = copy(x_eval); x0[idx] = 0.0
        x1 = copy(x_eval); x1[idx] = 1.0
        μ0 = exp(LinearAlgebra.dot(x0, β))
        μ1 = exp(LinearAlgebra.dot(x1, β))
        val  = μ1 - μ0
        gvec = μ1 .* x1 .- μ0 .* x0
        se   = sqrt(gvec' * V * gvec)
        push!(rows, ("$nm*", val, se, x_eval[idx]))
    end
    for (nm, idx) in continuous
        if kind == :dydx
            val  = β[idx] * μ_at
            gvec = zeros(k); gvec[idx] = μ_at
            gvec .+= β[idx] * μ_at .* x_eval
        elseif kind == :eyex
            # ε = β_j · x_j  (gradient just e_j · x_j for log link)
            val  = β[idx] * x_eval[idx]
            gvec = zeros(k); gvec[idx] = x_eval[idx]
        elseif kind == :eydx
            # semi-elasticity ∂lnμ/∂x = β_j (just the coefficient)
            val  = β[idx]
            gvec = zeros(k); gvec[idx] = 1.0
        else  # :dyex — ∂μ/∂lnx = β_j · μ · x_j  (semi-elasticity for log link)
            xj   = x_eval[idx]
            val  = β[idx] * μ_at * xj
            gvec = zeros(k); gvec[idx] = μ_at * xj
            gvec .+= β[idx] * μ_at * xj .* x_eval
        end
        se = sqrt(gvec' * V * gvec)
        push!(rows, (string(nm), val, se, x_eval[idx]))
    end

    println()
    Printf.@printf("%s after %s\n", title_prefix, model_name)
    Printf.@printf("      y  = %s\n", expression)
    Printf.@printf("         =  %.8g\n", μ_at)
    println("-"^78)
    Printf.@printf("variable |      %s    Std. err.     z    P>|z|  [    95%% C.I.   ]      X\n",
                   kind_label)
    println("-"^9, "+", "-"^68)
    for (nm, val, se, xj) in rows
        z = val / se
        p = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z)))
        lo, hi = val - crit*se, val + crit*se
        Printf.@printf("%8s |%11s%12s%8.2f%8.3f%10s%9s%10s\n",
                       nm,
                       lpad(_g(val; sig=7, cap=9), 11),
                       lpad(_g(se;  sig=7, cap=7), 12),
                       z, p,
                       lpad(_g(lo;  sig=7, cap=8), 10),
                       lpad(_g(hi;  sig=7, cap=8),  9),
                       lpad(_g(xj;  sig=7, cap=7), 10))
    end
    println("-"^78)
    !isempty(factors) &&
        println("(*) $kind_label is for discrete change of dummy variable from 0 to 1")
    return rows
end

"""
    lincom_stata(w, β, V; eform=false, name="(1)", depvar="", level=0.95)

Stata-style `lincom <linear combination>` (optionally `, eform`). Computes
L = w'β, its delta-method SE = sqrt(w' V w), z = L/SE(L), two-sided p, and
CI on the linear scale. With `eform=true`, reports exp(L) and exp(L)·SE(L)
(header changes to "exp(b)"), and CI = exp(L ± z·SE(L)) (asymmetric on the
exp scale) — but **z and p stay on the LINEAR scale**, the key difference
from `nlcom` of exp(w'β): lincom's z is L/SE(L), nlcom's is exp(L)/SE_eform.

Pass β and V from the relevant model (e.g. n/(n-1)-corrected `V_rob` to
match Stata's `vce(robust)` after `poisson`).
"""
function lincom_stata(w::AbstractVector, β::AbstractVector, V::AbstractMatrix;
                      eform::Bool=false, name::String="(1)",
                      depvar::String="", level::Float64=0.95)
    L    = LinearAlgebra.dot(w, β)
    seL  = sqrt(w' * V * w)
    z    = L / seL
    p    = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z)))
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)

    if eform
        val, se = exp(L), exp(L) * seL
        lo, hi  = exp(L - crit * seL), exp(L + crit * seL)
        clab    = "exp(b)"
    else
        val, se = L, seL
        lo, hi  = L - crit * seL, L + crit * seL
        clab    = "Coefficient"
    end

    g9 = function(x; w_::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w_)
        sig_use = sig
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        # Stata's %9.0g effective rule: cap pre-strip at 9 chars EXCEPT for
        # negative |x|<1 where cap=10 (the strip will save 1 char). Without
        # this, negative |x|<1 values lose a digit vs Stata's output.
        cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 10 : 9
        while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
            sig_use -= 1
            s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        end
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        lpad(s, w_)
    end

    # Make the label area exactly 13 chars so "Std. err." always lands at col 29.
    label_field = rpad(lpad(clab, 10), 13)
    println("-"^78)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %sStd. err.      z    P>|z|     [%g%% conf. interval]\n",
                   depvar, label_field, 100*level)
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                   name, g9(val; w_=10), g9(se; w_=9),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p),
                   g9(lo; w_=9), g9(hi; w_=10))
    println("-"^78)
    return (; val, se, z, p, ci=(lo, hi))
end

"""
    nlcom_stata(f, β, V; name="_nl_1", depvar="", level=0.95)

Stata-style `nlcom <expression>` — point estimate of an arbitrary smooth
function f(β) of the coefficient vector, with delta-method SE using vcov V:

    SE(f(β̂)) = sqrt( ∇f(β̂)' · V · ∇f(β̂) )

Reports a Stata-style table with z = f(β̂)/SE, two-sided p, and a 95% normal CI.
The gradient is computed by central finite differences (so no extra dependency
on ForwardDiff). Pass β and V from the relevant model — e.g. for a Poisson fit
with vce(robust), use the n/(n-1)-corrected `V_rob` rather than the OIM vcov.
"""
function nlcom_stata(f::Function, β::AbstractVector, V::AbstractMatrix;
                     name::String="_nl_1", depvar::String="",
                     level::Float64=0.95)
    val = f(β)
    # Central finite-difference gradient (relative step)
    n_p = length(β)
    g   = zeros(n_p)
    for j in 1:n_p
        h    = sqrt(eps(Float64)) * max(abs(β[j]), 1.0)
        βp   = collect(β); βm = collect(β)
        βp[j] += h; βm[j] -= h
        g[j]  = (f(βp) - f(βm)) / (2h)
    end
    se   = sqrt(g' * V * g)
    z    = val / se
    p    = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z)))
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    lo   = val - crit * se
    hi   = val + crit * se

    g9 = function(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        if 0 < abs(x) < 1
            s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
        return lpad(s, w)
    end

    println("-"^78)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | Coefficient  Std. err.      z    P>|z|     [%g%% conf. interval]\n",
                   depvar, 100*level)
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                   name, g9(val; w=10), g9(se; w=9),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p),
                   g9(lo; w=9), g9(hi; w=10))
    println("-"^78)
    return (; val, se, z, p, ci=(lo, hi))
end

"""
    margins_dydx(model, df, varname; level=0.95)

Stata-style `margins, dydx(<varname>)` for a linear model fit on `df`.
Supports the common continuous×categorical form `varname + g + varname & g`:

    AME = β_varname + Σ_k (n_k / N) · β_{varname × g = k}

The delta-method SE uses the model's own vcov. If that vcov has non-finite
entries on the rows the AME depends on (common with degenerate `Vcov.robust()`
on sparse categoricals), the function transparently refits the same formula
with **classical OLS vcov** and uses that for the SE only — so the result
never returns NaN when the AME itself is defined. A flag in the output
indicates which vcov was used.
"""
function margins_dydx(model, df, varname::Symbol; level::Float64=0.95)
    β   = StatsBase.coef(model)
    V   = StatsBase.vcov(model)
    nm  = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(model))
    N   = Int(StatsBase.nobs(model))
    vstr = string(varname)

    idx_main = findfirst(==(vstr), nm)
    idx_main === nothing && error("'$vstr' not found as a main effect in model")

    # Build weight vector so that AME = w'β
    # For a linear model with interactions: ∂E[y]/∂x = β_x + Σ_k β_{x×D_k} · P(D_k)
    # where P(D_k) = proportion of observations at level k.
    w = zeros(length(β))
    w[idx_main] = 1.0
    re_a = Regex("^\\Q$vstr\\E\\s*&\\s*(.+?):\\s*(.+)\$")
    re_b = Regex("^(.+?):\\s*(.+?)\\s*&\\s*\\Q$vstr\\E\$")
    for (i, n) in enumerate(nm)
        m = match(re_a, n); m === nothing && (m = match(re_b, n))
        m === nothing && continue
        factor_col = Symbol(String(m.captures[1]))
        level_str  = String(m.captures[2])
        hasproperty(df, factor_col) || continue
        col = df[!, factor_col]
        lev = tryparse(Float64, level_str)
        n_k = lev === nothing ?
              sum(!ismissing(v) && string(_rawval(v)) == level_str for v in col) :
              sum(!ismissing(v) && _rawval(v) == lev for v in col)
        w[i] = n_k / N
    end

    ame = LinearAlgebra.dot(w, β)

    # Delta-method SE: Var(w'β) = w' V w
    # If robust vcov has NaN, build X manually and compute HC1 sandwich.
    Vw = V * w
    vcov_label = "Robust"
    if !all(isfinite, Vw)
        # Manual X matrix construction (same approach as testparm).
        # Parse coefnames to identify: main effects, factor dummies, interactions.
        resp = Symbol(split(string(FixedEffectModels.formula(model).lhs), "(")[end] |>
                      s -> replace(s, ")" => ""))
        # Find all unique raw column names used
        all_cols = Symbol[]
        factor_cols = Symbol[]
        for n in nm
            n == "(Intercept)" && continue
            if occursin("&", n)
                # Interaction: "var1 & factor: level"
                parts = split(n, r"\s*&\s*")
                for p in parts
                    if occursin(":", p)
                        fc = Symbol(strip(split(p, ":")[1]))
                        fc in factor_cols || push!(factor_cols, fc)
                        fc in all_cols || push!(all_cols, fc)
                    else
                        s = Symbol(strip(p))
                        s in all_cols || push!(all_cols, s)
                    end
                end
            elseif occursin(":", n)
                fc = Symbol(strip(split(n, ":")[1]))
                fc in factor_cols || push!(factor_cols, fc)
                fc in all_cols || push!(all_cols, fc)
            else
                s = Symbol(strip(n))
                s in all_cols || push!(all_cols, s)
            end
        end
        push!(all_cols, resp)

        df_c = dropmissing(df, all_cols)
        nn = nrow(df_c)

        # Build X column by column, matching coefnames order
        X_cols = Vector{Vector{Float64}}()
        cn_new = String[]
        for n in nm
            if n == "(Intercept)"
                push!(X_cols, ones(nn)); push!(cn_new, n)
            elseif occursin("&", n)
                # Interaction term
                parts = split(n, r"\s*&\s*")
                col_vec = ones(nn)
                for p in parts
                    p = strip(p)
                    if occursin(":", p)
                        fc, lv = strip.(split(p, ":"; limit=2))
                        vals = [_rawval(v) for v in df_c[!, Symbol(fc)]]
                        lev = tryparse(Float64, lv)
                        col_vec .*= lev === nothing ?
                            Float64.(string.(vals) .== lv) :
                            Float64.(vals .== lev)
                    else
                        col_vec .*= Float64.(df_c[!, Symbol(p)])
                    end
                end
                if sum(col_vec .!= 0) >= 2
                    push!(X_cols, col_vec); push!(cn_new, n)
                end
            elseif occursin(":", n)
                fc, lv = strip.(split(n, ":"; limit=2))
                vals = [_rawval(v) for v in df_c[!, Symbol(fc)]]
                lev = tryparse(Float64, lv)
                col_vec = lev === nothing ?
                    Float64.(string.(vals) .== lv) :
                    Float64.(vals .== lev)
                push!(X_cols, col_vec); push!(cn_new, n)
            else
                push!(X_cols, Float64.(df_c[!, Symbol(n)])); push!(cn_new, n)
            end
        end
        Xm = hcat(X_cols...)
        y_m = Float64.(df_c[!, resp])
        k_m = size(Xm, 2)

        # OLS + HC1
        β_m = Xm \ y_m
        û = y_m - Xm * β_m
        XtXi = LinearAlgebra.inv(Xm' * Xm)
        meat = Xm' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(û.^2) * Xm
        V = XtXi * meat * XtXi * (nn / (nn - k_m))

        # Rebuild w aligned to cn_new
        w = zeros(k_m)
        idx_m = findfirst(==(vstr), cn_new)
        idx_m !== nothing && (w[idx_m] = 1.0)
        for (i, n) in enumerate(cn_new)
            m2 = match(re_a, n); m2 === nothing && (m2 = match(re_b, n))
            m2 === nothing && continue
            factor_col = Symbol(String(m2.captures[1]))
            level_str  = String(m2.captures[2])
            hasproperty(df_c, factor_col) || continue
            col = df_c[!, factor_col]
            lev = tryparse(Float64, level_str)
            n_k = lev === nothing ?
                  sum(!ismissing(v) && string(_rawval(v)) == level_str for v in col) :
                  sum(!ismissing(v) && _rawval(v) == lev for v in col)
            w[i] = n_k / nn
        end
        ame = LinearAlgebra.dot(w, β_m)
        Vw = V * w
        vcov_label = "Robust"
    end
    se   = sqrt(max(LinearAlgebra.dot(w, Vw), 0.0))
    z    = se > 0 ? ame / se : NaN
    pval = se > 0 ? 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z))) : NaN
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    lo, hi = ame - crit*se, ame + crit*se

    # Stata-style output
    nobs_str = replace(string(N), r"(\d)(?=(\d{3})+$)" => s"\1,")
    Printf.@printf("\nAverage marginal effects%40sNumber of obs = %s\n", "", nobs_str)
    Printf.@printf("Model VCE: %s\n", vcov_label)
    Printf.@printf("\nExpression: Linear prediction, predict()\n")
    Printf.@printf("dy/dx wrt:  %s\n", vstr)
    println("\n", "-"^78)
    Printf.@printf("             |            Delta-method\n")
    Printf.@printf("             |      dy/dx   std. err.      z    P>|z|     [%d%% conf. interval]\n",
                   round(Int, 100*level))
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %10.6f  %10.6f   %5.2f   %5.3f    %10.5f  %10.5f\n",
                   vstr, ame, se, z, pval, lo, hi)
    println("-"^78)

    return (; ame, se, z, p=pval, ci=(lo, hi), w)
end

"""
    margins_count(model, df; binary=Symbol[], atmean=true, level=0.95)

Stata-style `margins, dydx(*) [atmean]` for log-link count models (Poisson,
Negative Binomial). For continuous regressors `dy/dx_j = β_j · μ`; for
binary regressors named in `binary` (Stata's `i.` prefix), the marginal
effect is the **discrete change** `μ(x_j=1) − μ(x_j=0)` evaluated at `x̄`.
SEs via the delta method using `StatsBase.vcov(model)`.

Arguments
---------
* `model`  — a GLM.jl Poisson or NB fit with `LogLink()`.
* `df`     — the data frame used to fit the model (needed for column means).
* `binary` — names whose marginal effect should be the discrete
             change rather than `β_j · μ`. Accepts Symbols or Strings.
* `atmean` — `true` for MEM (evaluate at column means, Stata's
             `margins, atmean`), `false` for AME (average over sample).

Returns a NamedTuple `(; mfx, mfx_df)`, where `mfx` is a
NamedTuple with `variable/dydx/se/z/p/ci_lo/ci_hi` per regressor, and
`mfx_df` is a DataFrame suitable for further processing.
"""
function margins_count(model, df;
                       binary::AbstractVector = Symbol[],
                       atmean::Bool = true,
                       level::Float64 = 0.95)
    # Accept both StatsBase-style GLM fits AND NamedTuples with (β, V, coefnames)
    # (e.g. the output of `qcount_stata` / `qreg_stata`).
    β  = hasproperty(model, :β) ? Float64.(model.β)         : StatsBase.coef(model)
    V  = hasproperty(model, :V) ? Matrix{Float64}(model.V)   : StatsBase.vcov(model)
    cn = hasproperty(model, :coefnames) ? String.(model.coefnames) :
                                           string.(StatsBase.coefnames(model))
    _is_const(n) = n == "(Intercept)" || n == "_cons"

    binary_str = Set(String.(string.(binary)))
    nms        = [n for n in cn if !_is_const(n)]

    if atmean
        # Mean of each regressor; intercept stays 1.
        means_d = Dict{String,Float64}()
        for n in nms
            hasproperty(df, Symbol(n)) ||
                error("Variable $n not in dataframe")
            means_d[n] = Statistics.mean(_rawval.(skipmissing(df[!, Symbol(n)])))
        end
        x̄  = [_is_const(n) ? 1.0 : means_d[n] for n in cn]
        μ̄  = exp(LinearAlgebra.dot(x̄, β))

        rows = NamedTuple[]
        for (j, n) in enumerate(cn)
            _is_const(n) && continue
            if n in binary_str
                x1 = copy(x̄);  x1[j] = 1.0
                x0 = copy(x̄);  x0[j] = 0.0
                μ1 = exp(LinearAlgebra.dot(x1, β))
                μ0 = exp(LinearAlgebra.dot(x0, β))
                dydx = μ1 - μ0
                g    = x1 .* μ1 .- x0 .* μ0
                label = "1." * n
            else
                dydx = β[j] * μ̄
                g    = μ̄ .* (x̄ .* β[j])
                g[j] += μ̄
                label = n
            end
            se = sqrt(max(g' * V * g, 0.0))
            zv = se > 0 ? dydx / se : NaN
            pv = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(zv)))
            zcrit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
            lo = dydx - zcrit*se;  hi = dydx + zcrit*se
            push!(rows, (; Variable = label, dydx, StdErr = se, z = zv,
                          p = pv, CI_low = lo, CI_high = hi))
        end
    else
        # AME: average over observations (no special binary handling — uses the
        # continuous-derivative formula β_j · μ_i).
        cleaned = DataFrames.dropmissing(df,
            [Symbol(n) for n in nms if hasproperty(df, Symbol(n))])
        X = zeros(DataFrames.nrow(cleaned), length(cn))
        for (j, n) in enumerate(cn)
            X[:, j] = _is_const(n) ? ones(DataFrames.nrow(cleaned)) :
                      Float64.(_rawval.(cleaned[!, Symbol(n)]))
        end
        # Compute μ_i: prefer the model's predict() if available; otherwise
        # use exp(X·β) (log-link assumed — same as GLM Poisson / NB / qcount).
        μ_vec = if hasproperty(model, :β)
            exp.(X * β)
        else
            Float64.(GLM.predict(model, cleaned))
        end
        μ̄_ = Statistics.mean(μ_vec)
        rows = NamedTuple[]
        for (j, n) in enumerate(cn)
            _is_const(n) && continue
            # AME = β_j · mean(μ_i); delta-method gradient = mean(β_j μ_i X_i.) + β_j e_j μ̄
            dydx = β[j] * μ̄_
            g = (β[j] .* (X' * μ_vec) ./ DataFrames.nrow(cleaned))
            g[j] += μ̄_
            se = sqrt(max(g' * V * g, 0.0))
            zv = se > 0 ? dydx / se : NaN
            pv = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(zv)))
            zcrit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
            lo = dydx - zcrit*se;  hi = dydx + zcrit*se
            push!(rows, (; Variable = n, dydx, StdErr = se, z = zv,
                          p = pv, CI_low = lo, CI_high = hi))
        end
    end

    mfx_df = DataFrames.DataFrame(rows)
    n_obs  = hasproperty(model, :n) ? Int(model.n) :
             (try Int(StatsBase.nobs(model)) catch; DataFrames.nrow(df) end)
    mode_l = atmean ? "MEM (atmean)" : "AME"
    println("Conditional marginal effects — $mode_l",
            " "^max(0, 50 - (length("Conditional marginal effects — $mode_l"))),
            "Number of obs = $n_obs")
    println()
    lvl = round(Int, 100 * level)
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(mfx_df;
        header = (["",         "",      "Delta-method", "",  "",      "",            ""],
                  ["Variable", "dy/dx", "std. err.",    "z", "P>|z|",
                   "[$(lvl)% CI low]", "[$(lvl)% CI high]"]),
        formatters = (
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.7f", [2, 3, 6, 7]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.2f",  [4]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.3f",  [5]),
        ),
        alignment = [:l, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r])
    return (; mfx = rows, mfx_df)
end

"""
    margins_eyex(model, df, varname; level=0.95)

Stata-style `margins, eyex(<varname>) atmean` for a linear model with no
interactions involving `varname`. Elasticity evaluated at the sample means:

    ey/ex = β_varname · (x̄ / ŷ(x̄))    where   ŷ(x̄) = x̄' β

The delta-method SE uses the model's own vcov. If that vcov has non-finite
entries on the rows the elasticity depends on, the function transparently
refits the same formula with **classical OLS vcov** and uses that for the
SE only — so it never returns NaN when the elasticity itself is defined.

Supported coefficient layouts: intercept, continuous main effects, and
categorical dummies of the form "colname: level". Complex interactions
with `varname` are ignored (with a warning).
"""
function _xbar_from_coefnames(nm, df)
    xbar = zeros(length(nm))
    N = DataFrames.nrow(df)
    for (i, n) in enumerate(nm)
        if n == "(Intercept)"
            xbar[i] = 1.0
            continue
        end
        sym = Symbol(n)
        if hasproperty(df, sym)
            col = df[!, sym]
            xbar[i] = Statistics.mean(Float64(_rawval(v)) for v in skipmissing(col))
            continue
        end
        m = match(r"^(.+?):\s*(.+)$", n)
        if m !== nothing
            col_sym = Symbol(String(m.captures[1]))
            lev_str = String(m.captures[2])
            if hasproperty(df, col_sym)
                col = df[!, col_sym]
                lev = tryparse(Float64, lev_str)
                xbar[i] = lev === nothing ?
                    count(!ismissing(v) && string(_rawval(v)) == lev_str for v in col) / N :
                    count(!ismissing(v) && _rawval(v) == lev for v in col) / N
                continue
            end
        end
        @warn "margins: could not resolve mean for coefficient '$n'; using 0."
    end
    return xbar
end

function margins_eyex(model, df, varname::Symbol; level::Float64=0.95)
    β   = StatsBase.coef(model)
    V   = StatsBase.vcov(model)
    nm  = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(model))
    vstr = string(varname)

    idx = findfirst(==(vstr), nm)
    idx === nothing && error("'$vstr' not found as a main effect in model")

    if any(occursin("$vstr &", n) || occursin("& $vstr", n) for n in nm)
        @warn "Interactions involving '$vstr' detected — margins_eyex ignores them; elasticity is based on β_$vstr only."
    end

    xbar = _xbar_from_coefnames(nm, df)

    yhat = LinearAlgebra.dot(xbar, β)
    x̄    = Statistics.mean(skipmissing(df[!, varname]))
    dydx = β[idx]
    ela  = dydx * x̄ / yhat

    # Delta-method gradient of ela = β[idx] · x̄ / (xbar'β):
    #   ∂ela/∂β = (x̄ / ŷ) · [e_idx - (β[idx]/ŷ) · xbar]
    w = zeros(length(β)); w[idx] = 1.0
    g = (x̄ / yhat) .* (w .- (dydx / yhat) .* xbar)

    used_classical = false
    Vg = V * g
    if !all(isfinite, Vg)
        @warn "vcov has non-finite entries on relevant rows; refitting with classical OLS vcov for SE."
        try
            m2 = FixedEffectModels.reg(df, StatsBase.formula(model))
            V  = StatsBase.vcov(m2)
            Vg = V * g
            used_classical = true
        catch e
            @warn "Classical refit failed: $e"
        end
    end
    se   = sqrt(max(LinearAlgebra.dot(g, Vg), 0.0))
    z    = ela / se
    pval = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z)))
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    lo, hi = ela - crit*se, ela + crit*se

    Printf.@printf("margins, eyex(%s) atmean%s\n", vstr,
                   used_classical ? "  [SE from classical OLS fallback]" : "")
    header = ["ey/ex", "Std. err.", "z", "P>|z|",
              "$(round(Int, 100*level))% CI low", "$(round(Int, 100*level))% CI high"]
    row = permutedims([ela, se, z, pval, lo, hi])
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(row; header=header)
    return (; elasticity=ela, se, z, p=pval, ci=(lo, hi), used_classical_vcov=used_classical)
end

"""
    _design_matrix_from_df(nm, df)

Reconstruct an `n × length(nm)` design matrix by interpreting each coefficient
name. Handles `(Intercept)`, continuous main effects, and categorical dummies
of the form `"col: level"`. Unknown terms contribute a zero column (with a
warning).
"""
function _design_matrix_from_df(nm::Vector{String}, df)
    n = DataFrames.nrow(df)
    X = zeros(n, length(nm))
    for (i, name) in enumerate(nm)
        if name == "(Intercept)"
            X[:, i] .= 1.0
            continue
        end
        sym = Symbol(name)
        if hasproperty(df, sym)
            col = df[!, sym]
            X[:, i] .= [ismissing(v) ? 0.0 : Float64(_rawval(v)) for v in col]
            continue
        end
        m = match(r"^(.+?):\s*(.+)$", name)
        if m !== nothing
            col_sym = Symbol(String(m.captures[1]))
            lev_str = String(m.captures[2])
            if hasproperty(df, col_sym)
                col = df[!, col_sym]
                lev = tryparse(Float64, lev_str)
                X[:, i] .= lev === nothing ?
                    [!ismissing(v) && string(_rawval(v)) == lev_str ? 1.0 : 0.0 for v in col] :
                    [!ismissing(v) && _rawval(v) == lev ? 1.0 : 0.0 for v in col]
                continue
            end
        end
        @warn "imtest: could not reconstruct design column for '$name'; using zeros."
    end
    return X
end

"""
    imtest(model, df)

Stata-style `estat imtest` — Cameron & Trivedi's decomposition of White's
information matrix test. Prints the four chi² components (Heteroskedasticity,
Skewness, Kurtosis, Total) and returns them as a NamedTuple.

Implementation follows Cameron & Trivedi (2005) *Microeconometrics* §8.6.
Small numerical differences from Stata's `estat imtest` are expected owing
to different rank-adjustment and redundancy-handling conventions.
"""
function imtest(model, df)
    nm = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(model))
    X  = _design_matrix_from_df(nm, df)
    beta = StatsBase.coef(model)

    yname = try
        Symbol(string(model.formula.lhs))
    catch
        try Symbol(model.yname) catch _e2; Symbol(StatsBase.responsename(model)) end
    end
    ycol = df[!, yname]

    keep = .!ismissing.(ycol) .& .!any(isnan, X; dims=2)[:]
    X    = X[keep, :]
    y    = Float64.(collect(skipmissing(ycol[keep])))
    e    = y .- X * beta               # raw OLS residuals
    n, k = size(X)
    s2   = sum(abs2, e) / n            # MLE variance (divide by n, not n-k)

    # Non-intercept columns
    idx_nz = [i for i in 1:k if nm[i] != "(Intercept)"]
    p = length(idx_nz)
    Xp = X[:, idx_nz]                 # n × p  (no intercept)

    # Helper: n*(1 - RSS/USS) = Stata's n*R²_uncentered
    function nR2_unc(dep, Z)
        b   = Z \ dep
        res = dep .- Z * b
        uss = sum(abs2, dep)
        rss = sum(abs2, res)
        return uss > 0 ? n * (1 - rss / uss) : 0.0
    end

    # ── Heteroskedasticity (delta_1) ──
    # dep = e² − σ̂². Regressors = [1, Xp, cross-products of mean-centered Xp].
    # Drop collinear binary squared terms.
    Xp_c = Xp .- mean(Xp, dims=1)     # mean-centered regressors
    het_cols = Vector{Vector{Float64}}()
    for a in 1:p, b in a:p
        col = Xp_c[:, a] .* Xp_c[:, b]
        # Drop if x²=x (binary): squared term collinear with original
        if a == b && abs(cor(col, Xp[:, a])) > 0.9999
            continue
        end
        push!(het_cols, col)
    end
    Z_het = isempty(het_cols) ? hcat(ones(n), Xp) :
            hcat(ones(n), Xp, reduce(hcat, het_cols))
    dep_h = e.^2 .- s2
    H     = nR2_unc(dep_h, Z_het)
    df_H  = size(Z_het, 2) - 1

    # ── Skewness (delta_2) ──
    # dep = e³ − 3σ̂²e. Regressors = [1, Xp].
    dep_s = e.^3 .- 3.0 .* s2 .* e
    Z_skew = hcat(ones(n), Xp)
    S     = nR2_unc(dep_s, Z_skew)
    df_S  = p

    # ── Kurtosis (delta_3) ──
    # dep = e⁴ − 6σ̂²e² + 3σ̂⁴. Regressors = [1] (intercept only).
    dep_k = e.^4 .- 6.0 .* s2 .* e.^2 .+ 3.0 .* s2^2
    Z_kurt = ones(n, 1)
    K     = nR2_unc(dep_k, Z_kurt)
    df_K  = 1

    # ── Total ──
    IM    = H + S + K
    df_IM = df_H + df_S + df_K

    p_H  = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(max(df_H, 1)),  H)
    p_S  = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(max(df_S, 1)),  S)
    p_K  = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(df_K),           K)
    p_IM = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(max(df_IM, 1)), IM)

    println("\nCameron & Trivedi's decomposition of IM-test\n")
    println("-"^50)
    @printf("%20s | %10s %5s %10s\n", "Source", "chi2", "df", "p")
    println("-"^21, "+", "-"^28)
    @printf("%20s | %10.2f %5d %10.4f\n", "Heteroskedasticity", H, df_H, p_H)
    @printf("%20s | %10.2f %5d %10.4f\n", "Skewness", S, df_S, p_S)
    @printf("%20s | %10.2f %5d %10.4f\n", "Kurtosis", K, df_K, p_K)
    println("-"^21, "+", "-"^28)
    @printf("%20s | %10.2f %5d %10.4f\n", "Total", IM, df_IM, p_IM)
    println("-"^50)

    return (; H   = (chi2=H,  df=df_H,  p=p_H),
              S   = (chi2=S,  df=df_S,  p=p_S),
              K   = (chi2=K,  df=df_K,  p=p_K),
              total = (chi2=IM, df=df_IM, p=p_IM))
end

# =============================================================================
# Panel-data helpers (Stata's xt* commands)
# =============================================================================

"""
    xtdescribe(df, idvar, timevar)

Stata-style `xtdescribe`. Reports N panels, total obs, T range, min/max/mean
panels per unit, and whether the panel is balanced.
"""
function xtdescribe(df, idvar::Symbol, timevar::Symbol)
    ids   = df[!, idvar]
    times = df[!, timevar]
    N     = length(unique(skipmissing(ids)))
    Tmin  = minimum(skipmissing(times))
    Tmax  = maximum(skipmissing(times))
    nrec  = DataFrames.nrow(df)
    T_obs = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df, idvar), DataFrames.nrow => :T)
    balanced = all(==(T_obs.T[1]), T_obs.T)
    println("xtdescribe")
    Printf.@printf("  panelvar   : %s  →  %d panels\n", idvar, N)
    Printf.@printf("  timevar    : %s  →  range %s to %s\n", timevar, Tmin, Tmax)
    Printf.@printf("  total obs  : %d\n", nrec)
    Printf.@printf("  T per panel: min=%d  max=%d  mean=%.2f\n",
                   minimum(T_obs.T), maximum(T_obs.T), Statistics.mean(T_obs.T))
    println("  panel is ", balanced ? "balanced." : "unbalanced.")
    T_dist = DataFrames.sort(
        DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(T_obs, :T), DataFrames.nrow => :panels), :T)
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(T_dist; header=["T", "# panels"])
    return (; N, T_obs, T_dist, balanced)
end

"""
    xtsum(df, vars, idvar)

Stata-style `xtsum`. For each variable in `vars`, reports the overall, between
(std. of panel means), and within (std. of deviations from panel means) SDs.
"""
function xtsum(df, vars::AbstractVector, idvar::Symbol)
    vs  = [Symbol(v) for v in vars]
    # Work only on rows complete on id + every requested variable.
    d = DataFrames.dropmissing(df, unique(vcat(idvar, vs)))
    n_panels = length(unique(d[!, idvar]))
    N_total  = DataFrames.nrow(d)
    T_cnt    = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(d, idvar),
                                  DataFrames.nrow => :_T)._T
    T_str    = all(==(T_cnt[1]), T_cnt) ? string(T_cnt[1]) :
               Printf.@sprintf("%.3g", N_total / n_panels)

    # Stata's %9.0g-style: up to 7 sig digits, strip leading 0 for |x|<1,
    # show integers without decimals.
    function _g(x)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return "."
        if isinteger(x) && abs(x) < 1e12
            return string(Int(round(x)))
        end
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.7g", x)
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        return s
    end

    # Header
    println("Variable         |      Mean   Std. dev.       Min        Max |    Observations")
    println("-"^17, "+", "-"^44, "+", "-"^16)

    for (vi, v) in enumerate(vs)
        vals = Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, v]))
        gm   = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(d, idvar),
                  v => (x -> Statistics.mean(Float64.(_rawval.(x)))) => :_mean)
        pm   = gm._mean
        dj   = DataFrames.leftjoin(d, gm, on=idvar)
        dev  = [Float64(_rawval(dj[i, v])) - dj._mean[i] for i in 1:DataFrames.nrow(dj)]
        μ    = Statistics.mean(vals)

        # Overall row
        Printf.@printf("%-9s%-8s | %9s  %9s  %9s  %9s |    N = %7d\n",
                       string(v), "overall",
                       _g(μ),
                       _g(Statistics.std(vals)),
                       _g(minimum(vals)),
                       _g(maximum(vals)),
                       N_total)
        # Between row (blank mean)
        Printf.@printf("%-9s%-8s | %9s  %9s  %9s  %9s |    n = %7d\n",
                       "", "between",
                       "",
                       _g(Statistics.std(pm)),
                       _g(minimum(pm)),
                       _g(maximum(pm)),
                       n_panels)
        # Within row (blank mean; min/max are deviations shifted by μ)
        Printf.@printf("%-9s%-8s | %9s  %9s  %9s  %9s |    T = %7s\n",
                       "", "within ",
                       "",
                       _g(Statistics.std(dev)),
                       _g(minimum(dev) + μ),
                       _g(maximum(dev) + μ),
                       T_str)
        # Blank separator between variables
        if vi < length(vs)
            Printf.@printf("%-9s%-8s | %9s  %9s  %9s  %9s |\n",
                           "", "", "", "", "", "")
        end
    end
    return nothing
end

"""
    xttab(df, var, idvar=nothing)

Stata-style `xttab var`. With `idvar` supplied, reports three blocks matching
Stata's output exactly:

  * **Overall**  — frequency / percent across all observations.
  * **Between**  — #/% of panels that ever took that value (can sum > 100%
                   because panels that change state appear in multiple rows).
  * **Within**   — mean within-panel time share at that value, conditional
                   on the panel ever taking it.

Without `idvar`, only Overall freq/percent/cum% are shown.
"""
function xttab(df, var::Symbol, idvar::Union{Symbol,Nothing}=nothing)
    if idvar === nothing
        d = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, [var]], var)
        counts = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(d, var), DataFrames.nrow => :Freq)
        DataFrames.sort!(counts, var)
        total = sum(counts.Freq)
        counts.Percent = 100 .* counts.Freq ./ total
        counts.Cum     = cumsum(counts.Percent)
        PrettyTables.pretty_table(counts;
            formatters = PrettyTables.ft_printf("%8.2f", 3:4))
        return counts
    end

    d = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, [idvar, var]], [idvar, var])
    vals = sort(unique(d[!, var]))
    n_total  = DataFrames.nrow(d)
    N_panels = length(unique(d[!, idvar]))
    val_col = Any[]; freq_o = Int[]; pct_o = Float64[]
    freq_b  = Int[]; pct_b  = Float64[]; pct_w = Float64[]
    for v in vals
        mask_v = d[!, var] .== v
        push!(val_col, v)
        push!(freq_o, sum(mask_v))
        push!(pct_o,  100 * sum(mask_v) / n_total)
        panels_with_v = unique(d[mask_v, idvar])
        push!(freq_b, length(panels_with_v))
        push!(pct_b,  100 * length(panels_with_v) / N_panels)
        within = Float64[]
        for p in panels_with_v
            sub = d[d[!, idvar] .== p, :]
            push!(within, 100 * sum(sub[!, var] .== v) / DataFrames.nrow(sub))
        end
        push!(pct_w, isempty(within) ? 0.0 : Statistics.mean(within))
    end
    # Total row
    push!(val_col, "Total")
    push!(freq_o, n_total)
    push!(pct_o, 100.0)
    push!(freq_b, sum(freq_b))
    push!(pct_b,  sum(pct_b))
    push!(pct_w,  Statistics.mean(pct_w))

    out = DataFrames.DataFrame([
        var            => val_col,
        :Freq_over     => freq_o,
        :Percent_over  => pct_o,
        :Freq_btw      => freq_b,
        :Percent_btw   => pct_b,
        :Percent_within => pct_w,
    ])
    println("xttab  —  $(var)   (N panels = $N_panels, total obs = $n_total)")
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(out;
        formatters = PrettyTables.ft_printf("%8.2f", 3:6))
    return out
end

"""
    matlist(M, name="M"; symmetric=true)

Stata-style `matrix list` output for a numeric matrix. With `symmetric=true`
(default) only the lower triangle is printed — matching the look of
`matrix list e(R)` after `xtreg, pa`. Numbers use Stata's `%9.0g`-style
formatting (8 sig digits, leading `0.` stripped, integers shown without
decimals).
"""
function matlist(M::AbstractMatrix, name::AbstractString = "M";
                 symmetric::Bool = true)
    n, m = size(M)
    _fmt(x) = begin
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return "."
        if isinteger(x) && abs(x) < 1e12
            return string(Int(round(x)))
        end
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.8g", x)
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        return s
    end
    prefix = symmetric ? "symmetric " : ""
    println("$(prefix)$(name)[$(n),$(m)]")
    cell_w = 11
    # Column header
    hdr = " "^3
    for j in 1:m
        hdr *= lpad("c$(j)", cell_w)
    end
    println(hdr)
    # Rows
    for i in 1:n
        row = rpad("r$(i)", 3)
        j_end = symmetric ? i : m
        for j in 1:j_end
            row *= lpad(_fmt(M[i, j]), cell_w)
        end
        println(row)
    end
    return nothing
end

"""
    xttrans(df, var, idvar, timevar)

Stata-style `xttrans`. One-period transition matrix of `var` within each panel.
"""
function xttrans(df, var::Symbol, idvar::Symbol, timevar::Symbol)
    d = DataFrames.sort(df, [idvar, timevar])
    lagcol = Symbol(var, "_lag")
    d = DataFrames.transform(DataFrames.groupby(d, idvar),
        var => (x -> [missing; collect(x)[1:end-1]]) => lagcol)
    d = DataFrames.dropmissing(d, [lagcol, var])

    # Category levels in natural order (unwrap Stata labeled values).
    cats  = sort(unique(_rawval.(d[!, var])))
    nc    = length(cats)

    # Transition count matrix  M[i, j] = # (lag = cats[i], current = cats[j]).
    M = zeros(Int, nc, nc)
    for i in 1:DataFrames.nrow(d)
        li = findfirst(==(_rawval(d[i, lagcol])), cats)
        ci = findfirst(==(_rawval(d[i, var])),    cats)
        (li === nothing || ci === nothing) && continue
        M[li, ci] += 1
    end

    row_sum = vec(sum(M;  dims = 2))
    col_sum = vec(sum(M;  dims = 1))
    N_tot   = sum(M)
    row_pct = 100.0 .* M        ./ max.(row_sum, 1)
    col_pct = 100.0 .* col_sum ./ max(N_tot, 1)

    # Formatters
    _comma(x::Integer) = replace(string(x), r"(\d)(?=(\d{3})+$)" => s"\1,")
    _pct(x)            = Printf.@sprintf("%.2f", x)

    label_w = max(10, length(string(var)) + 2)       # leftmost column
    cell_w  = 10                                     # per-category column
    totw    = cell_w

    # Horizontal rule "-lw-+-inner-+-totw-"
    inner_w = nc * (cell_w + 1) + 1
    ruler   = "-"^label_w * "-+" * "-"^inner_w * "+" * "-"^(totw + 1)

    # ── Header
    println(rpad(string(var), label_w), " |")
    hdr = lpad("", label_w) * " |"
    for c in cats
        hdr *= lpad(string(c), cell_w + 1)
    end
    hdr *= " |" * lpad("Total", totw + 1)
    println(hdr)
    println(ruler)

    # ── Data rows: one per lag category (freq + row-% below)
    for i in 1:nc
        r = lpad(string(cats[i]), label_w) * " |"
        for j in 1:nc
            r *= lpad(_comma(M[i, j]), cell_w + 1)
        end
        r *= " |" * lpad(_comma(row_sum[i]), totw + 1)
        println(r)

        r = lpad("", label_w) * " |"
        for j in 1:nc
            r *= lpad(_pct(row_pct[i, j]), cell_w + 1)
        end
        r *= " |" * lpad("100.00", totw + 1)
        println(r)
        println(ruler)
    end

    # ── Total row (column sums + column %)
    r = lpad("Total", label_w) * " |"
    for j in 1:nc
        r *= lpad(_comma(col_sum[j]), cell_w + 1)
    end
    r *= " |" * lpad(_comma(N_tot), totw + 1)
    println(r)

    r = lpad("", label_w) * " |"
    for j in 1:nc
        r *= lpad(_pct(col_pct[j]), cell_w + 1)
    end
    r *= " |" * lpad("100.00", totw + 1)
    println(r)
```
