    return (; freq = M, row_pct = row_pct, col_sum = col_sum,
              row_sum = row_sum, col_pct = col_pct,
              categories = cats, N = N_tot)
end

"""
    hausman(m_fe, m_re; sigmamore=false)

Stata-style `hausman FE RE[, sigmamore]`. Tests
   H₀: RE is consistent and efficient (so `cov(β̂_FE − β̂_RE) = V_FE − V_RE`)
   H₁: FE is consistent, RE is not.

Accepts either `StatsBase`-compatible models (e.g. `FixedEffectModels.reg`)
or the NamedTuple outputs of `xtreg_re` / `hausman_taylor` / `arellano_bond`.
The intercept / `_cons` column is excluded from the comparison (matching
Stata's convention).

If `V_FE − V_RE` is not positive-definite, falls back to the Moore-Penrose
pseudo-inverse. With `sigmamore=true`, uses the FE residual variance to
rescale `V_RE` so that the difference is more likely to be PD
(analogous to Stata's `sigmamore` option).

Prints the per-coefficient (b) / (B) / (b-B) / sqrt(diag(V_b−V_B)) table
that Stata outputs, and returns a NamedTuple with the stats.
"""
function hausman(m_fe, m_re; sigmamore::Bool=false)
    nm_fe = _coefnames_of(m_fe)
    nm_re = _coefnames_of(m_re)
    # Intersect, drop intercept
    common = [v for v in intersect(nm_fe, nm_re)
              if !(v in ("_cons", "(Intercept)"))]
    i_fe = [findfirst(==(v), nm_fe) for v in common]
    i_re = [findfirst(==(v), nm_re) for v in common]
    β_fe = _coef_of(m_fe)[i_fe]
    β_re = _coef_of(m_re)[i_re]
    V_fe = _vcov_of(m_fe)[i_fe, i_fe]
    V_re = _vcov_of(m_re)[i_re, i_re]

    # Stata's sigmamore: rebuild V_FE using σ²_RE (common "efficient" variance)
    # so that V_FE − V_RE has the form σ²_RE[(X'QX)⁻¹ − (X*'X*)⁻¹], which is PSD.
    # σ²_RE comes from `xtreg_re.σ_ε²`; σ²_FE is read off the FixedEffectModels
    # fit via RSS/dof_residual — and since those two `dof_residual` conventions
    # aren't always identical, we take the ratio so V_FE is *explicitly* scaled
    # to use σ²_RE regardless of the underlying fit.
    function _σ2(m)
        hasproperty(m, :σ_ε)          && return m.σ_ε^2
        hasproperty(m, :rss) && hasproperty(m, :dof_residual) &&
            return m.rss / m.dof_residual
        try
            return StatsBase.deviance(m) / StatsBase.dof_residual(m)
        catch
            return missing
        end
    end
    if sigmamore
        σ2_fe, σ2_re = _σ2(m_fe), _σ2(m_re)
        if !ismissing(σ2_fe) && !ismissing(σ2_re) && σ2_fe > 0
            V_fe = (σ2_re / σ2_fe) .* V_fe      # rescale V_FE to use σ²_RE
        end
    end

    ΔV = V_fe - V_re
    # Drop rows with non-finite β or non-finite DIAGONAL of ΔV. (Checking the
    # full row would reject every coefficient if any column has a NaN
    # — which happens when the FE model absorbs a collinear regressor
    # such as a time-invariant variable.)
    bad = findall(k -> !isfinite(β_fe[k]) || !isfinite(β_re[k]) ||
                       !isfinite(ΔV[k, k]),
                  eachindex(common))
    if !isempty(bad)
        @warn "Hausman: dropping non-finite rows for $(common[bad])"
        keep   = setdiff(eachindex(common), bad)
        β_fe   = β_fe[keep];  β_re = β_re[keep]
        ΔV     = ΔV[keep, keep]
        common = common[keep]
    end
    # Scrub any residual NaN/Inf off-diagonals from dropping rows/cols.
    ΔV = replace(ΔV, NaN => 0.0, Inf => 0.0, -Inf => 0.0)
    d = β_fe .- β_re
    H = try
        d' * (ΔV \ d)
    catch
        @warn "V_FE − V_RE not positive-definite; using pseudoinverse."
        d' * LinearAlgebra.pinv(ΔV) * d
    end
    q = length(d)
    pv = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(max(q, 1)), H)

    # Stata-style layout (pipe-separated, %.7f-ish with stripped leading 0s)
    se_diff = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(ΔV), 0.0))

    _g(x, w) = begin
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, ".")
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.7f", x)
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, s)
    end

    println("                 ---- Coefficients ----")
    println("             |      (b)          (B)            (b-B)     sqrt(diag(V_b-V_B))")
    println("             |       FE           RE         Difference       Std. err.")
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    for k in eachindex(common)
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s   %10s       %10s       %10s\n",
                       common[k], _g(β_fe[k], 10), _g(β_re[k], 10),
                       _g(d[k], 10),  _g(se_diff[k], 10))
    end
    println("-"^78)
    println("                          b = Consistent under H0 and Ha; obtained from xtreg.")
    println("           B = Inconsistent under Ha, efficient under H0; obtained from xtreg.")
    println()
    println("Test of H0: Difference in coefficients not systematic")
    println()
    Printf.@printf("    chi2(%d) = (b-B)'[(V_b-V_B)^(-1)](b-B)\n", q)
    Printf.@printf("            = %.2f\n", H)
    Printf.@printf("Prob > chi2 = %6.4f\n", pv)
    return (; chi2=H, df=q, p=pv, diff=d, se_diff, vars=common)
end

"""
    estimates_table(models; labels=nothing, vars=nothing, stats=nothing)

Stata-style `estimates table` — side-by-side coefficient comparison with SE.
`models`   : a Vector of fitted models (any with StatsBase.coef/vcov/coefnames).
`labels`   : column labels (defaults to "M1", "M2", …).
`vars`     : variables to include (defaults to union of all model coefnames).
`stats`    : extra row names, e.g. [:N, :r2, :r2_a].
"""
# Accessor helpers — work for StatsBase-compatible models AND for the
# NamedTuple outputs of our xtreg_re / hausman_taylor / arellano_bond.
_coef_of(m)      = hasproperty(m, :β) ? m.β      : StatsBase.coef(m)
_coefnames_of(m) = hasproperty(m, :coefnames) ? string.(m.coefnames) : string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m))
_vcov_of(m)      = hasproperty(m, :V) ? m.V      : StatsBase.vcov(m)
_nobs_of(m)      = hasproperty(m, :n_obs) ? m.n_obs : try StatsBase.nobs(m) catch; missing end

function estimates_table(models::AbstractVector; labels=nothing, vars=nothing, stats=nothing)
    M = length(models)
    labels = labels === nothing ? ["M$i" for i in 1:M] : String.(labels)
    all_nm = vcat([_coefnames_of(m) for m in models]...)
    vars = vars === nothing ? unique(all_nm) : String.(vars)

    out = DataFrames.DataFrame(variable = String[])
    for l in labels
        out[!, Symbol(l)]          = String[]
        out[!, Symbol(l, "_se")]   = String[]
    end

    for v in vars
        row = Any[v]
        for m in models
            nm = _coefnames_of(m)
            i  = findfirst(==(v), nm)
            if i === nothing
                push!(row, ".");  push!(row, ".")
            else
                b  = _coef_of(m)[i]
                se = sqrt(max(_vcov_of(m)[i, i], 0.0))
                push!(row, Printf.@sprintf("%9.4f", b))
                push!(row, Printf.@sprintf("(%9.4f)", se))
            end
        end
        push!(out, row)
    end

    if stats !== nothing
        stat_getters = Dict(
            :N    => m -> Printf.@sprintf("%d",   _nobs_of(m)),
            :r2   => m -> Printf.@sprintf("%.4f", StatsBase.r2(m)),
            :r2_a => m -> Printf.@sprintf("%.4f", StatsBase.adjr2(m)),
            :rmse => m -> Printf.@sprintf("%.4f", sqrt(StatsBase.deviance(m)/StatsBase.dof_residual(m))),
        )
        for s in stats
            row = Any[string(s)]
            for m in models
                push!(row, haskey(stat_getters, s) ? try stat_getters[s](m); catch; "." end : ".")
                push!(row, "")
            end
            push!(out, row)
        end
    end

    println("estimates table — coefficients (SE below)")
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(out)
    return out
end

"""
    regress_stata(model; level=0.95)

Stata-style `regress` output — prints the ANOVA table, overall F-statistic,
R², adjusted R², Root MSE, and the coefficient table with t-statistics,
p-values, and confidence intervals, matching the format of Stata's
`regress` command. The intercept is relabelled `_cons`.

Works with any `StatsBase`-compatible OLS model, e.g. the output of
`FixedEffectModels.reg(df, @formula(y ~ x2 + x3))`.
"""
function regress_stata(model; level::Float64=0.95)
    β      = StatsBase.coef(model)
    V      = StatsBase.vcov(model)
    se     = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))
    nm_raw = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(model))
    nm     = replace.(nm_raw, "(Intercept)" => "_cons")
    has_int = any(==("_cons"), nm)

    n      = Int(StatsBase.nobs(model))
    rss    = StatsBase.deviance(model)
    r2     = StatsBase.r2(model)
    ar2    = StatsBase.adjr2(model)
    dof_r  = Int(StatsBase.dof_residual(model))
    k      = length(β) - (has_int ? 1 : 0)
    tss    = rss / max(1 - r2, eps())
    mss    = tss - rss
    rmse   = sqrt(rss / dof_r)
    Fstat  = (mss / k) / (rss / dof_r)
    pF     = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(k, dof_r), Fstat)

    yname = try
        string(StatsBase.responsename(model))
    catch
        hasproperty(model, :yname) ? string(model.yname) : "y"
    end

    t_stat = β ./ se
    tcrit  = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(dof_r), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    p_val  = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.TDist(dof_r), abs.(t_stat)))
    ci_lo  = β .- tcrit .* se
    ci_hi  = β .+ tcrit .* se

    # Stata %10.0g-style: `sig` significant digits, strip leading 0 for |x|<1,
    # right-aligned in width `w`.
    function _g(x, w; sig=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, ".")
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        if 0 < abs(x) < 1
            s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
        return Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, s)
    end

    # ── ANOVA table + summary-stats block
    println()
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s %10s %10s    %-13s = %10d\n",
                   "Source", "SS", "df", "MS", "Number of obs", n)
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^34, "   ",
            Printf.@sprintf("%-13s = %10.2f", "F($k, $dof_r)", Fstat))
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s %10d %s    %-13s = %10.4f\n",
                   "Model", _g(mss, 10), k, _g(mss/k, 10), "Prob > F", pF)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s %10d %s    %-13s = %10.4f\n",
                   "Residual", _g(rss, 10), dof_r, _g(rss/dof_r, 10),
                   "R-squared", r2)
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^34, "   ",
            Printf.@sprintf("%-13s = %10.4f", "Adj R-squared", ar2))
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s %10d %s    %-13s = %10.4f\n",
                   "Total", _g(tss, 10), n-1, _g(tss/(n-1), 10),
                   "Root MSE", rmse)
    println()

    # ── Coefficient table
    println("-"^78)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s  %9s  %6s  %5s     [%d%% conf. interval]\n",
                   yname, "Coefficient", "Std. err.", "t", "P>|t|",
                   round(Int, 100*level))
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    for i in eachindex(β)
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %6.2f  %5.3f  %s  %s\n",
                       nm[i],
                       _g(β[i], 10),
                       _g(se[i], 9),
                       t_stat[i],
                       p_val[i],
                       _g(ci_lo[i], 11),
                       _g(ci_hi[i], 11))
    end
    println("-"^78)
    return nothing
end

"""
    regress_stata(df, y, xs; level=0.95, vcov=nothing)

Convenience method: fit `y ~ xs…` on `df` with `FixedEffectModels.reg` and
print the Stata-style `regress` output. `y` is the dependent variable (Symbol
or String); `xs` is a Symbol, String, or vector of regressor names. Pass
`vcov=Vcov.robust()` (or similar) for non-default standard errors.

Example:

    regress_stata(df5, :y, [:x2, :x3])
    regress_stata(df5, :y, [:x2, :x3]; vcov=Vcov.robust())
"""
function regress_stata(df::DataFrames.AbstractDataFrame, y, xs;
                       level::Float64=0.95, vcov=nothing, weights=nothing)
    ys  = Symbol(y)
    xsv = xs isa Union{Symbol,AbstractString} ? [Symbol(xs)] :
          [Symbol(v) for v in xs]
    f = term(ys) ~ sum(term.(xsv))
    kw = weights === nothing ? (;) : (; weights=Symbol(weights))
    m = vcov === nothing ?
        FixedEffectModels.reg(df, f; kw...) :
        FixedEffectModels.reg(df, f, vcov; kw...)
    regress_stata(m; level=level)
    return nothing
end

"""
    sureg_fit(df, eqs::Pair...; reps=0, seed=nothing)

Silent companion to `sureg`: runs the same FGLS SUR (with optional bootstrap
vcov) and returns a `NamedTuple` of results — no printing. Use this before
`test_sureg` to run cross-equation Wald tests on the stored fit.

Fields: `β`, `V`, `coefnames` (as `"[eqname]var"`), `eqnames`, `cns`, `ks`,
`offsets`, `N`, `Σ̂`, `β_eq`, `V_eq`, `se_eq`, `rmse`, `r2s`, `chi2s`,
`pvals`, `reps`.
"""
function sureg_fit(df::DataFrames.AbstractDataFrame, eqs::Pair...;
                   reps::Int=0, seed::Union{Int,Nothing}=nothing,
                   constraints::AbstractVector=String[])
    M = length(eqs)
    all_vars = Symbol[]
    for (y, xs) in eqs
        push!(all_vars, Symbol(y))
        xsv = xs isa Union{Symbol,AbstractString} ? [Symbol(xs)] :
              [Symbol(v) for v in xs]
        append!(all_vars, xsv)
    end
    d = DataFrames.dropmissing(df, unique(all_vars))
    N = DataFrames.nrow(d)

    Xs      = Vector{Matrix{Float64}}(undef, M)
    Ys      = Vector{Vector{Float64}}(undef, M)
    cns     = Vector{Vector{String}}(undef, M)
    eqnames = Vector{String}(undef, M)
    for (j, (y, xs)) in enumerate(eqs)
        ys  = Symbol(y)
        xsv = xs isa Union{Symbol,AbstractString} ? [Symbol(xs)] :
              [Symbol(v) for v in xs]
        Xs[j] = hcat(ones(N), [Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, v])) for v in xsv]...)
        Ys[j] = Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, ys]))
        cns[j] = vcat("_cons", string.(xsv))  # intercept at column 1
        eqnames[j] = string(ys)
    end
    ks      = [size(X, 2) for X in Xs]
    kt      = sum(ks)
    offsets = vcat(0, cumsum(ks))

    # Full coefnames used for constraint parsing and reporting.
    coefnames_full = String[]
    for j in 1:M, i in 1:ks[j]
        push!(coefnames_full, "[$(eqnames[j])]$(cns[j][i])")
    end

    # Parse constraint strings into (R, r) — same grammar as `test_sureg`.
    R_con = zeros(0, kt); r_con = Float64[]; con_labels = String[]
    if !isempty(constraints)
        function _resolve(token::AbstractString)
            tok = String(strip(token))
            num = tryparse(Float64, tok)
            num !== nothing && return (Int[], num)
            if startswith(tok, "[") && occursin(']', tok)
                k = findfirst(==(tok), coefnames_full)
                k === nothing && error("Coefficient not found: $tok")
                return ([k], 0.0)
            end
            idx = Int[]
            for (k, cn) in enumerate(coefnames_full)
                m = match(r"^\[(.+)\](.+)$", cn)
                (m === nothing || m.captures[2] != tok) && continue
                push!(idx, k)
            end
            isempty(idx) && error("Coefficient not found in any equation: $tok")
            return (idx, 0.0)
        end
        R_rows = Vector{Vector{Float64}}()
        for c in constraints
            cs = strip(string(c))
            occursin('=', cs) ||
                error("Constraint must contain '=': got '$cs'")
            lhs_s, rhs_s = split(cs, '='; limit=2)
            lhs_idx, lhs_c = _resolve(lhs_s)
            rhs_idx, rhs_c = _resolve(rhs_s)
            (length(lhs_idx) > 1 || length(rhs_idx) > 1) &&
                error("Ambiguous short name in '$cs' — use [eq]name")
            row = zeros(kt)
            for k in lhs_idx; row[k] += 1.0; end
            for k in rhs_idx; row[k] -= 1.0; end
            push!(R_rows, row); push!(r_con, rhs_c - lhs_c)
            push!(con_labels,
                  !isempty(lhs_idx) && !isempty(rhs_idx) ?
                      "$(coefnames_full[lhs_idx[1]]) = $(coefnames_full[rhs_idx[1]])" :
                  !isempty(lhs_idx) ?
                      "$(coefnames_full[lhs_idx[1]]) = $(rhs_c)" :
                      "$(coefnames_full[rhs_idx[1]]) = $(lhs_c)")
        end
        R_con = reduce(vcat, permutedims.(R_rows))
    end
    has_con = !isempty(con_labels)

    # Projection of (β_u, V_u) onto Rβ = r.
    function _constrain(β_u, V_u)
        has_con || return (β_u, V_u)
        RVR   = R_con * V_u * R_con'
        VRT   = V_u * R_con'
        β_c   = β_u .- VRT * (RVR \ (R_con * β_u .- r_con))
        V_c   = V_u .- VRT * LinearAlgebra.inv(RVR) * R_con * V_u
        return (β_c, V_c)
    end

    # Step 1: OLS residuals → Σ̂
    β_ols = [Xs[j] \ Ys[j] for j in 1:M]
    e_ols = [Ys[j] - Xs[j] * β_ols[j] for j in 1:M]
    Σ̂ = [LinearAlgebra.dot(e_ols[i], e_ols[j]) / N for i in 1:M, j in 1:M]

    # Step 2: FGLS via blocked (Σ⁻¹ ⊗ I) sandwich
    Σinv = LinearAlgebra.inv(Σ̂)
    XWX  = zeros(kt, kt)
    XWY  = zeros(kt)
    for i in 1:M, j in 1:M
        ri = (offsets[i]+1):offsets[i+1]
        rj = (offsets[j]+1):offsets[j+1]
        XWX[ri, rj] .= Σinv[i, j] .* (Xs[i]' * Xs[j])
        XWY[ri]    .+= Σinv[i, j] .* (Xs[i]' * Ys[j])
    end
    β_u_full = XWX \ XWY
    V_u_full = LinearAlgebra.inv(XWX)
    β_sur, V_sur = _constrain(β_u_full, V_u_full)

    β_eq  = [β_sur[(offsets[j]+1):offsets[j+1]]                              for j in 1:M]
    V_eq  = [V_sur[(offsets[j]+1):offsets[j+1], (offsets[j]+1):offsets[j+1]] for j in 1:M]
    se_eq = [sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0)) for V in V_eq]
    e_sur = [Ys[j] - Xs[j] * β_eq[j] for j in 1:M]

    rmse  = [sqrt(LinearAlgebra.dot(e_sur[j], e_sur[j]) / N) for j in 1:M]
    r2s   = Float64[]; chi2s = Float64[]; pvals = Float64[]
    for j in 1:M
        rss = LinearAlgebra.dot(e_sur[j], e_sur[j])
        tss = sum((Ys[j] .- Statistics.mean(Ys[j])).^2)
        push!(r2s, 1 - rss / tss)
        slope = 2:ks[j]     # exclude intercept (column 1)
        βs = β_eq[j][slope]; Vs = V_eq[j][slope, slope]
        # pinv handles rank-deficient V_s when constraints bind on this block.
        χ2 = βs' * LinearAlgebra.pinv(Vs) * βs
        push!(chi2s, χ2)
        push!(pvals, 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(length(slope)), χ2))
    end

    # ── Bootstrap vcov (replaces V̂_SUR for the coefficient table SEs/CIs;
    #     summary-block χ² is unchanged and still uses the analytical V̂_SUR).
    if reps > 0
        seed !== nothing && Random.seed!(seed)
        β_boot = Matrix{Float64}(undef, reps, kt)
        for b in 1:reps
            idx  = StatsBase.sample(1:N, N; replace=true)
            Xs_b = [X[idx, :]   for X in Xs]
            Ys_b = [Y[idx]      for Y in Ys]
            β_ols_b = [Xs_b[j] \ Ys_b[j] for j in 1:M]
            e_b     = [Ys_b[j] - Xs_b[j] * β_ols_b[j] for j in 1:M]
            Σ_b     = [LinearAlgebra.dot(e_b[i], e_b[j]) / N for i in 1:M, j in 1:M]
            Σinv_b  = LinearAlgebra.inv(Σ_b)
            XWX_b = zeros(kt, kt); XWY_b = zeros(kt)
            for i in 1:M, j in 1:M
                ri = (offsets[i]+1):offsets[i+1]
                rj = (offsets[j]+1):offsets[j+1]
                XWX_b[ri, rj] .= Σinv_b[i, j] .* (Xs_b[i]' * Xs_b[j])
                XWY_b[ri]    .+= Σinv_b[i, j] .* (Xs_b[i]' * Ys_b[j])
            end
            β_ub = XWX_b \ XWY_b
            if has_con
                V_ub = LinearAlgebra.inv(XWX_b)
                β_ub, _ = _constrain(β_ub, V_ub)
            end
            β_boot[b, :] = β_ub
        end
        V_sur = Statistics.cov(β_boot)
        V_eq  = [V_sur[(offsets[j]+1):offsets[j+1], (offsets[j]+1):offsets[j+1]] for j in 1:M]
        se_eq = [sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0)) for V in V_eq]
    end

    return (; β=β_sur, V=V_sur, coefnames=coefnames_full,
              eqnames, cns, ks, offsets, N, Σ̂,
              β_eq, V_eq, se_eq,
              rmse, r2s, chi2s, pvals, reps,
              constraints=con_labels)
end

"""
    sureg(df, (y1 => [x1s…]), (y2 => [x2s…]), …;
          level=0.95, reps=0, seed=nothing)

Stata-style `sureg` — seemingly unrelated regression (FGLS). Each pair
`y => xs` becomes an equation `y ~ 1 + xs…`. Prints Stata's `sureg` summary
block (Obs, Params, RMSE, R², χ², P>χ²), the pooled coefficient table with
z-statistics, the residual correlation matrix, and the Breusch–Pagan test
of independence against a diagonal Σ.

Algorithm: equation-by-equation OLS gives Σ̂ = (eᵢ′eⱼ)/N; a single FGLS
step with W = Σ̂⁻¹ ⊗ Iₙ recovers β̂_SUR and V̂_SUR. The correlation matrix
and BP test use the OLS-step Σ̂ (matching Stata's default non-iterated
`sureg`).

Pass `reps=400, seed=10101` to mimic Stata's `bootstrap, reps(400) seed(10101): sureg …` —
β̂_SUR is kept as the observed coefficient, but V̂_SUR is replaced by the
sample covariance of B bootstrap replications (each a full SUR on a
resampled dataset). The coefficient table header changes to
"Observed coefficient | Bootstrap std. err. | Normal-based CI". The summary
block's χ² still uses the analytical V̂_SUR to match Stata.

Examples:

    sureg(df_sur,
          :ldrugexp => [:age, :age2, :actlim, :totchr, :medicaid, :private],
          :ltotothr => [:age, :age2, :educyr, :actlim, :totchr, :private])

    sureg(df_sur,
          :ldrugexp => [:age, :age2, :actlim, :totchr, :medicaid, :private],
          :ltotothr => [:age, :age2, :educyr, :actlim, :totchr, :private];
          reps=400, seed=10101)
"""
function sureg(df::DataFrames.AbstractDataFrame, eqs::Pair...;
               level::Float64=0.95, reps::Int=0,
               seed::Union{Int,Nothing}=nothing,
               constraints::AbstractVector=String[])
    fit = sureg_fit(df, eqs...; reps=reps, seed=seed, constraints=constraints)
    if !isempty(fit.constraints)
        println("Constraints imposed:")
        for (i, c) in enumerate(fit.constraints)
            Printf.@printf(" ( %d)  %s\n", i, c)
        end
        println()
    end
    M       = length(fit.eqnames)
    eqnames = fit.eqnames
    cns     = fit.cns
    ks      = fit.ks
    N       = fit.N
    β_eq    = fit.β_eq
    se_eq   = fit.se_eq
    rmse    = fit.rmse
    r2s     = fit.r2s
    chi2s   = fit.chi2s
    pvals   = fit.pvals
    Σ̂       = fit.Σ̂

    # ── Summary table (PrettyTables)
    println("Seemingly unrelated regression\n")
    summary_df = DataFrames.DataFrame(
        Equation    = eqnames,
        Obs         = fill(N, M),
        Params      = [k - 1 for k in ks],
        RMSE        = rmse,
        R_squared   = r2s,
        chi2        = chi2s,
        P_chi2      = pvals,
    )
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(summary_df;
        header     = ["Equation", "Obs", "Params", "RMSE", "R-squared",
                      "chi2", "P>chi2"],
        formatters = (
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.6f", [4]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.4f", [5, 7]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.2f", [6]),
        ),
        alignment  = [:l, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r],
    )

    # ── Coefficient table (PrettyTables), slopes first then intercept
    zcrit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    eqn_col  = String[]; var_col = String[]
    β_col    = Float64[]; se_col  = Float64[]
    z_col    = Float64[]; p_col   = Float64[]
    lo_col   = Float64[]; hi_col  = Float64[]
    hline_at = Int[]
    nrows    = 0
    for j in 1:M
        order = vcat(collect(2:ks[j]), 1)
        first_row = true
        for i in order
            β_i  = β_eq[j][i]
            se_i = se_eq[j][i]
            z_i  = β_i / se_i
            p_i  = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z_i)))
            push!(eqn_col, first_row ? eqnames[j] : "")
            push!(var_col, cns[j][i])
            push!(β_col, β_i);   push!(se_col, se_i)
            push!(z_col, z_i);   push!(p_col, p_i)
            push!(lo_col, β_i - zcrit * se_i)
            push!(hi_col, β_i + zcrit * se_i)
            first_row = false
            nrows += 1
        end
        j < M && push!(hline_at, nrows)
    end
    coef_df = DataFrames.DataFrame(
        Equation     = eqn_col,
        Variable     = var_col,
        Coefficient  = β_col,
        StdErr       = se_col,
        z            = z_col,
        p            = p_col,
        CI_low       = lo_col,
        CI_high      = hi_col,
    )
    lvl = round(Int, 100 * level)
    println()
    coef_header = reps > 0 ?
        (["", "", "Observed", "Bootstrap", "", "", "Normal-based", ""],
         ["Equation", "Variable", "coefficient", "std. err.",
          "z", "P>|z|", "[$(lvl)% CI low]", "[$(lvl)% CI high]"]) :
        ["Equation", "Variable", "Coefficient", "Std. err.",
         "z", "P>|z|", "[$(lvl)% CI low]", "[$(lvl)% CI high]"]
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(coef_df;
        header     = coef_header,
        formatters = (
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.7f", [3, 4, 7, 8]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.2f",  [5]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.3f",  [6]),
        ),
        body_hlines = hline_at,
        alignment   = [:l, :l, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r],
    )
    reps > 0 && Printf.@printf("  (bootstrap replications = %d)\n", reps)

    # ── Residual correlation matrix (from OLS-step Σ̂)
    Rmat = [Σ̂[i, j] / sqrt(Σ̂[i, i] * Σ̂[j, j]) for i in 1:M, j in 1:M]
    corr_strs = [j <= i ? Printf.@sprintf("%.4f", Rmat[i, j]) : ""
                 for i in 1:M, j in 1:M]
    println("\nCorrelation matrix of residuals:\n")
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(corr_strs;
        header     = eqnames,
        row_labels = eqnames,
        alignment  = fill(:r, M),
    )

    # ── Breusch–Pagan test of independence
    bp = 0.0
    for i in 1:M-1, j in (i+1):M
        bp += Rmat[i, j]^2
    end
    bp *= N
    df_bp = M * (M - 1) ÷ 2
    p_bp  = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(df_bp), bp)
    Printf.@printf("\nBreusch–Pagan test of independence: chi2(%d) = %.3f, Pr = %.4f\n",
                   df_bp, bp, p_bp)
    return nothing
end

"""
    test_sureg(fit, vars)

Stata-style `test <varlist>` after `sureg`/`sureg_fit`. Jointly tests that
every coefficient whose short name matches any of `vars` is zero **in every
equation where it appears** — exactly what Stata's `test age age2` does
after `sureg`. Uses the stored joint vcov from `fit`, so cross-equation
covariances are respected (and the bootstrap vcov is used if the fit was
made with `reps>0`).

Output mirrors Stata's:

    ( 1)  [eq1]v1 = 0
    ( 2)  [eq2]v1 = 0
    ...
            chi2(q) =   W
          Prob > chi2 =  p

Example:

    fit = sureg_fit(df_sur,
                    :ldrugexp => [:age, :age2, :actlim, :totchr, :medicaid, :private],
                    :ltotothr => [:age, :age2, :educyr, :actlim, :totchr, :private])
    test_sureg(fit, [:age, :age2])
"""
function test_sureg(fit, vars::AbstractVector;
                    df_F::Union{Integer,Nothing}=nothing)
    coefnames = fit.coefnames
    K         = length(coefnames)

    # Resolve a single token to either (idx=Int[], number) or
    # (coef_idx_list, 0.0). Short names may match >1 equation; `[eq]var` must
    # match exactly one.
    function _resolve(token::AbstractString)
        tok = String(strip(token))
        num = tryparse(Float64, tok)
        num !== nothing && return (Int[], num)
        if startswith(tok, "[") && occursin(']', tok)
            k = findfirst(==(tok), coefnames)
            k === nothing && error("Coefficient not found: $tok")
            return ([k], 0.0)
        end
        idx = Int[]
        for (k, cn) in enumerate(coefnames)
            m = match(r"^\[(.+)\](.+)$", cn)
            (m === nothing || m.captures[2] != tok) && continue
            push!(idx, k)
        end
        isempty(idx) && error("Coefficient not found in any equation: $tok")
        return (idx, 0.0)
    end

    R_rows = Vector{Vector{Float64}}()
    r_vals = Float64[]
    labels = String[]

    for v in vars
        vs = strip(string(v))
        if occursin('=', vs)
            # Equality restriction → one row.
            lhs_s, rhs_s = split(vs, '='; limit=2)
            lhs_idx, lhs_c = _resolve(lhs_s)
            rhs_idx, rhs_c = _resolve(rhs_s)
            length(lhs_idx) > 1 &&
                error("Ambiguous short name on lhs of '$vs' — use [eq]name")
            length(rhs_idx) > 1 &&
                error("Ambiguous short name on rhs of '$vs' — use [eq]name")
            row = zeros(K)
            for k in lhs_idx; row[k] += 1.0; end
            for k in rhs_idx; row[k] -= 1.0; end
            push!(R_rows, row)
            push!(r_vals, rhs_c - lhs_c)
            if !isempty(lhs_idx) && !isempty(rhs_idx)
                push!(labels,
                      "$(coefnames[lhs_idx[1]]) - $(coefnames[rhs_idx[1]]) = 0")
            elseif !isempty(lhs_idx)
                push!(labels, "$(coefnames[lhs_idx[1]]) = $(rhs_c)")
            else
                push!(labels, "$(coefnames[rhs_idx[1]]) = $(lhs_c)")
            end
        else
            # Bare token → one zero-restriction per matching coef.
            idx_all, _ = _resolve(vs)
            for k in idx_all
                row = zeros(K); row[k] = 1.0
                push!(R_rows, row)
                push!(r_vals, 0.0)
                push!(labels, "$(coefnames[k]) = 0")
            end
        end
    end
    isempty(R_rows) && error("No restrictions parsed from: $(vars)")

    R = reduce(vcat, permutedims.(R_rows))          # q × K
    r = r_vals
    d = R * fit.β .- r
    W = d' * LinearAlgebra.inv(R * fit.V * R') * d
    q = length(r)

    for (i, lbl) in enumerate(labels)
        Printf.@printf(" ( %d)  %s\n", i, lbl)
    end
    println()

    # F-form if df_F supplied (e.g. after `sqreg_stata`), else χ².
    if df_F === nothing
        p_chi = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(q), W)
        Printf.@printf("           chi2(%3d) = %7.2f\n", q, W)
        Printf.@printf("         Prob > chi2 = %7.4f\n", p_chi)
        return (; chi2=W, df=q, p=p_chi, restrictions=labels, R, r)
    else
        Fs  = W / q
        p_F = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(q, df_F), Fs)
        Printf.@printf("       F(%3d, %5d) = %7.2f\n", q, df_F, Fs)
        Printf.@printf("            Prob > F = %7.4f\n", p_F)
        return (; F=Fs, df1=q, df2=df_F, p=p_F,
                  chi2=W, restrictions=labels, R, r)
    end
end

"""
    reg3(df, (y1 => [rhs1…]), (y2 => [rhs2…]), …; level=0.95)

Stata-style `reg3` — three-stage least-squares estimation of a linear
simultaneous-equations system. Each pair `y => rhs` specifies one equation;
`rhs` can include variables that are dependent in another equation
(those are the endogenous RHS regressors). All variables that appear on
the RHS somewhere but are **not** a dependent variable anywhere form
the instrument set.

Algorithm:
  1. 2SLS equation-by-equation using `P_Z = Z(Z'Z)⁻¹Z'` (Z = all exog +
     `_cons`) to get initial residuals.
  2. `Σ̂ᵢⱼ = (eᵢ'eⱼ)/N` from the 2SLS residuals.
  3. Blocked GLS with weight `Σ̂⁻¹ ⊗ Iₙ` applied to the P_Z-projected
     regressors: `β̂_3SLS = (Σⱼ Σ̂⁻¹ᵢⱼ X̂ᵢ'X̂ⱼ)⁻¹ (Σⱼ Σ̂⁻¹ᵢⱼ X̂ᵢ'yⱼ)`.

Prints Stata's two-block output (summary + coefficient table via
`PrettyTables`) and the Endogenous/Exogenous footer. Returns the fitted
NamedTuple.
"""
function reg3(df::DataFrames.AbstractDataFrame, eqs::Pair...;
              level::Float64=0.95)
    M = length(eqs)

    # Detect endogenous (all LHS) and exogenous (RHS variables that are not LHS).
    endog_set = Set{Symbol}()
    for (y, _) in eqs
        push!(endog_set, Symbol(y))
    end
    exog_set = Symbol[]
    seen     = Set{Symbol}()
    for (_, rhs) in eqs
        for v in rhs
            sv = Symbol(v)
            if !(sv in endog_set) && !(sv in seen)
                push!(exog_set, sv); push!(seen, sv)
            end
        end
    end

    all_vars = Symbol[]
    for (y, rhs) in eqs
        push!(all_vars, Symbol(y))
        append!(all_vars, Symbol.(rhs))
    end
    d = DataFrames.dropmissing(df, unique(all_vars))
    N = DataFrames.nrow(d)

    Xs      = Vector{Matrix{Float64}}(undef, M)
    Ys      = Vector{Vector{Float64}}(undef, M)
    cns     = Vector{Vector{String}}(undef, M)
    eqnames = Vector{String}(undef, M)
    for (j, (y, rhs)) in enumerate(eqs)
        ys    = Symbol(y)
        rhs_v = [Symbol(v) for v in rhs]
        Xs[j] = hcat(ones(N), [Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, v])) for v in rhs_v]...)
        Ys[j] = Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, ys]))
        cns[j] = vcat("_cons", string.(rhs_v))
        eqnames[j] = string(ys)
    end
    ks       = [size(X, 2) for X in Xs]
    kt       = sum(ks)
    offsets  = vcat(0, cumsum(ks))

    # Instrument matrix Z = [_cons, exog…]
    Z     = hcat(ones(N),
                 [Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, v])) for v in exog_set]...)
    ZtZi  = LinearAlgebra.inv(Z' * Z)
    PX    = [Z * (ZtZi * (Z' * Xs[j])) for j in 1:M]            # X̂ⱼ = P_Z Xⱼ

    # Step 1: 2SLS for each equation → residuals for Σ̂
    β_2sls = Vector{Vector{Float64}}(undef, M)
    e_2sls = Vector{Vector{Float64}}(undef, M)
    for j in 1:M
        β_2sls[j] = (PX[j]' * PX[j]) \ (PX[j]' * Ys[j])
        e_2sls[j] = Ys[j] .- Xs[j] * β_2sls[j]
    end
    Σ̂    = [LinearAlgebra.dot(e_2sls[i], e_2sls[j]) / N for i in 1:M, j in 1:M]
    Σinv = LinearAlgebra.inv(Σ̂)

    # Step 2: 3SLS block GLS
    XWX = zeros(kt, kt)
    XWY = zeros(kt)
    for i in 1:M, j in 1:M
        ri = (offsets[i]+1):offsets[i+1]
        rj = (offsets[j]+1):offsets[j+1]
        XWX[ri, rj] .= Σinv[i, j] .* (PX[i]' * PX[j])
        XWY[ri]    .+= Σinv[i, j] .* (PX[i]' * Ys[j])
    end
    β_3sls = XWX \ XWY
    V_3sls = LinearAlgebra.inv(XWX)

    # Per-equation slices
    β_eq  = [β_3sls[(offsets[j]+1):offsets[j+1]]                              for j in 1:M]
    V_eq  = [V_3sls[(offsets[j]+1):offsets[j+1], (offsets[j]+1):offsets[j+1]] for j in 1:M]
    se_eq = [sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0)) for V in V_eq]
    e_3sls = [Ys[j] .- Xs[j] * β_eq[j] for j in 1:M]

    rmse   = [sqrt(LinearAlgebra.dot(e_3sls[j], e_3sls[j]) / N) for j in 1:M]
    r2s    = Float64[]; chi2s = Float64[]; pvals = Float64[]
    for j in 1:M
        rss = LinearAlgebra.dot(e_3sls[j], e_3sls[j])
        tss = sum((Ys[j] .- Statistics.mean(Ys[j])).^2)
        push!(r2s, 1 - rss / tss)                        # may be negative (3SLS)
        slope = 2:ks[j]
        βs = β_eq[j][slope]; Vs = V_eq[j][slope, slope]
        χ2 = βs' * (Vs \ βs)
        push!(chi2s, χ2)
        push!(pvals, 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(length(slope)), χ2))
    end

    # ── Summary table (PrettyTables) — identical layout to sureg
    println("Three-stage least-squares regression\n")
    summary_df = DataFrames.DataFrame(
        Equation   = eqnames,
        Obs        = fill(N, M),
        Params     = [k - 1 for k in ks],
        RMSE       = rmse,
        R_squared  = r2s,
        chi2       = chi2s,
        P_chi2     = pvals,
    )
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(summary_df;
        header     = ["Equation", "Obs", "Params", "RMSE", "R-squared",
                      "chi2", "P>chi2"],
        formatters = (
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.6f", [4]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.4f", [5, 7]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.2f", [6]),
        ),
        alignment  = [:l, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r],
    )

    # ── Coefficient table — slopes first, `_cons` last, one block per equation
    zcrit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    eqn_col  = String[]; var_col = String[]
    β_col    = Float64[]; se_col  = Float64[]
    z_col    = Float64[]; p_col   = Float64[]
    lo_col   = Float64[]; hi_col  = Float64[]
    hline_at = Int[]; nrows = 0
    for j in 1:M
        first_row = true
        order = vcat(collect(2:ks[j]), 1)
        for i in order
            β_i  = β_eq[j][i]
            se_i = se_eq[j][i]
            z_i  = β_i / se_i
            p_i  = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z_i)))
            push!(eqn_col, first_row ? eqnames[j] : "")
            push!(var_col, cns[j][i])
            push!(β_col, β_i);   push!(se_col, se_i)
            push!(z_col, z_i);   push!(p_col, p_i)
            push!(lo_col, β_i - zcrit * se_i)
            push!(hi_col, β_i + zcrit * se_i)
            first_row = false; nrows += 1
        end
        j < M && push!(hline_at, nrows)
    end
    coef_df = DataFrames.DataFrame(
        Equation    = eqn_col,
        Variable    = var_col,
        Coefficient = β_col,
        StdErr      = se_col,
        z           = z_col,
        p           = p_col,
        CI_low      = lo_col,
        CI_high     = hi_col,
    )
    lvl = round(Int, 100 * level)
    println()
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(coef_df;
        header = ["Equation", "Variable", "Coefficient", "Std. err.",
                  "z", "P>|z|", "[$(lvl)% CI low]", "[$(lvl)% CI high]"],
        formatters = (
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.7f", [3, 4, 7, 8]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.2f",  [5]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.3f",  [6]),
        ),
        body_hlines = hline_at,
        alignment   = [:l, :l, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r],
    )

    Printf.@printf("Endogenous: %s\n", join(sort(string.(collect(endog_set))), " "))
    Printf.@printf("Exogenous:  %s\n", join(string.(exog_set), " "))

    # Build joint coefnames for potential post-estimation (e.g. test_sureg-style).
    coefnames_full = String[]
    for j in 1:M, i in 1:ks[j]
        push!(coefnames_full, "[$(eqnames[j])]$(cns[j][i])")
    end
    return (; β=β_3sls, V=V_3sls, coefnames=coefnames_full,
              eqnames, cns, ks, offsets, N, Σ̂,
              β_eq, V_eq, se_eq, rmse, r2s, chi2s, pvals,
              endog=collect(endog_set), exog=exog_set)
end

"""
    qreg_stata(df, y, xs; τ=0.5, level=0.95)

Stata-style `qreg y xs…[, quantile(τ)]` — quantile regression by
minimizing the asymmetric "check" loss
`ρ_τ(u) = u · (τ − 1{u < 0})`. Slopes are listed first in the coefficient
table and `_cons` is last, matching Stata's display order.

Standard errors use the classical **iid** Koenker-Bassett formula
`V̂ = s(τ)² · (X'X)⁻¹`, with sparsity `ω = (û_{τ+h} − û_{τ−h})/(2h)` and
Hall-Sheather bandwidth `h = n^{-1/3} · z_{α/2}^{2/3} ·
(1.5φ(Φ⁻¹(τ))²/(2Φ⁻¹(τ)²+1))^{1/3}`. `s(τ) = √(τ(1−τ)) · ω`. Inference
is t-based on `n − k` df.

Pseudo R² (Koenker–Machado): `1 − MSD/RSD`, where MSD is the minimized
check-loss and RSD is the check-loss of the constant-only model (its
fit is the τ-th quantile of y).
"""
function qreg_stata(df, y, xs::AbstractVector; τ::Float64=0.5,
                    level::Float64=0.95,
                    max_iter::Int=5000, tol::Float64=1e-12,
                    quiet::Bool=false)
    ys   = Symbol(y)
    xsv  = [Symbol(v) for v in xs]
    d    = DataFrames.dropmissing(df, unique(vcat(ys, xsv)))
    n    = DataFrames.nrow(d)
    Y    = Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, ys]))
    Xmat = hcat([Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, v])) for v in xsv]..., ones(n))  # slopes, _cons
    k    = size(Xmat, 2)

    # ── IRLS (majorize-minimize on |u|) — robust for the non-smooth check loss.
    # Majorization: |u| ≤ u²/(2|u*|) + |u*|/2; minimizing the upper bound on
    # Σ ρ_τ(eᵢ) yields  β̂ = (X'WX)⁻¹ [X'W y + (2τ−1) · X'1],  with
    # weights wᵢ = 1/max(|eᵢ*|, δ).  δ is shrunk as iterations progress.
    β   = Xmat \ Y
    one = ones(n)
    δ   = 1e-2
    for iter in 1:max_iter
        e    = Y .- Xmat * β
        δ    = max(min(δ, 0.5 * Statistics.median(abs.(e)) + 1e-12), 1e-10)
        w    = 1.0 ./ max.(abs.(e), δ)
        rhs  = Xmat' * (w .* Y) .+ (2τ - 1) .* (Xmat' * one)
        XtWX = Xmat' * (w .* Xmat)
        β_nw = XtWX \ rhs
        dif  = sqrt(sum((β_nw .- β).^2)) / max(1.0, sqrt(sum(β.^2)))
        β    = β_nw
        dif < tol && break
    end
    û  = Y .- Xmat * β

    _rho(u, τ) = u >= 0 ? τ * u : (τ - 1) * u
    MSD   = sum(_rho(û[i], τ) for i in 1:n)
    q_y   = Statistics.quantile(Y, τ)
    RSD   = sum(_rho(Y[i] - q_y, τ) for i in 1:n)
    R²_ps = 1 - MSD / RSD

    # Hall-Sheather bandwidth and Koenker–Bassett (iid) sparsity
    α     = 1 - level
    z_α   = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - α/2)
    Φinv  = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), τ)
    φ_τ   = Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), Φinv)
    h     = n^(-1/3) * z_α^(2/3) *
            ((1.5 * φ_τ^2) / (2 * Φinv^2 + 1))^(1/3)
    p_up  = min(τ + h, 1 - 1/n)
    p_dn  = max(τ - h, 1/n)
    ω     = (Statistics.quantile(û, p_up) -
             Statistics.quantile(û, p_dn)) / (p_up - p_dn)
    s_τ   = sqrt(τ * (1 - τ)) * ω

    V     = s_τ^2 .* LinearAlgebra.inv(Xmat' * Xmat)
    se    = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))

    dofv  = n - k
    t_st  = β ./ se
    tcrit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(dofv), 1 - α/2)
    pvals = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.TDist(dofv), abs.(t_st)))
    ci_lo = β .- tcrit .* se
    ci_hi = β .+ tcrit .* se

    nms  = vcat(string.(xsv), "_cons")
    if !quiet
        _fmtn(x) = replace(string(x), r"(\d)(?=(\d{3})+$)" => s"\1,")
        title = τ == 0.5 ? "Median regression" :
                            Printf.@sprintf("Quantile regression (τ = %.2f)", τ)
        println()
        header_tail = Printf.@sprintf("Number of obs = %10s", _fmtn(n))
        println(title, " "^max(0, 52 - length(title)), header_tail)
        Printf.@printf("  Raw sum of deviations %.2f (about %g)\n", RSD, q_y)
        left3 = Printf.@sprintf("  Min sum of deviations %.3f", MSD)
        tail3 = Printf.@sprintf("Pseudo R2     = %10.4f", R²_ps)
        println(left3, " "^max(0, 52 - length(left3)), tail3)
        println()

        lvl  = round(Int, 100 * level)
        coef_df = DataFrames.DataFrame(
            Variable    = nms,
            Coefficient = β,
            StdErr      = se,
            t           = t_st,
            p           = pvals,
            CI_low      = ci_lo,
            CI_high     = ci_hi,
        )
        PrettyTables.pretty_table(coef_df;
            header = [string(ys), "Coefficient", "Std. err.",
                      "t", "P>|t|", "[$(lvl)% CI low]", "[$(lvl)% CI high]"],
            formatters = (
                PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.7f", [2, 3, 6, 7]),
                PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.2f",  [4]),
                PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.3f",  [5]),
            ),
            alignment = [:l, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r],
        )
    end

    return (; β, V, se, t=t_st, p=pvals, ci_lo, ci_hi,
              coefnames=nms, n, MSD, RSD, pseudo_R2=R²_ps, τ)
end

"""
    sqreg_stata(df, y, xs; τs=[0.25, 0.5, 0.75], reps=400,
                seed=nothing, level=0.95)

Stata-style `sqreg y xs…, q(τ₁ τ₂ …) reps(B)` — **simultaneous** quantile
regression at several τ values with **bootstrap** standard errors. Each
bootstrap replicate resamples rows once and re-fits every quantile on
the same sample, so covariance across quantiles is preserved (this is
what distinguishes `sqreg` from running `bsqreg` separately at each τ).

Prints the Stata header (`Number of obs`, a line per quantile with its
Pseudo R²) followed by a grouped coefficient table — one block per
quantile — with Bootstrap std. err. and t-based CIs on `n − k` df.
"""
function sqreg_stata(df, y, xs::AbstractVector;
                     τs::AbstractVector{<:Real}=[0.25, 0.50, 0.75],
                     reps::Int=400, seed::Union{Int,Nothing}=nothing,
                     level::Float64=0.95)
    ys  = Symbol(y)
    xsv = [Symbol(v) for v in xs]
    d   = DataFrames.dropmissing(df, unique(vcat(ys, xsv)))
    n   = DataFrames.nrow(d)
    k   = length(xsv) + 1
    Q   = length(τs)

    # Point estimates at each τ (silent).
    fits = [qreg_stata(d, ys, xsv; τ=Float64(τ), quiet=true) for τ in τs]
    β0   = [f.β for f in fits]

    # Paired bootstrap — resample rows once per rep, refit every τ.
    seed !== nothing && Random.seed!(seed)
    β_boot = [Matrix{Float64}(undef, reps, k) for _ in 1:Q]
    for b in 1:reps
        idx = StatsBase.sample(1:n, n; replace=true)
        db  = d[idx, :]
        for (j, τ) in enumerate(τs)
            β_boot[j][b, :] = qreg_stata(db, ys, xsv; τ=Float64(τ), quiet=true).β
        end
    end
    se = [vec(Statistics.std(β_boot[j]; dims=1)) for j in 1:Q]

    # Inference (t on n − k df)
    α     = 1 - level
    dofv  = n - k
    tcrit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(dofv), 1 - α/2)
    t_q   = [β0[j] ./ se[j] for j in 1:Q]
    p_q   = [2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.TDist(dofv), abs.(t_q[j])))
             for j in 1:Q]
    lo_q  = [β0[j] .- tcrit .* se[j] for j in 1:Q]
    hi_q  = [β0[j] .+ tcrit .* se[j] for j in 1:Q]

    _fmtn(x) = replace(string(x), r"(\d)(?=(\d{3})+$)" => s"\1,")
    nms  = vcat(string.(xsv), "_cons")

    # ─── Header block
    println()
    title = "Simultaneous quantile regression"
    Printf.@printf("%s%*sNumber of obs = %10s\n",
                   title, max(0, 52 - length(title)), "", _fmtn(n))
    left_boot = Printf.@sprintf("  bootstrap(%d) SEs", reps)
    Printf.@printf("%s%*s.%02d Pseudo R2 = %10.4f\n",
                   left_boot, max(0, 52 - length(left_boot)), "",
                   round(Int, 100*τs[1]), fits[1].pseudo_R2)
    for j in 2:Q
        Printf.@printf("%52s.%02d Pseudo R2 = %10.4f\n",
                       "", round(Int, 100*τs[j]), fits[j].pseudo_R2)
    end
    println()

    # ─── Grouped coefficient table in Stata's exact 78-col layout
    _g(x, w; sig=7) = begin
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, ".")
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, s)
    end
    lvl = round(Int, 100 * level)
    println("-"^78)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %22s\n", "", "Bootstrap")
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s  %9s  %6s  %5s     [%d%% conf. interval]\n",
                   string(ys), "Coefficient", "std. err.", "t", "P>|t|", lvl)
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    for j in 1:Q
        qlabel = Printf.@sprintf("q%02d", round(Int, 100*τs[j]))
        Printf.@printf("%-12s |\n", qlabel)
        for i in 1:k
            Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %7.2f  %6.3f  %s  %s\n",
                           nms[i],
                           _g(β0[j][i], 10),
                           _g(se[j][i], 9),
                           t_q[j][i], p_q[j][i],
                           _g(lo_q[j][i], 11),
                           _g(hi_q[j][i], 10))
        end
        j < Q && println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    end
    println("-"^78)

    # Joint (stacked) β, V, coefnames in `[qXX]var` format — enables
    # post-estimation tests across quantiles via `test_sureg`.
    β_joint = reduce(vcat, β0)
    boot_stack = reduce(hcat, β_boot)              # B × (Q·k)
    V_joint = Statistics.cov(boot_stack)
    coefnames_joint = String[]
    for τ in τs, v in nms
        push!(coefnames_joint,
              "[" * Printf.@sprintf("q%02d", round(Int, 100*τ)) * "]" * v)
    end

    return (; β = β_joint, V = V_joint, coefnames = coefnames_joint,
              β_by_q = β0, se, t = t_q, p = p_q,
              ci_lo = lo_q, ci_hi = hi_q,
              pseudo_R2 = [f.pseudo_R2 for f in fits],
              varnames = nms, τs, n, reps, df_resid = dofv)
end

"""
    qcount_stata(df, y, xs; τ=0.5, reps=500, seed=nothing, level=0.95)

Stata-style `qcount y xs…, q(τ) rep(B)` — Machado & Santos Silva (2005)
**quantile count regression**, matching Miranda's `qcount` on SSC.

Algorithm (single-loop form used by `qcount`):

  for `r = 1, …, reps`:
    1. Draw paired bootstrap indices (resample rows with replacement).
    2. Jitter:  `y*ᵢ = yᵢ + U(0,1)`.
    3. IRLS quantile regression at τ on `log(y*)` ⇒ `β̂_r`.

  * **Point estimate** `β̂ = mean(β̂_r)`.
  * **Variance** `V̂ = cov(β̂_r)`  (captures both sampling and jittering
    uncertainty, the same way Stata's `qcount` does).

Returns `(; β, V, se, coefnames, n, reps, τ)`.
"""
function qcount_stata(df, y, xs::AbstractVector;
                      τ::Float64=0.5, reps::Int=500,
                      seed::Union{Int,Nothing}=nothing,
                      level::Float64=0.95)
    ys  = Symbol(y)
    xsv = [Symbol(v) for v in xs]
    d   = DataFrames.dropmissing(df, unique(vcat(ys, xsv)))
    n   = DataFrames.nrow(d)
    Y   = Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, ys]))
    X   = hcat([Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, v])) for v in xsv]..., ones(n))  # slopes, _cons
    k   = size(X, 2)

    # IRLS on the majorization of |u| — same loop as `qreg_stata`.
    function _irls(Xm, Ym, τ; max_iter::Int=2000, tol::Float64=1e-10)
        β = Xm \ Ym;   oneVec = ones(length(Ym));   δ = 1e-2
        for _ in 1:max_iter
            e     = Ym .- Xm * β
            δ     = max(min(δ, 0.5 * Statistics.median(abs.(e)) + 1e-12), 1e-10)
            w     = 1.0 ./ max.(abs.(e), δ)
            rhs   = Xm' * (w .* Ym) .+ (2τ - 1) .* (Xm' * oneVec)
            XtWX  = Xm' * (w .* Xm)
            β_nw  = XtWX \ rhs
            dif   = sqrt(sum((β_nw .- β).^2)) / max(1.0, sqrt(sum(β.^2)))
            β     = β_nw
            dif < tol && break
        end
        return β
    end

    # Single-loop: each rep = (paired bootstrap) + (jitter) + (QR fit).
    seed !== nothing && Random.seed!(seed)
    β_draws = Matrix{Float64}(undef, reps, k)
    for r in 1:reps
        idx = StatsBase.sample(1:n, n; replace=true)
        Xb  = X[idx, :]
        Yb  = Y[idx]
        β_draws[r, :] = _irls(Xb, log.(Yb .+ rand(n)), τ)
    end

    β  = vec(Statistics.mean(β_draws; dims=1))
    V  = Statistics.cov(β_draws)
    se = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))

    # Inference (t on n − k df).
    α     = 1 - level
    dofv  = n - k
    t_st  = β ./ se
    tcrit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(dofv), 1 - α/2)
    pvals = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.TDist(dofv), abs.(t_st)))
    ci_lo = β .- tcrit .* se
    ci_hi = β .+ tcrit .* se

    _fmtn(x) = replace(string(x), r"(\d)(?=(\d{3})+$)" => s"\1,")
    _g(x, w; sig=7) = begin
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, ".")
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, s)
    end

    println()
    title = Printf.@sprintf("Jittered count-quantile regression (τ = %.2f)", τ)
    Printf.@printf("%s%*sNumber of obs = %10s\n",
                   title, max(0, 52 - length(title)), "", _fmtn(n))
    Printf.@printf("  No. jittered samples = %d\n\n", reps)

    nms = vcat(string.(xsv), "_cons")
    println("-"^78)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %22s\n", "", "Bootstrap")
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s  %9s  %6s  %5s     [%d%% conf. interval]\n",
                   string(ys), "Coefficient", "std. err.", "t", "P>|t|",
                   round(Int, 100*level))
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    for i in 1:k
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %7.2f  %6.3f  %s  %s\n",
                       nms[i], _g(β[i], 10), _g(se[i], 9),
                       t_st[i], pvals[i],
                       _g(ci_lo[i], 11), _g(ci_hi[i], 10))
    end
    println("-"^78)

    return (; β, V, se, t = t_st, p = pvals, ci_lo, ci_hi,
              coefnames = nms, n, reps, τ, df_resid = dofv)
end

"""
    svy_mean(df, var; stratum, psu, weight, level=0.95)

Stata-style `svy: mean <var>` — Horvitz–Thompson weighted mean of a single
variable with Taylor-linearized standard errors under a stratified single-
stage cluster design. Prints the Stata summary header (Number of strata,
PSUs, obs, Population size, Design df) followed by a `PrettyTables` block
with Mean / Linearized std. err. / confidence interval.

Variance formula: Var(ȳ) ≈ V̂(Σᵢ zᵢ) with zᵢ = wᵢ(yᵢ − ȳ)/Σwᵢ; for each
stratum `h` with `nₕ` PSUs, `V̂ = Σₕ (nₕ/(nₕ−1)) Σⱼ (uₕⱼ − ūₕ)²` where
`uₕⱼ = Σᵢ∈PSUⱼ zᵢ`. Equivalent to Stata's default `svyset ..., ` (with-
replacement PSU sampling, so no FPC is applied). Degrees of freedom =
`#PSUs − #strata`, used for the t-based CI.

Example:

    svy_mean(df_nh, :hgb; stratum=:strata, psu=:psu, weight=:finalwgt)
"""
function svy_mean(df, var; stratum::Symbol, psu::Symbol, weight::Symbol,
                  level::Float64=0.95)
    d = DataFrames.dropmissing(df, [Symbol(var), stratum, psu, weight])
    y = Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, Symbol(var)]))
    w = Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, weight]))
    h = _rawval.(d[!, stratum])
    c = _rawval.(d[!, psu])

    n    = length(y)
    W    = sum(w)
    ybar = sum(w .* y) / W
    z    = w .* (y .- ybar) ./ W               # Taylor-linearized score

    V = 0.0
    strata  = unique(h)
    n_psu   = 0
    for hv in strata
        in_h   = h .== hv
        psus_h = c[in_h]
        z_h    = z[in_h]
        ups    = unique(psus_h)
        nh     = length(ups)
        n_psu += nh
        nh < 2 && continue
        u = [sum(z_h[psus_h .== p]) for p in ups]
        ū = Statistics.mean(u)
        V += (nh / (nh - 1)) * sum((u .- ū).^2)
    end
    se    = sqrt(V)
    n_str = length(strata)
    dfree = n_psu - n_str
    tcrit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(dfree), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    ci_lo = ybar - tcrit * se
    ci_hi = ybar + tcrit * se

    _fmtn(x) = replace(string(x), r"(\d)(?=(\d{3})+$)" => s"\1,")

    println("Survey: Mean estimation\n")
    Printf.@printf("Number of strata = %-13d Number of obs   = %11s\n",
                   n_str, _fmtn(n))
    Printf.@printf("Number of PSUs   = %-13d Population size = %11s\n",
                   n_psu, _fmtn(Int(round(W))))
    Printf.@printf("%33s Design df       = %11d\n", "", dfree)
    println()

    lvl = round(Int, 100 * level)
    result = DataFrames.DataFrame(
        Variable = [string(var)],
        Mean     = [ybar],
        StdErr   = [se],
        CI_low   = [ci_lo],
        CI_high  = [ci_hi],
    )
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(result;
        header     = (["",         "",     "Linearized", "",                "" ],
                      ["Variable", "Mean", "std. err.",
                       "[$(lvl)% CI low]", "[$(lvl)% CI high]"]),
        formatters = PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.5f", 2:5),
        alignment  = [:l, :r, :r, :r, :r],
    )
    return (; mean=ybar, se=se, ci=(ci_lo, ci_hi), df=dfree,
              n_obs=n, n_strata=n_str, n_psu, popsize=W)
end

"""
    mean_stata(df, var; level=0.95)

Stata-style `mean <var>` — plain sample mean with SE = `s/√n` and a
t-based CI on df = n − 1. No weights, no clustering. Prints the compact
Stata "Mean estimation" header and a `PrettyTables` block with
Mean / Std. err. / CI.

Example:

    mean_stata(df_nh, :hgb)
"""
function mean_stata(df, var; level::Float64=0.95)
    vs = Symbol(var)
    d  = DataFrames.dropmissing(df, [vs])
    y  = Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, vs]))
    n  = length(y)
    μ  = Statistics.mean(y)
    s  = Statistics.std(y)           # sample std, ddof = 1
    se = s / sqrt(n)
    dfree = n - 1
    tcrit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(dfree), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    ci_lo = μ - tcrit * se
    ci_hi = μ + tcrit * se

    _fmtn(x) = replace(string(x), r"(\d)(?=(\d{3})+$)" => s"\1,")
    Printf.@printf("Mean estimation                          Number of obs = %s\n\n",
                   _fmtn(n))

    lvl = round(Int, 100 * level)
    result = DataFrames.DataFrame(
        Variable = [string(var)],
        Mean     = [μ],
        StdErr   = [se],
        CI_low   = [ci_lo],
        CI_high  = [ci_hi],
    )
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(result;
        header     = ["Variable", "Mean", "Std. err.",
                      "[$(lvl)% CI low]", "[$(lvl)% CI high]"],
        formatters = PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.5f", 2:5),
        alignment  = [:l, :r, :r, :r, :r],
    )
    return (; mean=μ, se=se, ci=(ci_lo, ci_hi), df=dfree, n_obs=n)
end

"""
    svy_regress(df, y, xs; stratum, psu, weight, level=0.95)

Stata-style `svy: regress <y> <xs…>` — weighted OLS with Taylor-linearized
(Horvitz–Thompson) cluster-robust standard errors under a stratified
single-stage cluster design. Prints the Stata header block (strata, PSUs,
obs, popsize, design df, model F, Prob > F, R²) followed by a
`PrettyTables` coefficient table with slopes first and `_cons` last.

Variance: `V̂(β̂) = A⁻¹ · V̂(Σ uᵢ) · A⁻¹` where `A = X'WX`, scores
`uᵢ = wᵢ xᵢ eᵢ`, and `V̂(Σ uᵢ) = Σₕ nₕ/(nₕ−1) · Σⱼ (Sₕⱼ − S̄ₕ)(Sₕⱼ − S̄ₕ)'`
with `Sₕⱼ = Σᵢ∈PSUⱼ uᵢ`. Degrees of freedom = #PSUs − #strata. The model
F uses Stata's first-order-corrected denominator df `= design_df − q + 1`.

Example:

    svy_regress(df_nh, :hgb, [:age, :female];
                stratum=:strata, psu=:psu, weight=:finalwgt)
"""
function svy_regress(df, y, xs::AbstractVector; stratum::Symbol, psu::Symbol,
                     weight::Symbol, level::Float64=0.95)
    ys   = Symbol(y)
    xsv  = [Symbol(v) for v in xs]
    d    = DataFrames.dropmissing(df, unique([ys, xsv..., stratum, psu, weight]))
    Y    = Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, ys]))
    w    = Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, weight]))
    h    = _rawval.(d[!, stratum])
    c    = _rawval.(d[!, psu])
    Xcols = [Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, v])) for v in xsv]
    X    = hcat(ones(length(Y)), Xcols...)
    cn   = vcat("_cons", string.(xsv))   # index 1 is intercept

    n    = length(Y)
    k    = size(X, 2)
    q    = k - 1                         # slopes (exclude intercept)

    # β̂ from weighted LS
    XW   = X .* w
    A    = XW' * X
    β    = A \ (XW' * Y)
    e    = Y .- X * β
    U    = XW .* e                       # n × k  (uᵢ = wᵢ xᵢ eᵢ)

    # Cluster/stratum aggregation
    strata = unique(h)
    V_meat = zeros(k, k)
    n_psu  = 0
    for hv in strata
        in_h = h .== hv
        psus = c[in_h]
        U_h  = U[in_h, :]
        ups  = unique(psus)
        nh   = length(ups)
        n_psu += nh
        nh < 2 && continue
        S = reduce(vcat, [sum(U_h[psus .== p, :]; dims=1) for p in ups])  # nh × k
        S̄ = Statistics.mean(S; dims=1)
        centered = S .- S̄
        V_meat .+= (nh / (nh - 1)) .* (centered' * centered)
    end
    A_inv = LinearAlgebra.inv(A)
    V     = A_inv * V_meat * A_inv
    se    = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))

    n_str = length(strata)
    dfree = n_psu - n_str

    t_stat = β ./ se
    tcrit  = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(dfree), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    pvals  = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.TDist(dfree), abs.(t_stat)))
    ci_lo  = β .- tcrit .* se
    ci_hi  = β .+ tcrit .* se

    # Model F (slopes = 0), first-order corrected df
    slope  = 2:k
    Wstat  = β[slope]' * (V[slope, slope] \ β[slope])
    Fstat  = Wstat / q
    df2    = dfree - q + 1
    pF     = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(q, df2), Fstat)

    # Weighted R²
    ybar_w = sum(w .* Y) / sum(w)
    rss_w  = sum(w .* e.^2)
    tss_w  = sum(w .* (Y .- ybar_w).^2)
    r2     = 1 - rss_w / tss_w
    W_tot  = sum(w)

    _fmtn(x) = replace(string(x), r"(\d)(?=(\d{3})+$)" => s"\1,")

    println("Survey: Linear regression\n")
    Printf.@printf("Number of strata = %-30dNumber of obs   = %11s\n",
                   n_str, _fmtn(n))
    Printf.@printf("Number of PSUs   = %-30dPopulation size = %11s\n",
                   n_psu, _fmtn(Int(round(W_tot))))
    Printf.@printf("%-49sDesign df       = %11d\n", "", dfree)
    Printf.@printf("%-49s%-16s= %11.2f\n", "", "F($q, $df2)",  Fstat)
    Printf.@printf("%-49s%-16s= %11.4f\n", "", "Prob > F",      pF)
    Printf.@printf("%-49s%-16s= %11.4f\n", "", "R-squared",     r2)
    println()

    # Coefficient table (slopes first, _cons last)
    order = vcat(collect(slope), 1)
    lvl   = round(Int, 100 * level)
    coef_df = DataFrames.DataFrame(
        Variable    = cn[order],
        Coefficient = β[order],
        StdErr      = se[order],
        t           = t_stat[order],
        p           = pvals[order],
        CI_low      = ci_lo[order],
        CI_high     = ci_hi[order],
    )
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(coef_df;
        header = (["",          "",            "Linearized", "",  "",      "",                "" ],
                  [string(ys),  "Coefficient", "std. err.",  "t", "P>|t|",
                   "[$(lvl)% CI low]", "[$(lvl)% CI high]"]),
        formatters = (
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.7f", [2, 3, 6, 7]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.2f", [4]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.3f", [5]),
        ),
        alignment  = [:l, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r],
    )

    return (; β, V, se, t=t_stat, p=pvals, ci_lo, ci_hi,
              coefnames=cn, n_obs=n, n_strata=n_str, n_psu,
              popsize=W_tot, df=dfree,
              F=Fstat, df1=q, df2, pF, r2)
end

"""
    regress_cluster(df, y, xs; cluster, weight=nothing, level=0.95)

Stata-style `regress y xs [pweight=w], vce(cluster id)` — weighted OLS
with one-way cluster-robust standard errors. Uses Stata's hc1-flavoured
adjustment `(M/(M−1)) · (N−1)/(N−k)` on the meat matrix, t-tests on
`M − 1` degrees of freedom, model F distributed `F(q, M−1)`, and the
familiar "(Std. err. adjusted for M clusters in <id>)" banner.

`cluster` is the cluster variable (Symbol); `weight=nothing` runs plain
OLS, otherwise treats the weights as pweights. Root MSE uses pweights
normalized to sum to N.

Example:

    df_nh.uniqpsu = 2 .* df_nh.strata .+ df_nh.psu
    regress_cluster(df_nh, :hgb, [:age, :female];
                    cluster=:uniqpsu, weight=:finalwgt)
"""
function regress_cluster(df, y, xs::AbstractVector; cluster::Symbol,
                         weight::Union{Symbol,Nothing}=nothing,
                         level::Float64=0.95)
    ys     = Symbol(y)
    xsv    = [Symbol(v) for v in xs]
    needed = unique(vcat(ys, xsv, cluster))
    weight !== nothing && push!(needed, weight)
    d = DataFrames.dropmissing(df, needed)
    Y = Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, ys]))
    Xcols = [Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, v])) for v in xsv]
    X  = hcat(ones(length(Y)), Xcols...)
    c  = _rawval.(d[!, cluster])
    w  = weight === nothing ? ones(length(Y)) :
         Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, weight]))
    cn = vcat("_cons", string.(xsv))

    n = length(Y); k = size(X, 2); q = k - 1

    XW = X .* w
    A  = XW' * X
    β  = A \ (XW' * Y)
    e  = Y .- X * β
    U  = XW .* e                          # scores uᵢ = wᵢ xᵢ eᵢ

    clusters = unique(c)
    M    = length(clusters)
    meat = zeros(k, k)
    for cl in clusters
        sel = c .== cl
        s   = sum(U[sel, :]; dims=1)       # 1 × k
        meat .+= s' * s
    end
    adj   = (M / (M - 1)) * ((n - 1) / (n - k))
    A_inv = LinearAlgebra.inv(A)
    V     = adj .* (A_inv * meat * A_inv)
    se    = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))

    dfree  = M - 1
    t_stat = β ./ se
    tcrit  = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(dfree), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    pvals  = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.TDist(dfree), abs.(t_stat)))
    ci_lo  = β .- tcrit .* se
    ci_hi  = β .+ tcrit .* se

    slope = 2:k
    Wstat = β[slope]' * (V[slope, slope] \ β[slope])
    Fstat = Wstat / q
    pF    = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(q, dfree), Fstat)

    # Weighted R² (sums weighted by w)
    ybar_w = sum(w .* Y) / sum(w)
    rss_w  = sum(w .* e.^2)
    tss_w  = sum(w .* (Y .- ybar_w).^2)
    r2     = 1 - rss_w / tss_w

    # Root MSE: normalize pweights to sum to N (Stata convention for pweights).
    w_norm = w .* (n / sum(w))
    rmse   = sqrt(sum(w_norm .* e.^2) / (n - k))

    _fmtn(x) = replace(string(x), r"(\d)(?=(\d{3})+$)" => s"\1,")

    Printf.@printf("%-48s%-18s= %10s\n",  "Linear regression", "Number of obs", _fmtn(n))
    Printf.@printf("%-48s%-18s= %10.2f\n", "", "F($q, $dfree)",  Fstat)
    Printf.@printf("%-48s%-18s= %10.4f\n", "", "Prob > F",       pF)
    Printf.@printf("%-48s%-18s= %10.4f\n", "", "R-squared",      r2)
    Printf.@printf("%-48s%-18s= %10.4f\n", "", "Root MSE",       rmse)
    println()
    Printf.@printf("%78s\n", "(Std. err. adjusted for $M clusters in $cluster)")

    order  = vcat(collect(slope), 1)
    lvl    = round(Int, 100 * level)
    coef_df = DataFrames.DataFrame(
        Variable    = cn[order],
        Coefficient = β[order],
        StdErr      = se[order],
        t           = t_stat[order],
        p           = pvals[order],
        CI_low      = ci_lo[order],
        CI_high     = ci_hi[order],
    )
    PrettyTables.pretty_table(coef_df;
        header = (["",          "",            "Robust",     "",  "",      "",                "" ],
                  [string(ys),  "Coefficient", "std. err.",  "t", "P>|t|",
                   "[$(lvl)% CI low]", "[$(lvl)% CI high]"]),
        formatters = (
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.7f", [2, 3, 6, 7]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.2f", [4]),
            PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.3f", [5]),
        ),
        alignment = [:l, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r],
    )
    return (; β, V, se, t=t_stat, p=pvals, ci_lo, ci_hi, coefnames=cn,
              n_obs=n, M, df=dfree, F=Fstat, df1=q, df2=dfree, pF, r2, rmse)
end

"""
    areg_stata(df, y, xs; absorb, cluster=nothing, level=0.95)

Stata-style `areg y xs…, absorb(id) [vce(cluster id)]` — LSDV / fixed-
effects regression with the panel indicators absorbed. Fit via
`FixedEffectModels.reg(... + fe(absorb), Vcov.cluster(cluster))` (or
`Vcov.simple()` when `cluster=nothing`). `xs` variables that are
collinear with the absorbed fixed effect (e.g. time-invariant regressors)
are flagged `(omitted)` in the coefficient table.

Prints the Stata `areg` header block — `Linear regression, absorbing
indicators`, `Absorbed variable`, F, Prob > F, R², Adj R², Root MSE — the
cluster banner, and the coefficient table. `_cons = ȳ − x̄'β̂`, with SE by
the delta method on the cluster vcov.
"""
function areg_stata(df, y, xs::AbstractVector; absorb::Symbol,
                    cluster::Union{Symbol,Nothing}=nothing,
                    level::Float64=0.95)
    ys  = Symbol(y)
    xsv = [Symbol(v) for v in xs]
    needed = unique(vcat(ys, xsv, absorb))
    cluster !== nothing && push!(needed, cluster)
    d = DataFrames.dropmissing(df, unique(needed))
    n = DataFrames.nrow(d)

    f    = term(ys) ~ sum(term.(xsv)) + FixedEffectModels.fe(absorb)
    vcov = cluster === nothing ? FixedEffectModels.Vcov.simple() :
                                 FixedEffectModels.Vcov.cluster(cluster)
    m    = FixedEffectModels.reg(d, f, vcov)

    β_all    = StatsBase.coef(m)
    V_all    = StatsBase.vcov(m)
    cn_all   = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m))

    # FixedEffectModels keeps dropped (collinear) regressors in the output
    # with NaN coef and NaN in the corresponding row AND column of V. Filter
    # on the DIAGONAL only — the off-diagonals linking a bad column to a good
    # row are NaN too, so row-wise `all(isfinite, …)` would reject every row.
    ok   = findall(i -> isfinite(β_all[i]) && isfinite(V_all[i, i]),
                   eachindex(β_all))
    β    = β_all[ok]
    V    = V_all[ok, ok]
    # Safety: any remaining non-finite cross terms collapse to 0 (those rows
    # are orthogonal to everything else the sandwich can say anything about).
    V    = replace(V, NaN => 0.0, Inf => 0.0, -Inf => 0.0)
    se   = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))
    cn_m = cn_all[ok]

    n_groups = length(unique(d[!, absorb]))
    rss   = StatsBase.deviance(m)
    r2    = StatsBase.r2(m)
    ar2   = StatsBase.adjr2(m)
    rmse  = sqrt(rss / Int(StatsBase.dof_residual(m)))

    # For cluster inference, df = #clusters − 1.
    if cluster !== nothing
        cluster_N = length(unique(d[!, cluster]))
        dof_r     = cluster_N - 1
    else
        cluster_N = 0
        dof_r     = Int(StatsBase.dof_residual(m))
    end

    q     = length(β)
    Fstat = q > 0 ? (β' * LinearAlgebra.inv(V) * β) / q : NaN
    pF    = q > 0 ? 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(q, dof_r), Fstat) :
                     NaN

    tcrit  = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(dof_r), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    t_st   = β ./ se
    pvals  = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.TDist(dof_r), abs.(t_st)))
    ci_lo  = β .- tcrit .* se
    ci_hi  = β .+ tcrit .* se

    # _cons = ȳ − x̄'β̂ (only valid β), SE via delta method on the cluster vcov.
    y_mean  = Statistics.mean(Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, ys])))
    x_means = Float64[Statistics.mean(Float64.(_rawval.(d[!, Symbol(nm)])))
                      for nm in cn_m]
    cons_b  = y_mean - LinearAlgebra.dot(x_means, β)
    cons_se = sqrt(max(x_means' * V * x_means, 0.0))
    cons_t  = cons_se > 0 ? cons_b / cons_se : NaN
    cons_p  = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.TDist(dof_r), abs(cons_t)))
    cons_lo = cons_b - tcrit * cons_se
    cons_hi = cons_b + tcrit * cons_se

    dropped = [v for v in string.(xsv) if !(v in cn_m)]

    _fmtn(x) = replace(string(x), r"(\d)(?=(\d{3})+$)" => s"\1,")
    _g(x, w; sig=7) = begin
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, ".")
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        0 < abs(x) < 1 && (s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
        Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, s)
    end

    # ── Header
    Printf.@printf("%-52s%-18s= %6s\n",
                   "Linear regression, absorbing indicators",
                   "Number of obs", _fmtn(n))
    Printf.@printf("%-52s%-18s= %6s\n",
                   "Absorbed variable: $absorb",
                   "No. of categories", _fmtn(n_groups))
    Printf.@printf("%-52s%-18s= %6.2f\n", "", "F($q, $dof_r)",  Fstat)
    Printf.@printf("%-52s%-18s= %6.4f\n", "", "Prob > F",       pF)
    Printf.@printf("%-52s%-18s= %6.4f\n", "", "R-squared",      r2)
    Printf.@printf("%-52s%-18s= %6.4f\n", "", "Adj R-squared",  ar2)
    Printf.@printf("%-52s%-18s= %6.4f\n", "", "Root MSE",       rmse)
    println()
    if cluster !== nothing
        Printf.@printf("%78s\n",
            "(Std. err. adjusted for $cluster_N clusters in $cluster)")
    end

    # ── Coefficient table
    lvl = round(Int, 100 * level)
    println("-"^78)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %22s\n", "", "Robust")
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s  %9s  %6s  %5s     [%d%% conf. interval]\n",
                   string(ys), "Coefficient", "std. err.",
                   "t", "P>|t|", lvl)
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    for v in string.(xsv)
        if v in dropped
            Printf.@printf("%12s | %10d  (omitted)\n", v, 0)
        else
            idx = findfirst(==(v), cn_m)
            Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %7.2f  %6.3f  %s  %s\n",
                           v, _g(β[idx], 10), _g(se[idx], 9),
                           t_st[idx], pvals[idx],
                           _g(ci_lo[idx], 11), _g(ci_hi[idx], 10))
        end
    end
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %7.2f  %6.3f  %s  %s\n",
                   "_cons", _g(cons_b, 10), _g(cons_se, 9),
                   cons_t, cons_p, _g(cons_lo, 11), _g(cons_hi, 10))
    println("-"^78)

    return (; β, V, se, coefnames = cn_m, dropped,
              r2, adj_r2 = ar2, rmse,
              F = Fstat, df1 = q, df2 = dof_r, pF,
              n_obs = n, n_groups, cluster_N,
              _cons = cons_b, _cons_se = cons_se)
end

"""
    ivregress_2sls(df, y, endog => instruments, exog=Symbol[];
                   robust=true, level=0.95, first=true)

Stata-style `ivregress 2sls y (endog = iv) x2list[, vce(robust) first]`.
Runs the model through `FixedEffectModels.reg`:
  * first stage: each endogenous variable regressed on `exog + instruments`
    (plain OLS with robust SE), printed with the familiar
    "Linear regression" header (F, R², Adj R², Root MSE, t-stats);
  * 2SLS:  `reg(df, @formula(y ~ exog + (endog ~ instruments)), vce)`,
    printed with Wald χ², R², Root MSE, z-stats, and Endogenous/Exogenous
    footer.

`endog => instruments` is a Pair where each side is a Symbol or vector of
Symbols. The endogenous variable is placed first in the 2SLS coefficient
table (matching Stata's convention); `_cons` is shown last.

Example:

    ivregress_2sls(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion => :ssiratio,
                   [:totchr, :age, :female, :blhisp, :linc];
                   robust=true, first=true)
"""
function ivregress_2sls(df, y, endog_iv::Pair, exog=Symbol[];
                        robust::Bool=true, level::Float64=0.95,
                        first::Bool=true)
    ys        = Symbol(y)
    endog     = endog_iv.first isa Union{Symbol,AbstractString} ?
                [Symbol(endog_iv.first)] :
                [Symbol(v) for v in endog_iv.first]
    iv        = endog_iv.second isa Union{Symbol,AbstractString} ?
                [Symbol(endog_iv.second)] :
                [Symbol(v) for v in endog_iv.second]
    exog_v    = isempty(exog) ? Symbol[] :
                (exog isa Union{Symbol,AbstractString} ?
                    [Symbol(exog)] : [Symbol(v) for v in exog])
    vcov      = robust ? FixedEffectModels.Vcov.robust() :
                         FixedEffectModels.Vcov.simple()
    _fmtn(x)  = replace(string(x), r"(\d)(?=(\d{3})+$)" => s"\1,")

    # Printer shared by the first-stage (OLS, t) and 2SLS (z) blocks.
    function _print(m, depname; mode::Symbol, endog_nms=nothing)
        β      = StatsBase.coef(m)
        V      = StatsBase.vcov(m)
        se     = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))
        nm_raw = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m))
        nm     = replace.(nm_raw, "(Intercept)" => "_cons")
        n      = Int(StatsBase.nobs(m))
        k      = length(β)
        dof_r  = Int(StatsBase.dof_residual(m))
        rss    = StatsBase.deviance(m)
        r2     = StatsBase.r2(m)
        rmse   = sqrt(rss / dof_r)

        cons_idx = findfirst(==("_cons"), nm)
        slope    = cons_idx === nothing ? collect(1:k) :
                    setdiff(1:k, [cons_idx])
        q        = length(slope)
        Wstat    = β[slope]' * LinearAlgebra.inv(V[slope, slope]) * β[slope]

        lvl = round(Int, 100 * level)

        if mode == :first
            Fstat = Wstat / q
            pF    = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(q, dof_r), Fstat)
            ar2   = 1 - (1 - r2) * (n - 1) / dof_r
            Printf.@printf("%-56s%-14s= %6s\n",  "", "Number of obs", _fmtn(n))
            Printf.@printf("%-56s%-14s= %6.2f\n","", "F($q, $dof_r)", Fstat)
            Printf.@printf("%-56s%-14s= %6.4f\n","", "Prob > F",      pF)
            Printf.@printf("%-56s%-14s= %6.4f\n","", "R-squared",     r2)
            Printf.@printf("%-56s%-14s= %6.4f\n","", "Adj R-squared", ar2)
            Printf.@printf("%-56s%-14s= %6.4f\n","", "Root MSE",      rmse)
            println()

            t_stat = β ./ se
            tcrit  = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(dof_r),
                                            1 - (1 - level) / 2)
            pvals  = 2 .* (1 .-
                     Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.TDist(dof_r), abs.(t_stat)))
            ci_lo  = β .- tcrit .* se
            ci_hi  = β .+ tcrit .* se
            order  = cons_idx === nothing ? slope : vcat(slope, cons_idx)

            coef_df = DataFrames.DataFrame(
                Variable    = nm[order],
                Coefficient = β[order],
                StdErr      = se[order],
                t           = t_stat[order],
                p           = pvals[order],
                CI_low      = ci_lo[order],
                CI_high     = ci_hi[order],
            )
            PrettyTables.pretty_table(coef_df;
                header = (["",         "",            "Robust",    "",  "",      "",                "" ],
                          [depname,    "Coefficient", "std. err.", "t", "P>|t|",
                           "[$(lvl)% CI low]", "[$(lvl)% CI high]"]),
                formatters = (
                    PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.7f", [2, 3, 6, 7]),
                    PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.2f", [4]),
                    PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.3f", [5]),
                ),
                alignment = [:l, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r],
            )
        else        # :second — 2SLS
            pW = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(q), Wstat)
            Printf.@printf("%-50s%-16s= %10s\n",
                           "Instrumental-variables 2SLS regression",
                           "Number of obs", _fmtn(n))
            Printf.@printf("%-50s%-16s= %10.2f\n", "", "Wald chi2($q)", Wstat)
            Printf.@printf("%-50s%-16s= %10.4f\n", "", "Prob > chi2",   pW)
            Printf.@printf("%-50s%-16s= %10.4f\n", "", "R-squared",     r2)
            Printf.@printf("%-50s%-16s= %10.4f\n", "", "Root MSE",      rmse)
            println()

            z_stat = β ./ se
            zcrit  = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(),
                                            1 - (1 - level) / 2)
            pvals  = 2 .* (1 .-
                     Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z_stat)))
            ci_lo  = β .- zcrit .* se
            ci_hi  = β .+ zcrit .* se

            # Put endogenous vars first, then the rest of the slopes, then _cons.
            endog_pos = Int[]
            if endog_nms !== nothing
                for ev in endog_nms
                    i = findfirst(==(string(ev)), nm)
                    i !== nothing && push!(endog_pos, i)
                end
            end
            rest = setdiff(slope, endog_pos)
            order = cons_idx === nothing ?
                    vcat(endog_pos, rest) :
                    vcat(endog_pos, rest, cons_idx)

            coef_df = DataFrames.DataFrame(
                Variable    = nm[order],
                Coefficient = β[order],
                StdErr      = se[order],
                z           = z_stat[order],
                p           = pvals[order],
                CI_low      = ci_lo[order],
                CI_high     = ci_hi[order],
            )
            PrettyTables.pretty_table(coef_df;
                header = (["",         "",            "Robust",    "",  "",      "",                "" ],
                          [depname,    "Coefficient", "std. err.", "z", "P>|z|",
                           "[$(lvl)% CI low]", "[$(lvl)% CI high]"]),
                formatters = (
                    PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.7f", [2, 3, 6, 7]),
                    PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.2f", [4]),
                    PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.3f", [5]),
                ),
                alignment = [:l, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r, :r],
            )
        end
    end

    # ── First-stage regressions
    first_stage_models = Any[]
    if first
        println("First-stage regressions")
        println("-"^23)
        println()
        rhs = vcat(exog_v, iv)
        for ev in endog
            f_fs = term(ev) ~ sum(term.(rhs))
            m_fs = FixedEffectModels.reg(df, f_fs, vcov)
            _print(m_fs, string(ev); mode=:first)
            println()
            push!(first_stage_models, m_fs)
        end
    end

    # ── 2SLS (build the IV formula dynamically via @formula + Meta.parse)
    exog_part  = isempty(exog_v) ? "1" : join(string.(exog_v), " + ")
    endog_part = join(string.(endog), " + ")
    iv_part    = join(string.(iv), " + ")
    fmla_str   = "@formula($(string(ys)) ~ $exog_part + ($endog_part ~ $iv_part))"
    f_iv       = Base.eval(@__MODULE__, Meta.parse(fmla_str))
    m_iv       = FixedEffectModels.reg(df, f_iv, vcov)
    _print(m_iv, string(ys); mode=:second, endog_nms=endog)
    Printf.@printf("Endogenous: %s\n", join(string.(endog), " "))
    Printf.@printf("Exogenous:  %s\n",
                   join(string.(vcat(exog_v, iv)), " "))

    return (; first_stage=first_stage_models, iv=m_iv)
end

"""
    iv_gmm(df, y, endog, instruments, exog=Symbol[];
           estimator=:twosls, wmatrix=:classical,
           cluster=nothing, iterate=false, max_iter=100, tol=1e-9)

Linear IV by 2SLS or GMM. Returns `(; β, V, se, coefnames, n)`. `coefnames`
is `["_cons", endog…, exog…]`.

`estimator`:
  * `:twosls` — β̂ with W = (Z'Z)⁻¹;
  * `:gmm`  — β̂ with W = Ω̂⁻¹.

`wmatrix` selects both the point-estimate weight (for `:gmm`) and the
reported vcov:
  * `:classical` (2SLS only): σ̂² · (X'Z W Z'X)⁻¹;
  * `:robust`   : Ω̂ = Σ zᵢzᵢ'ûᵢ²  (heteroskedasticity-robust);
  * `:cluster`  : Σ_g (Σ_i∈g zᵢûᵢ)(Σ_i∈g zᵢûᵢ)' with `cluster` Symbol.

`iterate=true` runs iterated GMM (β ↔ Ω̂ until `‖β−β_prev‖ < tol`).
"""
function iv_gmm(df, y, endog, instruments, exog=Symbol[];
