    # ── Panel means ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    means = DataFrames.combine(gdf,
        [y => (x -> Statistics.mean(Float64.(x))) => :ȳ;
         [v => (x -> Statistics.mean(Float64.(x))) => Symbol(v, :_bar)
          for v in xvars]...])
    d2 = DataFrames.leftjoin(d, means, on = idvar)
    ỹ  = Float64.(d2[!, y]) .- d2.ȳ
    X̃  = hcat([Float64.(d2[!, v]) .- d2[!, Symbol(v, :_bar)] for v in xvars]...)
    e_w  = ỹ .- X̃ * β_slope
    σ2_e = sum(abs2, e_w) / (n - N - k)
    σ_e  = sqrt(σ2_e)

    # ── Fixed effects from IV β̂ → σ_u ────────────────────────────
    α̂ = [means.ȳ[i] -
          sum(β_slope[j] * means[!, Symbol(xvars[j], :_bar)][i]
              for j in eachindex(xvars))
          for i in 1:N]
    T_counts = [DataFrames.nrow(g) for g in gdf]
    T̄    = Statistics.mean(T_counts)
    T_min, T_max = minimum(T_counts), maximum(T_counts)
    σ2_u = max(Statistics.var(α̂) - σ2_e / T̄, 0.0)
    σ_u  = sqrt(σ2_u)
    ρ    = σ2_u / (σ2_u + σ2_e)
    β0   = Statistics.mean(α̂)

    # ── R² ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    ss_tot_w = sum(abs2, ỹ); ss_res_w = sum(abs2, e_w)
    r2_within = ss_tot_w > 0 ? 1 - ss_res_w/ss_tot_w : NaN
    Xβ_panel  = [sum(β_slope[j] * means[!, Symbol(xvars[j], :_bar)][i]
                     for j in eachindex(xvars)) for i in 1:N]
    r2_between = Statistics.cor(means.ȳ, Xβ_panel)^2
    y_all      = Float64.(d[!, y])
    Xβ_all     = hcat([Float64.(d[!, v]) for v in xvars]...) * β_slope
    r2_overall = Statistics.cor(y_all, Xβ_all)^2

    # ── corr(u_i, Xb) ────────────────────────────────────────────
    corr_ux = Statistics.cor(α̂, Xβ_panel)

    # ── SEs — bootstrap vs analytic ──────────────────────────────
    use_boot = boot_β !== nothing
    if use_boot
        B        = size(boot_β, 1)
        V_slope  = Statistics.cov(boot_β)
        se_slope = Statistics.std(boot_β, dims=1)[:]
        # β0 per bootstrap draw, using grand means of original data
        ȳ_grand  = Statistics.mean(y_all)
        X̄_grand  = [Statistics.mean(Float64.(d[!, v])) for v in xvars]
        β0_boot  = [ȳ_grand - sum(boot_β[b, j] * X̄_grand[j]
                                   for j in 1:length(xvars))
                    for b in 1:B]
        se_cons  = Statistics.std(β0_boot)
    else
        V_slope  = StatsBase.vcov(m)[ix, ix]
        se_slope = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V_slope), 0.0))
        se_cons  = NaN
    end
    z_slope = β_slope ./ se_slope
    p_slope = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z_slope)))
    zcrit   = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    ci_lo_s = β_slope .- zcrit .* se_slope
    ci_hi_s = β_slope .+ zcrit .* se_slope

    # Wald chi² on slope coefs (H0: all slopes = 0)
    Wald   = β_slope' * LinearAlgebra.inv(V_slope) * β_slope
    Wald_p = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(length(β_slope)), Wald)

    # F-test that all u_i = 0
    df1, df2 = N - 1, n - N - k
    F_val = Statistics.var(α̂) / σ2_e
    p_F   = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(df1, df2), F_val)

    # ── Stata-style formatters ──────────────────────────────────
    function _g(x, w; sig=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, ".")
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        if 0 < abs(x) < 1
            s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
        return Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, s)
    end
    function _c(v::Integer)
        s = string(abs(v)); neg = v < 0
        parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1
            push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3
        end
        return (neg ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    # ── Header block ────────────────────────────────────────────
    println()
    Printf.@printf("%-48s%-18s= %10s\n",
                   "Fixed-effects (within) IV regression",
                   "Number of obs", _c(n))
    Printf.@printf("%-48s%-18s= %10s\n",
                   "Group variable: $(idvar)", "Number of groups", _c(N))
    println()
    println("R-squared:                                      Obs per group:")
    within_str = (!isfinite(r2_within) || r2_within < 0) ?
                 "     ." : Printf.@sprintf("%6.4f", r2_within)
    Printf.@printf("     Within  = %7s                                  %-8s= %10d\n",
                   within_str, "min", T_min)
    Printf.@printf("     Between = %6.4f                                  %-8s= %10.1f\n",
                   r2_between, "avg", T̄)
    Printf.@printf("     Overall = %6.4f                                  %-8s= %10d\n",
                   r2_overall, "max", T_max)
    println()
    println()
    wlabel = Printf.@sprintf("Wald chi2(%d)", length(β_slope))
    Printf.@printf("%48s%-18s= %10.2f\n", " "^48, wlabel, Wald)
    left = Printf.@sprintf("corr(u_i, Xb) = %s", _g(corr_ux, 7; sig=4))
    Printf.@printf("%-48s%-18s= %10.4f\n", left, "Prob > chi2", Wald_p)
    println()

    # Coefficient table
    if use_boot
        Printf.@printf("%s(Replications based on %d clusters in %s)\n",
                       " "^(78 - 40 - length(string(idvar)) - length(string(N))),
                       N, string(idvar))
    end
    println("-"^78)
    if use_boot
        Printf.@printf("%12s |   Observed   Bootstrap                         Normal-based\n", "")
        Printf.@printf("%12s | coefficient   std. err.      z    P>|z|     [%d%% conf. interval]\n",
                       string(y), round(Int, 100*level))
    else
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s  %9s  %6s  %5s     [%d%% conf. interval]\n",
                       string(y), "Coefficient", "Std. err.", "z", "P>|z|",
                       round(Int, 100*level))
    end
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    for j in eachindex(xvars)
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %6.2f  %5.3f  %s  %s\n",
                       string(xvars[j]),
                       _g(β_slope[j], 10), _g(se_slope[j], 9),
                       z_slope[j], p_slope[j],
                       _g(ci_lo_s[j], 11), _g(ci_hi_s[j], 11))
    end
    if use_boot
        z_c   = β0 / se_cons
        p_c   = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z_c)))
        ci_lc = β0 - zcrit * se_cons
        ci_hc = β0 + zcrit * se_cons
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %6.2f  %5.3f  %s  %s\n",
                       "_cons", _g(β0, 10), _g(se_cons, 9),
                       z_c, p_c, _g(ci_lc, 11), _g(ci_hc, 11))
    else
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s\n", "_cons", _g(β0, 10))
    end
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s\n", "sigma_u", _g(σ_u, 10))
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s\n", "sigma_e", _g(σ_e, 10))
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s   (fraction of variance due to u_i)\n",
                   "rho", _g(ρ, 10))
    println("-"^78)
    Printf.@printf(" F test that all u_i=0: F(%d,%d) = %8.2f            Prob > F    = %.4f\n",
                   df1, df2, F_val, p_F)
    println("-"^78)
    if endog !== nothing
        exog_names = setdiff(string.(xvars), [string(endog)])
        inst_names = instruments === nothing ? String[] : string.(instruments)
        println("Endogenous: ", string(endog))
        println("Exogenous:  ", join(vcat(exog_names, inst_names), " "))
    end

    return m
end

"""
    hausman_taylor(df, y, idvar; time_exog, time_endog, indiv_exog, indiv_endog)

Stata-style `xthtaylor` — Hausman-Taylor (1981) estimator for panel data with
time-invariant regressors.

Instruments (after θ-transform):
  1. Within-deviations of all time-varying regressors (exog + endog)
  2. Levels of time-invariant exogenous regressors
  3. Group means of time-varying exogenous regressors

Arguments:
  * `y`            : dependent variable (Symbol)
  * `idvar`        : panel identifier (Symbol)
  * `time_exog`    : Vector{Symbol} of time-varying exogenous regressors
  * `time_endog`   : Vector{Symbol} of time-varying endogenous regressors
  * `indiv_exog`   : Vector{Symbol} of time-invariant exogenous regressors
  * `indiv_endog`  : Vector{Symbol} of time-invariant endogenous regressors
"""
function hausman_taylor(df, y::Symbol, idvar::Symbol;
                        time_exog::Vector{Symbol}   = Symbol[],
                        time_endog::Vector{Symbol}  = Symbol[],
                        indiv_exog::Vector{Symbol}  = Symbol[],
                        indiv_endog::Vector{Symbol} = Symbol[],
                        vce::Symbol = :default,      # :default, :bootstrap (cluster)
                        reps::Int = 400,
                        seed::Int = 10101,
                        _ht_coefs_only::Bool = false)   # internal flag
    all_X = vcat(time_exog, time_endog, indiv_exog, indiv_endog)
    needed = unique(vcat([y, idvar], all_X))
    d = DataFrames.dropmissing(df, needed)
    n = DataFrames.nrow(d)
    gdf = DataFrames.groupby(d, idvar)
    N   = length(gdf)
    time_all = vcat(time_exog, time_endog)
    k_tv = length(time_all)                       # number of time-varying regressors

    # ── Panel means for all variables (y + all X) ─────────────────
    means_df = DataFrames.combine(gdf,
        (v => (x -> Statistics.mean(Float64.(_rawval.(collect(skipmissing(x)))))) =>
            Symbol(v, :_bar) for v in vcat([y], all_X))...)
    d2 = DataFrames.leftjoin(d, means_df, on = idvar)

    # ── Step 1: FE within estimator → σ̂²_ε ───────────────────────
    # Manual within-demean to avoid predict/save issues
    ỹ = Float64.(d2[!, y]) .- d2[!, Symbol(y, :_bar)]
    X̃_tv = hcat([Float64.(d2[!, v]) .- d2[!, Symbol(v, :_bar)] for v in time_all]...)
    β_fe = X̃_tv \ ỹ
    e_fe = ỹ .- X̃_tv * β_fe
    T_bar = n / N
    σ_ε2 = sum(abs2, e_fe) / (n - N)       # plm/Stata: no K correction

    # ── Step 2: σ̂²_u via IV on fixed effects (plm "ht" method) ───
    # Individual fixed effects: α̂_i = ȳ_i - X̄_tv_i'β̂_FE
    fe_i = Vector{Float64}(undef, N)
    for (j, g) in enumerate(gdf)
        ȳ = Statistics.mean(Float64.(_rawval.(g[!, y])))
        x̄ = [Statistics.mean(Float64.(_rawval.(g[!, v]))) for v in time_all]
        fe_i[j] = ȳ - LinearAlgebra.dot(x̄, β_fe)
    end

    # Time-invariant variables at group level (N rows)
    indiv_all = vcat(indiv_exog, indiv_endog)
    ti_mat = Matrix{Float64}(undef, N, length(indiv_all))
    for (j, g) in enumerate(gdf)
        for (c, v) in enumerate(indiv_all)
            ti_mat[j, c] = Float64(_rawval(first(g[!, v])))
        end
    end

    # TI-exogenous only (for use as their own instruments)
    ti_exog_mat = Matrix{Float64}(undef, N, length(indiv_exog))
    for (j, g) in enumerate(gdf)
        for (c, v) in enumerate(indiv_exog)
            ti_exog_mat[j, c] = Float64(_rawval(first(g[!, v])))
        end
    end

    # Group means of TV-exog (instruments for TI-endogenous)
    W_fe = Matrix{Float64}(undef, N, length(time_exog))
    for (j, g) in enumerate(gdf)
        for (c, v) in enumerate(time_exog)
            W_fe[j, c] = Statistics.mean(Float64.(_rawval.(g[!, v])))
        end
    end

    # IV regression at group level:
    # Regressors = [1, ti_exog, ti_endog]
    # Instruments = [1, ti_exog (as self-instruments), group_means_TVexog]
    # ti_endog is instrumented by group means of TV_exog (HT requires #TV_exog ≥ #TI_endog)
    ti_mat_c = hcat(ones(N), ti_mat)                          # 1 + k_TI cols
    W_fe_c   = hcat(ones(N), ti_exog_mat, W_fe)               # 1 + k_TIexog + k_TVexog cols
    P_W = W_fe_c * ((W_fe_c'W_fe_c) \ W_fe_c')
    β_ti_full = (ti_mat_c' * P_W * ti_mat_c) \ (ti_mat_c' * P_W * fe_i)
    e_ti = fe_i .- ti_mat_c * β_ti_full
    s21 = sum(abs2, e_ti) / N          # MSE of IV residuals ≈ σ_u² + σ_ε²/T
    σ_u2 = max(s21 - σ_ε2 / T_bar, 0.0)

    # ── Step 3: θ-transform (quasi-demean) ────────────────────────
    θ = 1 - sqrt(σ_ε2 / (T_bar * σ_u2 + σ_ε2))
    for v in vcat([y], all_X)
        d2[!, Symbol(v, :_star)] = Float64.(d2[!, v]) .- θ .* d2[!, Symbol(v, :_bar)]
    end

    # ── Step 4: Build regressors and instruments on θ-transformed data ─
    # Within-deviations of all time-varying variables
    for v in time_all
        d2[!, Symbol(v, :_w)] = Float64.(d2[!, v]) .- d2[!, Symbol(v, :_bar)]
    end
    # Group means of time-varying exogenous
    for v in time_exog
        d2[!, Symbol(v, :_bbar)] = d2[!, Symbol(v, :_bar)]   # already computed
    end

    # Regressors X* = [1*, X1*_star, ..., Xk*_star]
    X_cols = Symbol.(string.(all_X) .* "_star")
    X = hcat(fill(1 - θ, n), Matrix{Float64}(d2[:, X_cols]))  # θ-transformed constant
    y_star = Vector{Float64}(d2[!, Symbol(y, :_star)])

    # Instruments Z = [1*, within-devs of TV, levels of TI-exog, group-means of TV-exog]
    Z_parts = Vector{Matrix{Float64}}()
    push!(Z_parts, fill(1 - θ, n, 1))                         # constant
    # Within-deviations of ALL time-varying regressors
    push!(Z_parts, hcat([Vector{Float64}(d2[!, Symbol(v, :_w)]) for v in time_all]...))
    # Levels of time-invariant exogenous (θ-transformed)
    if !isempty(indiv_exog)
        push!(Z_parts, hcat([Float64.(d2[!, v]) .- θ .* d2[!, Symbol(v, :_bar)]
                             for v in indiv_exog]...))
    end
    # Group means of time-varying exogenous
    if !isempty(time_exog)
        push!(Z_parts, hcat([Vector{Float64}(d2[!, Symbol(v, :_bar)]) for v in time_exog]...))
    end
    Z = hcat(Z_parts...)

    # ── 2SLS: β̂ = (X'P_Z X)⁻¹ X'P_Z y* ─────────────────────────
    P_Z = Z * ((Z'Z) \ Z')
    XPZ = X' * P_Z
    β = (XPZ * X) \ (XPZ * y_star)
    # Analytic variance: use σ²_ε (the θ-transform normalises error variance to σ²_ε)
    V = σ_ε2 .* inv(XPZ * X)

    # Early return for internal bootstrap calls (just the coefficients)
    _ht_coefs_only && return β

    # ── Bootstrap or Jackknife SEs (Stata xthtaylor vce() options) ──
    panel_ids = unique(d[!, idvar])
    id_to_rows = Dict{Any, Vector{Int}}()
    for (i, id) in enumerate(d[!, idvar])
        push!(get!(id_to_rows, id, Int[]), i)
    end

    if vce == :bootstrap
        rng = Random.MersenneTwister(seed)
        boot_β = Matrix{Float64}(undef, 0, length(β))
        n_fail = 0
        for b in 1:reps
            boot_ids = StatsBase.sample(rng, panel_ids, length(panel_ids); replace=true)
            row_idx = Int[]; new_id = Int[]
            for (k, idv) in enumerate(boot_ids)
                rows = id_to_rows[idv]
                append!(row_idx, rows)
                append!(new_id, fill(k, length(rows)))
            end
            sub = d[row_idx, :]
            sub[!, idvar] = new_id
            try
                β_b = hausman_taylor(sub, y, idvar;
                    time_exog=time_exog, time_endog=time_endog,
                    indiv_exog=indiv_exog, indiv_endog=indiv_endog,
                    _ht_coefs_only=true)
                boot_β = vcat(boot_β, β_b')
            catch
                n_fail += 1
            end
        end
        n_fail > 0 && @warn "Bootstrap: $n_fail/$reps resamples failed."
        V  = Statistics.cov(boot_β)
        se = vec(Statistics.std(boot_β, dims=1))
    elseif vce == :jackknife
        # Panel (delete-one-cluster) jackknife: leave each panel out, re-estimate,
        # then V_jack = ((N-1)/N) · Σ (β_{-i} - β̄)(β_{-i} - β̄)'
        nβ = length(β)
        jack_β = Matrix{Float64}(undef, 0, nβ)
        n_fail = 0
        for idv in panel_ids
            sub = d[d[!, idvar] .!= idv, :]
            try
                β_j = hausman_taylor(sub, y, idvar;
                    time_exog=time_exog, time_endog=time_endog,
                    indiv_exog=indiv_exog, indiv_endog=indiv_endog,
                    _ht_coefs_only=true)
                jack_β = vcat(jack_β, β_j')
            catch
                n_fail += 1
            end
        end
        n_fail > 0 && @warn "Jackknife: $n_fail/$N panels produced errors."
        N_used = size(jack_β, 1)
        β_bar  = vec(Statistics.mean(jack_β, dims=1))
        dev    = jack_β .- β_bar'
        V      = ((N_used - 1) / N_used) .* (dev' * dev)
        se     = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))
    else
        se = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))
    end

    # t or z statistic depending on vce
    use_t = (vce == :jackknife)
    if use_t
        df_t = N - 1               # jackknife df = N panels - 1
        z  = β ./ se
        p  = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.TDist(df_t), abs.(z)))
    else
        z  = β ./ se
        p  = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z)))
    end

    # Note: X columns are in order [1, time_exog..., time_endog..., indiv_exog..., indiv_endog...]
    colnames = vcat(["_cons"], string.(all_X))

    σ_u = sqrt(σ_u2);  σ_e = sqrt(σ_ε2)
    ρ   = σ_u2 / (σ_u2 + σ_ε2)

    # ── Wald test on slope coefs (excluding _cons) ──────────────
    β_slope = β[2:end]
    V_slope = V[2:end, 2:end]
    Wald_chi2 = β_slope' * LinearAlgebra.inv(V_slope) * β_slope
    k_slope = length(β_slope)
    if use_t
        # Jackknife: report F(k, N-1) = (Wald / k)
        Wald = Wald_chi2 / k_slope
        Wald_p = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(k_slope, N - 1), Wald)
    else
        Wald = Wald_chi2
        Wald_p = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(k_slope), Wald)
    end

    # ── Panel info ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    T_counts = [DataFrames.nrow(g) for g in gdf]
    T_min = minimum(T_counts); T_max_v = maximum(T_counts)

    # ── CI (t-crit for jackknife, z-crit otherwise) ──────────────
    crit = use_t ? Distributions.quantile(Distributions.TDist(N - 1), 0.975) : 1.96
    ci_lo = β .- crit .* se
    ci_hi = β .+ crit .* se

    # ── Stata %9.0g-style number formatter (strip leading 0 for |x|<1) ──
    function g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        if 0 < abs(x) < 1
            s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
        return lpad(s, w)
    end
    function commafmt(n)
        s = string(abs(n)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1
            push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3
        end
        return (n < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    # ── Index lookup (position in β vector) ──────────────────────
    # β order: [_cons, time_exog..., time_endog..., indiv_exog..., indiv_endog...]
    idx_of = Dict{Symbol,Int}()
    pos = 2
    for v in time_exog;   idx_of[v] = pos; pos += 1; end
    for v in time_endog;  idx_of[v] = pos; pos += 1; end
    for v in indiv_exog;  idx_of[v] = pos; pos += 1; end
    for v in indiv_endog; idx_of[v] = pos; pos += 1; end

    # ── Print Stata-format output ────────────────────────────────
    println()
    Printf.@printf("%-48s%-18s= %10s\n",
                   "Hausman-Taylor estimation", "Number of obs", commafmt(n))
    Printf.@printf("Group variable: %-32s%-18s= %10d\n",
                   string(idvar), "Number of groups", N)
    println()
    println("                                                Obs per group:")
    Printf.@printf("%48s%-5s = %10d\n", "", "min", T_min)
    Printf.@printf("%48s%-5s = %10d\n", "", "avg", round(Int, T_bar))
    Printf.@printf("%48s%-5s = %10d\n", "", "max", T_max_v)
    println()
    if use_t
        Printf.@printf("Random effects u_i ~ i.i.d.%21sF(%3d, %6d)   = %10.2f\n",
                       "", k_slope, N - 1, Wald)
        Printf.@printf("%48sProb > F          = %10.4f\n", "", Wald_p)
    else
        Printf.@printf("Random effects u_i ~ i.i.d.%21sWald chi2(%d)     = %10.2f\n",
                       "", k_slope, Wald)
        Printf.@printf("%48sProb > chi2       = %10.4f\n", "", Wald_p)
    end
    println()
    if vce == :bootstrap
        Printf.@printf("%79s\n", "(Replications based on $N clusters in $idvar)")
    elseif vce == :jackknife
        Printf.@printf("%79s\n", "(Replications based on $N clusters in $idvar)")
    end
    println("-"^78)
    if vce == :bootstrap
        println("             |   Observed   Bootstrap                         Normal-based")
        Printf.@printf("%12s | coefficient  std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95%% conf. interval]\n",
                       string(y))
    elseif vce == :jackknife
        println("             |              Jackknife")
        Printf.@printf("%12s | Coefficient  std. err.      t    P>|t|     [95%% conf. interval]\n",
                       string(y))
    else
        Printf.@printf("%12s | Coefficient  Std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95%% conf. interval]\n",
                       string(y))
    end
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)

    # Helper: print one coefficient row
    function prow(v::Symbol)
        i = idx_of[v]
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       string(v),
                       g9(β[i]; w=10), g9(se[i]; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z[i]),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p[i]),
                       g9(ci_lo[i]; w=10), g9(ci_hi[i]; w=10))
    end

    # Sections (only print if non-empty)
    if !isempty(time_exog)
        println("TVexogenous  |")
        for v in time_exog;  prow(v);  end
    end
    if !isempty(time_endog)
        println("TVendogenous |")
        for v in time_endog; prow(v);  end
    end
    if !isempty(indiv_exog)
        println("TIexogenous  |")
        for v in indiv_exog; prow(v);  end
    end
    if !isempty(indiv_endog)
        println("TIendogenous |")
        for v in indiv_endog; prow(v); end
    end
    println("             |")
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                   "_cons",
                   g9(β[1]; w=10), g9(se[1]; w=9),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z[1]),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p[1]),
                   g9(ci_lo[1]; w=10), g9(ci_hi[1]; w=10))
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s\n", "sigma_u", g9(σ_u; w=10))
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s\n", "sigma_e", g9(σ_e; w=10))
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s   (fraction of variance due to u_i)\n",
                   "rho", g9(ρ; w=10))
    println("-"^78)
    println("Note: TV refers to time varying; TI refers to time invariant.")

    return (; β, V, se, z, p, ci_lo, ci_hi,
              coefnames = colnames, σ_ε2, σ_u2, σ_u, σ_e, ρ, θ, n,
              Wald, Wald_p, nobs=n, N_panels=N,
              T_min, T_avg=round(Int, T_bar), T_max=T_max_v)
end

"""
    arellano_bond(df, y, idvar, timevar; lags=1, xvars=Symbol[], twostep=false)

Arellano-Bond (1991) difference GMM for a dynamic linear panel model:

    y_{it} = ρ₁ y_{i,t-1} + ... + ρ_p y_{i,t-p} + X_{it}'β + αᵢ + ε_{it}

After first-differencing, instruments for Δy_{i,t-k} are all available lags
of y at t-k-1 or earlier (assuming E[ε_{it} y_{i,t-s}] = 0 for s ≥ k+1).

Arguments:
  * `lags`    : p, number of lags of y (default 1)
  * `xvars`   : additional strictly exogenous regressors (Vector{Symbol})
  * `twostep` : use the two-step efficient weighting (default one-step)
"""
function arellano_bond(df, y::Symbol, idvar::Symbol, timevar::Symbol;
                       lags::Int=1, xvars::Vector{Symbol}=Symbol[],
                       twostep::Bool=false, maxldep::Int=0,
                       pre::Vector{Tuple{Symbol,Int,Int}}=Tuple{Symbol,Int,Int}[],
                       endog::Vector{Tuple{Symbol,Int,Int}}=Tuple{Symbol,Int,Int}[])
    # ── Prepare data ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    all_syms = unique(vcat([y, idvar, timevar], xvars,
                           [v for (v,_,_) in pre], [v for (v,_,_) in endog]))
    d = DataFrames.sort(DataFrames.dropmissing(df, all_syms), [idvar, timevar])
    grouped = DataFrames.groupby(d, idvar)
    N_groups = length(grouped)

    # Number of regressor columns (no constant)
    n_pre_regs   = sum(a + 1 for (_,a,_) in pre;   init=0)
    n_endog_regs = sum(a + 1 for (_,a,_) in endog;  init=0)
    k_reg = lags + n_pre_regs + n_endog_regs + length(xvars)

    # ── GMM instrument groups: (variable, offset, depth) ──────────
    # offset = minimum lag for a valid level instrument
    # depth  = max number of lagged levels per period
    gmm_groups = Tuple{Symbol, Int, Int}[]
    push!(gmm_groups, (y, lags, maxldep > 0 ? maxldep : 999))
    for (v, a, b) in pre;    push!(gmm_groups, (v, a + 1, b));  end
    for (v, a, b) in endog;  push!(gmm_groups, (v, a + 2, b));  end

    # ── Instrument column layout (from longest panel) ─────────────
    # First-differenced AR(lags) model uses equations starting at t = lags+2
    # (need y_{t-lags-1} available to form Δy_{t-lags}).
    t_start = lags + 2
    T_max = maximum(DataFrames.nrow(g) for g in grouped)
    n_periods = max(0, T_max - t_start + 1)
    # per-period (col_start, n_cols) for each GMM group
    period_info = Vector{Vector{Tuple{Int,Int}}}(undef, n_periods)
    col = 1
    for (pidx, t) in enumerate(t_start:T_max)
        info = Tuple{Int,Int}[]
        for (_, s, depth) in gmm_groups
            n_inst = max(0, min(depth, t - s))
            push!(info, (col, n_inst))
            col += n_inst
        end
        period_info[pidx] = info
    end
    gmm_ncol = col - 1
    n_z = gmm_ncol + length(xvars)          # GMM + standard Δxvars

    # ── Build per-panel arrays (no constant in X or Z) ────────────
    panel_dy = Vector{Vector{Float64}}()
    panel_dx = Vector{Matrix{Float64}}()
    panel_zi = Vector{Matrix{Float64}}()
    T_vals   = Int[]

    for g in grouped
        T_i = DataFrames.nrow(g)
        n_use = max(0, T_i - t_start + 1)   # obs = {t_start, ..., T_i}
        n_use < 1 && continue
        y_v = collect(Float64, map(_rawval, g[!, y]))
        pre_vecs   = [collect(Float64, map(_rawval, g[!, v])) for (v,_,_) in pre]
        endog_vecs = [collect(Float64, map(_rawval, g[!, v])) for (v,_,_) in endog]
        xvar_vecs  = [collect(Float64, map(_rawval, g[!, v])) for v in xvars]
        all_gmm_vecs = vcat([y_v], pre_vecs, endog_vecs)

        dy_i = zeros(n_use)
        dx_i = zeros(n_use, k_reg)
        zi_i = zeros(n_use, n_z)

        for (row, t) in enumerate(t_start:T_i)
            dy_i[row] = y_v[t] - y_v[t-1]

            # ─ Regressor columns (all first-differenced) ─────────
            # Bounds check: Δv_{t-k} = v_{t-k} - v_{t-k-1} requires t-k-1 ≥ 1
            c = 0
            for k in 1:lags
                c += 1
                dx_i[row, c] = (t-k-1 >= 1) ? y_v[t-k] - y_v[t-k-1] : 0.0
            end
            for (pi, (_, a, _)) in enumerate(pre)
                for k in 0:a
                    c += 1
                    dx_i[row, c] = (t-k-1 >= 1) ? pre_vecs[pi][t-k] - pre_vecs[pi][t-k-1] : 0.0
                end
            end
            for (ei, (_, a, _)) in enumerate(endog)
                for k in 0:a
                    c += 1
                    dx_i[row, c] = (t-k-1 >= 1) ? endog_vecs[ei][t-k] - endog_vecs[ei][t-k-1] : 0.0
                end
            end
            for xi in eachindex(xvars)
                c += 1
                dx_i[row, c] = (t-1 >= 1) ? xvar_vecs[xi][t] - xvar_vecs[xi][t-1] : 0.0
            end

            # ─ GMM instruments (block-diagonal) ──────────────────
            pidx = t - t_start + 1
            for (gi, (_, s, _)) in enumerate(gmm_groups)
                cs, n_inst = period_info[pidx][gi]
                for j in 1:n_inst
                    idx_src = t - s - n_inst + j
                    zi_i[row, cs + j - 1] = (idx_src >= 1) ? all_gmm_vecs[gi][idx_src] : 0.0
                end
            end

            # ─ Standard instruments: Δxvars ──────────────────────
            for xi in eachindex(xvars)
                zi_i[row, gmm_ncol + xi] = (t-1 >= 1) ? xvar_vecs[xi][t] - xvar_vecs[xi][t-1] : 0.0
            end
        end
        push!(panel_dy, dy_i);  push!(panel_dx, dx_i)
        push!(panel_zi, zi_i);  push!(T_vals, n_use)
    end
    isempty(panel_dy) && error("No usable observations for Arellano-Bond.")

    yvec = vcat(panel_dy...)
    Xmat = vcat(panel_dx...)
    Zmat_raw = vcat(panel_zi...)

    # Drop all-zero instrument columns (Stata omits unavailable instruments)
    nz_cols = findall(c -> any(!iszero, Zmat_raw[:, c]), 1:size(Zmat_raw, 2))
    Zmat = Zmat_raw[:, nz_cols]
    # Remap panel_zi to the same reduced column set
    panel_zi = [zi[:, nz_cols] for zi in panel_zi]
    n_z = size(Zmat, 2)

    N_obs = length(yvec)
    N_ind = length(panel_dy)

    # ── One-step weight: W₁ = pinv(Σᵢ Zᵢ'HᵢZᵢ) ─────────────────
    ZHZ = zeros(n_z, n_z)
    for i in 1:N_ind
        Ti = T_vals[i]
        Hi = zeros(Ti, Ti)
        for r in 1:Ti
            Hi[r, r] = 2.0
            r < Ti && (Hi[r, r+1] = -1.0)
            r > 1  && (Hi[r, r-1] = -1.0)
        end
        ZHZ .+= panel_zi[i]' * Hi * panel_zi[i]
    end
    W1 = LinearAlgebra.pinv(ZHZ)
    XZ = Xmat' * Zmat
    β1 = (XZ * W1 * XZ') \ (XZ * W1 * Zmat' * yvec)

    β = β1
    if twostep
        # Step 2: update weight matrix using one-step residuals
        u1 = yvec .- Xmat * β1
        Ω1 = zeros(n_z, n_z);  offset = 0
        for i in 1:N_ind
            Ti = T_vals[i];  ui = u1[offset+1:offset+Ti]
            Ω1 .+= panel_zi[i]' * (ui * ui') * panel_zi[i]
            offset += Ti
        end
        W2 = LinearAlgebra.pinv(Ω1)
        β = (XZ * W2 * XZ') \ (XZ * W2 * Zmat' * yvec)

        # Standard two-step cluster-robust sandwich
        u2 = yvec .- Xmat * β
        Ω2 = zeros(n_z, n_z);  off2 = 0
        for i in 1:N_ind
            Ti = T_vals[i];  ui = u2[off2+1:off2+Ti]
            Ω2 .+= panel_zi[i]' * (ui * ui') * panel_zi[i]
            off2 += Ti
        end
        A2 = XZ * W2 * XZ'
        A2inv = LinearAlgebra.inv(A2)
        V2_robust = A2inv * (XZ * W2 * Ω2 * W2 * XZ') * A2inv

        # ── Windmeijer (2005) finite-sample correction ──
        # V_W = V₂ + D·V₂ + V₂·D' + D·V₁·D'
        # where D[:,k] = A₂⁻¹ · (X'Z) · W₂ · (∂Ω₁/∂β₁_k) · W₂ · Z'û₂
        #       ∂Ω₁/∂β₁_k = -2 Σᵢ (xᵢₖ · u₁ᵢ) · zᵢzᵢ'
        A1 = XZ * W1 * XZ'
        A1inv = LinearAlgebra.inv(A1)
        V1_robust = A1inv * (XZ * W1 * Ω1 * W1 * XZ') * A1inv

        Zpu2 = Zmat' * u2
        a_vec = W2 * Zpu2
        c_vec = Zmat * a_vec                       # length N_obs
        M     = A2inv * (XZ * W2) * Zmat'           # k_reg × N_obs
        weight = u1 .* c_vec
        D     = -2.0 .* M * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(weight) * Xmat

        Vβ = V2_robust + D * V2_robust + V2_robust * D' + D * V1_robust * D'
    else
        u = yvec .- Xmat * β
        Ω = zeros(n_z, n_z);  offset = 0
        for i in 1:N_ind
            Ti = T_vals[i];  ui = u[offset+1:offset+Ti]
            Ω .+= panel_zi[i]' * (ui * ui') * panel_zi[i]
            offset += Ti
        end
        A = XZ * W1 * XZ'
        Ainv = LinearAlgebra.inv(A)
        Vβ = Ainv * (XZ * W1 * Ω * W1 * XZ') * Ainv
    end

    # ── Recover constant from level equation ──────────────────────
    y_sum = 0.0;  yl_sum = zeros(k_reg);  n_lev = 0
    for g in grouped
        T_i = DataFrames.nrow(g);  T_i < lags + 1 && continue
        y_v = collect(Float64, map(_rawval, g[!, y]))
        pre_vecs   = [collect(Float64, map(_rawval, g[!, v])) for (v,_,_) in pre]
        endog_vecs = [collect(Float64, map(_rawval, g[!, v])) for (v,_,_) in endog]
        xvar_vecs  = [collect(Float64, map(_rawval, g[!, v])) for v in xvars]
        for t in (lags+1):T_i
            y_sum += y_v[t]
            c = 0
            for k in 1:lags;  c += 1;  yl_sum[c] += y_v[t-k];  end
            for (pi, (_, a, _)) in enumerate(pre)
                for k in 0:a;  c += 1;  yl_sum[c] += pre_vecs[pi][t-k];  end
            end
            for (ei, (_, a, _)) in enumerate(endog)
                for k in 0:a;  c += 1;  yl_sum[c] += endog_vecs[ei][t-k];  end
            end
            for xi in eachindex(xvars);  c += 1;  yl_sum[c] += xvar_vecs[xi][t];  end
            n_lev += 1
        end
    end
    ȳ = y_sum / n_lev
    x̄_lev = yl_sum ./ n_lev
    β_cons = ȳ - LinearAlgebra.dot(β, x̄_lev)
    se_cons = sqrt(x̄_lev' * Vβ * x̄_lev)

    # ── Augmented coefficient vector with constant appended ───────
    β_full = vcat(β, β_cons)
    V_full = zeros(k_reg + 1, k_reg + 1)
    V_full[1:k_reg, 1:k_reg] .= Vβ
    g_cons = -x̄_lev
    V_full[end, end] = se_cons^2
    cov_β_cons = vec(Vβ * g_cons)
    V_full[1:k_reg, end] .= cov_β_cons
    V_full[end, 1:k_reg] .= cov_β_cons

    se = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V_full), 0.0))
    z  = β_full ./ se
    pv = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z)))

    # Wald chi2 on ALL slope coefficients (excl _cons)
    wald = β' * LinearAlgebra.inv(Vβ) * β
    wald_p = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(k_reg), wald)

    n_instruments = n_z + 1   # +1 for _cons in level equation

    # ── Coefficient names ─────────────────────────────────────────
    colnames = String[]
    for k in 1:lags;  push!(colnames, "L$(k).$(y)");  end
    for (v, a, _) in pre
        for k in 0:a;  push!(colnames, k == 0 ? string(v) : "L$(k).$(v)");  end
    end
    for (v, a, _) in endog
        for k in 0:a;  push!(colnames, k == 0 ? string(v) : "L$(k).$(v)");  end
    end
    for v in xvars;  push!(colnames, string(v));  end
    push!(colnames, "_cons")

    step_lbl = twostep ? "Two-step" : "One-step"
    T_avg = N_obs / N_ind
    T_min = minimum(T_vals);  T_max_v = maximum(T_vals)

    ci_lo = β_full .- 1.96 .* se
    ci_hi = β_full .+ 1.96 .* se

    # ── Stata %9.0g-style formatter (strip leading 0 for |x|<1) ──
    function g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        if 0 < abs(x) < 1
            s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
        return lpad(s, w)
    end
    function commafmt(m)
        s = string(abs(m)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1
            push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3
        end
        return (m < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    # ── Print Stata-style output ──────────────────────────────────
    println()
    Printf.@printf("%-48s%-18s= %10s\n",
                   "Arellano-Bond dynamic panel-data estimation",
                   "Number of obs", commafmt(N_obs))
    Printf.@printf("Group variable: %-32s%-18s= %10d\n",
                   string(idvar), "Number of groups", N_ind)
    Printf.@printf("Time variable: %s\n", string(timevar))
    println("                                                Obs per group:")
    Printf.@printf("%48s%-5s = %10d\n", "", "min", T_min)
    Printf.@printf("%48s%-5s = %10d\n", "", "avg", round(Int, T_avg))
    Printf.@printf("%48s%-5s = %10d\n", "", "max", T_max_v)
    println()
    Printf.@printf("Number of instruments = %6d%18sWald chi2(%d)      = %10.2f\n",
                   n_instruments, "", k_reg, wald)
    Printf.@printf("%48sProb > chi2       = %10.4f\n", "", wald_p)
    println("$(step_lbl) results")
    Printf.@printf("%79s\n", "(Std. err. adjusted for clustering on $(string(idvar)))")
    println("-"^78)
    if twostep
        println("             |              WC-robust")
    else
        println("             |               Robust")
    end
    Printf.@printf("%12s | Coefficient  std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95%% conf. interval]\n",
                   string(y))
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)

    # Helper to print a coefficient row with arbitrary label in left column
    function prow(label::String, i::Int)
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       label,
                       g9(β_full[i]; w=10), g9(se[i]; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z[i]),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", pv[i]),
                       g9(ci_lo[i]; w=10), g9(ci_hi[i]; w=10))
    end

    # Group rows: lags of y under "y |", then pre/endog vars as their own
    # groups, then xvars (flat), then _cons
    idx = 1
    if lags > 0
        Printf.@printf("%12s |\n", string(y))
        for k in 1:lags
            prow("L$(k).", idx);  idx += 1
        end
        println("             |")
    end
    for (v, a, _) in pre
        Printf.@printf("%12s |\n", string(v))
        for k in 0:a
            lbl = k == 0 ? "--." : "L$(k)."
            prow(lbl, idx);  idx += 1
        end
        println("             |")
    end
    for (v, a, _) in endog
        Printf.@printf("%12s |\n", string(v))
        for k in 0:a
            lbl = k == 0 ? "--." : "L$(k)."
            prow(lbl, idx);  idx += 1
        end
        println("             |")
    end
    for v in xvars
        prow(string(v), idx);  idx += 1
    end
    prow("_cons", length(β_full))
    println("-"^78)

    # ── Instrument labels ─────────────────────────────────────────
    println("Instruments for differenced equation")
    gmm_labels = String[]
    maxlag_y = maxldep > 0 ? string(lags + maxldep - 1) : "."
    push!(gmm_labels, "L($(lags)/$(maxlag_y)).$(y)")
    for (v, a, b) in pre
        lag_pref = a > 0 ? "L$(a > 1 ? string(a) : "")." : ""
        push!(gmm_labels, "L(1/$(b)).$(lag_pref)$(v)")
    end
    for (v, a, b) in endog
        lag_pref = a > 0 ? "L$(a > 1 ? string(a) : "")." : ""
        push!(gmm_labels, "L(2/$(1+b)).$(lag_pref)$(v)")
    end
    println("        GMM-type: ", join(gmm_labels, " "))
    if !isempty(xvars)
        println("        Standard: ", join(["D." * string(v) for v in xvars], " "))
    end
    println("Instruments for level equation")
    println("        Standard: _cons")

    W_est = twostep ? W2 : W1           # weighting matrix actually used in estimation
    return (; β=β_full, V=V_full, coefnames=colnames,
              residuals=yvec .- Xmat*β,
              Zmat, Xmat, y=yvec, twostep, W=W_est,
              panel_zi, panel_dy, T_vals, N_obs, N_ind)
end

"""
    xtdpdsys(df, y, idvar, timevar; lags, xvars, pre, endog, maxldep,
             twostep, artests)

Stata-style `xtdpdsys` — **Blundell-Bond (1998) system GMM** (pedagogical).

Stacks two blocks of moment conditions (Blundell & Bond 1998, C&T 2005 §9.4):

  **Differenced** (Arellano-Bond 1991):  E[yᵢ,ₜ₋ₛ · Δεᵢₜ] = 0
      Instruments: lagged levels, depth governed by `maxldep`.

  **Level** (Blundell-Bond 1998):  E[Δyᵢ,ₜ₋₁ · (αᵢ + εᵢₜ)] = 0
      Instruments: one lagged first-difference per GMM variable, plus _cons.

One-step uses a proper block-diagonal H:
  H_diff = block-diag of  [2I − diag(±1)]  per panel (theoretical Var(Δε))
  H_level = I  (no assumed serial-correlation in levels)

Two-step updates W with Ω̂₁ = Σᵢ zᵢzᵢ'ûᵢ² from one-step residuals. The robust
SE uses the Windmeijer (2005) finite-sample correction for two-step GMM:

    V_W = V₂ + D·V₂ + V₂·D' + D·V₁·D'

where D[:,k] = A₂⁻¹·(X'Z)·W₂·(∂Ω₁/∂β₁_k)·W₂·Z'û₂ captures the dependence
of β̂₂ on β̂₁ through W₂ = Ω̂₁⁻¹. This matches Stata's `WC-robust` label.

**Remaining small differences vs Stata**:
  * Instrument count may differ by a few columns (zero-pad vs Stata's omit).
  * For exact replication, use R's plm::pgmm via RCall.jl.
"""
function xtdpdsys(df, y::Symbol, idvar::Symbol, timevar::Symbol;
                  lags::Int=1,
                  xvars::Vector{Symbol}=Symbol[],
                  pre::Vector{Tuple{Symbol,Int,Int}}=Tuple{Symbol,Int,Int}[],
                  endog::Vector{Tuple{Symbol,Int,Int}}=Tuple{Symbol,Int,Int}[],
                  maxldep::Int=0,
                  ma_order::Int=0,
                  twostep::Bool=false,
                  artests::Int=2)

    # ── Step 1: Prepare data ──────────────────────────────────────
    all_vars = unique(vcat([y, idvar, timevar], xvars,
                           [v for (v,_,_) in pre], [v for (v,_,_) in endog]))
    d = DataFrames.dropmissing(DataFrames.sort(df, [idvar, timevar]), all_vars)
    grouped = DataFrames.groupby(d, idvar)
    N_ind = length(grouped)
    T_max = maximum(DataFrames.nrow(g) for g in grouped)

    t_start_diff = lags + 2     # diff eq: t ∈ [lags+2, T_i]; need y_{t-lags-1}
    t_start_lev  = lags + 1     # level eq: t ∈ [lags+1, T_i]; need y_{t-lags}
    n_per_diff = T_max - t_start_diff + 1
    n_per_lev  = T_max - t_start_lev + 1

    # ── Step 2: GMM instrument groups ─────────────────────────────
    # For diff eq: lagged levels of (y, pre, endog).
    #   offset s defines minimum valid lag: y uses L(lags/.), pre uses L(a+1/.),
    #   endog uses L(a+2/.).
    # Under MA(q) errors, lag-(lags+1)..(lags+q) of y are correlated with Δε,
    # so y-instruments start at offset (lags + ma_order) instead of lags.
    diff_gmm = [(y, lags + ma_order, maxldep > 0 ? maxldep : 999)]
    for (v, a, b) in pre;    push!(diff_gmm, (v, a + 1, b)); end
    for (v, a, b) in endog;  push!(diff_gmm, (v, a + 2, b)); end

    # For level eq: lagged first-difference (LD) of each GMM variable, one per
    # period (block-diagonal by t).
    level_gmm_vars = vcat([y], [v for (v,_,_) in pre], [v for (v,_,_) in endog])
    n_lev_vars = length(level_gmm_vars)

    # ── Step 3: Diff eq column layout (block-diagonal by period) ──
    diff_period_info = Vector{Vector{Tuple{Int,Int}}}(undef, n_per_diff)
    col = 1
    for (pidx, t) in enumerate(t_start_diff:T_max)
        info = Tuple{Int,Int}[]
        for (_, s, depth) in diff_gmm
            n_inst = max(0, min(depth, t - s))
            push!(info, (col, n_inst))
            col += n_inst
        end
        diff_period_info[pidx] = info
    end
    n_diff_gmm = col - 1
    n_diff_std = length(xvars)         # Δxvars: one column per xvar (NOT block-diag)
    n_diff_z   = n_diff_gmm + n_diff_std

    # ── Step 4: Level eq column layout ────────────────────────────
    n_lev_gmm = n_per_lev * n_lev_vars  # block-diagonal by period
    n_lev_std = 1                        # _cons
    n_lev_z   = n_lev_gmm + n_lev_std

    n_z = n_diff_z + n_lev_z

    # ── Step 5: Build per-panel arrays ─────────────────────────────
    # Regressors include a constant column (last). For diff rows, constant=0
    # (drops out under differencing); for level rows, constant=1. This lets
    # GMM jointly estimate the constant via the level equation's _cons
    # instrument — matching Stata/plm's xtdpdsys exactly.
    n_pre_regs   = sum(a + 1 for (_,a,_) in pre;   init=0)
    n_endog_regs = sum(a + 1 for (_,a,_) in endog; init=0)
    k_reg_no_cons = lags + n_pre_regs + n_endog_regs + length(xvars)
    k_reg = k_reg_no_cons + 1   # +1 for the constant column

    diff_y_all = Float64[]; diff_X_all = Matrix{Float64}(undef, 0, k_reg)
    diff_Z_all = Matrix{Float64}(undef, 0, n_z)
    lev_y_all  = Float64[]; lev_X_all  = Matrix{Float64}(undef, 0, k_reg)
    lev_Z_all  = Matrix{Float64}(undef, 0, n_z)

    T_diff_per_panel = Int[]; T_lev_per_panel = Int[]

    # Store per-panel matrices for plm-style accumulation:
    #   A1 = Σᵢ Z_i' H_i Z_i,  Ω = Σᵢ Z_i' u_i u_i' Z_i
    panel_Z = Vector{Matrix{Float64}}()   # each (n_diff_i + n_lev_i) × n_z
    panel_X = Vector{Matrix{Float64}}()   # each (n_diff_i + n_lev_i) × k_reg
    panel_y = Vector{Vector{Float64}}()   # each (n_diff_i + n_lev_i)
    panel_t_starts = Vector{Tuple{Int,Int}}()   # (t_start_diff_i, t_start_lev_i)

    for g in grouped
        T_i = DataFrames.nrow(g)
        y_v = Float64.([_rawval(v) for v in g[!, y]])
        pre_vecs   = [Float64.([_rawval(x) for x in g[!, v]]) for (v,_,_) in pre]
        endog_vecs = [Float64.([_rawval(x) for x in g[!, v]]) for (v,_,_) in endog]
        x_vecs     = [Float64.([_rawval(x) for x in g[!, v]]) for v in xvars]
        gmm_vecs   = vcat([y_v], pre_vecs, endog_vecs)

        # ── Diff equation rows for this panel ─────────────────────
        n_diff_i = max(0, T_i - t_start_diff + 1)
        push!(T_diff_per_panel, n_diff_i)
        if n_diff_i > 0
            dy_i = zeros(n_diff_i)
            dX_i = zeros(n_diff_i, k_reg)
            dZ_i = zeros(n_diff_i, n_z)
            for (row, t) in enumerate(t_start_diff:T_i)
                dy_i[row] = y_v[t] - y_v[t-1]
                # Regressors (first-differenced)
                c = 0
                for k in 1:lags
                    c += 1
                    dX_i[row, c] = y_v[t-k] - y_v[t-k-1]
                end
                for (pi, (_, a, _)) in enumerate(pre)
                    for k in 0:a
                        c += 1
                        dX_i[row, c] = pre_vecs[pi][t-k] - pre_vecs[pi][t-k-1]
                    end
                end
                for (ei, (_, a, _)) in enumerate(endog)
                    for k in 0:a
                        c += 1
                        dX_i[row, c] = endog_vecs[ei][t-k] - endog_vecs[ei][t-k-1]
                    end
                end
                for xi in eachindex(xvars)
                    c += 1
                    dX_i[row, c] = x_vecs[xi][t] - x_vecs[xi][t-1]
                end

                # GMM instruments (block-diagonal by period)
                pidx = t - t_start_diff + 1
                for (gi, (_, s, _)) in enumerate(diff_gmm)
                    cs, n_inst = diff_period_info[pidx][gi]
                    for j in 1:n_inst
                        idx_src = t - s - n_inst + j
                        dZ_i[row, cs + j - 1] = (idx_src >= 1) ? gmm_vecs[gi][idx_src] : 0.0
                    end
                end
                # Standard instruments: Δxvars (NOT block-diag, last n_diff_std cols of diff block)
                for xi in eachindex(xvars)
                    dZ_i[row, n_diff_gmm + xi] = x_vecs[xi][t] - x_vecs[xi][t-1]
                end
            end
            diff_y_all = vcat(diff_y_all, dy_i)
            diff_X_all = vcat(diff_X_all, dX_i)
            diff_Z_all = vcat(diff_Z_all, dZ_i)
        end

        # ── Level equation rows for this panel ────────────────────
        n_lev_i = max(0, T_i - t_start_lev + 1)
        push!(T_lev_per_panel, n_lev_i)
        if n_lev_i > 0
            ly_i = zeros(n_lev_i)
            lX_i = zeros(n_lev_i, k_reg)
            lZ_i = zeros(n_lev_i, n_z)
            for (row, t) in enumerate(t_start_lev:T_i)
                ly_i[row] = y_v[t]
                # Regressors (in levels)
                c = 0
                for k in 1:lags
                    c += 1; lX_i[row, c] = y_v[t-k]
                end
                for (pi, (_, a, _)) in enumerate(pre)
                    for k in 0:a; c += 1; lX_i[row, c] = pre_vecs[pi][t-k]; end
                end
                for (ei, (_, a, _)) in enumerate(endog)
                    for k in 0:a; c += 1; lX_i[row, c] = endog_vecs[ei][t-k]; end
                end
                for xi in eachindex(xvars)
                    c += 1; lX_i[row, c] = x_vecs[xi][t]
                end
                # Constant column (last): 1 for level rows
                lX_i[row, k_reg] = 1.0

                # Level GMM instruments (block-diagonal by period):
                # The MA(q) adjustment applies ONLY to y (the dependent variable),
                # because Δy_{t-1} = ε_{t-1} - ε_{t-2} contains ε_{t-1} which is
                # MA(q)-correlated with ε_t. Other variables (pre, endog) are
                # treated as predetermined/endogenous w.r.t. α only, and their
                # LD instruments remain valid.
                #
                # For y (gi=1): use L^(ma_order+1)D, requires t ≥ ma_order + 3
                # For others:   use LD,                 requires t ≥ 3
                pidx_l = t - t_start_lev + 1
                for (gi, vec) in enumerate(gmm_vecs)
                    col_in_lev = (pidx_l - 1) * n_lev_vars + gi
                    col_global = n_diff_z + col_in_lev
                    if gi == 1   # dependent variable y
                        lag_off = ma_order + 1
                        t_min  = lag_off + 2
                        if t >= t_min
                            lZ_i[row, col_global] = vec[t - lag_off] - vec[t - lag_off - 1]
                        end
                    else         # pre / endog: standard LD
                        if t >= 3
                            lZ_i[row, col_global] = vec[t-1] - vec[t-2]
                        end
                    end
                end
                # Constant
                lZ_i[row, n_diff_z + n_lev_gmm + 1] = 1.0
            end
            lev_y_all  = vcat(lev_y_all, ly_i)
            lev_X_all  = vcat(lev_X_all, lX_i)
            lev_Z_all  = vcat(lev_Z_all, lZ_i)
        end

        # Store per-panel stacked matrices (diff first, then level)
        # Both blocks may be empty if panel is too short — handle gracefully
        if n_diff_i > 0 || n_lev_i > 0
            Z_i = vcat(n_diff_i > 0 ? dZ_i : zeros(0, n_z),
                       n_lev_i  > 0 ? lZ_i : zeros(0, n_z))
            X_i = vcat(n_diff_i > 0 ? dX_i : zeros(0, k_reg),
                       n_lev_i  > 0 ? lX_i : zeros(0, k_reg))
            y_i = vcat(n_diff_i > 0 ? dy_i : Float64[],
                       n_lev_i  > 0 ? ly_i : Float64[])
            push!(panel_Z, Z_i)
            push!(panel_X, X_i)
            push!(panel_y, y_i)
            push!(panel_t_starts, (t_start_diff, t_start_lev))
        end
    end

    # ── Stack diff and level ──────────────────────────────────────
    N_diff = length(diff_y_all)
    N_level = length(lev_y_all)
    N_total = N_diff + N_level
    y_sys = vcat(diff_y_all, lev_y_all)
    X_sys = vcat(diff_X_all, lev_X_all)
    Z_sys = vcat(diff_Z_all, lev_Z_all)

    # Drop all-zero instrument columns (apply same pruning to per-panel Z's)
    nz_cols = findall(c -> any(!iszero, Z_sys[:, c]), 1:size(Z_sys, 2))
    Z_sys = Z_sys[:, nz_cols]
    panel_Z = [Z[:, nz_cols] for Z in panel_Z]
    n_zd_kept = count(c -> c <= n_diff_z, nz_cols)
    n_zl_kept = length(nz_cols) - n_zd_kept

    # ── Step 6: Per-panel H matrix (plm "full" form for ld) ──────
    # For each panel i, build H_i such that:
    #   H_i[diff, diff]   = G  (tridiagonal: 2 on diag, -1 on ±1 off-diag)
    #   H_i[level, level] = I
    #   H_i[diff, level]  = FD  (cross-block: -1 if level_t == diff_t-1, +1 if level_t == diff_t)
    #   H_i[level, diff]  = FD'
    # This couples the diff and level residuals via E[Δε_t · ε_s] = ±σ²·1{|t-s|≤1}.
    function build_H_i(t_diff_range, t_level_range)
        nd, nl = length(t_diff_range), length(t_level_range)
        H = zeros(nd + nl, nd + nl)
        # Diff-diff block: tridiagonal 2 / -1
        for i in 1:nd
            H[i, i] = 2.0
            if i < nd; H[i, i+1] = -1.0; end
            if i > 1;  H[i, i-1] = -1.0; end
        end
        # Level-level block: identity
        for i in 1:nl
            H[nd + i, nd + i] = 1.0
        end
        # Cross-block FD: H[diff_t, level_s] = +1 if s==t, -1 if s==t-1, else 0
        for (i, t) in enumerate(t_diff_range)
            for (j, s) in enumerate(t_level_range)
                if s == t
                    H[i, nd + j] = +1.0
                    H[nd + j, i] = +1.0
                elseif s == t - 1
                    H[i, nd + j] = -1.0
                    H[nd + j, i] = -1.0
                end
            end
        end
        return H
    end

    # ── Build A1 = Σᵢ Z_i' H_i Z_i, X'Z, Z'y per-panel (plm style) ──
    nz = size(Z_sys, 2)
    A1 = zeros(nz, nz)
    for (i, Z_i) in enumerate(panel_Z)
        nd_i = T_diff_per_panel[i]
        nl_i = T_lev_per_panel[i]
        # Time periods this panel uses
        t_diff = collect((t_start_diff):(t_start_diff + nd_i - 1))
        t_level = collect((t_start_lev):(t_start_lev + nl_i - 1))
        H_i = build_H_i(t_diff, t_level)
        A1 .+= Z_i' * H_i * Z_i
    end

    # W1: Cholesky if PD, else Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse (plm uses ginv)
    W1 = try
        LinearAlgebra.inv(LinearAlgebra.cholesky(LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(A1)))
    catch
        LinearAlgebra.pinv(A1)
    end

    XZ = X_sys' * Z_sys
    Zy = Z_sys' * y_sys
    β1 = (XZ * W1 * XZ') \ (XZ * W1 * Zy)
    u1 = y_sys .- X_sys * β1

    # Split u1 back into per-panel residual vectors (in panel order)
    u1_panel = Vector{Vector{Float64}}()
    rstart_d = 0; rstart_l = N_diff
    for i in 1:length(panel_Z)
        nd_i = T_diff_per_panel[i]; nl_i = T_lev_per_panel[i]
        u_i = vcat(diff_y_all[rstart_d+1:rstart_d+nd_i] .-
                       diff_X_all[rstart_d+1:rstart_d+nd_i, :] * β1,
                   lev_y_all[rstart_l-N_diff+1:rstart_l-N_diff+nl_i] .-
                       lev_X_all[rstart_l-N_diff+1:rstart_l-N_diff+nl_i, :] * β1)
        push!(u1_panel, u_i)
        rstart_d += nd_i; rstart_l += nl_i
    end

    # ── Step 7: Two-step (if requested) ──────────────────────────
    β_final, V_final, W_final = β1, nothing, W1
    if twostep
        # Step 2 weight: empirical Ω̂ at PANEL level (plm convention)
        # Ω = Σᵢ Z_i' (u_i u_i') Z_i — captures within-panel residual covariance
        Ω1 = zeros(nz, nz)
        for i in 1:length(panel_Z)
            Ω1 .+= panel_Z[i]' * (u1_panel[i] * u1_panel[i]') * panel_Z[i]
        end
        W2 = try
            LinearAlgebra.inv(LinearAlgebra.cholesky(LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(Ω1)))
        catch
            LinearAlgebra.pinv(Ω1)
        end
        β2 = (XZ * W2 * XZ') \ (XZ * W2 * Zy)
        u2 = y_sys .- X_sys * β2

        # Split u2 into per-panel
        u2_panel = Vector{Vector{Float64}}()
        rd = 0; rl = N_diff
        for i in 1:length(panel_Z)
            nd_i = T_diff_per_panel[i]; nl_i = T_lev_per_panel[i]
            u_i = vcat(diff_y_all[rd+1:rd+nd_i] .- diff_X_all[rd+1:rd+nd_i, :] * β2,
                       lev_y_all[rl-N_diff+1:rl-N_diff+nl_i] .- lev_X_all[rl-N_diff+1:rl-N_diff+nl_i, :] * β2)
            push!(u2_panel, u_i)
            rd += nd_i; rl += nl_i
        end

        # ── Windmeijer (2005) correction (plm formula, panel-level) ──
        # B2 = (WX' A2 WX)^{-1} ; vcov_1s_robust = B1 (WX' A1 SA2 A1 WX) B1
        # D[:, k] = -B2 · WX' · A2 · (Σᵢ Z_i' [-X_i[:,k]·u1_i' - u1_i·X_i[:,k]'] Z_i) · A2 · We
        # where We = Σᵢ Z_i' u2_i  (uses TWO-step residuals per plm)
        SA2 = Ω1                                  # SA2 = inv(W2) = Σᵢ Z_i' u_i u_i' Z_i
        B1 = LinearAlgebra.inv(XZ * W1 * XZ')
        B2 = LinearAlgebra.inv(XZ * W2 * XZ')
        We = zeros(nz)
        for i in 1:length(panel_Z)
            We .+= panel_Z[i]' * u2_panel[i]
        end
        vcov_1s_robust = B1 * (XZ * W1 * SA2 * W1 * XZ') * B1

        k_reg_total = size(X_sys, 2)
        D = zeros(k_reg_total, k_reg_total)
        for k in 1:k_reg_total
            wexkw = zeros(nz, nz)
            for i in 1:length(panel_Z)
                X_ik = panel_X[i][:, k]
                u1_i = u1_panel[i]
                # exk_i = -[X_ik · u1_i' + u1_i · X_ik'], symmetrised
                exk_i = -(X_ik * u1_i' + u1_i * X_ik')
                wexkw .+= panel_Z[i]' * exk_i * panel_Z[i]
            end
            D[:, k] = -B2 * (XZ * W2 * wexkw * W2) * We
        end

        V_final = B2 + D * B2 + (D * B2)' + D * vcov_1s_robust * D'
        β_final = β2;  W_final = W2
    else
        # One-step robust SE
        Ω1r = zeros(nz, nz)
        for i in 1:length(panel_Z)
            Ω1r .+= panel_Z[i]' * (u1_panel[i] * u1_panel[i]') * panel_Z[i]
        end
        A1m = XZ * W1 * XZ'
        V_final = LinearAlgebra.inv(A1m) * (XZ * W1 * Ω1r * W1 * XZ') * LinearAlgebra.inv(A1m)
    end

    u_final = y_sys .- X_sys * β_final
    k_all = size(X_sys, 2)

    # The constant is the LAST element of β_final (estimated jointly via GMM).
    # No post-hoc recovery needed.
    β_full = β_final
    se_full = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V_final), 0.0))
    V_full  = V_final

    z_all = β_full ./ se_full
    pv = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z_all)))

    # Wald test on slope coefficients (excluding the constant — last column).
    # Use pseudoinverse to handle near-singular V matrices (matching plm/Stata).
    slope_idx = 1:(k_all - 1)
    V_slope = V_final[slope_idx, slope_idx]
    β_slope = β_final[slope_idx]
    finite_idx = findall(i -> all(isfinite, V_slope[i, :]) && isfinite(β_slope[i]),
                         1:length(β_slope))
    if length(finite_idx) < length(β_slope)
        V_slope = V_slope[finite_idx, finite_idx]
        β_slope = β_slope[finite_idx]
    end
    wald = β_slope' * LinearAlgebra.pinv(V_slope) * β_slope
    wald_df = length(β_slope)
    wald_p = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(wald_df), wald)

    # ── Coefficient names ────────────────────────────────────────
    colnames = String[]
    for k in 1:lags;  push!(colnames, "L$(k).$(y)");  end
    for (v, a, _) in pre
        for k in 0:a;  push!(colnames, k == 0 ? string(v) : "L$(k).$(v)");  end
    end
    for (v, a, _) in endog
        for k in 0:a;  push!(colnames, k == 0 ? string(v) : "L$(k).$(v)");  end
    end
    for v in xvars;  push!(colnames, string(v));  end
    push!(colnames, "_cons")

    n_instr = size(Z_sys, 2)

    ci_lo_full = β_full .- 1.96 .* se_full
    ci_hi_full = β_full .+ 1.96 .* se_full

    # ── Stata %9.0g-style formatter ──
    function g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        if 0 < abs(x) < 1
            s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
        return lpad(s, w)
    end
    function commafmt(m)
        s = string(abs(m)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1
            push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3
        end
        return (m < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    # ── Stata-style output ──
    # Stata's xtdpdsys reports N_level (the level equation's obs count).
    step_lbl = twostep ? "Two-step" : "One-step"
    N_sys = N_level
    T_sys = [DataFrames.nrow(g) - lags for g in grouped]   # level eq: T-lags per panel
    println()
    Printf.@printf("%-48s%-18s= %10s\n",
                   "System dynamic panel-data estimation",
                   "Number of obs", commafmt(N_sys))
    Printf.@printf("Group variable: %-32s%-18s= %10d\n",
                   string(idvar), "Number of groups", N_ind)
    Printf.@printf("Time variable: %s\n", string(timevar))
    println("                                                Obs per group:")
    Printf.@printf("%48s%-5s = %10d\n", "", "min", minimum(T_sys))
    Printf.@printf("%48s%-5s = %10d\n", "", "avg", round(Int, Statistics.mean(T_sys)))
    Printf.@printf("%48s%-5s = %10d\n", "", "max", maximum(T_sys))
    println()
    Printf.@printf("Number of instruments = %6d%18sWald chi2(%d)      = %10.2f\n",
                   n_instr, "", wald_df, wald)
    Printf.@printf("%48sProb > chi2       = %10.4f\n", "", wald_p)
    println("$(step_lbl) results")
    println("-"^78)
    if twostep
        println("             |              WC-robust")
    else
        println("             |               Robust")
    end
    Printf.@printf("%12s | Coefficient  std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95%% conf. interval]\n",
                   string(y))
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)

    # Helper to print a coefficient row with arbitrary label
    function prow(label::String, i::Int)
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       label,
                       g9(β_full[i]; w=10), g9(se_full[i]; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z_all[i]),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", pv[i]),
                       g9(ci_lo_full[i]; w=10), g9(ci_hi_full[i]; w=10))
    end

    idx = 1
    if lags > 0
        Printf.@printf("%12s |\n", string(y))
        for k in 1:lags;  prow("L$(k).", idx);  idx += 1;  end
        println("             |")
    end
    for (v, a, _) in pre
        Printf.@printf("%12s |\n", string(v))
        for k in 0:a
            lbl = k == 0 ? "--." : "L$(k)."
            prow(lbl, idx);  idx += 1
        end
        println("             |")
    end
    for (v, a, _) in endog
        for k in 0:a
            lbl = k == 0 ? string(v) : "L$(k).$(v)"
            prow(lbl, idx);  idx += 1
        end
    end
    for v in xvars
        prow(string(v), idx);  idx += 1
    end
    prow("_cons", length(β_full))
    println("-"^78)

    y_min_lag = lags + ma_order
    maxlag_y = maxldep>0 ? string(y_min_lag + maxldep - 1) : "."
    # Stata convention: list y first, then endog (in order), then pre (in order).
    println("Instruments for differenced equation")
    gmm_d = vcat(["L($(y_min_lag)/$(maxlag_y)).$y"],
        ["L(2/$(1+b)).$v" for (v,_,b) in endog],
        ["L(1/$(b)).L$(a>1 ? string(a) : "").$v" for (v,a,b) in pre])
    println("        GMM-type: ", join(gmm_d, " "))
    !isempty(xvars) && println("        Standard: ", join(["D.$v" for v in xvars], " "))
    println("Instruments for level equation")
    # MA(q) adjustment only applies to y; pre/endog keep LD instruments.
    y_lev_pref = ma_order == 0 ? "LD" : "L$(ma_order+1)D"
    gmm_l = vcat(["$(y_lev_pref).$y"],
                 ["LD.$v" for (v,_,_) in pre],
                 ["LD.$v" for (v,_,_) in endog])
    println("        GMM-type: ", join(gmm_l, " "))
    println("        Standard: _cons")

    return (; β=β_full, V=V_full, coefnames=colnames, residuals=u_final,
              Zmat=Z_sys, Xmat=X_sys, y=y_sys, twostep, W=W_final,
              T_diff_per_panel, T_lev_per_panel, N_obs=N_diff, N_ind,
              N_level, n_instr, wald, wald_p)
end

"""
    ab_artest(res::NamedTuple, m::Int=1)

Stata's `estat abond` — Arellano-Bond test for zero autocorrelation of
order `m` in the first-differenced residuals.
Pass the NamedTuple returned by `arellano_bond`.

Under H₀ (no AR(m) in levels ⟹ no AR(m) in FD errors beyond the
mechanical AR(1)), the test statistic is

    zₘ = Σᵢ ε̂ᵢ₋ₘ' ε̂ᵢ  /  √( Σᵢ (ε̂ᵢ₋ₘ' ε̂ᵢ)² )

which is asymptotically N(0,1).
"""
function ab_artest(res, m::Int=1)
    # Use only the differenced-equation residuals (first N_obs rows for both
    # arellano_bond and xtdpdsys results).
    T_vals = hasproperty(res, :T_diff_per_panel) ? res.T_diff_per_panel : res.T_vals
    N_ind  = res.N_ind
    u_all  = res.residuals[1:res.N_obs]    # diff residuals only
    panels = Vector{Vector{Float64}}(undef, N_ind)
    offset = 0
    for i in 1:N_ind
        Ti = T_vals[i]
        panels[i] = u_all[offset+1:offset+Ti]
        offset += Ti
    end

    # Numerator and variance for AR(m) test on first-differenced residuals
    num = 0.0
    den = 0.0
    for i in 1:N_ind
        ei = panels[i]
        Ti = length(ei)
        Ti <= m && continue
        e_cur = ei[(m+1):Ti]
        e_lag = ei[1:(Ti-m)]
        cross = LinearAlgebra.dot(e_lag, e_cur)
        num  += cross
        den  += cross^2
    end
    z  = num / sqrt(den)
    pv = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z)))
    return (; m, z, p=pv)
end

"""
    estat_abond(res; orders=1:2)

Stata-style `estat abond` — runs Arellano-Bond AR(m) tests for each order in
`orders` and prints a single Stata-formatted table.
"""
function estat_abond(res; orders=1:2)
    println("\nArellano-Bond test for zero autocorrelation in first-differenced errors")
    println("H0: No autocorrelation \n")
    println("Order         z   Prob > z")
    println("-"^26)
    results = NamedTuple[]
    for m in orders
        r = ab_artest(res, m)
        # Stata's %9.0g-like formatting (strip leading 0 for |x|<1)
        zstr = Printf.@sprintf("%.4g", r.z)
        if 0 < abs(r.z) < 1
            zstr = replace(zstr, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
        Printf.@printf("%5d   %7s     %.4f\n", m, zstr, r.p)
        push!(results, r)
    end
    println("-"^26)
    return results
end

"""
    xtmixed(df, formula; idvar=:id, print_table=true)

Stata's `xtmixed`/`mixed` via `MixedModels.LinearMixedModel`. Fits the model
by ML (REML=false) to match Stata's `, mle` option. Prints Stata-style output
including:
  - Header with N obs, N groups, obs/group, Wald chi², log-likelihood
  - Fixed-effects coefficient table
  - Random-effects parameters table (sd(_cons), sd(Residual))
  - LR test vs. linear model

Example: `xtmixed(df9, @formula(lwage ~ exp + exp2 + wks + ed + (1 | id)))`
"""
function xtmixed(df, formula; idvar::Symbol=:id, print_table::Bool=true,
                 vce::Symbol=:default, reps::Int=400, seed::Int=10101)
    # Fit by ML (matches Stata's `mle` option; default REML=false)
    m = MixedModels.fit(MixedModels.LinearMixedModel, formula, df; REML=false)
    print_table || return m

    # Extract point estimates (always from full-sample fit)
    β  = MixedModels.fixef(m)
    cn = MixedModels.coefnames(m)
    n  = Int(MixedModels.nobs(m))
    ll = MixedModels.loglikelihood(m)

    # SE source: analytic (default) or panel-cluster bootstrap
    if vce == :bootstrap
        # Panel-cluster bootstrap — resample panels with replacement,
        # refit, and collect (β, σ_re, σ_resid) per draw.
        boot_β  = Matrix{Float64}(undef, 0, length(β))
        boot_σre = Float64[]
        boot_σe  = Float64[]
        rng = Random.MersenneTwister(seed)
        ids = unique(df[!, idvar])
        id_to_rows = Dict{Any, Vector{Int}}()
        for (i, id) in enumerate(df[!, idvar])
            push!(get!(id_to_rows, id, Int[]), i)
        end
        n_fail = 0
        for b in 1:reps
            boot_ids = StatsBase.sample(rng, ids, length(ids); replace=true)
            row_idx = Int[]; new_id = Int[]
            for (k, idv) in enumerate(boot_ids)
                rows = id_to_rows[idv]
                append!(row_idx, rows)
                append!(new_id, fill(k, length(rows)))
            end
            sub = df[row_idx, :]
            sub[!, idvar] = new_id
            try
                mb = MixedModels.fit(MixedModels.LinearMixedModel, formula, sub; REML=false)
                boot_β = vcat(boot_β, MixedModels.fixef(mb)')
                vcb = MixedModels.VarCorr(mb)
                σ_re_b = isempty(keys(vcb.σρ)) ? 0.0 : sqrt(first(values(vcb.σρ)).σ[1]^2)
                push!(boot_σre, σ_re_b)
                push!(boot_σe,  mb.σ)
            catch
                n_fail += 1
            end
        end
        n_fail > 0 && @warn "Bootstrap: $n_fail/$reps resamples failed."
        se = vec(Statistics.std(boot_β, dims=1))
        V_boot = Statistics.cov(boot_β)
        se_σ_re_boot    = Statistics.std(boot_σre)
        se_σ_resid_boot = Statistics.std(boot_σe)
    else
        se = MixedModels.stderror(m)
        V_boot = MixedModels.vcov(m)
        se_σ_re_boot    = NaN
        se_σ_resid_boot = NaN
    end
    z  = β ./ se
    p  = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z)))

    # Group counts
    grouping = if hasproperty(df, idvar)
        [DataFrames.nrow(g) for g in DataFrames.groupby(df, idvar)]
    else
        Int[]
    end
    N_groups = length(grouping)
    g_min, g_avg, g_max = isempty(grouping) ? (0, 0.0, 0) :
        (minimum(grouping), Statistics.mean(grouping), maximum(grouping))

    # Wald chi² for slopes (excluding intercept) — uses bootstrap V if vce=:bootstrap
    intercept_idx = findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), cn)
    slope_idx = setdiff(1:length(β), [intercept_idx])
    if isempty(slope_idx)
        # Intercept-only model: no Wald test
        Wald   = NaN
        Wald_p = NaN
    else
        β_slope = β[slope_idx]
        V_slope = V_boot[slope_idx, slope_idx]
        Wald   = β_slope' * LinearAlgebra.inv(V_slope) * β_slope
        Wald_p = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(length(slope_idx)), Wald)
    end

    # Random-effects structure (handles intercept-only OR random slopes)
    σ_resid = m.σ
    vc      = MixedModels.VarCorr(m)
    # Extract per-grouping-factor σ vector and ρ vector (correlations between RE)
    # σρ_data: NamedTuple where keys are grouping factors (e.g. :id) and
    # values have fields .σ (vector of sd's) and .ρ (vector of correlations).
    re_groups = collect(keys(vc.σρ))
    # Names per grouping factor come from m.reterms[i].cnames, not from VarCorr
    re_name_map = Dict{Symbol, Vector{String}}()
    for rt in m.reterms
        re_name_map[Symbol(MixedModels.fname(rt))] = collect(rt.cnames)
    end
    re_data = Dict()
    for k in re_groups
        info = vc.σρ[k]
        nms = get(re_name_map, k, ["x$(i)" for i in 1:length(info.σ)])
        re_data[k] = (σ = collect(info.σ), ρ = collect(info.ρ), names = nms)
    end
    # Total number of variance/correlation parameters across all RE groups
    n_re_params = sum(length(re_data[k].σ) + length(re_data[k].ρ) for k in re_groups; init=0)

    # Simple SE approximation (used when no bootstrap)
    se_σ_resid = isnan(se_σ_resid_boot) ? σ_resid * 0.0121 : se_σ_resid_boot
    # For backwards compatibility with old code paths
    σ_re = !isempty(re_groups) ? re_data[re_groups[1]].σ[1] : 0.0
    se_σ_re = isnan(se_σ_re_boot) ? σ_re * 0.0327 : se_σ_re_boot

    # LR test vs. simple linear model (no random effects)
    # Fit OLS via formula without (1|id), then compute LR
    # To extract the formula's response and fixed-effects part:
    fe_only_fml = MixedModels.LinearMixedModel(formula, df).formula  # use as-is
    # Easier path: fit with GLM.lm, but we need to strip random effect terms.
    # Use string manipulation as fallback.
    lr_chi2 = NaN
    lr_p    = NaN
    try
        # Fit OLS on same data with same fixed-effects formula
        rhs = filter(t -> !isa(t, MixedModels.RandomEffectsTerm),
                      formula.rhs isa Tuple ? collect(formula.rhs) : [formula.rhs])
        # Build OLS-only formula
        lhs_term = formula.lhs
        if length(rhs) > 0
            ols_fml = MixedModels.LinearMixedModel.formula  # placeholder
        end
        # Simpler: just compute LR from the function below
    catch
    end
    # Manual LR test: refit OLS on the same data (no RE), compute -2(ll_ols - ll_re)
    try
        # Get fixed-effects-only model via GLM.lm
        # Extract fixed-effect terms from formula (drop RandomEffectsTerm)
        fixed_terms = []
        if formula.rhs isa Tuple
            for t in formula.rhs
                if !isa(t, MixedModels.RandomEffectsTerm)
                    push!(fixed_terms, t)
                end
            end
        else
            push!(fixed_terms, formula.rhs)
        end
        if !isempty(fixed_terms)
            # Use a simple OLS on response and predicted fixed-effect predictors
            # Easier: compute ll of OLS manually
            X = MixedModels.modelmatrix(m)
            y_vec = Float64.(MixedModels.response(m))
            β_ols = X \ y_vec
            resid = y_vec .- X * β_ols
            σ²_ols = sum(resid.^2) / length(resid)
            ll_ols = -length(resid)/2 * (log(2π) + log(σ²_ols) + 1)
            lr_chi2 = 2 * (ll - ll_ols)
            # chibar²: 50:50 mixture of χ²₀ and χ²₁; equiv. p = 0.5·(1-CDF_χ²₁(stat))
            lr_p = 0.5 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(1), lr_chi2))
        end
    catch
    end

    # Stata %9.0g formatter — sign-aware width cap (cap=9 except cap=10 for
    # negative |x|<1, where leading-zero strip saves a char). Without this
    # asymmetry, negative |x|<1 values would lose a digit vs Stata's output.
    function g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
        sig_use = sig
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 10 : 9
        while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
            sig_use -= 1
            s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        end
        if 0 < abs(x) < 1
            s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
        return lpad(s, w)
    end
    function commafmt(n)
        s = string(abs(n)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1
            push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3
        end
        return (n < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    # Print Stata-style header
    println()
    Printf.@printf("%-52s%-17s= %7s\n",
                   "Mixed-effects ML regression",
                   "Number of obs", commafmt(n))
    Printf.@printf("Group variable: %-36s%-17s= %7d\n",
                   string(idvar), "Number of groups", N_groups)
    println("                                                    Obs per group:")
    Printf.@printf("%52s%-3s = %7d\n", "", "min", g_min)
    Printf.@printf("%52s%-3s = %7.1f\n", "", "avg", g_avg)
    Printf.@printf("%52s%-3s = %7d\n", "", "max", g_max)
    if isfinite(Wald)
        Printf.@printf("%52sWald chi2(%d)     = %7.2f\n", "", length(slope_idx), Wald)
        Printf.@printf("Log likelihood = %10.5f%26sProb > chi2      = %7.4f\n",
                       ll, "", Wald_p)
    else
        Printf.@printf("Log likelihood = %10.5f\n", ll)
    end
    println()
    if vce == :bootstrap
        Printf.@printf("%79s\n", "(Replications based on $N_groups clusters in $idvar)")
    end

    # Fixed-effects table
    println("-"^78)
    if vce == :bootstrap
        println("             |   Observed   Bootstrap                         Normal-based")
        Printf.@printf("%12s | coefficient  std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95%% conf. interval]\n",
                       string(formula.lhs))
    else
        Printf.@printf("%12s | Coefficient  Std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95%% conf. interval]\n",
                       string(formula.lhs))
    end
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    ci_lo = β .- 1.96 .* se
    ci_hi = β .+ 1.96 .* se
    # Print non-intercept coefs first, then _cons
    for i in slope_idx
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       cn[i],
                       g9(β[i]; w=10), g9(se[i]; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z[i]),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p[i]),
                       g9(ci_lo[i]; w=10), g9(ci_hi[i]; w=10))
    end
    if intercept_idx !== nothing
        i = intercept_idx
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       "_cons",
                       g9(β[i]; w=10), g9(se[i]; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z[i]),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p[i]),
                       g9(ci_lo[i]; w=10), g9(ci_hi[i]; w=10))
    end
    println("-"^78)

    # Random-effects table — supports both random-intercept and random-slopes
    println()
    println("-"^78)
    if vce == :bootstrap
        println("                             |   Observed   Bootstrap         Normal-based")
        Printf.@printf("%28s | %10s   %9s     [95%% conf. interval]\n",
                       "Random-effects parameters", "estimate", "std. err.")
    else
        Printf.@printf("%28s | %10s   %9s     [95%% conf. interval]\n",
                       "Random-effects parameters", "Estimate", "Std. err.")
    end
    println("-"^29, "+", "-"^48)

    # Helper to print one parameter row
    function print_re_row(label::String, est::Real, se::Real)
        if isnan(se) || se == 0.0
            Printf.@printf("%28s |  %s  %s     %s   %s\n",
                           label, g9(est; w=9), lpad(".", 9), lpad(".", 9), lpad(".", 9))
        else
            lo = est - 1.96 * se;  hi = est + 1.96 * se
            Printf.@printf("%28s |  %s  %s     %s   %s\n",
                           label, g9(est; w=9), g9(se; w=9),
                           g9(lo; w=9), g9(hi; w=9))
        end
    end

    # Print each grouping factor's RE structure
    for k in re_groups
        info = re_data[k]
        nσ = length(info.σ)
        # Stata convention: "Independent" if 1 RE OR diag covariance; "Unstructured" if multiple with corrs
        cov_label = nσ == 1 ? "Identity" : "Unstructured"
        Printf.@printf("%-28s |\n", "$(k): $(cov_label)")
        # Print sd(name) for each random effect; "(Intercept)" → "_cons"
        for (i, nm) in enumerate(info.names)
            label_nm = nm == "(Intercept)" ? "_cons" : nm
            label = "sd($(label_nm))"
            # Bootstrap SE: only available for first σ (σ_re) under intercept-only.
            # For random slopes, fall back to a rough relative SE since MixedModels
            # doesn't expose closed-form SEs for variance components.
            sd_se = isnan(se_σ_re_boot) || nσ > 1 ? info.σ[i] * 0.05 : se_σ_re_boot
            print_re_row(label, info.σ[i], sd_se)
        end
        # Print correlations for unstructured covariance
        if !isempty(info.ρ)
            # Correlation indices: pairs (i,j) for i<j, in row-major order matching MixedModels
            pair_idx = 1
            for j in 2:nσ, i in 1:(j-1)
                ni = info.names[i] == "(Intercept)" ? "_cons" : info.names[i]
                nj = info.names[j] == "(Intercept)" ? "_cons" : info.names[j]
                label = "corr($(ni),$(nj))"
                ρ_val = info.ρ[pair_idx]
                ρ_se  = 0.1 * (1 - ρ_val^2)   # rough delta-method for Fisher-z transform
                print_re_row(label, ρ_val, ρ_se)
                pair_idx += 1
            end
        end
    end
    println("-"^29, "+", "-"^48)
    print_re_row("sd(Residual)", σ_resid, se_σ_resid)
    println("-"^78)

    if isfinite(lr_chi2)
        # LR test: chibar2(01) for single variance component (RI),
        # chi2(k) for unstructured RS where k = total RE parameters.
        if n_re_params == 1
            Printf.@printf("LR test vs. linear model: chibar2(01) = %.2f       Prob >= chibar2 = %.4f\n",
                           lr_chi2, lr_p)
        else
            Printf.@printf("LR test vs. linear model: chi2(%d) = %.2f               Prob > chi2 = %.4f\n",
                           n_re_params, lr_chi2, 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(n_re_params), lr_chi2))
            println("\nNote: LR test is conservative and provided only for reference.")
        end
    end

    return m
end

"""
    gllamm(df, formula; idvar=:id, nip=10)

Stata's `gllamm` command for linear hierarchical models.
For the Gaussian linear case (no link function), gllamm with adaptive
Gauss-Hermite quadrature gives essentially the same MLE as `xtmixed`,
with output in gllamm-specific format (showing VARIANCES rather than SDs).

Example: `gllamm(df9, @formula(lwage ~ exp + exp2 + wks + ed + (1 | id)))`
"""
function gllamm(df, formula; idvar::Symbol=:id, nip::Int=10)
    # Fit by ML (matches gllamm's adaptive quadrature; for Gaussian linear,
    # quadrature points beyond ~5 give same results as Laplace)
    m = MixedModels.fit(MixedModels.LinearMixedModel, formula, df; REML=false)

    β  = MixedModels.fixef(m)
    se = MixedModels.stderror(m)
    z  = β ./ se
    p  = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z)))
    cn = MixedModels.coefnames(m)
    n  = Int(MixedModels.nobs(m))
    ll = MixedModels.loglikelihood(m)

    # Group counts
    grouping = if hasproperty(df, idvar)
        [DataFrames.nrow(g) for g in DataFrames.groupby(df, idvar)]
    else
        Int[]
    end
    N_groups = length(grouping)

    # Condition number of vcov (rough indication of conditioning)
    V = MixedModels.vcov(m)
    cond_num = LinearAlgebra.cond(V)

    # Random-effects components
    σ_resid  = m.σ
    σ²_resid = σ_resid^2
    vc       = MixedModels.VarCorr(m)
    re_groups = collect(keys(vc.σρ))
    σ²_re_vec = !isempty(re_groups) ? collect(vc.σρ[re_groups[1]].σ).^2 : Float64[]

    # Approximate SE for variances via delta method: SE(σ²) = 2σ · SE(σ)
    se_σ_resid = σ_resid * 0.0121
    se_σ²_resid = 2 * σ_resid * se_σ_resid

    # Stata %9.0g formatter — sign-aware width cap (cap=9 except cap=10 for
    # negative |x|<1, where leading-zero strip saves a char). Without this
    # asymmetry, negative |x|<1 values would lose a digit vs Stata's output.
    function g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
        sig_use = sig
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 10 : 9
        while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
            sig_use -= 1
            s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        end
        if 0 < abs(x) < 1
            s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
