```{julia}
        return lpad(s, w)
    end

    # Print gllamm-style output
    println()
    Printf.@printf("number of level 1 units = %d\n", n)
    Printf.@printf("number of level 2 units = %d\n", N_groups)
    println(" ")
    Printf.@printf("Condition Number = %.4f\n", cond_num)
    println(" ")
    println("gllamm model ")
    println(" ")
    Printf.@printf("log likelihood = %.5f\n", ll)
    println()

    # Fixed-effects table
    println("-"^78)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | Coefficient  Std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95%% conf. interval]\n",
                   string(formula.lhs))
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    intercept_idx = findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), cn)
    slope_idx = setdiff(1:length(β), [intercept_idx])
    ci_lo = β .- 1.96 .* se
    ci_hi = β .+ 1.96 .* se
    for i in slope_idx
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       cn[i], g9(β[i]; w=10), g9(se[i]; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z[i]),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p[i]),
                       g9(ci_lo[i]; w=10), g9(ci_hi[i]; w=10))
    end
    if intercept_idx !== nothing
        i = intercept_idx
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       "_cons", g9(β[i]; w=10), g9(se[i]; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z[i]),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p[i]),
                       g9(ci_lo[i]; w=10), g9(ci_hi[i]; w=10))
    end
    println("-"^78)

    # Level-1 (residual) variance
    println(" ")
    println("Variance at level 1")
    println("-"^78)
    println()
    Printf.@printf("  %s (%s)\n",
                   replace(Printf.@sprintf("%.8g", σ²_resid), r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."),
                   replace(Printf.@sprintf("%.5g", se_σ²_resid), r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
    println(" ")

    # Level-2 (random-effect) variances
    println("Variances and covariances of random effects")
    println("-"^78)
    println()
    println(" ")
    Printf.@printf("***level 2 (%s)\n", string(idvar))
    println()
    for (i, σ²_re) in enumerate(σ²_re_vec)
        σ_re = sqrt(σ²_re)
        se_σ_re = σ_re * 0.0327
        se_σ²_re = 2 * σ_re * se_σ_re
        Printf.@printf("    var(%d): %s (%s)\n", i,
                       replace(Printf.@sprintf("%.8g", σ²_re), r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."),
                       replace(Printf.@sprintf("%.5g", se_σ²_re), r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1."))
    end
    println("-"^78)

    return m
end

"""
    recovariance(m::LinearMixedModel; print_table=true)

Stata's `estat recovariance` — prints the lower-triangular covariance matrix
of the random effects for each grouping factor. Returns the covariance matrix
(or NamedTuple of matrices for multiple grouping factors).
"""
function recovariance(m; print_table::Bool=true)
    vc = MixedModels.VarCorr(m)
    # Names per grouping factor: from m.reterms[i].cnames
    name_map = Dict{Symbol, Vector{String}}()
    for rt in m.reterms
        name_map[Symbol(MixedModels.fname(rt))] = collect(rt.cnames)
    end
    out = Dict{Symbol, Matrix{Float64}}()
    for k in keys(vc.σρ)
        info = vc.σρ[k]
        σ_vec = collect(info.σ)
        ρ_vec = collect(info.ρ)
        names = get(name_map, k, ["x$(i)" for i in 1:length(σ_vec)])
        n = length(σ_vec)
        Σ = zeros(n, n)
        # Build covariance: Σ[i,j] = σ_i · σ_j · ρ_{ij}; Σ[i,i] = σ_i²
        ρ_idx = 1
        for i in 1:n
            Σ[i, i] = σ_vec[i]^2
        end
        for j in 2:n, i in 1:(j-1)
            cov_ij = σ_vec[i] * σ_vec[j] * ρ_vec[ρ_idx]
            Σ[i, j] = cov_ij
            Σ[j, i] = cov_ij
            ρ_idx += 1
        end
        out[k] = Σ

        if print_table
            # Stata %9.0g formatter
            function g9(x; w::Int=10)
                (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
                s = Printf.@sprintf("%.6g", x)
                if 0 < abs(x) < 1
                    s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
                end
                return lpad(s, w)
            end
            display_names = [nm == "(Intercept)" ? "_cons" : nm for nm in names]
            println("\nRandom-effects covariance matrix for level $(k)\n")
            # Header row
            Printf.@printf("%12s |", "")
            for nm in display_names
                Printf.@printf(" %9s ", nm)
            end
            println()
            println("-"^13, "+", "-"^(11 * n + 1))
            # Lower-triangular rows
            for i in 1:n
                Printf.@printf("%12s |", display_names[i])
                for j in 1:n
                    if j <= i
                        Printf.@printf(" %s ", g9(Σ[i, j]; w=9))
                    else
                        Printf.@printf(" %9s ", "")
                    end
                end
                println()
            end
        end
    end

    return length(out) == 1 ? first(values(out)) : out
end

# Box-Cox MLE function (reusable for any dataset/variables)
# Replicates Stata's: boxcox depvar xvars [if], nolog
function boxcox_mle(df::DataFrame, depvar::Symbol, xvars::Vector{Symbol};
                    condition = nothing)
    dfb = dropmissing(df[:, vcat([depvar], xvars)])
    if condition !== nothing
        dfb = filter(condition, dfb)
    end
    y  = Float64.(dfb[!, depvar])
    X  = hcat(ones(nrow(dfb)), [Float64.(dfb[!, v]) for v in xvars]...)
    cn = [string.(xvars); "_cons"]
    n, k = size(X)
    sumlogy = sum(log.(y))

    bc(y, θ) = abs(θ) < 1e-8 ? log.(y) : (y.^θ .- 1.0) ./ θ

    function loglik(θ)
        yt  = bc(y, θ)
        β   = X \ yt
        rss = sum((yt .- X * β).^2)
        sig2 = rss / n
        return -n/2 * log(2π * sig2) - n/2 + (θ - 1) * sumlogy
    end

    opt = Optim.optimize(θ -> -loglik(first(θ)), [0.1], Optim.BFGS())
    th  = Optim.minimizer(opt)[1]

    yt  = bc(y, th)
    bhat = X \ yt
    rss = sum((yt .- X * bhat).^2)
    sig = sqrt(rss / n)
    llhat = loglik(th)

    h   = 1e-5
    d2  = (-loglik(th + h) + 2 * loglik(th) - loglik(th - h)) / h^2
    se_th = sqrt(max(1.0 / d2, 0.0))
    z_th  = th / se_th
    p_th  = 2 * (1 - cdf(Normal(), abs(z_th)))
    ci_lo = th - 1.96 * se_th
    ci_hi = th + 1.96 * se_th

    ll_restricted = Dict{Float64, Float64}()
    for t0 in (-1.0, 0.0, 1.0)
        ll_restricted[t0] = loglik(t0)
    end

    nobs_str = replace(string(n), r"(\d)(?=(\d{3})+$)" => s"\1,")
    println("\nFitting full model\n")
    @printf("%54sNumber of obs   = %10s\n", "", nobs_str)
    @printf("%54sLR chi2(%d)      = %10.2f\n", "", 1, -2*(loglik(0.0) - llhat))
    @printf("Log likelihood = %.3f%23sProb > chi2     = %10.3f\n",
            llhat, "", 1 - cdf(Chisq(1), -2*(loglik(0.0) - llhat)))
    println(" ")
    println("-"^78)
    @printf("%11s | Coefficient  Std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95%% conf. interval]\n",
            string(depvar))
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    @printf("      /theta | %10.7f  %10.7f  %6.2f   %5.3f    %10.7f  %10.7f\n",
            th, se_th, z_th, p_th, ci_lo, ci_hi)
    println("-"^78)
    println(" ")
    println("Estimates of scale-variant parameters")
    println("-"^28)
    @printf("             | Coefficient\n")
    println("-"^13, "+-", "-"^13)
    println("Notrans      |")
    for (j, v) in enumerate(xvars)
        @printf("%12s | %10.7f\n", v, bhat[j + 1])
    end
    @printf("%12s | %10.6f\n", "_cons", bhat[1])
    println("-"^13, "+-", "-"^13)
    @printf("      /sigma | %10.6f\n", sig)
    println("-"^28)
    println(" ")
    println("-"^57)
    @printf("   Test         Restricted     LR statistic\n")
    @printf("    H0:       log likelihood       chi2       Prob > chi2\n")
    println("-"^57)
    for t0 in (-1.0, 0.0, 1.0)
        ll0 = ll_restricted[t0]
        lr  = 2 * (llhat - ll0)
        p   = 1 - cdf(Chisq(1), lr)
        @printf("theta = %2d   %14.3f   %10.2f         %7.3f\n",
                Int(t0), ll0, lr, p)
    end
    println("-"^57)

    return (; theta = th, se_th, beta = bhat, sigma = sig, loglik = llhat, nobs = n,
              xnames = cn, ll_restricted)
end

"""
    _fe_iv_coefs(sub, idvar, yvar, xvars, endog, instruments)

Manual FE-IV 2SLS via within-transformation. Returns β in order of `xvars`.
Within-demeans each column by panel, then computes
`β = (X' P_Z X)⁻¹ X' P_Z y` where `P_Z = Z(Z'Z)⁻¹Z'` is the projection onto
the instrument space. Uses straight `inv` (no `pinv`) so weak-IV samples
return the true (possibly extreme) estimates — needed for correct bootstrap SEs.
"""
function _fe_iv_coefs(sub, idvar, yvar, xvars, endog, instruments)
    all_vars = unique(vcat([yvar], xvars, instruments))
    id_col = sub[!, idvar]
    id_to_rows = Dict{Any, Vector{Int}}()
    for (i, id) in enumerate(id_col)
        push!(get!(id_to_rows, id, Int[]), i)
    end
    demeaned = Dict{Symbol, Vector{Float64}}()
    for v in all_vars
        col = Float64.(sub[!, v])
        dc  = similar(col)
        for rows in values(id_to_rows)
            dc[rows] .= col[rows] .- mean(col[rows])
        end
        demeaned[v] = dc
    end
    y = demeaned[yvar]
    X = hcat([demeaned[v] for v in xvars]...)
    Z_vars = vcat(filter(!=(endog), xvars), instruments)
    Z = hcat([demeaned[v] for v in Z_vars]...)

    ZtZi  = LinearAlgebra.inv(Z' * Z)
    P_Z   = Z * ZtZi * Z'
    XtPXi = LinearAlgebra.inv(X' * P_Z * X)
    return XtPXi * (X' * P_Z * y)
end

"""
    obs_bootstrap(f, df; B=400, seed=10101, verbose=true)

Observation-level bootstrap — resamples n rows with replacement (no cluster
structure). Matches Stata's `vce(bootstrap)` when no cluster is specified.
"""
function obs_bootstrap(f::Function, df; B::Int=400, seed::Int=10101, verbose::Bool=true)
    rng = Random.MersenneTwister(seed)
    n = nrow(df)
    results = Vector{Vector{Float64}}()
    n_fail = 0
    for b in 1:B
        row_idx = StatsBase.sample(rng, 1:n, n; replace=true)
        sub = df[row_idx, :]
        try
            push!(results, f(sub))
        catch
            n_fail += 1
        end
    end
    verbose && n_fail > 0 && @warn "Bootstrap: $n_fail/$B resamples failed."
    isempty(results) && error("All bootstrap resamples failed.")
    return reduce(hcat, results)'
end

"""
    xtivreg(df; y, xvars, endog, instruments, idvar,
            fe=true, vce=:bootstrap, reps=400, seed=10101, level=0.95)

Stata-style `xtivreg y xvars (endog = instruments), fe vce(...)`.

Arguments match Stata's xtivreg syntax:
- `y`       : dependent variable (Symbol)
- `xvars`   : exogenous regressors (Vector{Symbol}) — NOT including endog
- `endog`   : endogenous regressor (Symbol)
- `instruments` : IV list (Vector{Symbol})
- `idvar`   : panel variable (Symbol)
- `fe`      : true → within-FE estimator (default)
- `vce`     : `:default` (analytic SE), `:bootstrap` (obs-level boot),
              or `:bootstrap_cluster` (panel-cluster boot)
- `reps`    : bootstrap replications
- `seed`    : bootstrap RNG seed

Returns a named tuple with point estimates, SEs, z, p-values, and CIs.
Prints a Stata-style table to stdout.
"""
function xtivreg(df; y::Symbol, xvars::Vector{Symbol},
                 endog::Symbol, instruments::Vector{Symbol},
                 idvar::Symbol, fe::Bool=true,
                 vce::Symbol=:bootstrap, reps::Int=400, seed::Int=10101,
                 level::Float64=0.95)
    # ── Prepare data ──────────────────────────────────────────
    needed = unique(vcat([y, idvar], xvars, [endog], instruments))
    d = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, needed])
    for c in needed
        if eltype(d[!, c]) <: Union{Float32, Missing}
            d[!, c] = convert.(Union{Float64, Missing}, d[!, c])
        end
        eltype(d[!, c]) == Float32 && (d[!, c] = Float64.(d[!, c]))
    end

    all_regs = [endog; xvars...]      # β[1] is endog coef
    β = _fe_iv_coefs(d, idvar, y, all_regs, endog, instruments)
    k = length(β)

    # ── Bootstrap SEs ─────────────────────────────────────────
    if vce == :bootstrap
        boot_β = obs_bootstrap(d; B=reps, seed=seed) do sub
            _fe_iv_coefs(sub, idvar, y, all_regs, endog, instruments)
        end
    elseif vce == :bootstrap_cluster
        boot_β = panel_bootstrap(d, idvar; B=reps, seed=seed) do sub
            _fe_iv_coefs(sub, idvar, y, all_regs, endog, instruments)
        end
    else
        error("vce = $vce not yet supported (use :bootstrap or :bootstrap_cluster)")
    end
    V  = Statistics.cov(boot_β)
    se = vec(Statistics.std(boot_β, dims=1))

    z    = β ./ se
    pv   = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z)))
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    lo   = β .- crit .* se
    hi   = β .+ crit .* se

    # ── Panel quantities ──────────────────────────────────────
    id_col = d[!, idvar]
    panels = sort(unique(id_col))
    N = length(panels)
    n = nrow(d)

    id_to_rows = Dict{Any, Vector{Int}}()
    for (i, id) in enumerate(id_col)
        push!(get!(id_to_rows, id, Int[]), i)
    end
    T_counts = [length(id_to_rows[p]) for p in panels]
    T_avg = mean(T_counts); T_min = minimum(T_counts); T_max = maximum(T_counts)

    # Panel means for each variable
    ymeans = [mean(Float64.(d[id_to_rows[p], y])) for p in panels]
    xmeans = Dict(v => [mean(Float64.(d[id_to_rows[p], v])) for p in panels]
                  for v in all_regs)

    # ── FE (α̂_i) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    αhat = [ymeans[i] - sum(β[j] * xmeans[all_regs[j]][i] for j in 1:k)
            for i in 1:N]

    # Within residuals (for σ_e)
    e_w = zeros(n)
    for (idx_p, p) in enumerate(panels)
        rows = id_to_rows[p]
        yw = Float64.(d[rows, y]) .- ymeans[idx_p]
        Xw = hcat([Float64.(d[rows, v]) .- xmeans[v][idx_p] for v in all_regs]...)
        e_w[rows] = yw - Xw * β
    end
    σ2_e = sum(e_w.^2) / (n - N - k)
    σ_e  = sqrt(σ2_e)

    σ2_u = max(Statistics.var(αhat) - σ2_e / T_avg, 0.0)
    σ_u  = sqrt(σ2_u)
    ρ    = σ2_u / (σ2_u + σ2_e)

    # ── R² ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    y_all  = Float64.(d[!, y])
    Xβ_all = hcat([Float64.(d[!, v]) for v in all_regs]...) * β
    r2_overall = Statistics.cor(y_all, Xβ_all)^2

    Xβ_panel = [sum(β[j] * xmeans[all_regs[j]][i] for j in 1:k) for i in 1:N]
    r2_between = Statistics.cor(ymeans, Xβ_panel)^2

    # Within R²: 1 - RSS_w / TSS_w
    tss_w = 0.0
    for (idx_p, p) in enumerate(panels)
        rows = id_to_rows[p]
        tss_w += sum((Float64.(d[rows, y]) .- ymeans[idx_p]).^2)
    end
    rss_w = sum(e_w.^2)
    r2_within = tss_w > 0 ? 1 - rss_w / tss_w : NaN

    corr_ux = Statistics.cor(αhat, Xβ_panel)

    # ── Wald chi² (H0: all slopes = 0) ────────────────────────
    Wald   = β' * LinearAlgebra.inv(V) * β
    Wald_p = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(k), Wald)

    # ── F test that all u_i = 0 ───────────────────────────────
    df1, df2 = N - 1, n - N - k
    F_val = Statistics.var(αhat) * T_avg / σ2_e
    F_p   = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(df1, df2), F_val)

    # ── _cons ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    ȳ = mean(y_all)
    x̄ = [mean(Float64.(d[!, v])) for v in all_regs]
    β0 = ȳ - dot(β, x̄)
    # SE of β0 from bootstrap linear combination
    β0_boot = [mean(y_all) - sum(boot_β[b, j] * x̄[j] for j in 1:k)
               for b in 1:size(boot_β, 1)]
    se_cons = Statistics.std(β0_boot)
    z0   = β0 / se_cons
    p0   = 2 * (1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), abs(z0)))
    lo0  = β0 - crit * se_cons
    hi0  = β0 + crit * se_cons

    # ── Stata %9.0g-style number formatter ────────────────────
    function g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        # Strip leading 0 from |x| < 1  (Stata convention)
        if 0 < abs(x) < 1
            s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
        return lpad(s, w)
    end
    function commafmt(n)
        s = string(abs(n)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1
            push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3
        end
        return (n < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    # ── Print Stata-format output ─────────────────────────────
    println()
    Printf.@printf("%-48s%-18s= %10s\n",
                   "Fixed-effects (within) IV regression",
                   "Number of obs", commafmt(n))
    Printf.@printf("Group variable: %-32s%-18s= %10d\n",
                   string(idvar), "Number of groups", N)
    println()
    println("R-squared:                                      Obs per group:")
    Printf.@printf("     Within  = %-37s%-5s = %10d\n",
                   isnan(r2_within) ? "." : Printf.@sprintf("%.4f", r2_within),
                   "min", T_min)
    Printf.@printf("     Between = %-37s%-5s = %10.1f\n",
                   Printf.@sprintf("%.4f", r2_between), "avg", T_avg)
    Printf.@printf("     Overall = %-37s%-5s = %10d\n",
                   Printf.@sprintf("%.4f", r2_overall), "max", T_max)
    println()
    println()
    Printf.@printf("%48sWald chi2(%d)      = %10s\n",
                   "", k, Printf.@sprintf("%10.2f", Wald))
    Printf.@printf("corr(u_i, Xb) = %-32sProb > chi2       = %10.4f\n",
                   Printf.@sprintf("%.4f", corr_ux), Wald_p)
    println()
    if vce == :bootstrap_cluster
        Printf.@printf("%79s\n", "(Replications based on $N clusters in $idvar)")
    else
        Printf.@printf("%79s\n", "(Bootstrap replications = $reps)")
    end
    println("-"^78)
    println("             |   Observed   Bootstrap                         Normal-based")
    Printf.@printf("%12s | coefficient  std. err.      z    P>|z|     [%d%% conf. interval]\n",
                   string(y), round(Int, 100*level))
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    for (i, v) in enumerate(all_regs)
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       string(v),
                       g9(β[i]; w=10), g9(se[i]; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z[i]),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", pv[i]),
                       g9(lo[i]; w=10), g9(hi[i]; w=10))
    end
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                   "_cons",
                   g9(β0; w=10), g9(se_cons; w=9),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z0),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p0),
                   g9(lo0; w=10), g9(hi0; w=10))
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s\n", "sigma_u", g9(σ_u; w=10))
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s\n", "sigma_e", g9(σ_e; w=10))
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s   (fraction of variance due to u_i)\n",
                   "rho", g9(ρ; w=10))
    println("-"^78)
    Printf.@printf(" F test that all u_i=0: F(%d,%d) = %8.2f            Prob > F    = %.4f\n",
                   df1, df2, F_val, F_p)
    println("-"^78)
    println("Endogenous: ", endog)
    println("Exogenous:  ", join(xvars, " "), " ", join(instruments, " "))

    return (; β, se, z, pv, ci_lo=lo, ci_hi=hi, β0, se_cons,
              σ_u, σ_e, ρ, r2_within, r2_between, r2_overall,
              corr_ux, Wald, Wald_p, F_val, F_p,
              nobs=n, N_panels=N, T_min, T_avg, T_max,
              coefnames=string.(all_regs))
end

"""
    estat_hettest(model, df; vars=nothing, iid=false, mtest=false)

Breusch-Pagan / Cook-Weisberg test for heteroskedasticity.
Replicates Stata's `estat hettest [varlist] [, iid] [, mtest]`.

- No `vars`:  test against fitted values (1 df).
- With `vars`: test against the listed variables (joint + individual).
- `iid=true`:  uses the NR^2 form (regress u^2/s^2 on z; LM = n*R^2)
               matching Stata's `estat hettest, iid`.
- `iid=false` (default): uses the score form (Koenker, 1981) which is
               robust to non-normal errors — this is Stata's default.
- `mtest=true`: also reports Bonferroni and Sidak adjusted p-values
               for each variable individually.
"""
function estat_hettest(model, df;
                       vars::Union{Nothing, Vector{Symbol}} = nothing,
                       iid::Bool = false,
                       mtest::Bool = false)
    beta = StatsBase.coef(model)
    nm   = StatsBase.coefnames(model)
    n    = Int(StatsBase.nobs(model))

    # Get fitted values and residuals
    # Build yhat from coefficients and data
    yhat = zeros(n)
    uhat = zeros(n)

    # Use dropmissing on needed columns
    needed = Symbol[]
    for name in nm
        name == "(Intercept)" && continue
        s = Symbol(strip(split(name, ":")[1]))
        hasproperty(df, s) && push!(needed, s)
    end
    resp = Symbol(split(string(FixedEffectModels.formula(model).lhs), "(")[end] |>
                  s -> replace(s, ")" => ""))
    push!(needed, resp)
    unique!(needed)
    dfc = dropmissing(df, needed)

    # Predict using FixedEffectModels
    yhat = FixedEffectModels.predict(model, dfc)
    uhat = Float64.(dfc[!, resp]) .- yhat

    sigma2 = sum(uhat.^2) / n     # MLE variance (divide by n)

    if vars === nothing
        z = hcat(ones(n), yhat)
        varnames = ["Fitted values"]
        q = 1
    else
        z = hcat(ones(n), [Float64.([_rawval(x) for x in dfc[!, v]]) for v in vars]...)
        varnames = string.(vars)
        q = length(vars)
    end

    # Helper: compute BP chi2 for given dep variable and regressors
    function _bp_chi2(dep, z, is_iid)
        b = z \ dep
        fitted = z * b
        if is_iid
            # Koenker (iid) form: n * R^2 of e^2 on z
            ss_tot = sum((dep .- mean(dep)).^2)
            ss_res = sum((dep .- fitted).^2)
            return ss_tot > 0 ? n * (1 - ss_res / ss_tot) : 0.0
        else
            # Normal (default) form: h = e^2/sigma^2, regress h on z, chi2 = ESS/2
            # Equivalently: ESS of e^2 on z, divided by 2*sigma^4
            ess = sum((fitted .- mean(dep)).^2)
            return ess / (2 * sigma2^2)
        end
    end

    chi2 = _bp_chi2(uhat.^2, z, iid)
    pval = 1 - cdf(Chisq(q), chi2)

    # Print Stata-style output
    form = iid ? "i.i.d." : "Normal"
    if mtest && vars !== nothing && length(vars) > 1
        # mtest format: individual + simultaneous table
        println("\nBreusch-Pagan / Cook-Weisberg test for heteroskedasticity")
        @printf("Assumption: %s error terms\n", form)
        println("H0: Constant variance\n")
        println("-"^38)
        @printf("    Variable |      chi2   df        p\n")
        println("-"^13, "+", "-"^24)

        dep_sq = uhat.^2
        for v in vars
            zj = hcat(ones(n), Float64.([_rawval(x) for x in dfc[!, v]]))
            chi2_j = _bp_chi2(dep_sq, zj, iid)
            p_j = 1 - cdf(Chisq(1), chi2_j)
            @printf("%12s | %9.2f    1   %.4f*\n", v, chi2_j, p_j)
        end
        println("-"^13, "+", "-"^24)
        @printf("Simultaneous | %9.2f    %d   %.4f\n", chi2, q, pval)
        println("-"^38)
        println("* Unadjusted p-values")
    else
        # Standard format: single test
        var_label = vars === nothing ? "Fitted values of $(resp)" : join(varnames, ", ")
        println("\nBreusch-Pagan / Cook-Weisberg test for heteroskedasticity")
        @printf("Assumption: %s error terms\n", form)
        println("Variable: $(var_label)")
        println("\nH0: Constant variance\n")
        @printf("    chi2(%d) = %6.2f\n", q, chi2)
        @printf("Prob > chi2 = %.4f\n", pval)
    end

    return (; chi2, df = q, p = pval)
end

"""
    xtgls_stata(df, y, xvars; panelvar, timevar,
                panels=:correlated, corr=:psar1, level=0.95)

Stata-equivalent `xtgls` for balanced panels. Replicates

    xtset <panelvar> <timevar>
    xtgls <y> <xvars>, panels(<panels>) corr(<corr>)

Supported options:
- `panels`:  `:iid`, `:heteroskedastic`, `:correlated` (default)
- `corr`:    `:independent`, `:ar1`, `:psar1` (default)

Prints a Stata-style header + coefficient table and returns a NamedTuple with
fields `β`, `V`, `se`, `z`, `p`, `coefnames`, `n_obs`, `N_panels`, `T_periods`,
`ρ`, `Wald`, `Wald_p`, `panels`, `corr`, `y_name`.
"""
function xtgls_stata(df, y, xvars; panelvar::Symbol, timevar::Symbol,
                    panels::Symbol=:correlated, corr::Symbol=:psar1,
                    level::Float64=0.95)
    ys  = Symbol(y)
    xsv = [Symbol(v) for v in xvars]
    dfs = sort(df, [panelvar, timevar])
    pan = sort(unique(dfs[!, panelvar]))
    N   = length(pan)
    n   = DataFrames.nrow(dfs)
    T   = n ÷ N

    yv = Float64.(dfs[!, ys])
    X  = hcat(ones(n), hcat([Float64.(dfs[!, v]) for v in xsv]...))
    k  = size(X, 2)
    pidx = [((i-1)*T+1):(i*T) for i in 1:N]

    # Step 1: OLS residuals → AR(1) coefficients (Stata's rhotype=regress).
    # Note: do NOT clamp ρ — Stata allows |ρ| ≥ 1 (explosive AR1). The PW
    # first-obs weight below uses max(1-ρ², 0) which handles |ρ| ≥ 1 by
    # zeroing that panel's first observation.
    e0 = yv - X * (X \ yv)
    ρ = if corr == :independent
        zeros(N)
    elseif corr == :ar1
        num = sum(LinearAlgebra.dot(e0[pidx[i]][2:end], e0[pidx[i]][1:end-1]) for i in 1:N)
        den = sum(LinearAlgebra.dot(e0[pidx[i]][1:end-1], e0[pidx[i]][1:end-1]) for i in 1:N)
        fill(num/den, N)
    elseif corr == :psar1
        [LinearAlgebra.dot(e0[pidx[i]][2:end], e0[pidx[i]][1:end-1]) /
         LinearAlgebra.dot(e0[pidx[i]][1:end-1], e0[pidx[i]][1:end-1])
         for i in 1:N]
    else
        error("Unknown corr: $corr (use :independent, :ar1, or :psar1)")
    end

    # Step 2: Prais-Winsten transformation (if any ρ ≠ 0)
    ỹ = copy(yv); X̃ = copy(X)
    if any(!=(0.0), ρ)
        for i in 1:N
            idx = pidx[i]
            s = sqrt(max(1 - ρ[i]^2, 0.0))    # = 0 when |ρ| ≥ 1
            ỹ[idx[1]]    = s * yv[idx[1]]
            X̃[idx[1], :] = s * X[idx[1], :]
            for t in 2:T
                ỹ[idx[t]]    = yv[idx[t]] - ρ[i] * yv[idx[t-1]]
                X̃[idx[t], :] = X[idx[t], :] - ρ[i] * X[idx[t-1], :]
            end
        end
    end

    # Step 3: Cross-sectional/heteroskedastic covariance from residuals of an
    # OLS fit on the PW-transformed data.  Stata drops the first observation
    # per panel from Σ̂ when corr is ar1/psar1 (to remove the PW first-obs
    # heteroskedasticity), and uses divisor = number of obs used in Σ̂.
    e_pw      = ỹ - X̃ * (X̃ \ ỹ)
    drop_first = corr != :independent
    idx_Σ(i) = drop_first ? pidx[i][2:end] : pidx[i]
    T_eff = drop_first ? (T - 1) : T          # effective T used in Σ̂ estimation
    Σ_inv = if panels == :iid
        denom = drop_first ? (n - N - k) : (n - k)
        σ2    = sum(abs2, vcat([e_pw[idx_Σ(i)] for i in 1:N]...)) / max(denom, 1)
        Matrix{Float64}(LinearAlgebra.I(N) ./ σ2)
    elseif panels == :heteroskedastic
        σ2_i = [sum(abs2, e_pw[idx_Σ(i)]) / T_eff for i in 1:N]
        Matrix{Float64}(LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(1.0 ./ σ2_i))
    elseif panels == :correlated
        E = hcat([e_pw[idx_Σ(i)] for i in 1:N]...)    # T_eff × N
        # Stata uses divisor = T_eff (number of rows in E), not T.
        LinearAlgebra.inv(E' * E / T_eff)
    else
        error("Unknown panels: $panels (use :iid, :heteroskedastic, :correlated)")
    end

    # Step 4: GLS with Ω⁻¹ = Σ⁻¹ ⊗ I_T, applied time-period-by-time-period
    XΩX = zeros(k, k); XΩy = zeros(k)
    for t in 1:T
        rows = [(i-1)*T + t for i in 1:N]
        Xt = X̃[rows, :]; yt = ỹ[rows]
        XΩX += Xt' * Σ_inv * Xt
        XΩy += Xt' * Σ_inv * yt
    end
    β  = XΩX \ XΩy
    V  = LinearAlgebra.inv(XΩX)
    se = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))
    z  = β ./ se
    pv = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z)))
    zcrit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    ci_lo = β .- zcrit .* se
    ci_hi = β .+ zcrit .* se

    Wald   = β[2:end]' * LinearAlgebra.inv(V[2:end, 2:end]) * β[2:end]
    Wald_p = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(k-1), Wald)

    ncov = panels == :iid             ? 1        :
           panels == :heteroskedastic ? N        :
                                        N*(N+1) ÷ 2
    nρ   = corr == :independent ? 0 :
           corr == :ar1         ? 1 :
                                  N         # psar1

    coefnames = ["(Intercept)"; string.(xsv)]

    # Stata %9.0g-style formatter (strip leading 0 for |x|<1)
    function _g(x, w; sig=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, ".")
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        if 0 < abs(x) < 1
            s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
        return Printf.@sprintf("%*s", w, s)
    end

    panel_desc = panels == :iid             ? "homoskedastic" :
                 panels == :heteroskedastic ? "heteroskedastic" :
                                              "heteroskedastic with cross-sectional correlation"
    corr_desc  = corr == :independent ? "no autocorrelation" :
                 corr == :ar1         ? "common AR(1)"       :
                                        "panel-specific AR(1)"

    println()
    println("Cross-sectional time-series FGLS regression")
    println()
    println("Coefficients:  generalized least squares")
    println("Panels:        ", panel_desc)
    println("Correlation:   ", corr_desc)
    println()
    Printf.@printf("Estimated covariances      = %9d          Number of obs     = %10d\n",
                   ncov, n)
    Printf.@printf("Estimated autocorrelations = %9d          Number of groups  = %10d\n",
                   nρ, N)
    Printf.@printf("Estimated coefficients     = %9d          Time periods      = %10d\n",
                   k, T)
    wlabel = Printf.@sprintf("Wald chi2(%d)", k-1)
    Printf.@printf("%s%-18s= %10.2f\n", " "^48, wlabel, Wald)
    Printf.@printf("%sProb > chi2       = %10.4f\n", " "^48, Wald_p)
    println()

    println("-"^78)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s  %9s  %6s  %5s     [%d%% conf. interval]\n",
                   string(ys), "Coefficient", "Std. err.", "z", "P>|z|",
                   round(Int, 100*level))
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    for j in 2:k
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %6.2f  %5.3f  %s  %s\n",
                       string(xsv[j-1]), _g(β[j],10), _g(se[j],9),
                       z[j], pv[j], _g(ci_lo[j],11), _g(ci_hi[j],11))
    end
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %6.2f  %5.3f  %s  %s\n",
                   "_cons", _g(β[1],10), _g(se[1],9),
                   z[1], pv[1], _g(ci_lo[1],11), _g(ci_hi[1],11))
    println("-"^78)

    return (β=β, V=V, se=se, z=z, p=pv, coefnames=coefnames,
            n_obs=n, N_panels=N, T_periods=T, ρ=ρ,
            Wald=Wald, Wald_p=Wald_p, panels=panels, corr=corr, y_name=ys)
end

"""
    poisson_stata(df, formula; vce=:robust)

Stata's `poisson y x1 x2 ..., vce(robust)` — fits Poisson GLM with log link
and prints output in Stata format including:
  - Number of obs, Wald chi², Prob > chi²
  - Log pseudolikelihood, Pseudo R²
  - Coefficient table with robust SEs (HC1 sandwich)

Returns the fitted GLM model.
"""

"""
    estat_ic_stata(fit; model_name=".")

Stata-style `estat ic` — Akaike and Bayesian information criteria.
`fit` is a NamedTuple with fields `n`, `k`, `ll`, `pseudo_r2` (any chunk-1
fitter return value works: `poisson_stata`, future glm/nbreg/probit
fitters, etc.).

  - `ll(null)` is recovered from `ll / (1 − pseudo_r2)`.
  - `df` reported is `k`, the number of estimated parameters (slopes + cons).
  - AIC = −2·ll + 2·k                 (Stata's `e(aic)`)
  - BIC = −2·ll + k·log(n)           (Stata's `e(bic)`)

Reproduces Stata's wide-table layout with a dash-pipe separator.
"""
function estat_ic_stata(fit; model_name::String=".")
    n  = fit.n
    k  = fit.k
    ll = fit.ll
    ll_null = ll / (1 - fit.pseudo_r2)
    aic = -2*ll + 2*k
    bic = -2*ll + k*log(n)

    commafmt(num::Integer) = begin
        s = string(abs(num)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1; push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3; end
        (num < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    println("\nAkaike's information criterion and Bayesian information criterion\n")
    println("-"^78)
    println("       Model |          N   ll(null)  ll(model)      df        AIC        BIC")
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %10s %10.6g %10.7g %7d %10.6g %10.7g\n",
                   model_name, commafmt(n), ll_null, ll, k, aic, bic)
    println("-"^78)
    return (; n, k, ll, ll_null, aic, bic)
end

function poisson_stata(df, formula; vce::Symbol=:robust, level::Float64=0.95,
                       quiet::Bool=false,
                       layout::Union{Nothing,AbstractVector}=nothing,
                       header_style::Symbol=:poisson)
    # Drop missing for variables in formula
    needed = StatsModels.termvars(formula)
    dfc = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, needed])
    for c in needed
        col = dfc[!, c]
        if eltype(col) <: Union{Missing, Float32} || eltype(col) === Float32
            dfc[!, c] = Float64.(col)
        end
    end

    # Fit Poisson GLM
    m = GLM.glm(formula, dfc, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink())
    β  = GLM.coef(m)
    cn = GLM.coefnames(m)
    n  = Int(StatsBase.nobs(m))
    yv = Float64.(GLM.response(m))
    μ  = GLM.predict(m)
    Xm = GLM.modelmatrix(m)
    k  = size(Xm, 2)

    # SE: robust sandwich by default. Stata's `poisson, vce(robust)` applies
    # an n/(n-1) finite-sample correction (NOT n/(n-k) as for `regress`).
    # This is the same multiplier Stata documents under [R] _robust.
    if vce == :robust
        H = Xm' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(μ) * Xm
        meat = Xm' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal((yv .- μ).^2) * Xm
        Hinv = LinearAlgebra.inv(LinearAlgebra.cholesky(LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(H)))
        V = Hinv * meat * Hinv * (n / (n - 1))
    else
        V = Matrix(GLM.vcov(m))
    end
    se = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))
    z  = β ./ se
    p  = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z)))
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    ci_lo = β .- crit .* se
    ci_hi = β .+ crit .* se

    # Wald chi² on slopes (excluding intercept)
    intercept_idx = findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), cn)
    slope_idx = setdiff(1:length(β), [intercept_idx])
    Wald = NaN; Wald_p = NaN
    if !isempty(slope_idx)
        β_slope = β[slope_idx]
        V_slope = V[slope_idx, slope_idx]
        Wald = β_slope' * LinearAlgebra.inv(V_slope) * β_slope
        Wald_p = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(length(slope_idx)), Wald)
    end

    # Log pseudolikelihood and Pseudo R² (McFadden's-like: 1 - LL/LL_null)
    ll  = GLM.loglikelihood(m)
    # Null model: intercept only
    m_null = GLM.glm(StatsModels.term(Symbol(formula.lhs)) ~ StatsModels.term(1),
                     dfc, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink())
    ll_null = GLM.loglikelihood(m_null)
    pseudo_r2 = 1 - ll / ll_null

    # Stata %9.0g formatter — sign-aware width cap (cap=9 except cap=10 for
    # negative |x|<1, where leading-zero strip saves a char). Without this
    # asymmetry, negative |x|<1 values would lose a digit vs Stata's output.
    function g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
        sig_use = sig
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 10 : 9
        while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
            sig_use -= 1
            s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        end
        if 0 < abs(x) < 1
            s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
        return lpad(s, w)
    end
    function commafmt(num)
        s = string(abs(num)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1
            push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3
        end
        return (num < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    # Skip the entire Stata-style printout when quiet=true (mirrors Stata's
    # `quietly poisson ...`). Useful for chapter chunks that just need β/V/X
    # for downstream margins/dydx/etc. and don't want to repeat the table.
    if !quiet
    # Print Stata-style header. Two layouts:
    #   :poisson — Stata's `poisson` command output. Top-left says "Poisson
    #              regression", and the right column lists Number of obs /
    #              Wald chi2(k) / Prob > chi2 / Pseudo R2 over four lines,
    #              with "Log pseudolikelihood = …" on the last line at left.
    #   :ml      — Stata's `ml maximize` output. No "Poisson regression"
    #              header; the right column lists Number of obs / Wald
    #              chi2(k) / Prob > chi2 over three lines, with "Log
    #              [pseudo]likelihood = …" on the third line at left, and
    #              no Pseudo R2 row.
    println()
    if header_style == :ml
        # Stata `ml maximize` right-block format: 13-char left-padded label,
        # " = ", then a value field whose width is the max of the three
        # value strings plus 1 (so the longest value sits 1 space after `=`,
        # shorter ones are right-aligned). The whole right block is positioned
        # flush right at col 78 by adjusting the leading whitespace.
        nstr = commafmt(n)
        wstr = isfinite(Wald) ? Printf.@sprintf("%.2f", Wald) : ""
        pstr = isfinite(Wald) ? Printf.@sprintf("%.4f", Wald_p) : ""
        vw   = maximum(length, (nstr, wstr, pstr))
        rblock = 13 + 3 + vw
        lead   = max(0, 78 - rblock)
        ws     = " " ^ lead
        println(ws, rpad("Number of obs", 13), " = ", lpad(nstr, vw))
        if isfinite(Wald)
            println(ws, rpad("Wald chi2($(length(slope_idx)))", 13),
                    " = ", lpad(wstr, vw))
        end
        ll_label = vce == :robust ? "Log pseudolikelihood" : "Log likelihood"
        ll_str   = Printf.@sprintf("%s = %.3f", ll_label, ll)
        println(rpad(ll_str, lead), rpad("Prob > chi2", 13), " = ", lpad(pstr, vw))
    else
        Printf.@printf("%-56s%-13s = %6s\n",
                       "Poisson regression", "Number of obs", commafmt(n))
        if isfinite(Wald)
            Printf.@printf("%56s%-13s = %6.2f\n", "",
                           "Wald chi2($(length(slope_idx)))", Wald)
            Printf.@printf("%56s%-13s = %6.4f\n", "", "Prob > chi2", Wald_p)
        end
        Printf.@printf("Log pseudolikelihood = %.3f%23s%-13s = %6.4f\n",
                       ll, "", "Pseudo R2", pseudo_r2)
    end
    println()

    # Coefficient table. Stata uses lowercase "std. err." under the "Robust"
    # subheader for `vce(robust)` and capital "Std. err." for the default OIM.
    println("-"^78)
    if vce == :robust
        println("             |               Robust")
    end
    se_label = vce == :robust ? "std. err." : "Std. err."
    Printf.@printf("%12s | Coefficient  %s      z    P>|z|     [95%% conf. interval]\n",
                   string(formula.lhs), se_label)
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)

    # `_print_row(label, idx)` renders a single row in Stata's standard
    # 7-column layout. `_lookup(key)` finds the GLM-order index from a
    # coefname or returns the index directly.
    _print_row(label, idx) = Printf.@printf(
        "%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
        label, g9(β[idx]; w=10), g9(se[idx]; w=9),
        Printf.@sprintf("%7.2f", z[idx]),
        Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p[idx]),
        g9(ci_lo[idx]; w=9), g9(ci_hi[idx]; w=10))
    _lookup(key) = key isa Integer ? Int(key) :
        (let i = findfirst(==(string(key)), cn)
            i === nothing && error("'$(key)' not found in coefnames=$(cn)")
            i
        end)

    if layout === nothing
        # Default layout: slopes (in formula order) first, then _cons.
        for i in slope_idx
            _print_row(cn[i], i)
        end
        intercept_idx !== nothing && _print_row("_cons", intercept_idx)
    else
        # Custom layout. Each entry is one of:
        #   :blank                       → empty separator row "             |"
        #   String  => key                → simple row, label = String
        #   Vector{String} => key         → multi-line interaction block: all
        #                                    but the last element are header
        #                                    lines (printed as `<line>|`),
        #                                    last element is the row label.
        # `key` can be a coefname (matched against the GLM-order coefnames)
        # or a 1-based integer index into β.
        for entry in layout
            if entry === :blank
                println("             |")
            elseif entry isa Pair
                lab, key = entry.first, entry.second
                # `label => :omitted` — Stata-style "(omitted)" row for a
                # variable that was dropped due to collinearity. Prints
                # "        label |          0  (omitted)" with no key lookup.
                if key === :omitted
                    Printf.@printf("%12s |          0  (omitted)\n", string(lab))
                    continue
                end
                idx = _lookup(key)
                if lab isa AbstractVector
                    for h in lab[1:end-1]
                        println(h, "|")
                    end
                    _print_row(string(lab[end]), idx)
                else
                    _print_row(string(lab), idx)
                end
            else
                error("Unsupported layout entry: $entry (use :blank or label=>key)")
            end
        end
    end
    println("-"^78)
    end  # if !quiet

    # Return values in *Stata's display order*: slopes first, then _cons.
    # That way the margins / nlcom / lincom / dydx helpers can be called
    # directly on these without any reordering, and the constant always
    # sits at the end of β (matching the convention used throughout the
    # downstream chapter chunks).
    if intercept_idx === nothing
        ord = collect(slope_idx)
        cn_ord = cn[ord]
    else
        ord = vcat(collect(slope_idx), [intercept_idx])
        cn_ord = vcat(cn[slope_idx], "_cons")
    end
    β_ord  = β[ord]
    se_ord = se[ord]
    V_ord  = V[ord, ord]
    X_ord  = Xm[:, ord]   # last column = ones (the constant)

    return (; model=m, df=dfc, formula,
              β=β_ord, se=se_ord, V=V_ord, X=X_ord,
              coefnames=cn_ord,
              Wald, Wald_p, ll, pseudo_r2, n, k,
              # Originals (GLM order) preserved for any code that needs them:
              β_glm=β, V_glm=V, X_glm=Xm, coefnames_glm=cn)
end

"""
    glm_stata(df, formula; family=:poisson, link=:log, vce=:robust)

Stata's `glm y x..., family(poisson) link(log) vce(robust)`. Prints the
GLM-format header (deviance, Pearson chi², AIC, BIC, variance/link function)
followed by a coefficient table with robust SEs.
"""
function glm_stata(df, formula; family::Symbol=:poisson, link::Symbol=:log,
                    vce::Symbol=:robust, level::Float64=0.95)
    needed = StatsModels.termvars(formula)
    dfc = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, needed])
    for c in needed
        col = dfc[!, c]
        if eltype(col) <: Union{Missing, Float32} || eltype(col) === Float32
            dfc[!, c] = Float64.(col)
        end
    end

    fam = family == :poisson  ? Distributions.Poisson() :
          family == :binomial ? Distributions.Binomial() :
          family == :gaussian ? Distributions.Normal()   :
          error("family=$family not supported")
    lnk = link == :log     ? GLM.LogLink()    :
          link == :identity ? GLM.IdentityLink() :
          link == :logit   ? GLM.LogitLink()  :
          link == :probit  ? GLM.ProbitLink() :
          error("link=$link not supported")

    m = GLM.glm(formula, dfc, fam, lnk)
    β  = GLM.coef(m)
    cn = GLM.coefnames(m)
    n  = Int(StatsBase.nobs(m))
    yv = Float64.(GLM.response(m))
    μ  = GLM.predict(m)
    Xm = GLM.modelmatrix(m)
    k  = size(Xm, 2)
    df_resid = n - k

    if vce == :robust && family == :poisson
        H = Xm' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(μ) * Xm
        meat = Xm' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal((yv .- μ).^2) * Xm
        Hinv = LinearAlgebra.inv(LinearAlgebra.cholesky(LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(H)))
        V = Hinv * meat * Hinv * (n / (n - k))
    else
        V = Matrix(GLM.vcov(m))
    end
    se = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))
    z  = β ./ se
    p  = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z)))
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    ci_lo = β .- crit .* se
    ci_hi = β .+ crit .* se

    deviance_val = GLM.deviance(m)
    pearson = family == :poisson ? sum((yv .- μ).^2 ./ μ) : NaN
    dev_per_df  = deviance_val / df_resid
    pear_per_df = pearson / df_resid

    ll  = GLM.loglikelihood(m)
    aic = (-2 * ll + 2 * k) / n
    bic = deviance_val - df_resid * log(n)

    var_desc  = family == :poisson ? "V(u) = u" :
                family == :binomial ? "V(u) = u·(1-u/n)" : "V(u) = 1"
    link_desc = link == :log ? "g(u) = ln(u)" :
                link == :identity ? "g(u) = u" :
                link == :logit ? "g(u) = ln(u/(1-u))" :
                link == :probit ? "g(u) = invΦ(u)" : "g(u) = ?"
    fam_label = family == :poisson ? "[Poisson]" :
                family == :binomial ? "[Binomial]" : "[$family]"
    lnk_label = link == :log ? "[Log]" :
                link == :identity ? "[Identity]" :
                link == :logit ? "[Logit]" :
                link == :probit ? "[Probit]" : "[$link]"

    function g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig, x)
        if 0 < abs(x) < 1
            s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
        return lpad(s, w)
    end
    function commafmt(num)
        s = string(abs(num)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1; push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3; end
        return (num < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    println()
    Printf.@printf("%-50s%-15s = %10s\n", "Generalized linear models",
                   "Number of obs", commafmt(n))
    Printf.@printf("%-50s%-15s = %10d\n", "Optimization     : ML",
                   "Residual df", df_resid)
    Printf.@printf("%-50s%-15s = %10d\n", "", "Scale parameter", 1)
    Printf.@printf("%-15s = %12.5f                   %-15s = %10.5f\n",
                   "Deviance", deviance_val, "(1/df) Deviance", dev_per_df)
    Printf.@printf("%-15s = %12.5f                   %-15s = %10.5f\n",
                   "Pearson", pearson, "(1/df) Pearson", pear_per_df)
    println()
    Printf.@printf("%-50s%s\n", "Variance function: $(var_desc)", fam_label)
    Printf.@printf("%-50s%s\n", "Link function    : $(link_desc)", lnk_label)
    println()
    Printf.@printf("%50s%-15s = %10.6f\n", "", "AIC", aic)
    Printf.@printf("Log pseudolikelihood = %.5f%18s%-15s = %10.3f\n",
                   ll, "", "BIC", bic)
    println()

    println("-"^78)
    if vce == :robust
        println("             |               Robust")
    end
    Printf.@printf("%12s | Coefficient  std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95%% conf. interval]\n",
                   string(formula.lhs))
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    intercept_idx = findfirst(==("(Intercept)"), cn)
    slope_idx = setdiff(1:length(β), [intercept_idx])
    for i in slope_idx
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       cn[i], g9(β[i]; w=10), g9(se[i]; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z[i]),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p[i]),
                       g9(ci_lo[i]; w=10), g9(ci_hi[i]; w=10))
    end
    if intercept_idx !== nothing
        i = intercept_idx
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       "_cons", g9(β[i]; w=10), g9(se[i]; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z[i]),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p[i]),
                       g9(ci_lo[i]; w=10), g9(ci_hi[i]; w=10))
    end
    println("-"^78)

    return (; model=m, β, se, V, ll, deviance=deviance_val, pearson, aic, bic, n)
end

"""
    gmm_stata(df, depvar, regs, instruments; mean_fn=:exp, onestep=true,
              level=0.95)

Stata's `gmm (y - mean_fn(xβ + b0)), instruments(z1 z2 ...) onestep`.

Nonlinear-IV / GMM estimation of a model with conditional mean μ(x'β + b0):
moment condition is `E[z · (y - μ(x'β + b0))] = 0`.

Arguments:
- `depvar`     : dependent variable (Symbol)
- `regs`       : regressors that enter the linear index (Vector{Symbol})
- `instruments`: instrument variables (Vector{Symbol})
- `mean_fn`    : `:exp` (Poisson-mean) or `:identity`
- `onestep`    : true → identity W₁; false → two-step with optimal W₂

Just-identified case (#regs = #instruments) gives Q ≈ 0.
Returns a NamedTuple with β, se, V, etc., and prints Stata-style output.
"""
function gmm_stata(df, depvar::Symbol, regs::Vector{Symbol},
                   instruments::Vector{Symbol};
                   mean_fn::Symbol=:exp, onestep::Bool=true,
                   level::Float64=0.95)
    needed = unique(vcat([depvar], regs, instruments))
    dfc = DataFrames.dropmissing(df[:, needed])
    for c in needed
        col = dfc[!, c]
        if eltype(col) <: Union{Missing, Float32} || eltype(col) === Float32
            dfc[!, c] = Float64.(col)
        end
    end
    n = DataFrames.nrow(dfc)
    y  = Float64.(dfc[!, depvar])
    X  = hcat([Float64.(dfc[!, v]) for v in regs]...)         # n × k
    Z  = hcat([Float64.(dfc[!, v]) for v in instruments]...)  # n × m
    # Add constant to X (b0) and Z (_cons)
    X = hcat(X, ones(n))
    Z = hcat(Z, ones(n))
    k = size(X, 2)        # number of parameters (including b0)
    m_cnt = size(Z, 2)    # number of moment conditions

    # Mean function and its gradient w.r.t. β.
    # NOTE: use *anonymous* closures (`β -> ...`) bound to a local name. Named
    # short-form methods (`μ_fn(β) = ...`) inside an if/else inside a function
    # body get hoisted to the enclosing module's method table, so the inner
    # `X` reference resolves to whatever global `X` exists (from a previous
    # chunk run) — not the local `X` we just built. That made μ_fn(β_final)
    # after Newton return values consistent with a *different* design matrix,
    # which silently inflated SEs by 4–10× for some coefficients.
    μ_fn, ∂μ_fn = if mean_fn == :exp
        (β -> exp.(X * β)), (β -> exp.(X * β) .* X)
    elseif mean_fn == :identity
        (β -> X * β), (β -> X)
    else
        error("mean_fn=$mean_fn not supported")
    end

    # GMM objective: g(β)' W g(β) where g(β) = (1/n) Σ z_i (y_i - μ(x_i'β))
    function gmm_obj(β, W)
        u = y .- μ_fn(β)
        g = Z' * u ./ n
        return n * (g' * W * g)
    end

    # Step 1: identity weight matrix
    W1 = LinearAlgebra.I(m_cnt) |> Matrix

    # Just-identified case (k == m): solve the moment conditions directly via
    # Newton-Raphson. This is MUCH more reliable than minimizing the non-convex
    # GMM objective with Optim, which can land on spurious roots.
    if k == m_cnt && mean_fn == :exp
        # Newton: β_{k+1} = β_k + (Z' diag(μ) X)^{-1} · Z'(y - μ)
        β1 = zeros(k)
        # Warm start from Poisson MLE (using ALL regressors as instruments
        # of themselves — i.e., GLM Poisson regression).
        try
            df_init = DataFrames.DataFrame(_y = y)
            for (j, v) in enumerate(regs)
                df_init[!, v] = X[:, j]
            end
            fml_init = StatsModels.term(:_y) ~ sum(StatsModels.term.(regs))
            m_init = GLM.glm(fml_init, df_init,
                             Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink())
            β_init_glm = GLM.coef(m_init)
            β1 = vcat(β_init_glm[2:end], β_init_glm[1])
        catch
        end
        # Newton iteration on Z'(y - exp(Xβ)) = 0
        for iter in 1:200
            μ = exp.(X * β1)
            r = Z' * (y .- μ)            # m-vector of moment values
            # Jacobian: ∂r/∂β = -Z' diag(μ) X  (m × k)
            J = -(Z' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(μ) * X)
            # Update: solve J · Δ = -r, β_new = β_old + Δ
            Δ = try
                -(J \ r)
            catch
                -(LinearAlgebra.pinv(J) * r)
            end
            β1 = β1 + Δ
            if maximum(abs.(r)) < 1e-9 || maximum(abs.(Δ)) < 1e-12
                break
            end
        end
        # Compute final Q
        u1 = y .- μ_fn(β1)
        g1 = Z' * u1 ./ n
        Q1 = n * (g1' * W1 * g1)
    else
        # Overidentified: minimize via Optim, warm-started from Poisson MLE
        β_init = if mean_fn == :exp
            try
                df_init = DataFrames.DataFrame(_y = y)
                for (j, v) in enumerate(regs)
                    df_init[!, v] = X[:, j]
                end
                fml_init = StatsModels.term(:_y) ~ sum(StatsModels.term.(regs))
                m_init = GLM.glm(fml_init, df_init,
                                 Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink())
                β_init_glm = GLM.coef(m_init)
                vcat(β_init_glm[2:end], β_init_glm[1])
            catch
                zeros(k)
            end
        else
            zeros(k)
        end
        res1 = Optim.optimize(β -> gmm_obj(β, W1), β_init, Optim.LBFGS();
                                autodiff = :forward, inplace = false)
        β1 = Optim.minimizer(res1)
        Q1 = Optim.minimum(res1)
    end

    if onestep
        β_final = β1
        Q_final = Q1
    else
        # Step 2: optimal W = (1/n Σ z z' u²)⁻¹
        u1 = y .- μ_fn(β1)
        Ω1 = (Z' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(u1.^2) * Z) ./ n
        W2 = LinearAlgebra.inv(Ω1)
        res2 = Optim.optimize(β -> gmm_obj(β, W2), β1, Optim.BFGS();
                                autodiff = :forward, inplace = false)
        β_final = Optim.minimizer(res2)
        Q_final = Optim.minimum(res2)
    end

    # Robust sandwich VCE
    u_final = y .- μ_fn(β_final)
    μ_final = μ_fn(β_final)
    # D̂ = ∂M/∂β' = -Σ z_i μ_i x_i' = -Z'diag(μ)X (k×k in just-identified)
    Dhat = -(Z' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(μ_final) * X)
    # Ŝ = Σ z_i z_i' u_i² (uncentered moment outer-product)
    Shat = Z' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(u_final.^2) * Z
    if k == m_cnt
        # Just-identified: V = D̂⁻¹ Ŝ (D̂')⁻¹ directly. Avoid the squared-form
        # bread = inv(Gbar'Gbar) which doubles the condition number — when an
        # instrument is weak (e.g. firmsize → private), Gbar is ill-conditioned
        # and the squared form blows up some SEs while leaving others intact.
        # Use LU-based triangular solves (\, /) rather than forming inv(D̂).
        V = (Dhat \ Shat) / Dhat'
    elseif onestep
        # Overidentified one-step: V = (G'WG)⁻¹ G'W Ŝ WG (G'WG)⁻¹ (in sum form,
        # equivalent to Hansen's mean form with extra n cancelling).
        bread = LinearAlgebra.inv(Dhat' * Dhat)
        V = bread * (Dhat' * Shat * Dhat) * bread
    else
        # Overidentified two-step with optimal W = inv(Ŝ): V = (G' inv(Ŝ) G)⁻¹
        V = LinearAlgebra.inv(Dhat' * LinearAlgebra.inv(Shat) * Dhat)
    end
    V = (V .+ V') ./ 2  # symmetrize numerical noise
    se = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))
    z = β_final ./ se
    p = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z)))
    crit = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), 1 - (1-level)/2)
    ci_lo = β_final .- crit .* se
    ci_hi = β_final .+ crit .* se

    # Stata %9.0g formatter — sign-aware width cap (cap=9 except cap=10 for
    # negative |x|<1, where leading-zero strip saves a char). Without this
    # asymmetry, negative |x|<1 values would lose a digit vs Stata's output.
    function g9(x; w::Int=10, sig::Int=7)
        (ismissing(x) || !isfinite(x)) && return lpad(".", w)
        sig_use = sig
        s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        cap = (0 < abs(x) < 1 && x < 0) ? 10 : 9
        while length(s) > cap && sig_use > 1
            sig_use -= 1
            s = Printf.@sprintf("%.*g", sig_use, x)
        end
        if 0 < abs(x) < 1
            s = replace(s, r"^(-?)0\." => s"\1.")
        end
        return lpad(s, w)
    end
    function commafmt(num)
        s = string(abs(num)); parts = String[]; i = length(s)
        while i >= 1; push!(parts, s[max(1, i-2):i]); i -= 3; end
        return (num < 0 ? "-" : "") * join(reverse(parts), ",")
    end

    # Print Stata-style output
    println()
    Printf.@printf("Final GMM criterion Q(b) = %.2e\n", Q_final / n)
    println()
    if k == m_cnt
        println("note: model is exactly identified.")
        println()
    end
    println("GMM estimation ")
    println()
    Printf.@printf("Number of parameters = %3d\n", k)
    Printf.@printf("Number of moments    = %3d\n", m_cnt)
    Printf.@printf("Initial weight matrix: Unadjusted%17sNumber of obs   = %10s\n",
                   "", commafmt(n))
    println()
    println("-"^78)
    println("             |               Robust")
    println("             | Coefficient  std. err.      z    P>|z|     [95% conf. interval]")
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    # Print regressors first, then b0 (last)
    for i in 1:(k-1)
        Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                       string(regs[i]),
                       g9(β_final[i]; w=10), g9(se[i]; w=9),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z[i]),
                       Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p[i]),
                       g9(ci_lo[i]; w=10), g9(ci_hi[i]; w=10))
    end
    println("-"^13, "+", "-"^64)
    Printf.@printf("%12s | %s  %s  %s   %s    %s  %s\n",
                   "/b0",
                   g9(β_final[end]; w=10), g9(se[end]; w=9),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%6.2f", z[end]),
                   Printf.@sprintf("%.3f", p[end]),
                   g9(ci_lo[end]; w=10), g9(ci_hi[end]; w=10))
    println("-"^78)
    println("Instruments for equation 1: ", join(instruments, " "), " _cons")

    return (; β=β_final, se, V, Q=Q_final, n, k, m=m_cnt,
              coefnames=vcat(string.(regs), "/b0"))
end

"""
    _coef_se(model, var::String)

Helper: extract (coef, se) for a named coefficient from a FixedEffectModels /
GLM / similar model. Returns (NaN, NaN) if not found.
"""
function _coef_se(model, var::String)
    cn = StatsBase.coefnames(model)
    i = findfirst(==(var), cn)
    isnothing(i) && return (NaN, NaN)
    return (StatsBase.coef(model)[i], sqrt(StatsBase.vcov(model)[i, i]))
end

"""
    _coef_se_re(res, var::Symbol)

Helper: extract (coef, se) for a named coefficient from an `xtreg_re` result.
The variable name is matched in `res.coefnames` (strings); pass `:_cons` for
the intercept. Returns (NaN, NaN) if not found.
"""
function _coef_se_re(res, var::Symbol)
    target = var == :_cons ? "_cons" : string(var)
    i = findfirst(==(target), res.coefnames)
    isnothing(i) && return (NaN, NaN)
    return (res.β[i], res.se[i])
end
```

## 1.3 Command syntax and operators

### 1.3.1 Basic command syntax

### 1.3.2 Example: The summarize command

```{julia}
auto = DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("auto.dta"))
typeof(auto)
```

```{julia}
# summarize
summarize_stata(auto)
```

```{julia}
# summarize mpg price weight
summarize_stata(auto, [:mpg, :price, :weight])
```

```{julia}
# summarize price, detail
summarize_stata(auto, :price; detail=true)
```

### 1.3.3 Example: The regress command

```{julia}
# regress mpg price weight
model1 = FixedEffectModels.reg(auto, @formula(mpg ~ price + weight))
println(model1)
```

```{julia}
# by foreign: regress mpg price weight if weight < 4000, vce(robust)
for g in groupby(filter(r -> r.weight < 4000, auto), :foreign)
    println("\nforeign = $(first(g.foreign))")
    m = FixedEffectModels.reg(DataFrame(g), @formula(mpg ~ price + weight))
    println(m)
end
```

```{julia}
# quietly regress mpg price weight
model_quiet = FixedEffectModels.reg(auto, @formula(mpg ~ price + weight));
#nothing  # suppress output
```

```{julia}
# Factor variable for rep78 - base category is omitted
auto.rep78_cat = CategoricalArrays.categorical(auto.rep78)
println("Summary of rep78 indicator levels:")
println(combine(groupby(auto, :rep78_cat), nrow => :count))
```

```{julia}
# summarize i.rep78  -- expand rep78 into 0/1 dummies (drop missings), summarize each
vals = [_rawval(v) for v in skipmissing(auto.rep78)]
levs = sort(unique(vals))
dummies = DataFrame((Symbol("$(Int(l)).rep78") => Int.(vals .== l) for l in levs)...)
summarize_stata(dummies)
```

```{julia}
# Factor variable for rep78 - no category is omitted (all dummies)
println("Counts for each level of rep78:")
for lev in sort(unique(auto.rep78))
    n = sum(auto.rep78 .== lev)
    println("  rep78 == $lev : N = $n, Mean = $(round(mean(auto.rep78 .== lev), digits=4))")
end
```

```{julia}
# summarize i.rep78#i.foreign, allbaselevels
# Drop rows where either factor is missing, then build a 0/1 dummy for every (rep78, foreign) cell
sub   = dropmissing(auto[:, [:rep78, :foreign]])
rep78v  = [_rawval(v) for v in sub.rep78]
foreignv = [_rawval(v) for v in sub.foreign]
rlevs = sort(unique(rep78v))
flevs = sort(unique(foreignv))
dummies = DataFrame((Symbol("$(Int(r)).rep78#$(Int(f)).foreign") =>
                     Int.((rep78v .== r) .& (foreignv .== f))
                     for r in rlevs, f in flevs)...)
summarize_stata(dummies)
```

```{julia}
# summarize i.rep78#c.weight
# For each level r of rep78: weight when rep78==r, else 0 (drop rows where rep78 is missing)
sub = dropmissing(auto[:, [:rep78, :weight]])
rep78v = [_rawval(v) for v in sub.rep78]
wv     = Float64.(sub.weight)
rlevs  = sort(unique(rep78v))
inter = DataFrame((Symbol("$(Int(r)).rep78#c.weight") =>
                   ifelse.(rep78v .== r, wv, 0.0) for r in rlevs)...)
summarize_stata(inter)
```

```{julia}
# Factor variables for interaction between two continuous variables
# regress mpg price c.weight c.weight#c.weight
# NOTE: auto.dta stores weight as Int16 — squaring Int16 overflows silently in Julia,
# so we cast to Float64 first to match Stata's numeric precision.
auto.weight_sq = Float64.(auto.weight) .^ 2
model_sq = FixedEffectModels.reg(auto, @formula(mpg ~ price + weight + weight_sq))
println(model_sq)
```

```{julia}
# regress mpg price c.weight c.weight#c.weight  (inline — no intermediate column)
square(x) = Float64(x)^2   # cast to Float64 to avoid Int16 overflow
model_sq_inline = FixedEffectModels.reg(auto, @formula(mpg ~ price + weight + square(weight)))
println(model_sq_inline)
```

### 1.3.4 Abbreviations, case sensitivity, and wildcards

```{julia}
# summarize t*     (variables starting with t)
summarize_stata(auto, "t*")
```

```{julia}
# summarize *e     (variables ending with e)
summarize_stata(auto, "*e")
```

```{julia}
# summarize price-weight    (range of columns, by column order)
summarize_stata(auto, "price-weight")
```

```{julia}

println(-2 * (9 / (8 + 2 - 7))^2)
```

```{julia}
# display 2/0
println(2 / 0)  # Julia returns Inf
```

```{julia}
# regress mpg price weight if weight <= 4000 & price <= 10000
sub = filter(r -> r.weight <= 4000 && r.price <= 10000, auto)
model_sub = FixedEffectModels.reg(sub, @formula(mpg ~ price + weight))
println(model_sub)
```

### 1.4.4 A three-step process

1. Create or change the do-file.
2. Execute the do-file in Stata.
3. Read the resulting log with a text editor.

## 1.6 Using results from Stata commands

### 1.6.1 Using results from the r-class command summarize

```{julia}
# Illustrate use of summary statistics for mpg
println("Summary of mpg:")
println("  N     = ", length(auto.mpg))
println("  Mean  = ", round(mean(auto.mpg), digits=4))
println("  Std   = ", round(std(auto.mpg), digits=4))
println("  Min   = ", minimum(auto.mpg))
println("  Max   = ", maximum(auto.mpg))
```

```{julia}
summarize_stata(auto, :mpg)
```

```{julia}
# return list equivalent - stored results
r_mean = mean(auto.mpg)
r_sd   = std(auto.mpg)
r_min  = minimum(auto.mpg)
r_max  = maximum(auto.mpg)
r_N    = length(auto.mpg)
r_sum  = sum(auto.mpg)
println("r(N)    = ", r_N)
println("r(mean) = ", r_mean)
println("r(Var) = ", r_sd^2)
println("r(sd)   = ", r_sd)
println("r(min)  = ", r_min)
println("r(max)  = ", r_max)
println("r(sum)  = ", r_sum)
```

```{julia}
# Illustrate use of r()
range_mpg = r_max - r_min
println("Sample range = ", range_mpg)
```

```{julia}
# Save a result as a scalar
mpgmean = r_mean
```

### 1.6.2 Using results from the e-class command regress

```{julia}
# regress mpg price weight
model_ols = FixedEffectModels.reg(auto, @formula(mpg ~ price + weight))
println(model_ols)
```

```{julia}
# ereturn list
ereturn_list(model_ols);
```

```{julia}
# ereturn list after regress
println("Number of obs = ", StatsBase.nobs(model_ols))
println("R-squared     = ", round(StatsBase.r2(model_ols), digits=6))
println("Adj R-squared = ", round(StatsBase.adjr2(model_ols), digits=6))
println("Coefficients  = ", StatsBase.coef(model_ols))
println("Std errors    = ", StatsBase.stderror(model_ols))
```

```{julia}
# Use of e() where scalar -- compute R-squared manually
tss = sum((auto.mpg .- mean(auto.mpg)).^2)
rss = GLM.deviance(model_ols)
r2_manual = 1 - rss / tss
println("r-squared = ", round(r2_manual, digits=6))
```

```{julia}
# Use of e() where matrix
best   = StatsBase.coef(model_ols)
bprice = best[2]  # coefficient on price
Vest   = StatsBase.vcov(model_ols)
Vprice = Vest[2, 2]
tprice = bprice / sqrt(Vprice)
println("t statistic for H0: b_price = 0 is ", round(tprice, digits=4))
```

## 1.7 Global and local macros

### 1.7.1 Global macros

```{julia}
# Global macro definition and use
xlist = [:price, :weight]
f = Term(:mpg) ~ sum(Term.(xlist))
model_g = FixedEffectModels.reg(auto, f)
println(model_g)
```

### 1.7.2 local macros

```{julia}
# Local macro definition and use
xlist = [:price, :weight]
f = Term(:mpg) ~ sum(Term.(xlist))
model_l = FixedEffectModels.reg(auto, f)
println(model_l)
```

```{julia}
# Local macro definition without double quotes
y = :mpg
f = Term(y) ~ sum(Term.(xlist))
model_l2 = FixedEffectModels.reg(auto, f)
println(model_l2)
```

```{julia}
# Local macro definition through function evaluation
z = 2 + 2
println(z)
```

### 1.7.3 Scalar or macro?

```{julia}
# In Julia, variables persist until they go out of scope
b = 3
c = 4
d = 5
println(b, "   ", c)  # display variables
```

```{julia}
# display the scalar
println(d)
```

```{julia}
# After clearing workspace, variables are gone
# In Julia, variables remain in scope unless explicitly removed
println(b, "   ", c)
```

```{julia}
# display the scalar
# println(d)  # would still work in Julia
```

## 1.8 Looping commands

```{julia}
# Make artificial dataset of 100 observations on 4 uniform variables
Random.seed!(10101)
df_loop = DataFrame(
    x1var = Random.rand(100),
    x2var = Random.rand(100),
    x3var = Random.rand(100),
    x4var = Random.rand(100)
)
```

```{julia}
# Manually obtain the sum of four variables
df_loop.sum_var = df_loop.x1var .+ df_loop.x2var .+ df_loop.x3var .+ df_loop.x4var
println("Summary of sum:")
println("  N    = ", length(df_loop.sum_var))
println("  Mean = ", round(mean(df_loop.sum_var), digits=4))
println("  Std  = ", round(std(df_loop.sum_var), digits=4))
println("  Min  = ", round(minimum(df_loop.sum_var), digits=4))
println("  Max  = ", round(maximum(df_loop.sum_var), digits=4))
```

### 1.8.1 The foreach loop

```{julia}
# foreach loop with a variable list
df_loop.sum_var .= 0.0
for var in [:x1var, :x2var, :x3var, :x4var]
    df_loop.sum_var .+= df_loop[!, var]
end
println("Summary of sum (foreach):")
println("  Mean = ", round(mean(df_loop.sum_var), digits=4))
println("  Std  = ", round(std(df_loop.sum_var), digits=4))
```

### 1.8.2 The forvalues loop

```{julia}
# forvalues loop to create a sum of variables
df_loop.sum_var .= 0.0
for i in 1:4
    df_loop.sum_var .+= df_loop[!, Symbol("x$(i)var")]
end
println("Summary of sum (forvalues):")
println("  Mean = ", round(mean(df_loop.sum_var), digits=4))
println("  Std  = ", round(std(df_loop.sum_var), digits=4))
```

```{julia}
# foreach loop with a number list
df_loop.sum_var .= 0.0
for i in [1, 2, 3, 4]
    df_loop.sum_var .+= df_loop[!, Symbol("x$(i)var")]
end
println("Summary of sum (numlist):")
println("  Mean = ", round(mean(df_loop.sum_var), digits=4))
```

### 1.8.3 The while loop

```{julia}
# While loop and local variables to create a sum of variables
df_loop.sum_var .= 0.0
i = 1
while i <= 4
    df_loop.sum_var .+= df_loop[!, Symbol("x$(i)var")]
    i += 1
end
println("Summary of sum (while):")
println("  Mean = ", round(mean(df_loop.sum_var), digits=4))
println("  Std  = ", round(std(df_loop.sum_var), digits=4))
```

### 1.8.4 The continue command

## 1.10 Template do-file

```{julia}
# mus01p2template.jl is a template script
# Julia equivalent of Stata template do-file

# Program parameters
numobs = 100
seed   = 10101

# Generate data and summarize
Random.seed!(seed)
df_template = DataFrame(xvar = Random.rand(numobs))
df_template.yvar = df_template.xvar .^ 2
println("Summary:")
println(DataFrames.describe(df_template))

# Demonstrate use of stored results
xmean = mean(df_template.xvar)
xmin  = minimum(df_template.xvar)
xmax  = maximum(df_template.xvar)
println("\nSample range = ", xmax - xmin)

# Regression
model_template = FixedEffectModels.reg(df_template, @formula(yvar ~ xvar))
r2_val = StatsBase.r2(model_template)
println("r-squared = ", round(r2_val, digits=6))
```



### 2.3.7 Inputting text data in fixed format

```{julia}
# Read data from fixed-format text file using custom parsing
lines = readlines("mus2/mus202file3.txt")
df_fixed = DataFrame(
    name   = [strip(l[1:min(10,length(l))]) for l in lines],
    age    = [parse(Int, strip(l[11:min(12,length(l))])) for l in lines],
    female = [parse(Int, strip(l[13:min(13,length(l))])) for l in lines],
    income = [parse(Float64, strip(l[14:min(20,length(l))])) for l in lines]
)
println(df_fixed)
```
