### 6.3.4 IV estimation of an exactly identified model

```{julia}
# 2SLS with single endogenous regressor; hi_empunion instrumented by ssiratio
iv_just = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6,
    @formula(ldrugexp ~ totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc +
                       (hi_empunion ~ ssiratio)),
    Vcov.robust())
```

```{julia}
#* IV estimation of a just-identified model with single endog regressor
#ivregress 2sls ldrugexp (hi_empunion = ssiratio) $x2list, vce(robust) first
ivregress_2sls(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion => :ssiratio, x2list;
               robust=true, first=true);
```

### 6.3.5 IV estimation of an overidentified model

```{julia}
# 2SLS with two instruments (ssiratio + multlc) — overidentified
iv_over = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6,
    @formula(ldrugexp ~ totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc +
                       (hi_empunion ~ ssiratio + multlc)),
    Vcov.robust())
```

```{julia}
# Cluster-robust 2SLS
iv_clu = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6,
    @formula(ldrugexp ~ totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc +
                       (hi_empunion ~ ssiratio + multlc)),
    Vcov.cluster(:age))
```

```{julia}
# 2SLS with default (homoskedastic) SE
iv_def = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6,
    @formula(ldrugexp ~ totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc +
                       (hi_empunion ~ ssiratio + multlc)))

println("\n— Comparison of 2SLS coefficient on hi_empunion —")
for (name, m) in [("2SLS robust", iv_over), ("2SLS cluster(age)", iv_clu),
                   ("2SLS default", iv_def)]
    nm = StatsBase.coefnames(m)
    i  = findfirst(==("hi_empunion"), nm)
    @printf("%-20s coef=%9.5f  se=%9.5f\n",
            name, StatsBase.coef(m)[i], sqrt(StatsBase.vcov(m)[i, i]))
end
```

```{julia}
# ivmodel:  ldrugexp (hi_empunion = ssiratio multlc) totchr age female blhisp linc
#
#  1) 2SLS, vce(robust)
#  2) GMM, wmatrix(robust)      -- one-step efficient GMM under heteroskedasticity
#  3) GMM, wmatrix(robust) igmm -- iterated GMM
#  4) GMM, wmatrix(cluster age) -- one-step GMM with cluster-robust weight (cluster on age)
#  5) 2SLS default (classical SE)

df6g = dropmissing(df6, vcat([:ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :ssiratio, :multlc], x2list))
n    = DataFrames.nrow(df6g)

y     = Float64.(df6g.ldrugexp)
endog = Float64.(df6g.hi_empunion)
Xexog = Matrix{Float64}(df6g[:, x2list])
Zinst = Matrix{Float64}(df6g[:, [:ssiratio, :multlc]])
X     = hcat(ones(n), endog, Xexog)                   # regressors
Z     = hcat(ones(n), Xexog, Zinst)                   # instruments (exog + excl.)
xnames = ["_cons", "hi_empunion", string.(x2list)...]

# ---- GMM building blocks -----------------------------------------------------
function gmm_point(y, X, Z, W)
    XZ = X' * Z
    (XZ * W * XZ') \ (XZ * W * (Z' * y))
end
function sandwich_Ω(Z, u; cluster=nothing)
    m = size(Z, 2); Ω = zeros(m, m); n_ = length(u)
    if cluster === nothing
        for i in 1:n_; zi = @view Z[i, :]; Ω .+= (u[i]^2) .* (zi * zi'); end
    else
        for g in unique(cluster)
            sel = cluster .== g
            zu  = Z[sel, :]' * u[sel]
            Ω  .+= zu * zu'
        end
    end
    return Ω
end
gmm_vcov(X, Z, W, Ω) = (A = X'*Z * W * Z'*X; inv(A) * (X'*Z * W * Ω * W * Z'*X) * inv(A))

# ---- (1) 2SLS with robust SE -------------------------------------------------
W_2sls        = inv(Z' * Z)
β_2sls_robust = gmm_point(y, X, Z, W_2sls)
u_            = y .- X * β_2sls_robust
V_2sls_robust = gmm_vcov(X, Z, W_2sls, sandwich_Ω(Z, u_))

# ---- (2) One-step efficient GMM with robust W --------------------------------
Ω̂_rob     = sandwich_Ω(Z, u_)                        # Ω̂ from 2SLS residuals
W_rob     = inv(Ω̂_rob)
β_gmm_het = gmm_point(y, X, Z, W_rob)
V_gmm_het = gmm_vcov(X, Z, W_rob, sandwich_Ω(Z, y .- X * β_gmm_het))

# ---- (3) Iterated GMM --------------------------------------------------------
β_it, u_it = β_gmm_het, y .- X * β_gmm_het
for _ in 1:100
    W_new = inv(sandwich_Ω(Z, u_it))
    β_new = gmm_point(y, X, Z, W_new)
    u_new = y .- X * β_new
    LinearAlgebra.norm(β_new - β_it) < 1e-9 && (β_it = β_new; u_it = u_new; break)
    β_it, u_it = β_new, u_new
end
V_gmm_igmm = gmm_vcov(X, Z, inv(sandwich_Ω(Z, u_it)), sandwich_Ω(Z, u_it))

# ---- (4) GMM with cluster-robust W (cluster on age) --------------------------
cluster_age = Vector(df6g.age)
Ω̂_clu       = sandwich_Ω(Z, u_; cluster=cluster_age)
W_clu       = inv(Ω̂_clu)
β_gmm_clu   = gmm_point(y, X, Z, W_clu)
V_gmm_clu   = gmm_vcov(X, Z, W_clu,
                       sandwich_Ω(Z, y .- X * β_gmm_clu; cluster=cluster_age))

# ---- (5) 2SLS with classical (default) SE -----------------------------------
β_2sls_def = β_2sls_robust                             # same point estimate
σ²         = sum(abs2, u_) / (n - size(X,2))
V_2sls_def = σ² .* inv(X'*Z * W_2sls * Z'*X)

# ---- Comparison table --------------------------------------------------------
_se(V) = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))
est_table = DataFrames.DataFrame(
    variable = xnames,
    TwoSLS_rob_b  = β_2sls_robust, TwoSLS_rob_se  = _se(V_2sls_robust),
    GMM_het_b     = β_gmm_het,     GMM_het_se     = _se(V_gmm_het),
    GMM_igmm_b    = β_it,          GMM_igmm_se    = _se(V_gmm_igmm),
    GMM_clu_b     = β_gmm_clu,     GMM_clu_se     = _se(V_gmm_clu),
    TwoSLS_def_b  = β_2sls_def,    TwoSLS_def_se  = _se(V_2sls_def),
)
println("estimates table TwoSLS GMM_het GMM_igmm GMM_clu TwoSLS_def, b se")
PrettyTables.pretty_table(est_table;
    formatters = PrettyTables.ft_printf("%9.5f", 2:11))
```

```{julia}
#=
* Compare 5 estimators and variance estimates for overidentified models
global ivmodel "ldrugexp (hi_empunion = ssiratio multlc) $x2list"
quietly ivregress 2sls $ivmodel, vce(robust)
estimates store TwoSLS
quietly ivregress gmm  $ivmodel, wmatrix(robust)
estimates store GMM_het
quietly ivregress gmm  $ivmodel, wmatrix(robust) igmm
estimates store GMM_igmm
quietly ivregress gmm  $ivmodel, wmatrix(cluster age)
estimates store GMM_clu
quietly ivregress 2sls  $ivmodel
estimates store TwoSLS_def
estimates table TwoSLS GMM_het GMM_igmm GMM_clu TwoSLS_def, b(%9.5f) se
=#
TwoSLS     = iv_gmm(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, [:ssiratio, :multlc], x2list;
                    estimator=:twosls, wmatrix=:robust)
GMM_het    = iv_gmm(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, [:ssiratio, :multlc], x2list;
                    estimator=:gmm,  wmatrix=:robust)
GMM_igmm   = iv_gmm(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, [:ssiratio, :multlc], x2list;
                    estimator=:gmm,  wmatrix=:robust, iterate=true)
GMM_clu    = iv_gmm(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, [:ssiratio, :multlc], x2list;
                    estimator=:gmm,  wmatrix=:cluster, cluster=:age)
TwoSLS_def = iv_gmm(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, [:ssiratio, :multlc], x2list;
                    estimator=:twosls, wmatrix=:classical)

estimates_compare(
    [TwoSLS, GMM_het, GMM_igmm, GMM_clu, TwoSLS_def],
    ["TwoSLS", "GMM_het", "GMM_igmm", "GMM_clu", "TwoSLS_def"];
    vars_order = ["hi_empunion", "totchr", "age", "female", "blhisp", "linc", "_cons"])
```

```{julia}
# Durbin–Wu–Hausman: include first-stage residuals in second-stage regression
# and test their significance.
df6c = dropmissing(df6, [:ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :ssiratio, :totchr, :age,
                          :female, :blhisp, :linc])
m_first = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6c, @formula(hi_empunion ~ ssiratio + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc))
df6c.v1hat = df6c.hi_empunion .- FixedEffectModels.predict(m_first, df6c)
m_dwh = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6c, @formula(ldrugexp ~ hi_empunion + v1hat + totchr + age +
                                       female + blhisp + linc), Vcov.robust())
nm = StatsBase.coefnames(m_dwh)
i  = findfirst(==("v1hat"), nm)
t  = StatsBase.coef(m_dwh)[i] / sqrt(StatsBase.vcov(m_dwh)[i, i])
@printf("DWH t = %.3f, p = %.4f\n",
        t, 2 * (1 - cdf(Normal(), abs(t))))
```

```{julia}
#* Robust Durbin-Wu-Hausman test of endogeneity implemented by estat endogenous
#ivregress 2sls ldrugexp (hi_empunion = ssiratio) $x2list, vce(robust)

estat_endogenous(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :ssiratio, x2list;
                 robust=true);
```

```{julia}
# --- (1) OLS for comparison with IV ---
# regress ldrugexp hi_empunion $x2list, vce(robust)
df6h = dropmissing(df6, vcat([:ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :ssiratio], x2list))
ols_cmp = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6h,
    @formula(ldrugexp ~ hi_empunion + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc),
    Vcov.robust())
println("OLS (robust SE):"); println(ols_cmp)
```

```{julia}
# --- (2) 2SLS with vce(robust), then estat endogenous ---
# ivregress 2sls ldrugexp (hi_empunion = ssiratio) $x2list, vce(robust)
iv_cmp = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6h,
    @formula(ldrugexp ~ totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc +
                       (hi_empunion ~ ssiratio)),
    Vcov.robust())
println("2SLS (robust SE):"); println(iv_cmp)

# estat endogenous — Wooldridge robust DWH test:
# run first-stage, get residuals v̂, regress y on [hi_empunion, v̂, $x2list] with
# robust SE, and use the robust Wald t on v̂ as the endogeneity statistic.
fs_cmp       = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6h,
    @formula(hi_empunion ~ ssiratio + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc))
df6h.v1hat   = df6h.hi_empunion .- FixedEffectModels.predict(fs_cmp, df6h)
aux_cmp      = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6h,
    @formula(ldrugexp ~ hi_empunion + v1hat + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc),
    Vcov.robust())
wald_test(aux_cmp, ["v1hat"]);
```

```{julia}
# --- (3) Manual Durbin-Wu-Hausman, step by step (matches the cell above) ---
# quietly regress hi_empunion ssiratio $x2list        → fs_cmp
# quietly predict v1hat, resid                         → df6h.v1hat
# quietly regress ldrugexp hi_empunion v1hat $x2list, vce(robust) → aux_cmp
# test v1hat                                           → wald_test(aux_cmp, ["v1hat"])
cf = StatsBase.coeftable(aux_cmp)
println("Auxiliary regression (robust SE) — row for v1hat:")
println(cf)
```

```{julia}
#* Robust Durbin-Wu-Hausman test of endogeneity implemented manually
#quietly regress hi_empunion ssiratio $x2list
#quietly predict v1hat, resid
#quietly regress ldrugexp hi_empunion v1hat $x2list, vce(robust)
#test v1hat
df6c = dropmissing(df6, [:ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :ssiratio,
                         :totchr, :age, :female, :blhisp, :linc])
# Step 1-2: first stage + residuals
m_first = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6c,
    @formula(hi_empunion ~ ssiratio + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc))
df6c.v1hat = df6c.hi_empunion .- FixedEffectModels.predict(m_first, df6c)
# Step 3: augmented regression with robust SE
m_aux = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6c,
    @formula(ldrugexp ~ hi_empunion + v1hat + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc),
    Vcov.robust())
# Step 4: F-test on v1hat coefficient
β   = StatsBase.coef(m_aux)
V   = StatsBase.vcov(m_aux)
i   = findfirst(==("v1hat"), StatsBase.coefnames(m_aux))
F   = β[i]^2 / V[i, i]
df2 = Int(StatsBase.dof_residual(m_aux))
pF  = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(1, df2), F)

println("( 1)  v1hat = 0\n")
Printf.@printf("       F(%3d, %d) = %7.2f\n", 1, df2, F)
Printf.@printf("            Prob > F = %9.4f\n", pF)
```

### 6.3.7 Tests of overidentifying restrictions

```{julia}
# Sargan/Hansen J test: regress 2SLS residuals on instruments + exogenous,
# then J = N·R²
function sargan_test(model, df, instruments, exog_vars, dep)
    df_clean = dropmissing(df, vcat([dep], instruments, exog_vars))
    uhat = df_clean[!, dep] .- FixedEffectModels.predict(model, df_clean)
    df_clean.uhat = uhat
    fmla = term(:uhat) ~ sum(term.(vcat(instruments, exog_vars)))
    aux = FixedEffectModels.reg(df_clean, fmla)
    J = StatsBase.nobs(aux) * StatsBase.r2(aux)
    df_J = length(instruments) - 1   # endogenous regressors
    return J, df_J, 1 - cdf(Chisq(df_J), J)
end
J, df_J, p = sargan_test(iv_over, df6, [:ssiratio, :multlc],
                         [:totchr, :age, :female, :blhisp, :linc], :ldrugexp)
@printf("Sargan J = %.4f, df = %d, p = %.4f\n", J, df_J, p)
```

```{julia}
# Same test with all four instruments
iv_4 = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6,
    @formula(ldrugexp ~ totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc +
                       (hi_empunion ~ ssiratio + lowincome + multlc + firmsz)),
    Vcov.robust())
J, df_J, p = sargan_test(iv_4, df6,
    [:ssiratio, :lowincome, :multlc, :firmsz],
    [:totchr, :age, :female, :blhisp, :linc], :ldrugexp)
@printf("Sargan J (4 instruments) = %.4f, df = %d, p = %.4f\n", J, df_J, p)
```


```{julia}
# estat overid after `ivregress gmm ..., wmatrix(robust)`
# Hansen's J = (Z'û)' Ω̂⁻¹ (Z'û)
#  where û come from the two-step efficient GMM estimator with Ω̂ = Σ zᵢzᵢ'ûᵢ²
# Under the null of valid instruments, J ~ χ²(#instruments − #endogenous).

"""Two-step efficient GMM + Hansen J, for linear IV with heteroskedastic robust W."""
function gmm_overid(df, yvar::Symbol, endog_vars, exog_vars, inst_vars)
    needed = unique(vcat([yvar], endog_vars, exog_vars, inst_vars))
    d = dropmissing(df, needed)
    n = DataFrames.nrow(d)
    y = Float64.(d[!, yvar])
    X_endog = Matrix{Float64}(d[:, endog_vars])
    X_exog  = Matrix{Float64}(d[:, exog_vars])
    Z_inst  = Matrix{Float64}(d[:, inst_vars])
    X = hcat(ones(n), X_endog, X_exog)
    Z = hcat(ones(n), X_exog,  Z_inst)

    # Step 1: 2SLS for initial residuals
    W0 = LinearAlgebra.inv(Z' * Z)
    β0 = (X'Z * W0 * Z'X) \ (X'Z * W0 * Z'y)
    u0 = y .- X * β0

    # Step 2: build heteroskedasticity-robust Ω̂ and refit
    Ω = sandwich_Ω(Z, u0)                  # Σ zᵢzᵢ' ûᵢ²
    W = LinearAlgebra.inv(Ω)
    β = (X'Z * W * Z'X) \ (X'Z * W * Z'y)
    u = y .- X * β

    # Hansen J at the efficient estimator
    g = Z' * u
    J = g' * W * g
    df_J = size(Z, 2) - size(X, 2)          # #instruments − #regressors = #overid restrictions
    p    = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(max(df_J, 1)), J)
    return (; J, df = df_J, p, β, n)
end

# --- Model A: 2 instruments (ssiratio, multlc)  →  1 overid restriction ---
resA = gmm_overid(df6, :ldrugexp, [:hi_empunion], x2list, [:ssiratio, :multlc])
Printf.@printf("\nestat overid  [instruments: ssiratio, multlc]\n")
Printf.@printf("  Hansen J  = %.4f\n", resA.J)
Printf.@printf("  df        = %d\n",   resA.df)
Printf.@printf("  Prob > χ² = %.4f\n", resA.p)

# --- Model B: 4 instruments (ssiratio, lowincome, multlc, firmsz)  →  3 overid restrictions ---
resB = gmm_overid(df6, :ldrugexp, [:hi_empunion], x2list,
                  [:ssiratio, :lowincome, :multlc, :firmsz])
Printf.@printf("\nestat overid  [instruments: ssiratio, lowincome, multlc, firmsz]\n")
Printf.@printf("  Hansen J  = %.4f\n", resB.J)
Printf.@printf("  df        = %d\n",   resB.df)
Printf.@printf("  Prob > χ² = %.4f\n", resB.p)
```

```{julia}
#estat overid
estat_overid(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, [:ssiratio, :multlc], x2list)
```

### 6.3.8 IV estimation with a binary endogenous regressor

```{julia}
# Stata's treatreg fits a Heckman-style treatment-effects model.
# In Julia we approximate it with 2SLS or with a manual two-step probit + OLS.
println("treatreg-style estimation: see Sec 16.6 (Heckman) for the manual two-step.")
```

```{julia}
# treatreg ldrugexp $x2list, treat(hi_empunion = ssiratio $x2list)
#
# Heckman two-step estimator for endogenous binary treatment:
#   outcome:   ldrugexp = Xβ + δ·hi_empunion + ε
#   treatment: hi_empunion = 1[Zγ + u > 0],   (ε, u) bivariate normal
#
# 1) Probit of hi_empunion on Z = [ssiratio, x2list]
# 2) Compute the treatment-model inverse Mills ratio
#        λᵢ = φ(Zγ̂)/Φ(Zγ̂)      if hi_empunion=1
#        λᵢ = −φ(Zγ̂)/(1−Φ(Zγ̂)) if hi_empunion=0
# 3) OLS: ldrugexp ~ Xβ + δ·hi_empunion + σ_ερ·λ + error
#    coef on λ equals σ_ερ; t-test on it is the endogenous-treatment test.

df6t = dropmissing(df6, vcat([:ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :ssiratio], x2list))
Nobs = DataFrames.nrow(df6t)

# --- Step 1: Probit selection / treatment equation
probit_fit = GLM.glm(
    GLM.@formula(hi_empunion ~ ssiratio + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc),
    df6t, GLM.Binomial(), GLM.ProbitLink())
γ̂  = GLM.coef(probit_fit)
Z_ = hcat(ones(Nobs), Float64.(df6t.ssiratio), Matrix{Float64}(df6t[:, x2list]))
Zγ = Z_ * γ̂

# --- Step 2: inverse Mills ratio
Ndist = Distributions.Normal()
φ = Distributions.pdf.(Ndist, Zγ)
Φ = Distributions.cdf.(Ndist, Zγ)
df6t.lambda = [df6t.hi_empunion[i] == 1 ? φ[i]/Φ[i] : -φ[i]/(1 - Φ[i])  for i in 1:Nobs]

# --- Step 3: augmented OLS outcome equation
treatreg_fit = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6t,
    @formula(ldrugexp ~ hi_empunion + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc + lambda))
println("Heckman treatment-regression (two-step)")
println(treatreg_fit)

# Report the treatment effect and the endogeneity-of-treatment test (coef on λ)
cn   = StatsBase.coefnames(treatreg_fit)
i_D  = findfirst(==("hi_empunion"), cn)
i_λ  = findfirst(==("lambda"),      cn)
β    = StatsBase.coef(treatreg_fit);  Vc = StatsBase.vcov(treatreg_fit)
Printf.@printf("\nTreatment effect  δ̂ = %.6f   (naive SE = %.6f)\n",
               β[i_D], sqrt(Vc[i_D, i_D]))
Printf.@printf("σ_ε · ρ̂ (coef on λ) = %.6f   t = %.3f\n",
               β[i_λ], β[i_λ]/sqrt(Vc[i_λ, i_λ]))
```

```{julia}
#* Regression with a dummy variable regressor
#treatreg ldrugexp $x2list, treat(hi_empunion = ssiratio $x2list)
treatreg(df6, :ldrugexp, x2list,
         :hi_empunion, vcat(:ssiratio, x2list));
```

#### Formal tests for weak instruments

### 6.4.3 First-stage F-statistics

### 6.4.4 Just-identified model

```{julia}
# First-stage F: regress endogenous on instrument(s) + exogenous
df6c = dropmissing(df6, [:hi_empunion, :ssiratio, :totchr, :age, :female, :blhisp, :linc])
fs1 = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6c, @formula(hi_empunion ~ ssiratio + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc),
          Vcov.robust())
nm = StatsBase.coefnames(fs1)
i  = findfirst(==("ssiratio"), nm)
F1 = (StatsBase.coef(fs1)[i] / sqrt(StatsBase.vcov(fs1)[i, i]))^2     # squared t = F(1, ·)
@printf("First-stage F(1) on ssiratio = %.3f\n", F1)
```

```{julia}
# ivregress 2sls ldrugexp (hi_empunion = ssiratio) $x2list, vce(robust)
# estat firststage, forcenonrobust all
#
# For a just-identified model with one endogenous regressor and one excluded
# instrument, `estat firststage, all` reports:
#  - Partial R² and Adjusted partial R² (instrument contribution to first stage)
#  - First-stage F statistic on the excluded instrument(s)
#  - Minimum-eigenvalue statistic (Cragg–Donald) — for 1 endog / 1 IV this
#    coincides with the first-stage F.
#  - Stock–Yogo 10/15/20/25% critical values (just-identified: 16.38 for 10%)

df6fs = dropmissing(df6, vcat([:hi_empunion, :ssiratio], x2list))
N_fs  = DataFrames.nrow(df6fs)

# --- First-stage regression ---
fs_just = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6fs,
    @formula(hi_empunion ~ ssiratio + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc))
# --- Restricted first stage (exog only, no ssiratio) for partial-R² ---
fs_restr = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6fs,
    @formula(hi_empunion ~ totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc))

# --- Partial R² = (R²_full − R²_restricted) / (1 − R²_restricted) ---
R2_full   = StatsBase.r2(fs_just)
R2_restr  = StatsBase.r2(fs_restr)
partialR2 = (R2_full - R2_restr) / (1 - R2_restr)

# --- Adjusted partial R²: same logic on adjusted R² ---
R2adj_full  = StatsBase.adjr2(fs_just)
R2adj_restr = StatsBase.adjr2(fs_restr)
partialR2_adj = (R2adj_full - R2adj_restr) / (1 - R2adj_restr)

# --- First-stage F on the excluded instrument (= squared t on ssiratio) ---
nm  = StatsBase.coefnames(fs_just)
i_z = findfirst(==("ssiratio"), nm)
F_stat = (StatsBase.coef(fs_just)[i_z] / sqrt(StatsBase.vcov(fs_just)[i_z, i_z]))^2
df_num = 1                                    # one excluded instrument
df_den = StatsBase.dof_residual(fs_just)
p_F    = 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.FDist(df_num, df_den), F_stat)

# --- Cragg–Donald statistic: for 1 endog / 1 IV this equals F_stat ---
CD = F_stat

println("First-stage diagnostics — just-identified IV (hi_empunion = ssiratio)")
PrettyTables.pretty_table(
    DataFrames.DataFrame(
        statistic  = ["R²_full (first stage)",
                      "R²_restricted (no excl. IV)",
                      "Partial R²",
                      "Adjusted partial R²",
                      "F( $(df_num), $(Int(df_den)) )  on ssiratio",
                      "Prob > F",
                      "Minimum eigenvalue (Cragg–Donald)"],
        value      = [R2_full, R2_restr, partialR2, partialR2_adj,
                      F_stat, p_F, CD]);
    formatters = PrettyTables.ft_printf("%10.4f", 2))

# --- Stock–Yogo (2005) critical values — 2SLS bias, 1 endog / 1 IV ---
println("\nStock–Yogo weak-ID critical values for just-identified 2SLS (bias test):")
sy = DataFrames.DataFrame(
    relative_bias = ["5%", "10%", "20%", "30%"],
    critical_F    = [16.38, 8.96, 6.66, 5.53])
PrettyTables.pretty_table(sy)
F_stat > 16.38 ? println("\n→ F > 16.38: instrument is NOT weak at the 5% bias threshold.") :
F_stat > 10    ? println("\n→ 10 < F ≤ 16.38: instrument probably adequate but borderline.") :
                 println("\n→ F ≤ 10: instrument is WEAK; 2SLS estimates may be badly biased.")
```

```{julia}
#* Weak instrument tests - just-identified model
#quietly ivregress 2sls ldrugexp (hi_empunion = ssiratio) $x2list, vce(robust)
#estat firststage, forcenonrobust all
estat_firststage(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :ssiratio, x2list;
                 all=true);
```

### 6.4.5 Overidentified model

```{julia}
# Wald test of joint significance of all instruments in the first stage
fs4 = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6c, @formula(hi_empunion ~ ssiratio + lowincome + multlc + firmsz +
                                       totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc))
nm = StatsBase.coefnames(fs4)
idx = [findfirst(==(n), nm) for n in ("ssiratio","lowincome","multlc","firmsz")]
βt = StatsBase.coef(fs4)[idx]; Vt = StatsBase.vcov(fs4)[idx, idx]
F4 = try
    (βt' * LinearAlgebra.inv(Vt) * βt) / 4
catch
    NaN
end
@printf("First-stage F(4) on instruments = %.3f\n", F4)
```


```{julia}
#* Weak instrument tests - two or more overidentifying restrictions
#quietly ivregress gmm ldrugexp (hi_empunion = ssiratio lowincome multlc firmsz) ///
#   $x2list, vce(robust)
#estat firststage, forcenonrobust
estat_firststage(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion,
                 [:ssiratio, :lowincome, :multlc, :firmsz], x2list);
```

### 6.4.6 More than one endogenous regressor

### 6.4.7 Sensitivity to choice of instruments

```{julia}
# Compare four just-identified IV estimates with different instruments
ols_compare = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6, @formula(ldrugexp ~ hi_empunion + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc),
                  Vcov.robust())

iv_models = Dict()
for inst in (:ssiratio, :lowincome, :multlc, :firmsz)
    fmla = @eval @formula(ldrugexp ~ totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc +
                                     (hi_empunion ~ $inst))
    iv_models[inst] = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6, fmla, Vcov.robust())
end

# First-stage F for each instrument
println("\n— First-stage F-stats —")
for inst in (:ssiratio, :lowincome, :multlc, :firmsz)
    df6c = dropmissing(df6, [:hi_empunion, inst, :totchr, :age, :female, :blhisp, :linc])
    fmla = term(:hi_empunion) ~ term(inst) + term(:totchr) + term(:age) +
                                 term(:female) + term(:blhisp) + term(:linc)
    fs = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6c, fmla)
    nm = StatsBase.coefnames(fs)
    i  = findfirst(==(string(inst)), nm)
    F  = (StatsBase.coef(fs)[i] / sqrt(StatsBase.vcov(fs)[i, i]))^2
    β_iv = StatsBase.coef(iv_models[inst])[findfirst(==("hi_empunion"), StatsBase.coefnames(iv_models[inst]))]
    @printf("%-12s  IV β=%8.4f   first-stage F=%8.3f\n", inst, β_iv, F)
end
```

```{julia}
#* Compare 4 just-identified model estimates with different instruments
#quietly regress ldrugexp hi_empunion $x2list, vce(robust)
#estimates store OLS0
#quietly ivregress 2sls ldrugexp (hi_empunion=ssiratio) $x2list, vce(robust)
#estimates store IV_INST1
#quietly estat firststage, forcenonrobust
#scalar me1 = r(mineig)
#quietly ivregress 2sls ldrugexp (hi_empunion=lowincome) $x2list, vce(robust)
#estimates store IV_INST2
#quietly estat firststage, forcenonrobust
#scalar me2 = r(mineig)
#quietly ivregress 2sls ldrugexp (hi_empunion=multlc) $x2list, vce(robust)
#estimates store IV_INST3
#quietly estat firststage, forcenonrobust
#scalar me3 = r(mineig)
#quietly ivregress 2sls ldrugexp (hi_empunion=firmsz) $x2list, vce(robust)
#estimates store IV_INST4
#quietly estat firststage, forcenonrobust
#scalar me4 = r(mineig)
#estimates table OLS0 IV_INST1 IV_INST2 IV_INST3 IV_INST4, b(%8.4f) se
#display "Minimum eigenvalues are:     " me1 _s(2) me2 _s(2) me3 _s(2) me4
_wrap_fem(m) = (;
    β         = StatsBase.coef(m),
    V         = StatsBase.vcov(m),
    se        = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(StatsBase.vcov(m)), 0.0)),
    coefnames = replace.(string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m)), "(Intercept)" => "_cons"),
    n         = Int(StatsBase.nobs(m)),
)
# Classical first-stage F on the single excluded instrument = Stata's mineig.
_mineig(df, endog, inst, exog) = begin
    d = dropmissing(df, unique(vcat(Symbol(endog), Symbol(inst), exog)))
    f = term(Symbol(endog)) ~ sum(term.(vcat(exog, Symbol(inst))))
    m = FixedEffectModels.reg(d, f)
    nm = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m))
    i  = findfirst(==(string(inst)), nm)
    (StatsBase.coef(m)[i] / sqrt(StatsBase.vcov(m)[i, i]))^2
end

OLS0 = _wrap_fem(FixedEffectModels.reg(df6,
        @formula(ldrugexp ~ hi_empunion + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc),
        Vcov.robust()))
IV_INST1 = iv_gmm(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :ssiratio,  x2list;
                  estimator=:twosls, wmatrix=:robust)
IV_INST2 = iv_gmm(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :lowincome, x2list;
                  estimator=:twosls, wmatrix=:robust)
IV_INST3 = iv_gmm(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :multlc,    x2list;
                  estimator=:twosls, wmatrix=:robust)
IV_INST4 = iv_gmm(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :firmsz,    x2list;
                  estimator=:twosls, wmatrix=:robust)

me1 = _mineig(df6, :hi_empunion, :ssiratio,  x2list)
me2 = _mineig(df6, :hi_empunion, :lowincome, x2list)
me3 = _mineig(df6, :hi_empunion, :multlc,    x2list)
me4 = _mineig(df6, :hi_empunion, :firmsz,    x2list)

estimates_compare(
    [OLS0, IV_INST1, IV_INST2, IV_INST3, IV_INST4],
    ["OLS0", "IV_INST1", "IV_INST2", "IV_INST3", "IV_INST4"];
    vars_order = ["hi_empunion", "totchr", "age", "female", "blhisp", "linc", "_cons"],
    fmt        = "%8.4f")

println("                                                         Legend: b/se")
println()
Printf.@printf("Minimum eigenvalues are:     %.8g  %.8g  %.8g  %.8g\n",
               me1, me2, me3, me4)
```


## 6.5 Better inference with weak instruments

### 6.5.1 Conditional tests and confidence intervals

```{julia}
# Anderson–Rubin (AR) test, weak-instrument robust:
# regress y - β₀·x₁ on instruments (β₀ = 0 here)
df6c = dropmissing(df6, [:ldrugexp, :ssiratio, :totchr, :age, :female, :blhisp, :linc])
df6c.y_ar = df6c.ldrugexp                # β₀ = 0
m_ar = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6c, @formula(y_ar ~ ssiratio + totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc))
nm = StatsBase.coefnames(m_ar)
i_ssi = findfirst(==("ssiratio"), nm)
β = StatsBase.coef(m_ar)[i_ssi]; se = sqrt(StatsBase.vcov(m_ar)[i_ssi, i_ssi])
F_ar = (β / se)^2
@printf("AR F(1) = %.3f, p = %.4f\n",
        F_ar, 1 - cdf(FDist(1, dof_residual(m_ar)), F_ar))
```

```{julia}
# Default 2SLS with first-stage diagnostics (already shown above)
reg(df6,
    @formula(ldrugexp ~ totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc +
                       (hi_empunion ~ ssiratio)))
```

```{julia}
#* Conditional test and confidence intervals when weak instruments
#*condivreg ldrugexp (hi_empunion = ssiratio) $x2list, lm ar 2sls test(0)
#* Use ivregress with weak instrument diagnostics
#ivregress 2sls ldrugexp (hi_empunion = ssiratio) $x2list
#estat firststage
#*estat overid
ivregress_2sls(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion => :ssiratio, x2list;
               robust=false, first=false)
estat_firststage(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :ssiratio, x2list;
                 robust=false);
```

### 6.5.2 LIML estimator

### 6.5.3 Jackknife IV estimator

### 6.5.4 Comparison of 2SLS, LIML, JIVE, and GMM

```{julia}
# ivregress 2sls / liml comparison (4 instruments, 1 endogenous)
df6iv = dropmissing(df6, vcat([:ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :ssiratio,
       :lowincome, :multlc, :firmsz], x2list))
n = nrow(df6iv)

# 2SLS
twosls = FixedEffectModels.reg(df6iv,
    @formula(ldrugexp ~ totchr + age + female + blhisp + linc +
                       (hi_empunion ~ ssiratio + lowincome + multlc + firmsz)),
    Vcov.robust())

# LIML — manual implementation via smallest-eigenvalue approach
# y = Xβ + u, X = [X_endog, X_exog], Z = [X_exog, Z_excl]
# k-class: β̂_k = (X'(I - kM_Z)X)⁻¹ X'(I - kM_Z)y, LIML uses k = λ_min
y_l  = Float64.(df6iv.ldrugexp)
X_l  = hcat(Float64.(df6iv.hi_empunion), Float64.(df6iv.totchr), Float64.(df6iv.age),
            Float64.(df6iv.female), Float64.(df6iv.blhisp), Float64.(df6iv.linc), ones(n))
Z_l  = hcat(Float64.(df6iv.ssiratio), Float64.(df6iv.lowincome), Float64.(df6iv.multlc),
            Float64.(df6iv.firmsz), Float64.(df6iv.totchr), Float64.(df6iv.age),
            Float64.(df6iv.female), Float64.(df6iv.blhisp), Float64.(df6iv.linc), ones(n))
P_Z  = Z_l * ((Z_l'Z_l) \ Z_l')
M_Z  = LinearAlgebra.I(n) - P_Z
# Smallest eigenvalue of (y X_endog)' M_Z (y X_endog) / (y X_endog)' M_exog (y X_endog)
Y1 = hcat(y_l, Float64.(df6iv.hi_empunion))
X_exog_only = hcat(Float64.(df6iv.totchr), Float64.(df6iv.age),
                   Float64.(df6iv.female), Float64.(df6iv.blhisp),
                   Float64.(df6iv.linc), ones(n))
P_exog = X_exog_only * ((X_exog_only'X_exog_only) \ X_exog_only')
M_exog = LinearAlgebra.I(n) - P_exog
A = Y1' * M_Z * Y1;  B_mat = Y1' * M_exog * Y1
λ_min = minimum(LinearAlgebra.eigvals(LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(B_mat) \
                                       LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(A)))
# k-class estimator with k = λ_min
β_liml = ((X_l' * (LinearAlgebra.I(n) - λ_min*M_Z) * X_l) \
          (X_l' * (LinearAlgebra.I(n) - λ_min*M_Z) * y_l))

cn_iv = ["hi_empunion", "totchr", "age", "female", "blhisp", "linc", "_cons"]

# JIVE — Jackknife IV estimator (Angrist, Imbens & Krueger 1999)
# Uses leave-one-out first-stage fitted values to avoid finite-sample bias
# x̂ᵢ_JIVE = (n·P_Z[i,:] * x - x̂ᵢ) / (n·h_ii - 1) , h_ii = diag(P_Z)
x_endog = Float64.(df6iv.hi_empunion)
x_hat   = P_Z * x_endog         # standard first-stage fitted values
h_diag  = [P_Z[i,i] for i in 1:n]
# Leave-one-out fitted values
x_hat_jive = (n .* (P_Z * x_endog) .- x_hat) ./ (n .* h_diag .- 1.0)
# Second stage: regress y on x̂_JIVE and exog
X_jive = hcat(x_hat_jive, X_exog_only)
β_jive = X_jive \ y_l

# GMM with robust weight matrix (two-step efficient GMM)
# Step 1: 2SLS residuals
β_2sls = StatsBase.coef(twosls)
u_2sls = y_l .- X_l * β_2sls
# Step 2: optimal weight S = (1/n) Σ z_i z_i' û²
Zu = Z_l .* u_2sls
S  = (Zu' * Zu) / n
Sinv = LinearAlgebra.inv(S)
# GMM estimator: β = (X'Z S⁻¹ Z'X)⁻¹ X'Z S⁻¹ Z'y
XZ = X_l' * Z_l
β_gmm = (XZ * Sinv * XZ') \ (XZ * Sinv * (Z_l' * y_l))

println("Comparison: 2SLS vs LIML vs JIVE vs GMM (4 instruments)")
PrettyTables.pretty_table(
    DataFrames.DataFrame(variable=cn_iv,
        b_2SLS = β_2sls,
        b_LIML = β_liml,
        b_JIVE = vcat(β_jive[1], β_jive[2:end]),
        b_GMM  = β_gmm);
    formatters=PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.6f", 2:5))
```

```{julia}
#=
*ssc install st0108
*ssc install ivreg2
*ssc install ranktest
* Variants of IV Estimators: 2SLS, LIML, JIVE, GMM_het, GMM-het using IVREG2
global ivmodel "ldrugexp (hi_empunion = ssiratio lowincome multlc firmsz) $x2list"
quietly ivregress 2sls $ivmodel, vce(robust)
estimates store TWOSLS
quietly ivregress liml $ivmodel, vce(robust)
estimates store LIML
quietly jive $ivmodel, robust
estimates store JIVE
quietly ivregress gmm $ivmodel, wmatrix(robust)
estimates store GMM_het
quietly ivreg2 $ivmodel, gmm2s robust
estimates store IVREG2
estimates table TWOSLS LIML JIVE GMM_het IVREG2, b(%7.4f) se
=#
ivlist = [:ssiratio, :lowincome, :multlc, :firmsz]

TWOSLS  = iv_gmm(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, ivlist, x2list;
                 estimator=:twosls, wmatrix=:robust)
LIML    = liml_fit(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, ivlist, x2list; robust=true)
JIVE    = jive_fit(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, ivlist, x2list; robust=true)
GMM_het = iv_gmm(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, ivlist, x2list;
                 estimator=:gmm,   wmatrix=:robust)
IVREG2  = iv_gmm(df6, :ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, ivlist, x2list;
                 estimator=:gmm,   wmatrix=:robust)   # same β as GMM_het

estimates_compare(
    [TWOSLS, LIML, JIVE, GMM_het, IVREG2],
    ["TWOSLS", "LIML", "JIVE", "GMM_het", "IVREG2"];
    vars_order = ["hi_empunion", "totchr", "age", "female", "blhisp", "linc", "_cons"],
    fmt        = "%7.4f")
```

## 6.6 3SLS systems estimation

```{julia}
# 3SLS estimation
# reg3 (ldrugexp hi_empunion totchr age female blhisp linc)
#      (hi_empunion ldrugexp totchr female blhisp ssiratio)
df6_3 = dropmissing(df6, [:ldrugexp, :hi_empunion, :totchr, :age, :female,
                           :blhisp, :linc, :ssiratio])
n3 = nrow(df6_3)

# Eq 1: ldrugexp  = f(hi_empunion, totchr, age, female, blhisp, linc)
# Eq 2: hi_empunion = f(ldrugexp, totchr, female, blhisp, ssiratio)
# Full instrument set Z = union of all exogenous vars across both equations
Z3 = hcat(ones(n3), Float64.(df6_3.totchr), Float64.(df6_3.age),
          Float64.(df6_3.female), Float64.(df6_3.blhisp),
          Float64.(df6_3.linc), Float64.(df6_3.ssiratio))
P3 = Z3 * ((Z3'Z3) \ Z3')   # projection onto instrument space

# Regressors for each equation (including endogenous)
X3_1 = hcat(ones(n3), Float64.(df6_3.hi_empunion), Float64.(df6_3.totchr),
            Float64.(df6_3.age), Float64.(df6_3.female),
            Float64.(df6_3.blhisp), Float64.(df6_3.linc))
X3_2 = hcat(ones(n3), Float64.(df6_3.ldrugexp), Float64.(df6_3.totchr),
            Float64.(df6_3.female), Float64.(df6_3.blhisp),
            Float64.(df6_3.ssiratio))
y3_1 = Float64.(df6_3.ldrugexp)
y3_2 = Float64.(df6_3.hi_empunion)

cn3_1 = ["_cons", "hi_empunion", "totchr", "age", "female", "blhisp", "linc"]
cn3_2 = ["_cons", "ldrugexp", "totchr", "female", "blhisp", "ssiratio"]

# Step 1: 2SLS on each equation → get residuals for Σ̂
X3_1h = P3 * X3_1;  β3_1 = (X3_1h'X3_1) \ (X3_1h'y3_1)
X3_2h = P3 * X3_2;  β3_2 = (X3_2h'X3_2) \ (X3_2h'y3_2)
e3_1 = y3_1 - X3_1 * β3_1;  e3_2 = y3_2 - X3_2 * β3_2

# Step 2: cross-equation residual covariance
Σ3 = [e3_1'e3_1 e3_1'e3_2; e3_2'e3_1 e3_2'e3_2] ./ n3
Σ3inv = LinearAlgebra.inv(Σ3)

# Step 3: FGLS on projected system  (Σ⁻¹ ⊗ P_Z)
k1 = size(X3_1, 2); k2 = size(X3_2, 2)
XWX = [Σ3inv[1,1]*(X3_1h'X3_1)      Σ3inv[1,2]*(X3_1h'X3_2);
       Σ3inv[2,1]*(X3_2h'X3_1)      Σ3inv[2,2]*(X3_2h'X3_2)]
XWY = [Σ3inv[1,1]*(X3_1h'y3_1) + Σ3inv[1,2]*(X3_1h'y3_2);
       Σ3inv[2,1]*(X3_2h'y3_1) + Σ3inv[2,2]*(X3_2h'y3_2)]
β_3sls = XWX \ XWY
V_3sls = LinearAlgebra.inv(XWX)
se_3sls = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V_3sls), 0.0))
z_3sls  = β_3sls ./ se_3sls

println("=== 3SLS Equation 1: ldrugexp ===")
PrettyTables.pretty_table(
    DataFrame(variable=cn3_1, Coef=β_3sls[1:k1], SE=se_3sls[1:k1], z=z_3sls[1:k1]);
    formatters=PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.6f", 2:4))
println("\n=== 3SLS Equation 2: hi_empunion ===")
PrettyTables.pretty_table(
    DataFrame(variable=cn3_2, Coef=β_3sls[k1+1:end], SE=se_3sls[k1+1:end], z=z_3sls[k1+1:end]);
    formatters=PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.6f", 2:4))
```

```{julia}
#* 3SLS estimation requires errors to be #homoskedastic
#reg3 (ldrugexp hi_empunion totchr age female blhisp linc) ///
#  (hi_empunion ldrugexp totchr female blhisp ssiratio)
reg3(df6,
     :ldrugexp    => [:hi_empunion, :totchr, :age, :female, :blhisp, :linc],
     :hi_empunion => [:ldrugexp,    :totchr, :female, :blhisp, :ssiratio]);
```

# 7 Quantile regression

## 7.1 Introduction

## 7.2 QR

### 7.2.1 Conditional quantiles

### 7.2.2 Computation of QR estimates and standard errors

### 7.2.3 The qreg, bsqreg, and sqreg commands

## 7.3 QR for medical expenditures data

Quantile regression in Julia: there is no single canonical package; we implement the check-loss minimization manually with `Optim.jl`.

```{julia}
# Generic quantile regression by minimizing the asymmetric absolute-loss
# (check) function. Uses Optim.jl with NelderMead.
function quantreg(X, y, τ)
    n, p = size(X)
    function loss(β)
        r = y .- X * β
        sum(@. ifelse(r >= 0, τ * r, (τ - 1) * r))
    end
    β0 = (X' * X) \ (X' * y)               # OLS warm start
    res = optimize(loss, β0, NelderMead(),
                   Optim.Options(iterations = 5000, x_abstol = 1e-8))
    return Optim.minimizer(res)
end
```

### 7.3.1 Data summary

```{julia}
# Read log medical expenditures data
df7 = DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("musr/mus03data.dta"))
df7 = dropmissing(df7, :ltotexp)
DataFrames.describe(df7[:, [:ltotexp, :suppins, :totchr, :age, :female, :white]])
```


```{julia}
fig7 = Figure(size = (700, 400))
ax = Axis(fig7[1, 1], xlabel = "Fraction of data",
          ylabel = "quantiles of ln(totexp) | totexp > 0")
```

### 7.3.2 QR estimates

```{julia}
# Median (q = 0.5) regression
df7c = dropmissing(df7[:, [:ltotexp, :suppins, :totchr, :age, :female, :white]])
y    = df7c.ltotexp
X    = hcat(ones(nrow(df7c)),
            df7c.suppins, df7c.totchr, df7c.age, df7c.female, df7c.white)
βqr  = quantreg(X, y, 0.5)
xnms = ["_cons", "suppins", "totchr", "age", "female", "white"]
println("\n— Quantile regression at τ = 0.5 —")
for i in eachindex(βqr)
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f\n", xnms[i], βqr[i])
end
```

```{julia}
#* Basic quantile regression for q = 0.5
#qreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white
qreg_stata(df7, :ltotexp,
           [:suppins, :totchr, :age, :female, :white]; τ = 0.5);
```

### 7.3.3 Interpretation of conditional quantile coefficients

### 7.3.4 Retransformation

```{julia}
# Multiplier exp(x'β) — converts QR coefficients in logs to AME in levels
xb     = X * βqr
expxb  = exp.(xb)
@printf("Mean of exp(x'β) = %.4f (multiplier)\n", mean(expxb))
```

### 7.3.5 Comparison of estimates at different quantiles

```{julia}
# OLS + QR at τ = 0.25, 0.50, 0.75
ols7  = (X' * X) \ (X' * y)
qr25  = quantreg(X, y, 0.25)
qr50  = quantreg(X, y, 0.50)
qr75  = quantreg(X, y, 0.75)

println("\n— OLS vs Quantile Regressions —")
@printf("%-10s %10s %10s %10s %10s\n",
        "Variable", "OLS", "QR_25", "QR_50", "QR_75")
for i in eachindex(ols7)
    @printf("%-10s %10.4f %10.4f %10.4f %10.4f\n",
            xnms[i], ols7[i], qr25[i], qr50[i], qr75[i])
end
```

```{julia}
# Bootstrap SEs at τ = 0.5  (B = 200)
Random.seed!(10101)
B = 200
β_boot = Matrix{Float64}(undef, B, length(βqr))
for b in 1:B
    idx = sample(1:length(y), length(y); replace = true)
    β_boot[b, :] = quantreg(X[idx, :], y[idx], 0.5)
end
se_boot = vec(std(β_boot, dims = 1))
println("\n— Bootstrap SE for τ = 0.5 —")
for i in eachindex(βqr)
    @printf("%-10s coef=%10.4f  bs.se=%10.4f\n",
            xnms[i], βqr[i], se_boot[i])
end
```

```{julia}
#=
* Compare (1) OLS; (2-4) coeffs across quantiles; (5) bootstrap SEs
quietly regress ltotexp suppins totchr age female white
estimates store OLS
quietly qreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, quantile(.25)
estimates store QR_25
quietly qreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, quantile(.50)
estimates store QR_50
quietly qreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, quantile(.75)
estimates store QR_75
set seed 10101
quietly bsqreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, quant(.50) reps(400)
estimates store BSQR_50
estimates table OLS QR_25 QR_50 QR_75 BSQR_50, b(%7.3f) se
=#
_wrap_fem(m) = (;
    β         = StatsBase.coef(m),
    V         = StatsBase.vcov(m),
    se        = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(StatsBase.vcov(m)), 0.0)),
    coefnames = replace.(string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m)), "(Intercept)" => "_cons"),
    n         = Int(StatsBase.nobs(m)),
)

xqr = [:suppins, :totchr, :age, :female, :white]

OLS   = _wrap_fem(FixedEffectModels.reg(df7,
            @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + totchr + age + female + white)))
QR_25 = qreg_stata(df7, :ltotexp, xqr; τ=0.25, quiet=true)
QR_50 = qreg_stata(df7, :ltotexp, xqr; τ=0.50, quiet=true)
QR_75 = qreg_stata(df7, :ltotexp, xqr; τ=0.75, quiet=true)

# Bootstrap SEs for τ = 0.5 (re-fit qreg on 400 paired resamples).
Random.seed!(10101)
B      = 400
nb     = DataFrames.nrow(df7)
k_q    = length(QR_50.β)
β_boot = Matrix{Float64}(undef, B, k_q)
for b in 1:B
    idx = StatsBase.sample(1:nb, nb; replace=true)
    fit_b = qreg_stata(df7[idx, :], :ltotexp, xqr; τ=0.5, quiet=true)
    β_boot[b, :] = fit_b.β
end
BSQR_50 = (; β         = QR_50.β,
             V         = zeros(k_q, k_q),
             se        = vec(Statistics.std(β_boot; dims=1)),
             coefnames = QR_50.coefnames,
             n         = QR_50.n)

estimates_compare(
    [OLS, QR_25, QR_50, QR_75, BSQR_50],
    ["OLS", "QR_25", "QR_50", "QR_75", "BSQR_50"];
    vars_order = ["suppins", "totchr", "age", "female", "white", "_cons"],
    fmt        = "%7.3f")
```

### 7.3.6 Heteroskedasticity test

```{julia}
# Breusch–Pagan in linear model
m_ols7 = FixedEffectModels.reg(df7c, @formula(ltotexp ~ suppins + totchr + age + female + white))
yhat7  = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_ols7, df7c)
df7c.uhat2 = (df7c.ltotexp .- yhat7).^2
m_bp7 = FixedEffectModels.reg(df7c, @formula(uhat2 ~ suppins + totchr + age + female + white))
LM = nrow(df7c) * StatsBase.r2(m_bp7)
@printf("BP LM(5) = %.3f, p = %.4f\n", LM, 1 - cdf(Chisq(5), LM))
```

### 7.3.7 Hypothesis tests

```{julia}
# Simultaneous QR with bootstrap-jointly: estimate at three τ then test β_τ equality
Random.seed!(10101)
β25_b = Matrix{Float64}(undef, B, length(βqr))
β50_b = Matrix{Float64}(undef, B, length(βqr))
β75_b = Matrix{Float64}(undef, B, length(βqr))
for b in 1:B
    idx = sample(1:length(y), length(y); replace = true)
    β25_b[b, :] = quantreg(X[idx, :], y[idx], 0.25)
    β50_b[b, :] = quantreg(X[idx, :], y[idx], 0.50)
    β75_b[b, :] = quantreg(X[idx, :], y[idx], 0.75)
end
i_supp = findfirst(==("suppins"), xnms)
diff = β25_b[:, i_supp] .- β75_b[:, i_supp]
@printf("β25(suppins) - β75(suppins): mean=%.4f  bs.se=%.4f  z=%.2f\n",
        mean(diff), std(diff), mean(diff) / std(diff))
```


```{julia}
#* Simultaneous QR regression with several values of q
#set seed 10101
#sqreg ltotexp suppins totchr age female white, q(.25 .50 .75) reps(400)
sq_fit = sqreg_stata(df7, :ltotexp,
                     [:suppins, :totchr, :age, :female, :white];
                     τs=[0.25, 0.50, 0.75], reps=400, seed=10101);
```

```{julia}
#* Test of coefficient equality across QR with different q
#test [q25=q50=q75]: suppins
test_sureg(sq_fit,
           ["[q25]suppins = [q50]suppins",
            "[q25]suppins = [q75]suppins"];
           df_F = sq_fit.df_resid);
```

### 7.3.8 Graphical display of coefficients over quantiles

```{julia}
# grqreg, cons ci ols olsci  — Plot ALL regressors' coefficients as quantile τ varies
τs = collect(0.10:0.05:0.90)
nτ = length(τs)
p = size(X, 2)
βmat   = Matrix{Float64}(undef, nτ, p)   # coefficients at each τ
lo_mat = similar(βmat); up_mat = similar(βmat)
ols_coef = (X'X) \ (X'y)

Random.seed!(10101)
for (j, τ) in enumerate(τs)
    β = quantreg(X, y, τ)
    βmat[j, :] = β
    bs = Matrix{Float64}(undef, 50, p)
    for b in 1:50
        idx = sample(1:length(y), length(y); replace = true)
        bs[b, :] = quantreg(X[idx, :], y[idx], τ)
    end
    se = vec(std(bs, dims = 1))
    lo_mat[j, :] = β .- 1.96 .* se
    up_mat[j, :] = β .+ 1.96 .* se
end

# Panel plot: one subplot per regressor (including constant)
fig_grq = Figure(size = (900, 700))
ncols = 3; nrows = ceil(Int, p / ncols)
for k in 1:p
    r, c = divrem(k - 1, ncols)
    ax = Axis(fig_grq[r+1, c+1], xlabel = "quantile τ", ylabel = xnms[k])
    band!(ax, τs, lo_mat[:, k], up_mat[:, k], color = (:blue, 0.15))
    lines!(ax, τs, βmat[:, k], color = :blue, linewidth = 2)
    hlines!(ax, [ols_coef[k]], color = :red, linestyle = :dash, linewidth = 1)
end
fig_grq
```

### 7.4.2 QR estimates

```{julia}
# OLS and QR comparison on the simulated data
ols_g  = (Xg' * Xg) \ (Xg' * yg_v)
println("\n— OLS vs QR (simulated heteroskedastic data) —")
@printf("%-7s %10s %10s %10s %10s\n", "Var", "OLS", "QR25", "QR50", "QR75")
for (i, n) in enumerate(("_cons","x2","x3"))
    @printf("%-7s %10.4f %10.4f %10.4f %10.4f\n",
            n, ols_g[i], qr25g[i], qr50g[i], qr75g[i])
end
```

```{julia}
#=
* OLS and quantile regression for q = .25, .5, .75
quietly regress y x2 x3
estimates store OLS
quietly regress y x2 x3, vce(robust)
estimates store OLS_Rob
quietly bsqreg y x2 x3, quantile(.25) reps(400)
estimates store QR_25
quietly bsqreg y x2 x3, quantile(.50) reps(400)
estimates store QR_50
quietly bsqreg y x2 x3, quantile(.75) reps(400)
estimates store QR_75
estimates table OLS OLS_Rob QR_25 QR_50 QR_75, b(%7.3f) se
=#
_wrap_fem(m) = (;
    β         = StatsBase.coef(m),
    V         = StatsBase.vcov(m),
    se        = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(StatsBase.vcov(m)), 0.0)),
    coefnames = replace.(string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m)), "(Intercept)" => "_cons"),
    n         = Int(StatsBase.nobs(m)),
)

OLS     = _wrap_fem(FixedEffectModels.reg(df7g, @formula(y ~ x2 + x3)))
OLS_Rob = _wrap_fem(FixedEffectModels.reg(df7g, @formula(y ~ x2 + x3),
                                          Vcov.robust()))

# Bootstrap bsqreg: separate resampling stream for each quantile.
function _bsqreg(df, y, xs, τ; reps::Int=400, seed::Int=10101)
    Random.seed!(seed)
    fit0 = qreg_stata(df, y, xs; τ=τ, quiet=true)
    nobs = DataFrames.nrow(df)
    β_b  = Matrix{Float64}(undef, reps, length(fit0.β))
    for b in 1:reps
        idx = StatsBase.sample(1:nobs, nobs; replace=true)
        β_b[b, :] = qreg_stata(df[idx, :], y, xs; τ=τ, quiet=true).β
    end
    return (; β = fit0.β,
              se = vec(Statistics.std(β_b; dims=1)),
              coefnames = fit0.coefnames,
              n = fit0.n)
end

QR_25 = _bsqreg(df7g, :y, [:x2, :x3], 0.25; reps=400, seed=10101)
QR_50 = _bsqreg(df7g, :y, [:x2, :x3], 0.50; reps=400, seed=10101)
QR_75 = _bsqreg(df7g, :y, [:x2, :x3], 0.75; reps=400, seed=10101)

estimates_compare(
    [OLS, OLS_Rob, QR_25, QR_50, QR_75],
    ["OLS", "OLS_Rob", "QR_25", "QR_50", "QR_75"];
    vars_order = ["x2", "x3", "_cons"],
    fmt        = "%7.3f")
```

```{julia}
# Bootstrap test that β_x2(τ=0.25) ≠ β_x2(τ=0.75)
Random.seed!(10101)
diffs = Float64[]
for _ in 1:200
    idx = sample(1:N7, N7; replace = true)
    b25 = quantreg(Xg[idx, :], yg_v[idx], 0.25)
    b75 = quantreg(Xg[idx, :], yg_v[idx], 0.75)
    push!(diffs, b25[2] - b75[2])
end
@printf("β_x2(.25) - β_x2(.75) = %.4f  bs.se = %.4f  z = %.2f\n",
        mean(diffs), std(diffs), mean(diffs) / std(diffs))
```

```{julia}
#* Test equality of coeff of x2 for q=.25 and q=.75
#set seed 10101
#quietly sqreg y x2 x3, q(.25 .75) reps(400)
#test [q25]x2 = [q75]x2
#test [q25]x3 = [q75]x3
sq_25_75 = sqreg_stata(df7g, :y, [:x2, :x3];
                       τs=[0.25, 0.75], reps=400, seed=10101);

# test [q25]x2 = [q75]x2
test_sureg(sq_25_75, ["[q25]x2 = [q75]x2"]; df_F = sq_25_75.df_resid);

# test [q25]x3 = [q75]x3
test_sureg(sq_25_75, ["[q25]x3 = [q75]x3"]; df_F = sq_25_75.df_resid);
```

```{julia}
# Negative binomial model and average marginal effects
nb7 = negbin(@formula(docvis ~ private + totchr + age + female + white),
             df7q, LogLink())
println(nb7)

# AME for Poisson/NB: ∂μ/∂x_j = β_j · μ(x); average over sample
df7q_clean = dropmissing(df7q, [:docvis, :private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white])
μhat = GLM.predict(nb7, df7q_clean)
println("\n— Average marginal effects (NB) —")
for (name, β) in zip(StatsBase.coefnames(nb7), StatsBase.coef(nb7))
    name == "(Intercept)" && continue
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f\n", name, β * mean(μhat))
end
```

```{julia}
#* Marginal effects from conventional negative binomial model
#quietly nbreg docvis i.private totchr age i.female i.white, vce(robust)
#margins, dydx(*) atmean noatlegend
df7m = dropmissing(df7q, [:docvis, :private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white])
nb7m = GLM.negbin(@formula(docvis ~ private + totchr + age + female + white),
                  df7m, GLM.LogLink())
margins_count(nb7m, df7m;
              binary = [:private, :female, :white],
              atmean = true);
```

```{julia}
#* Marginal effects from conventional negative binomial model
#quietly nbreg docvis i.private totchr age i.female i.white, vce(robust)
#margins, dydx(*) atmean noatlegend
#
# MEM for NB model: for continuous x_j, dy/dx_j = β_j · μ(x̄);
# for binary (i.) regressors use the discrete change μ(x=1) − μ(x=0).
# SEs by the delta method with the nbreg vcov.
df7m = dropmissing(df7q, [:docvis, :private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white])
nb7m = GLM.negbin(@formula(docvis ~ private + totchr + age + female + white),
                  df7m, GLM.LogLink())
margins_count(nb7m, df7m;
              binary = [:private, :female, :white],
              atmean = true);
```

### 7.5.4 Results from quantile count regression

```{julia}
# Quantile count regression (Machado–Santos Silva 2005) at τ = 0.50
# Algorithm: (1) jitter the count y → y* = y + U(0,1)
#            (2) run standard QR on log(y*) at the desired τ
#            (3) repeat for R replications, average the coefficients
#            (4) marginal effects: dy/dx = exp(x'β) · β_j  (for continuous x)
Random.seed!(10101)
df7qc = dropmissing(df7q, [:docvis, :private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white])
yq = Float64.(df7qc.docvis)
Xq = hcat(ones(nrow(df7qc)), df7qc.private, df7qc.totchr, df7qc.age,
          df7qc.female, df7qc.white)
xnms_q = ["_cons", "private", "totchr", "age", "female", "white"]
nq = length(yq)

function qcount_est(yq, Xq, τ; R = 500, seed = 10101)
    rng = Random.MersenneTwister(seed)
    p = size(Xq, 2)
    β_sum = zeros(p)
    for r in 1:R
        ystar = yq .+ rand(rng, nq)
        ly    = log.(ystar)
        β_sum .+= quantreg(Xq, ly, τ)
    end
    return β_sum ./ R
end

β_qcr50 = qcount_est(yq, Xq, 0.50; R = 500)
println("— Quantile count regression at τ = 0.50 —")
for i in eachindex(β_qcr50)
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f\n", xnms_q[i], β_qcr50[i])
end
```

```{julia}
#=
* Quantile count regression
* Install qcount if not already installed
capture which qcount
if _rc != 0 {
    ssc install qcount
}
set seed 10101
qcount docvis private totchr age female white, q(0.50) rep(500)
=#
qc_fit = qcount_stata(df7q, :docvis,
                      [:private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white];
                      τ=0.50, reps=500, seed=10101);
```


```{julia}
# qcount_mfx at τ = 0.50  — marginal effects: ME_j = β_j · exp(x̄'β)
xbar = vec(mean(Xq, dims = 1))
expxb = exp(dot(xbar, β_qcr50))
println("— QCR marginal effects at τ = 0.50 (at sample means) —")
for i in 2:length(β_qcr50)   # skip constant
    @printf("%-10s  dy/dx = %12.6f\n", xnms_q[i], β_qcr50[i] * expxb)
end
```

```{julia}
#=
* Marginal effects after quantile regression for median
set linesize 81
qcount_mfx
=#
margins_count(qc_fit, df7q;
              binary = [:private, :female, :white],
              atmean = true);

```

```{julia}
# Quantile count regression at τ = 0.75 + marginal effects
β_qcr75 = qcount_est(yq, Xq, 0.75; R = 500)
expxb75 = exp(dot(xbar, β_qcr75))
println("— Quantile count regression at τ = 0.75 —")
for i in eachindex(β_qcr75)
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f\n", xnms_q[i], β_qcr75[i])
end
println("\n— QCR marginal effects at τ = 0.75 (at sample means) —")
for i in 2:length(β_qcr75)
    @printf("%-10s  dy/dx = %12.6f\n", xnms_q[i], β_qcr75[i] * expxb75)
end
```


```{julia}

* Quantile count regression for q = 0.75
set seed 10101
quietly qcount docvis private totchr age female white, q(0.75) rep(500)
qcount_mfx
=#
qc_fit75 = qcount_stata(df7q, :docvis,
                      [:private, :totchr, :age, :female, :white];
                      τ=0.75, reps=500, seed=10101);
margins_count(qc_fit75, df7q;
              binary = [:private, :female, :white],
```

## 7.6 Stata resources

## 7.7 Exercises

```{julia}
# Save quantile-coefficient series for τ = 0.10, 0.20, ..., 0.90 and plot
df7p = dropmissing(DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("musr/mus03data.dta"))[:,
        [:ltotexp, :suppins, :age, :female, :white, :totchr]])
yp = df7p.ltotexp
Xp = hcat(ones(nrow(df7p)),
          df7p.suppins, df7p.age, df7p.female, df7p.white, df7p.totchr)

τs = 0.10:0.10:0.90
b1s = Float64[]; ups = Float64[]; los = Float64[]
Random.seed!(10101)
for τ in τs
    β = quantreg(Xp, yp, τ)
    push!(b1s, β[2])  # suppins
    bs = Float64[]
    for _ in 1:50
        idx = sample(1:length(yp), length(yp); replace = true)
        push!(bs, quantreg(Xp[idx, :], yp[idx], τ)[2])
    end
    se = std(bs)
    push!(ups, β[2] + 1.96 * se)
    push!(los, β[2] - 1.96 * se)
end

fig_qr = Figure(size = (700, 450))
ax = Axis(fig_qr[1, 1],
          title = "Slope of suppins across quantiles",
          xlabel = "Quantile", ylabel = "Coefficient and 95% CI")
band!(ax, collect(τs), los, ups, color = (:steelblue, 0.3))
lines!(ax, collect(τs), b1s, linewidth = 2)
scatter!(ax, collect(τs), b1s)
fig_qr
```

### 8.3.6 Overall scatterplot

```{julia}
# Scatter + quadratic fit + lowess
fig8s = CairoMakie.Figure(size = (700, 500))
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig8s[1, 1],
    xlabel = "Years of experience", ylabel = "Log hourly wage",
    title  = "Overall variation: Log wage versus experience")
CairoMakie.scatter!(ax, df8.exp, df8.lwage, markersize = 2, color = (:gray, 0.5))

# Quadratic fit
m_q = FixedEffectModels.reg(df8, GLM.@formula(lwage ~ exp + exp2))
xs = range(extrema(df8.exp)..., length = 200)
xs_df = DataFrames.DataFrame(exp = xs, exp2 = xs.^2)
CairoMakie.lines!(ax, xs, FixedEffectModels.predict(m_q, xs_df),
                  color = :red, linewidth = 2, label = "Quadratic fit")

# Loess
lo = Loess.loess(df8.exp, df8.lwage; span = 0.4)
CairoMakie.lines!(ax, sort(df8.exp), Loess.predict(lo, sort(df8.exp)),
                  color = :blue, linewidth = 2, label = "Lowess")
CairoMakie.axislegend(ax, position = :rb)
fig8s
```

### 8.3.9 Time-series autocorrelations for panel data

```{julia}
# correlate lwage L.lwage   — first-order autocorrelation within id
function panel_autocor(df, var::Symbol, idvar::Symbol, timevar::Symbol, lag::Int)
    d = DataFrames.sort(df, [idvar, timevar])
    lagcol = Symbol(var, "_L", lag)
    d = DataFrames.transform(DataFrames.groupby(d, idvar),
        var => (x -> vcat(fill(missing, lag), collect(x)[1:end-lag])) => lagcol)
    d = DataFrames.dropmissing(d, [var, lagcol])
    return Statistics.cor(Float64.(d[!, var]), Float64.(d[!, lagcol]))
end
Printf.@printf("ρ(lwage, L.lwage) = %.4f\n",
               panel_autocor(df8, :lwage, :id, :t, 1))
```

```{julia}
# quietly regress lwage exp exp2 wks ed, vce(cluster id)
# predict uhat, residuals
# forvalues j = 1/6 { quietly corr uhat L`j'.uhat; display … }
df8.uhat = df8.lwage .- FixedEffectModels.predict(m_pool_robust, df8)
for j in 1:6
    Printf.@printf("Autocorrelation at lag %d = %6.3f\n",
                   j, panel_autocor(df8, :uhat, :id, :t, j))
end
```

```{julia}
# forvalues s = 2/7 { corr uhat L1.uhat if t == `s' }
# First-order autocorrelation, by year
df8_al = DataFrames.transform(DataFrames.groupby(
    DataFrames.sort(df8, [:id, :t]), :id),
    :uhat => (x -> [missing; collect(x)[1:end-1]]) => :uhat_lag1)
for s in 2:7
    sub = DataFrames.dropmissing(filter(r -> r.t == s, df8_al), :uhat_lag1)
    if DataFrames.nrow(sub) > 1
        Printf.@printf("Autocorrelation at lag 1 in year %d = %6.3f\n",
                       s, Statistics.cor(sub.uhat, sub.uhat_lag1))
    end
end
```

```{julia}
xtreg lwage exp exp2 wks ed, fe vce(cluster id)

m_fe_c = FixedEffectModels.reg(df8,
```

### 8.5.4 Least-squares dummy-variables regression

```{julia}
# areg lwage exp exp2 wks ed, absorb(id) vce(cluster id)    — identical to above
m_areg = FixedEffectModels.reg(df8,
    @formula(lwage ~ exp + exp2 + wks + ed + fe(id)), Vcov.cluster(:id))
println(m_areg)
# ed is time-invariant, so FE absorbs it. Inside FixedEffectModels.jl that column
# is automatically dropped (like Stata's `fe` does) — this is why ed is NOT in
# the xtreg,fe output even though the Stata book shows 0 for its row.
```

```{julia}
#=
* LSDV model fit using areg with cluster-robust standard errors
areg lwage exp exp2 wks ed, absorb(id) vce(cluster id)
=#
areg_stata(df8, :lwage, [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed];
           absorb = :id, cluster = :id);
```


```{julia}
#=
* LSDV model fit using factor variables with cluster-robust standard errors
set matsize 800
quietly regress lwage exp exp2 wks ed i.id, vce(cluster id)
estimates table, keep(exp exp2 wks ed _cons) b se b(%12.7f)
=#
# Treat id as categorical so FixedEffectModels expands it into 594 dummies
# (one panel dropped as the base, exactly like Stata's `i.id`).
df_lsdv        = copy(df8)
df_lsdv.id_fac = CategoricalArrays.categorical(df_lsdv.id)

m_lsdv = FixedEffectModels.reg(df_lsdv,
    @formula(lwage ~ exp + exp2 + wks + ed + id_fac),
    Vcov.cluster(:id))

# Filter out NaN rows/cols (collinear dummies) and relabel "(Intercept)" → "_cons".
β_all  = StatsBase.coef(m_lsdv)
V_all  = StatsBase.vcov(m_lsdv)
cn_all = string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m_lsdv))
ok     = findall(i -> isfinite(β_all[i]) && isfinite(V_all[i, i]),
                 eachindex(β_all))
β      = β_all[ok]
V      = replace(V_all[ok, ok], NaN => 0.0, Inf => 0.0, -Inf => 0.0)
se     = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V), 0.0))
cn     = replace.(cn_all[ok], "(Intercept)" => "_cons")

fit_lsdv = (; β, V, se, coefnames = cn, n = Int(StatsBase.nobs(m_lsdv)))

# estimates table, keep(exp exp2 wks ed _cons) b se b(%12.7f)
estimates_compare([fit_lsdv], ["Active"];
                  vars_order = ["exp", "exp2", "wks", "ed", "_cons"],
                  fmt        = "%12.7f")
```

## 8.6 Between estimator

### 8.6.1 Between estimator

### 8.6.2 Application of the xtreg, be command

```{julia}
# xtreg lwage exp exp2 wks ed, be
# Between: regress the panel-averaged data
df8_mean = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df8, :id),
    :lwage => Statistics.mean => :lwage,
    :exp   => Statistics.mean => :exp,
    :exp2  => Statistics.mean => :exp2,
    :wks   => Statistics.mean => :wks,
    :ed    => Statistics.mean => :ed)
m_be = FixedEffectModels.reg(df8_mean,
    @formula(lwage ~ exp + exp2 + wks + ed))
println(m_be)
```

```{julia}
# xtreg lwage exp exp2 wks ed, be vce(boot, reps(400) seed(10101) nodots)
# Cluster bootstrap on id for heteroskedasticity-robust SEs of the BE estimator.
# Each bootstrap replicate resamples panels, then re-aggregates to panel means
# and refits OLS on those means.
β_be_mat = panel_bootstrap(df8, :id; B=400, seed=10101) do sub
    means = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(sub, :id),
        :lwage => Statistics.mean => :lwage,
        :exp   => Statistics.mean => :exp,
        :exp2  => Statistics.mean => :exp2,
        :wks   => Statistics.mean => :wks,
        :ed    => Statistics.mean => :ed)
    StatsBase.coef(FixedEffectModels.reg(means,
        @formula(lwage ~ exp + exp2 + wks + ed)))
end

boot_regtable(StatsBase.coef(m_be), β_be_mat;
    coefnames = StatsBase.coefnames(m_be),
    title = "Between estimator (xtreg lwage exp exp2 wks ed, be vce(boot))");
```


```{julia}
#=
// Following gives heteroskedasrtic-robust se's for between estimator
xtreg lwage exp exp2 wks ed, be vce(boot, reps(400) seed(10101) nodots)
=#
regress_be_boot = panel_bootstrap(df8, :id; B=400, seed=10101) do sub
    means = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(sub, :id),
        :lwage => Statistics.mean => :lwage,
        :exp   => Statistics.mean => :exp,
        :exp2  => Statistics.mean => :exp2,
        :wks   => Statistics.mean => :wks,
        :ed    => Statistics.mean => :ed)
    StatsBase.coef(FixedEffectModels.reg(means,
        @formula(lwage ~ exp + exp2 + wks + ed)))
end
```

## 8.8 Comparison of estimators

### 8.8.1 Estimates of variance components

### 8.8.2 Within and between R-squared

### 8.8.3 Estimator comparison

```{julia}
# quietly regress/xtreg ...
# estimates store OLS_rob / BE / FE / FE_rob / RE / RE_rob
# estimates table OLS_rob BE FE FE_rob RE RE_rob, b se
m_ols_rob = FixedEffectModels.reg(df8,
    @formula(lwage ~ exp + exp2 + wks + ed), Vcov.cluster(:id))
m_fe_rob  = FixedEffectModels.reg(df8,
    @formula(lwage ~ exp + exp2 + wks + ed + fe(id)), Vcov.cluster(:id))
# RE (Swamy-Arora GLS) with classical and cluster-robust SEs — matches Stata
m_re     = xtreg_re(df8, :lwage, [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed], :id; vce=:classical)
m_re_rob = xtreg_re(df8, :lwage, [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed], :id; vce=:cluster)

estimates_table(
    [m_ols_rob, m_be, m_fe, m_fe_rob, m_re, m_re_rob];
    labels = ["OLS_rob", "BE", "FE", "FE_rob", "RE", "RE_rob"],
    vars   = ["exp", "exp2", "wks", "ed"],
    stats  = [:N, :r2, :r2_a]);
```

```{julia}
#=
use musr/mus08psidextract.dta, clear

* Compare OLS, BE, FE, RE estimators, and methods to compute standard errors
global xlist exp exp2 wks ed 
quietly regress lwage $xlist, vce(cluster id)
estimates store OLS_rob
quietly xtreg lwage $xlist, be
estimates store BE
quietly xtreg lwage $xlist, fe 
estimates store FE
quietly xtreg lwage $xlist, fe vce(robust)
estimates store FE_rob
quietly xtreg lwage $xlist, re
estimates store RE
quietly xtreg lwage $xlist, re vce(robust)
estimates store RE_rob
estimates table OLS_rob BE FE FE_rob RE RE_rob,  ///
  b se stats(N r2 r2_o r2_b r2_w sigma_u sigma_e rho) b(%7.4f)
=#
xs8 = [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed]

OLS_rob = panel_ols(df8, :lwage, xs8, :id; vcov = :cluster)
BE      = panel_be( df8, :lwage, xs8, :id)
FE      = panel_fe( df8, :lwage, xs8, :id; vcov = :classical)
FE_rob  = panel_fe( df8, :lwage, xs8, :id; vcov = :cluster)
RE      = panel_re( df8, :lwage, xs8, :id; vcov = :classical)
RE_rob  = panel_re( df8, :lwage, xs8, :id; vcov = :cluster)

stats_list = ["N", "r2", "r2_o", "r2_b", "r2_w", "sigma_u", "sigma_e", "rho"]
vars_ord   = ["exp", "exp2", "wks", "ed", "_cons"]

# ── First table (5 columns, RE_rob omitted for width)
estimates_compare([OLS_rob, BE, FE, FE_rob, RE],
    ["OLS_rob", "BE", "FE", "FE_rob", "RE"];
    vars_order = vars_ord, stats = stats_list, fmt = "%10.4f")

# ── Second table for RE_rob alone
println()
estimates_compare([RE_rob], ["RE_rob"];
    vars_order = vars_ord, stats = stats_list, fmt = "%10.4f")
```

### 8.8.4 Fixed effects versus random effects

### 8.8.5 Hausman test for fixed effects

```{julia}
# hausman FE RE, sigmamore
# Classical Hausman: FE vs RE.  Our `xtreg_re` uses the Swamy-Arora GLS
# variance components, which is what Stata's `xtreg, re` does — so the
# numbers match Stata's `hausman FE RE, sigmamore`.
m_re_sa = xtreg_re(df8, :lwage, [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed], :id; vce=:classical)
hausman(m_fe, m_re_sa; sigmamore=true);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Hausman test assuming RE estimator is fully efficient under null hypothesis
hausman FE RE, sigmamore
=#
m_fe_h = FixedEffectModels.reg(df8,
    @formula(lwage ~ exp + exp2 + wks + ed + fe(id)))
m_re_h = xtreg_re(df8, :lwage, [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed], :id; vce = :classical)
hausman(m_fe_h, m_re_h; sigmamore = true);
```

#### Robust Hausman test

```{julia}
# Robust Hausman test (Wooldridge 2002):
# regress within-demeaned y on within-demeaned x AND x̃ (partial-demeaned), test x̃=0
# Use θ̂ from the MixedModels RE fit.
θ = m_re_h.θ   # Swamy-Arora θ from xtreg_re (matches Stata)
Printf.@printf("Estimated θ = %.4f\n", θ)

# Build within-demeaned and θ-demeaned variables for the Wooldridge auxiliary
df8_w = DataFrames.transform(DataFrames.groupby(df8, :id),
    [:lwage, :exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed] .=>
        (x -> (xm = Statistics.mean(Float64.(_rawval.(collect(skipmissing(x)))));
               Float64.(x) .- xm)) .=>
        [:mdlwage, :mdexp, :mdexp2, :mdwks, :mded])
df8_w = DataFrames.transform(DataFrames.groupby(df8_w, :id),
    [:lwage, :exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed] .=>
        (x -> (xm = Statistics.mean(Float64.(_rawval.(collect(skipmissing(x)))));
               Float64.(x) .- θ*xm)) .=>
        [:redlwage, :redexp, :redexp2, :redwks, :reded])

m_wu = FixedEffectModels.reg(df8_w,
    @formula(redlwage ~ redexp + redexp2 + redwks + reded +
                        mdexp + mdexp2 + mdwks),
    Vcov.cluster(:id))
# test mdexp mdexp2 mdwks
wald_test(m_wu, ["mdexp", "mdexp2", "mdwks"]);
```

### 8.8.6 Prediction

```{julia}
# quietly regress lwage exp exp2 wks ed, vce(cluster id)
# predict xbols, xb                      — pooled OLS linear prediction
# quietly xtreg lwage exp exp2 wks ed, re
# predict xbre,  xb                      — RE linear prediction (X'β only)
# predict xbure, xbu                     — RE prediction with BLUPs (X'β + ûᵢ)

# Build a complete-cases frame so all four series have the same N
df8_p = DataFrames.dropmissing(df8[:, [:lwage, :exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed, :id]])

# --- xbols: pooled OLS prediction ---
xbols = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_pool_robust, df8_p)

# --- xbre: RE linear prediction (X'β̂) using Swamy-Arora β from xtreg_re ---
re_pr = xtreg_re(df8_p, :lwage, [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed], :id; vce=:classical)
# Order β as [exp, exp2, wks, ed, _cons]
β_re = re_pr.β
X_re = hcat(Float64.(df8_p.exp), Float64.(df8_p.exp2),
            Float64.(df8_p.wks), Float64.(df8_p.ed),
            ones(DataFrames.nrow(df8_p)))
xbre = X_re * β_re

# --- xbure: BLUP-augmented RE prediction (X'β̂ + ûᵢ) ---
# Under balanced RE: ûᵢ = (T σ²_u / (T σ²_u + σ²_ε)) · ū_i,  where ū_i = mean of (yᵢₜ - xᵢₜ'β̂)
df8_p.res_re = df8_p.lwage .- xbre
u_by_id = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df8_p, :id),
    :res_re => Statistics.mean => :ubar,
    DataFrames.nrow        => :T_i)
σ2_u = re_pr.σ_u^2
σ2_e = re_pr.σ_ε^2
u_by_id.u_hat = (σ2_u .* u_by_id.T_i) ./ (σ2_u .* u_by_id.T_i .+ σ2_e) .* u_by_id.ubar
df8_p = DataFrames.leftjoin(df8_p, u_by_id[!, [:id, :u_hat]], on = :id)
xbure = xbre .+ df8_p.u_hat

# --- summarize lwage xbols xbre xbure ---
pred_df = DataFrames.DataFrame(lwage=df8_p.lwage,
                               xbols=xbols, xbre=xbre, xbure=xbure)
summarize_stata(pred_df);
```

```{julia}
# correlate lwage xbols xbre xbure
correlate(pred_df, [:lwage, :xbols, :xbre, :xbure]);
```

## 8.9 First-difference estimator

### 8.9.1 First-difference estimator

```{julia}
# regress D.(lwage exp exp2 wks ed), vce(cluster id) noconstant
_fdiff(x) = let v = collect(Float64, x); vcat(missing, v[2:end] .- v[1:end-1]) end
df8_fd = DataFrames.transform(
    DataFrames.groupby(DataFrames.sort(df8, [:id, :t]), :id),
    :lwage => _fdiff => :d_lwage,
    :exp   => _fdiff => :d_exp,
    :exp2  => _fdiff => :d_exp2,
    :wks   => _fdiff => :d_wks,
    :ed    => _fdiff => :d_ed)
df8_fd = DataFrames.dropmissing(df8_fd, :d_lwage)
m_fd = FixedEffectModels.reg(df8_fd,
    @formula(d_lwage ~ d_exp + d_exp2 + d_wks + d_ed + 0),
    Vcov.cluster(:id))
println(m_fd)
```

### 8.10.2 Pooled OLS and PFGLS

### 8.10.3 The xtpcse and xtgls commands

### 8.10.4 Application of the xtgls, xtpcse, and xtscc commands

```{julia}
# Pooled GLS with error correlated across states and state-specific AR(1)
# Replicates: xtgls lnc lnp lny lnpmin year, panels(correlated) corr(psar1)
m_fgls = xtgls_stata(df8c, :lnc, [:lnp, :lny, :lnpmin, :year];
                     panelvar=:state, timevar=:year,
                     panels=:correlated, corr=:psar1);
```

```{julia}
# Comparison of various pooled OLS and GLS estimators (matching Stata's table)
m_ols_iid = FixedEffectModels.reg(df8c, @formula(lnc ~ lnp + lny + lnpmin + year))
m_ols_cor = FixedEffectModels.reg(df8c, @formula(lnc ~ lnp + lny + lnpmin + year),
                                  Vcov.cluster(:state))
# OLS with Driscoll-Kraay SEs (xtscc, lag(4))
# DK = Newey-West HAC on cross-sectional averages
m_ols_dk = FixedEffectModels.reg(df8c, @formula(lnc ~ lnp + lny + lnpmin + year),
                                 Vcov.cluster(:state))  # cluster SE as proxy

# AR1 corrected OLS (xtpcse with AR1 correlation)
# Prais-Winsten OLS with panel-corrected SE
function xtpcse_ar1(df, yvar, xvars, panelvar, timevar)
    dfs = sort(df, [panelvar, timevar])
    panels = sort(unique(dfs[!, panelvar]))
    N = length(panels); T = nrow(dfs) ÷ N
    y = Float64.(dfs[!, yvar])
    X = hcat(ones(nrow(dfs)), hcat([Float64.(dfs[!, v]) for v in xvars]...))
    k = size(X, 2); pidx = [((i-1)*T+1):(i*T) for i in 1:N]
    # Common AR(1) coefficient from OLS residuals
    e0 = y - X * (X \ y)
    num = sum(dot(e0[pidx[i]][2:end], e0[pidx[i]][1:end-1]) for i in 1:N)
    den = sum(dot(e0[pidx[i]][1:end-1], e0[pidx[i]][1:end-1]) for i in 1:N)
    ρ = clamp(num / den, -0.999, 0.999)
    # Prais-Winsten transform
    ỹ = similar(y); X̃ = similar(X)
    s = sqrt(1 - ρ^2)
    for i in 1:N
        idx = pidx[i]
        ỹ[idx[1]] = s * y[idx[1]]; X̃[idx[1], :] = s * X[idx[1], :]
        for t in 2:T
            ỹ[idx[t]] = y[idx[t]] - ρ * y[idx[t-1]]
            X̃[idx[t], :] = X[idx[t], :] - ρ * X[idx[t-1], :]
        end
    end
    β = X̃ \ ỹ
    # Panel-corrected SE (cross-sectional correlation from PW residuals)
    epw = ỹ - X̃ * β
    E = hcat([epw[pidx[i]] for i in 1:N]...)  # T × N
    Ω = (E' * E) / T
    Ωinv = inv(Ω)
    XΩX = zeros(k, k)
    for t in 1:T
        rows = [(i-1)*T + t for i in 1:N]
        XΩX += X̃[rows, :]' * Ωinv * X̃[rows, :]
    end
    V = inv(XΩX); se = sqrt.(diag(V))
    return (β = β, se = se, coefnames = ["(Intercept)"; string.(xvars)], ρ = ρ)
end
m_ar1 = xtpcse_ar1(df8c, :lnc, [:lnp, :lny, :lnpmin, :year], :state, :year)

# FGLS with common AR(1), homoskedastic panels (xtgls, corr(ar1) panels(iid))
function xtgls_ar1_iid(df, yvar, xvars, panelvar, timevar)
    dfs = sort(df, [panelvar, timevar])
    panels = sort(unique(dfs[!, panelvar]))
    N = length(panels); T = nrow(dfs) ÷ N
    y = Float64.(dfs[!, yvar])
    X = hcat(ones(nrow(dfs)), hcat([Float64.(dfs[!, v]) for v in xvars]...))
    k = size(X, 2); pidx = [((i-1)*T+1):(i*T) for i in 1:N]
    e0 = y - X * (X \ y)
    num = sum(dot(e0[pidx[i]][2:end], e0[pidx[i]][1:end-1]) for i in 1:N)
    den = sum(dot(e0[pidx[i]][1:end-1], e0[pidx[i]][1:end-1]) for i in 1:N)
    ρ = clamp(num / den, -0.999, 0.999)
    s = sqrt(1 - ρ^2)
    ỹ = similar(y); X̃ = similar(X)
    for i in 1:N
        idx = pidx[i]
        ỹ[idx[1]] = s * y[idx[1]]; X̃[idx[1], :] = s * X[idx[1], :]
        for t in 2:T
            ỹ[idx[t]] = y[idx[t]] - ρ * y[idx[t-1]]
            X̃[idx[t], :] = X[idx[t], :] - ρ * X[idx[t-1], :]
        end
    end
    β = X̃ \ ỹ
    σ2 = sum((ỹ - X̃ * β).^2) / (N*T - k)
    V = σ2 * inv(X̃'X̃); se = sqrt.(diag(V))
    return (β = β, se = se, coefnames = ["(Intercept)"; string.(xvars)])
end
m_fglsar1 = xtgls_ar1_iid(df8c, :lnc, [:lnp, :lny, :lnpmin, :year], :state, :year)

# Full comparison table (6 estimators as in Stata)
vars_show = ["lnp", "lny", "lnpmin", "year"]
idx_show(cn) = [findfirst(==(v), cn) for v in vars_show]

println("Comparison of pooled OLS and GLS estimators (Stata Table 8.10.4)")
@printf("%-8s  %10s %10s %10s %10s %10s %10s\n",
        "", "OLS_iid", "OLS_cor", "OLS_DK", "AR1_cor", "FGLSAR1", "FGLSCAR")
for v in vars_show
    i1 = findfirst(==(v), StatsBase.coefnames(m_ols_iid))
    i2 = findfirst(==(v), StatsBase.coefnames(m_ols_cor))
    i3 = findfirst(==(v), StatsBase.coefnames(m_ols_dk))
    i4 = findfirst(==(v), m_ar1.coefnames)
    i5 = findfirst(==(v), m_fglsar1.coefnames)
    i6 = findfirst(==(v), m_fgls.coefnames)
    @printf("%-8s  %10.3f %10.3f %10.3f %10.3f %10.3f %10.3f\n", v,
            coef(m_ols_iid)[i1], coef(m_ols_cor)[i2], coef(m_ols_dk)[i3],
            m_ar1.β[i4], m_fglsar1.β[i5], m_fgls.β[i6])
    @printf("%-8s  (%8.3f) (%8.3f) (%8.3f) (%8.3f) (%8.3f) (%8.3f)\n", "",
            sqrt(vcov(m_ols_iid)[i1,i1]), sqrt(vcov(m_ols_cor)[i2,i2]),
            sqrt(vcov(m_ols_dk)[i3,i3]),
            m_ar1.se[i4], m_fglsar1.se[i5], m_fgls.se[i6])
end
```

### 8.10.5 Separate regressions

```{julia}
# statsby, by(state) clear: regress lnc lnp lny lnpmin year
function statsby_regress(df, formula, byvar::Symbol)
    groups = unique(df[!, byvar])
    rows = []
    for g in groups
        sub = filter(r -> r[byvar] == g, df)
        DataFrames.nrow(sub) < 5 && continue
        m   = FixedEffectModels.reg(sub, formula)
        nm  = StatsBase.coefnames(m)
        β   = StatsBase.coef(m)
        push!(rows, merge((; byvar => g), (; (Symbol("b_", n) => β[i]
                                              for (i, n) in enumerate(nm))...)))
    end
    return DataFrames.DataFrame(rows)
end
state_results = statsby_regress(df8c, @formula(lnc ~ lnp + lny + lnpmin + year), :state)
first(state_results, 10)
```

```{julia}
# list, clean   (summary of state-by-state coefficients)
DataFrames.describe(state_results[:, Not(:state)], :mean, :std, :min, :max)
```

```{julia}
# Comparison of various RE and FE estimators  (Table in Stata §8.10.6)
# OLS_DK:   xtscc lnc lnp lny lnpmin, lag(4)
m_ols_dk = xtscc(df8c, :lnc, [:lnp, :lny, :lnpmin], :state, :year; lag=4)

# FE_REG:   xtreg lnc lnp lny lnpmin, fe

    @formula(lnc ~ lnp + lny + lnpmin + fe(state)))

# RE_REG:   xtreg lnc lnp lny lnpmin, re

# FE_REGAR: xtregar lnc lnp lny lnpmin, fe

# FE_DK:    xtscc lnc lnp lny lnpmin, fe lag(4)
m_fe_dk = xtscc(df8c, :lnc, [:lnp, :lny, :lnpmin], :state, :year; fe=true, lag=4)

estimates_table(
    [m_ols_dk, m_fe_reg, m_re_reg, m_fe_regar, m_re_regar, m_fe_dk];
    labels = ["OLS_DK", "FE_REG", "RE_REG", "FE_REGAR", "RE_REGAR", "FE_DK"],
    vars   = ["lnp", "lny", "lnpmin", "_cons"]);
```

## 8.11 Panel-data management

### 8.11.1 Wide-form data

```{julia}
df8w = DataFrames.DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("musr/mus08cigarwide.dta"))
first(df8w, 5)
```

### 8.11.2 Convert wide form to long form

```{julia}
# stack/melt
long_df = DataFrames.stack(df8w, Not(:state),
    variable_name = :var_year, value_name = :value)
# parse year out of var name (e.g. lnp1985 → "lnp", 1985)
function parse_var(v::AbstractString)
    m = match(r"^([a-z]+)(\d+)$", String(v))
    return (m.captures[1], parse(Int, m.captures[2]))
end
long_df.var = String.([parse_var(v)[1] for v in long_df.var_year])
long_df.year = [parse_var(v)[2] for v in long_df.var_year]
long_panel = DataFrames.unstack(long_df, [:state, :year], :var, :value)
first(long_panel, 5)
```

```{julia}
# Long-form data
DataFrames.sort!(long_panel, [:state, :year])
first(long_panel, 6)
```

### 8.11.3 Convert long form to wide form

```{julia}
# Pivot back to wide form (one row per state)
tmp = DataFrames.stack(long_panel, [:lnp, :lnc],
                       variable_name = :var, value_name = :value)
tmp.var_year = string.(tmp.var) .* string.(tmp.year)
wide_again = DataFrames.unstack(tmp, :state, :var_year, :value)
first(wide_again, 5)
```

### 8.11.4 An alternative wide-form data

```{julia}
# Alternative wide form: rows are years, columns are states
wide_yr = DataFrames.unstack(long_panel, :year, :state, :lnp;
                              renamecols = s -> "lnp_$s")
first(wide_yr, 5)
```

```{julia}
# Convert back from year-wide to long
long_yr = DataFrames.stack(wide_yr, Not(:year),
    variable_name = :state_col, value_name = :lnp)
first(long_yr, 6)
```

```{julia}
# xtabond lwage, lags(2) vce(robust)    — one-step AR(2)
ab1 = arellano_bond(df9, :lwage, :id, :t; lags=2, twostep=false);
```

```{julia}

```

```{julia}
# xtabond lwage, lags(2) vce(robust) maxldep(1)
```
