## 15.4 Multinomial logit model

### 15.4.1 Multinomial logit in Julia

There is no canonical Julia package for multinomial logit; we implement it
manually with `Optim.jl`.

### 15.4.2 Application

```{julia}
# Multinomial logit MLE with base outcome 1 (beach).
# Parameters: for each non-base alternative j ∈ {2, 3, 4}, the coefficients
# (intercept, β_income).
function mnl_nll(params, y, X, J)
    n, k = size(X)
    # params is length k*(J-1): reshape to k×(J-1)
    B = reshape(params, k, J - 1)
    # Linear predictor for each non-base alt
    eta = X * B          # n × (J-1)
    # P_j = exp(η_j) / (1 + Σ exp(η_l))
    den = 1.0 .+ sum(exp.(eta), dims = 2)
    ll  = 0.0
    for i in 1:n
        if y[i] == 1     # base alternative
            ll += -log(den[i])
        else
            j = Int(y[i]) - 1
            ll += eta[i, j] - log(den[i])
        end
    end
    return -ll
end

df15c = DataFrames.dropmissing(df15[:, [:mode, :income]])
y15 = Float64.(df15c.mode)
X15 = hcat(ones(DataFrames.nrow(df15c)), Float64.(df15c.income))
J = 4  # number of alternatives

res_mnl = Optim.optimize(p -> mnl_nll(p, y15, X15, J), zeros(2 * (J - 1)),
                          Optim.LBFGS(); autodiff = :forward)
β_mnl = reshape(Optim.minimizer(res_mnl), 2, J - 1)
println("\n— Multinomial logit (base = mode 1, beach) —")
for (j, alt) in enumerate(("pier", "private", "charter"))
    @printf("%-10s  intercept = %9.6f   income = %9.6f\n",
            alt, β_mnl[1, j], β_mnl[2, j])
end
@printf("logL = %.4f\n", -Optim.minimum(res_mnl))
```

```{julia}
# Wald test of joint significance of income (across all 3 non-base equations)
# Need the Hessian for SEs
H = ForwardDiff_Hess = nothing
try
    using ForwardDiff
    H = ForwardDiff.hessian(p -> mnl_nll(p, y15, X15, J), Optim.minimizer(res_mnl))
    V = LinearAlgebra.inv(H)
    inc_idx = [2, 4, 6]   # income coefficients
    βi = Optim.minimizer(res_mnl)[inc_idx]
    Vi = V[inc_idx, inc_idx]
    W  = βi' * LinearAlgebra.inv(Vi) * βi
    @printf("Joint Wald χ²(3) for income = %.3f, p = %.4f\n",
            W, 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(3), W))
catch
    println("Need ForwardDiff for Hessian — skipping Wald test")
end
```

### 15.4.3 Coefficient interpretation

```{julia}
# Relative-risk ratios = exp(β)
println("\n— Relative-risk ratios (exp(β)) —")
for (j, alt) in enumerate(("pier", "private", "charter"))
    @printf("%-10s  exp(intercept) = %9.6f   exp(β_income) = %9.6f\n",
            alt, exp(β_mnl[1, j]), exp(β_mnl[2, j]))
end
```

### 15.4.4 Predicted probabilities

```{julia}
# Save model for later sections
const MNL_β = β_mnl
```

```{julia}
# Predicted probabilities for each mode
function mnl_probs(B, X, J)
    eta = X * B
    den = 1.0 .+ sum(exp.(eta), dims = 2)
    P   = zeros(size(X, 1), J)
    P[:, 1] = 1.0 ./ den
    for j in 2:J
        P[:, j] = exp.(eta[:, j - 1]) ./ den
    end
    return P
end

P_mnl = mnl_probs(β_mnl, X15, J)
df15c.pmlogit1 = P_mnl[:, 1]
df15c.pmlogit2 = P_mnl[:, 2]
df15c.pmlogit3 = P_mnl[:, 3]
df15c.pmlogit4 = P_mnl[:, 4]

# Compare with empirical frequencies
println("\n— Predicted vs observed shares —")
for j in 1:J
    @printf("Mode %d: pred mean = %.4f, observed share = %.4f\n",
            j, Statistics.mean(P_mnl[:, j]),
            Statistics.mean(df15c.mode .== j))
end
```

### 15.4.5 Marginal effects

```{julia}
# Average predicted probability of mode 3 (private)
@printf("Sample average P(mode = 3) = %.4f\n",
        Statistics.mean(P_mnl[:, 3]))
```

```{julia}
# AME of income on P(mode = 3): finite difference
function ame_mnl(B, X, J, j_target; col = 2, h = 0.01)
    X_p = copy(X); X_p[:, col] .+= h
    X_m = copy(X); X_m[:, col] .-= h
    P_p = mnl_probs(B, X_p, J)
    P_m = mnl_probs(B, X_m, J)
    return Statistics.mean((P_p[:, j_target] .- P_m[:, j_target]) ./ (2h))
end
@printf("AME of income on P(mode=3) = %.6f\n",
        ame_mnl(β_mnl, X15, J, 3))
```

```{julia}
# AME of income on P(mode=3) at the mean of regressors
xbar = [1.0; Statistics.mean(df15c.income)]
P_xbar = mnl_probs(β_mnl, reshape(xbar, 1, 2), J)[1, :]
# At-mean derivative of P_3 w.r.t. income (analytical):
#   ∂P_3/∂x = P_3 · (β_3 - Σ_l β_l P_l)
β_inc = [0.0; β_mnl[2, :]]   # β for base alt is 0
mean_β = sum(β_inc .* P_xbar)
@printf("MEM of income on P(mode=3) = %.6f\n",
        P_xbar[3] * (β_mnl[2, 2] - mean_β))
```

## 15.5 Conditional logit model

### 15.5.1 Wide-form to long-form conversion

```{julia}
# Show wide-form data
df15[1:1, [:mode, :price, :pbeach, :ppier, :pprivate, :pcharter]]
```

```{julia}
# Reshape from wide to long: one row per (case, alternative)
df15.id = 1:DataFrames.nrow(df15)

function wide_to_long(df)
    rows = []
    for r in eachrow(df)
        for (j, alt) in enumerate(("beach", "pier", "private", "charter"))
            push!(rows, (id       = r.id,
                          fishmode = alt,
                          d        = (r.mode == j ? 1 : 0),
                          p        = r[Symbol("p$alt")],
                          q        = r[Symbol("q$alt")],
                          income   = r.income,
                          mode     = r.mode))
        end
    end
    return DataFrames.DataFrame(rows)
end
df15long = wide_to_long(df15)
DataFrames.describe(df15long)
```

```{julia}
# First case after reshape
df15long[1:4, :]
```

### 15.5.2 Conditional logit (manual)

### 15.5.3 The dogit model

### 15.5.4 Application

```{julia}
# Conditional logit MLE: P_j = exp(α_j + β_p p_j + β_q q_j + γ_j income) /
#                              Σ_l exp(...)
# Base alternative: beach (so α_beach, γ_beach = 0)
function clogit_nll(params, df_long, base = "beach")
    alts = unique(df_long.fishmode)
    K_alt = length(alts) - 1   # number of non-base alternatives
    # Layout: β_p, β_q, then α_j (K_alt) and γ_j (K_alt) for non-base alts
    β_p = params[1]
    β_q = params[2]
    α   = params[3:(2 + K_alt)]
    γ   = params[(3 + K_alt):(2 + 2 * K_alt)]
    nonbase = filter(!=(base), alts)
    α_dict  = Dict(zip(nonbase, α))
    γ_dict  = Dict(zip(nonbase, γ))
    α_dict[base] = 0.0
    γ_dict[base] = 0.0

    ll = 0.0
    for grp in DataFrames.groupby(df_long, :id)
        utils = β_p .* grp.p .+ β_q .* grp.q .+
                [α_dict[a] for a in grp.fishmode] .+
                [γ_dict[a] for a in grp.fishmode] .* grp.income
        max_u = maximum(utils)
        sum_e = sum(exp.(utils .- max_u))
        i_chosen = findfirst(grp.d .== 1)
        ll += (utils[i_chosen] - max_u) - log(sum_e)
    end
    return -ll
end

p0 = zeros(2 + 2 * 3)   # 2 alt-specific (p, q) + 3 alt fixed effects + 3 income slopes
res_cl = Optim.optimize(p -> clogit_nll(p, df15long), p0,
                         Optim.LBFGS(); autodiff = :forward,
                         Optim.Options(iterations = 200))
β_cl = Optim.minimizer(res_cl)
println("\n— Conditional logit (base = beach) —")
@printf("β_p = %.6f\n", β_cl[1])
@printf("β_q = %.6f\n", β_cl[2])
nonbase = ["pier", "private", "charter"]
for (i, alt) in enumerate(nonbase)
    @printf("α_%-8s = %9.6f   γ_%-8s = %9.6f\n",
            alt, β_cl[2 + i], alt, β_cl[5 + i])
end
@printf("logL = %.4f\n", -Optim.minimum(res_cl))
```

```{julia}
# Save coefficients for comparison later
const CL_β = β_cl
```

### 15.5.5 Relationship to multinomial logit

### 15.5.6 Coefficient interpretation

### 15.5.7 Predicted probabilities

```{julia}
# Predicted probabilities for the conditional logit
function clogit_probs(params, df_long, base = "beach")
    alts = unique(df_long.fishmode)
    nonbase = filter(!=(base), alts)
    β_p = params[1]; β_q = params[2]
    α = Dict(zip(nonbase, params[3:5])); α[base] = 0.0
    γ = Dict(zip(nonbase, params[6:8])); γ[base] = 0.0
    probs = Float64[]
    for grp in DataFrames.groupby(df_long, :id)
        utils = β_p .* grp.p .+ β_q .* grp.q .+
                [α[a] for a in grp.fishmode] .+
                [γ[a] for a in grp.fishmode] .* grp.income
        max_u = maximum(utils)
        e = exp.(utils .- max_u)
        append!(probs, e ./ sum(e))
    end
    return probs
end
df15long.pasclogit = clogit_probs(β_cl, df15long)
DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df15long, :fishmode),
    :d         => Statistics.mean => :mean_d,
    :pasclogit => Statistics.mean => :mean_pred,
    :pasclogit => Statistics.std  => :sd_pred)
```

### 15.5.8 Marginal effects

```{julia}
# Alternatives present in the model
println("Alternatives: ", unique(df15long.fishmode))
```

```{julia}
# Marginal effect at mean of price (analytic, own and cross effects)
mean_p_beach = Statistics.mean(filter(r -> r.fishmode == "beach", df15long).p)
@printf("Mean p_beach = %.4f\n", mean_p_beach)
println("Analytical own/cross MEs require derivatives — see manual finite diff below.")
```

```{julia}
# Average MEs of beach price change (manual finite difference)
df15long_p = copy(df15long)
sd_p = Statistics.std(df15long_p.p)
δ    = sd_p / 1000
df15long_p.p[df15long_p.fishmode .== "beach"] .+= δ
df15long_p.pnew = clogit_probs(β_cl, df15long_p)
df15long_p.dpdbeach = (df15long_p.pnew .- df15long_p.pasclogit) ./ δ
DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df15long_p, :fishmode),
    :dpdbeach => Statistics.mean => :mean_AME)
```

## 15.6 Nested logit model

### 15.6.1 Relaxing the IIA assumption

### 15.6.2 NL model

```{julia}
# nlogit: nesting structure
# shore → {pier, beach}    boat → {private, charter}
println("Nesting tree:")
println("  shore → pier, beach")
println("  boat  → private, charter")
```

### 15.6.3 Nested logit in Julia

### 15.6.4 Model estimates

```{julia}
# Nested logit via manual MLE (Optim.jl)
# P(j in nest k) = exp(V_j/λ_k) / D_k   where D_k = Σ_{l∈k} exp(V_l/λ_k)
# IV_k = log(D_k)   (inclusive value)
# P(nest k) = exp(λ_k IV_k + W_k) / Σ_m exp(λ_m IV_m + W_m)
# V_j = β_price p_j + β_catch c_j + ASC_j
nests = Dict("shore" => ["pier","beach"], "boat" => ["private","charter"])
nest_of = Dict("pier"=>"shore","beach"=>"shore","private"=>"boat","charter"=>"boat")
alts = ["beach","pier","private","charter"]   # beach = base for ASC
n_alts = length(alts)

function nlogit_nll(θ, df, alts, nests, nest_of, base_alt)
    # θ layout: [β_price, β_catch, ASC_pier, ASC_private, ASC_charter, λ_shore, λ_boat]
    β_p, β_c = θ[1], θ[2]
    asc = Dict(base_alt => 0.0, "pier" => θ[3], "private" => θ[4], "charter" => θ[5])
    λ = Dict("shore" => exp(θ[6]), "boat" => exp(θ[7]))   # ensure λ > 0
    ids = unique(df.id)
    ll = 0.0
    for id in ids
        sub = filter(r -> r.id == id, df)
        chosen = sub.fishmode[findfirst(==(1), sub.d)]
        V = Dict{String,Float64}()
        for a in alts
            row = findfirst(==(a), sub.fishmode)
            row === nothing && continue
            V[a] = β_p * sub.p[row] + β_c * sub.q[row] + get(asc, a, 0.0)
        end
        # Nest-level inclusive values
        IV = Dict{String,Float64}()
        for (k, members) in nests
            IV[k] = log(sum(exp(V[m] / λ[k]) for m in members if haskey(V, m); init=1e-300))
        end
        denom_top = sum(exp(λ[k] * IV[k]) for (k, _) in nests)
        k_chosen = nest_of[chosen]
        p_nest = exp(λ[k_chosen] * IV[k_chosen]) / denom_top
        p_alt_in_nest = exp(V[chosen] / λ[k_chosen]) /
                        sum(exp(V[m] / λ[k_chosen]) for m in nests[k_chosen] if haskey(V, m))
        ll += log(max(p_nest * p_alt_in_nest, 1e-300))
    end
    return -ll
end

res_nl = Optim.optimize(
    θ -> nlogit_nll(θ, df15long, alts, nests, nest_of, "beach"),
    vcat(zeros(5), [0.0, 0.0]),
    Optim.LBFGS(); autodiff=:forward,
    Optim.Options(iterations=500))

θ_nl = Optim.minimizer(res_nl)
cn_nl = ["price", "catch", "ASC_pier", "ASC_private", "ASC_charter",
         "λ_shore (log)", "λ_boat (log)"]
λ_shore = exp(θ_nl[6]); λ_boat = exp(θ_nl[7])
println("Nested logit  (shore/boat nesting)")
Printf.@printf("  N = %d (cases)   Log-likelihood = %.4f\n",
               length(unique(df15long.id)), -Optim.minimum(res_nl))
Printf.@printf("  λ_shore = %.4f   λ_boat = %.4f\n", λ_shore, λ_boat)
PrettyTables.pretty_table(
    DataFrames.DataFrame(variable=cn_nl, estimate=θ_nl);
    formatters=PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.6f", 2))
```

## 15.7 Multinomial probit model

### 15.7.1 MNP

### 15.7.2 The mprobit-style model

```{julia}
# mprobit mode income, baseoutcome(1)
# Multinomial probit via GHK (Geweke-Hajivassiliou-Keane) simulator.
# J alternatives, base = 1.  Utility: V_j = x'β_j + ε_j, ε ~ N(0, I).
# Probability of choice j for case i requires (J-1)-dimensional normal CDF,
# approximated by the GHK sequential conditioning algorithm.

function ghk_prob(V, Σ_chol, R; rng = Random.GLOBAL_RNG)
    # V = [V_1 - V_base, V_2 - V_base, ...] length J-1
    # Σ_chol = Cholesky factor of the differenced error covariance
    # Returns simulated probability that all elements of η > -V (choice = base)
    J1 = length(V)
    prob = 0.0
    for r in 1:R
        p_r = 1.0
        η = zeros(J1)
        for j in 1:J1
            a_j = -V[j] - dot(Σ_chol[j, 1:j-1], η[1:j-1])
            a_j /= Σ_chol[j, j]
            Φ_a = Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), a_j)
            p_r *= (1 - Φ_a)
            # Truncated normal draw (below a_j is failure region)
            u = Φ_a + (1 - Φ_a) * rand(rng)
            η[j] = Distributions.quantile(Distributions.Normal(), clamp(u, 1e-10, 1-1e-10))
        end
        prob += p_r
    end
    return prob / R
end

# Estimate MNP for fishing data (case-invariant regressors: income)
# J=4 modes: beach(1), pier(2), private(3), charter(4); base=1
# β_j = [const_j, income_j] for j=2,3,4  (3 equations × 2 params = 6)
df15_mnp = filter(r -> !ismissing(r.mode) && !ismissing(r.income), df15)
modes = sort(unique(df15_mnp.mode))
J = length(modes)
rng_ghk = Random.MersenneTwister(10101)

function mnp_loglik(θ, df, modes; R = 50, rng = rng_ghk)
    J = length(modes); J1 = J - 1
    β = reshape(θ[1:J1*2], 2, J1)   # 2 × (J-1)
    # Identity error covariance for differenced utilities (simplest case)
    L = Matrix{Float64}(LinearAlgebra.I, J1, J1)
    ll = 0.0
    for i in 1:nrow(df)
        x = [1.0, df.income[i]]
        yi = df.mode[i]
        base = modes[1]
        # Compute V_j - V_base for j ≠ base
        Vdiff = [dot(β[:, j], x) for j in 1:J1]
        # Probability of chosen alternative requires permuting so chosen = base
        ci = findfirst(==(yi), modes)
        if ci == 1
            # Base alternative: prob = P(all Vdiff + η < 0)
            p = ghk_prob(-Vdiff, L, R; rng = rng)
        else
            # Reorder so chosen alternative is the reference
            Vdiff_new = Float64[]
            for j in 1:J
                j == ci && continue
                v_j = (j == 1 ? 0.0 : dot(β[:, j-1], x))
                v_ci = dot(β[:, ci-1], x)
                push!(Vdiff_new, v_j - v_ci)
            end
            p = ghk_prob(-Vdiff_new, L, R; rng = rng)
        end
        ll += log(max(p, 1e-12))
    end
    return -ll
end

# Optimize (takes some time with R=50 draws)
θ0_mnp = zeros(2 * (J - 1))
res_mnp = Optim.optimize(θ -> mnp_loglik(θ, df15_mnp, modes; R = 50, rng = rng_ghk),
                          θ0_mnp, Optim.NelderMead(),
                          Optim.Options(iterations = 2000, show_trace = false))
β_mnp = reshape(Optim.minimizer(res_mnp)[1:2*(J-1)], 2, J-1)
println("— Multinomial probit (mprobit mode income, baseoutcome(1)) —")
println("Log-likelihood = ", round(-Optim.minimum(res_mnp), digits=2))
for j in 1:(J-1)
    @printf("Mode %d vs base: _cons = %8.4f  income = %8.4f\n",
            modes[j+1], β_mnp[1, j], β_mnp[2, j])
end
```

### 15.8.3 Data preparation

```{julia}
# Add case-invariant regressors interacted with alternative dummies
df15long.dpier    = Int.(df15long.fishmode .== "pier")
df15long.dprivate = Int.(df15long.fishmode .== "private")
df15long.dcharter = Int.(df15long.fishmode .== "charter")
df15long.ypier    = df15long.dpier    .* df15long.income
df15long.yprivate = df15long.dprivate .* df15long.income
df15long.ycharter = df15long.dcharter .* df15long.income
DataFrames.describe(df15long[:, [:dpier, :dprivate, :dcharter,
                                  :ypier, :yprivate, :ycharter]])
```

### 15.8.4 Application

```{julia}
# mixlogit d q dpier dprivate ypier yprivate, group(id) rand(p)
# Mixed (random-parameters) logit via simulated MLE.
# p has a normally distributed random coefficient: β_p ~ N(b_p, σ_p²)
# Fixed coefficients: q, dpier, dprivate, ypier, yprivate
# We use Halton draws for simulation integration.

function halton_seq(n, base=2)
    h = zeros(n)
    for i in 1:n
        f, r = 1.0, 0.0
        idx = i
        while idx > 0
            f /= base
            r += f * (idx % base)
            idx = idx ÷ base
        end
        h[i] = r
    end
    return h
end

function mixlogit_nll(θ, df, id_col, choice_col, alts;
                       fixed_vars, rand_vars, R = 100, base_alt = "pier")
    n_fix = length(fixed_vars)
    n_rnd = length(rand_vars)
    β_fix = θ[1:n_fix]              # fixed coefficients
    b_rnd = θ[n_fix+1:n_fix+n_rnd]  # means of random coefficients
    σ_rnd = exp.(θ[n_fix+n_rnd+1:end])  # log-σ → σ (positive)

    groups = unique(df[!, id_col])
    N = length(groups)
    halton = halton_seq(R)
    η_draws = Distributions.quantile.(Distributions.Normal(), clamp.(halton, 0.001, 0.999))

    ll = 0.0
    for g in groups
        rows = findall(df[!, id_col] .== g)
        y = df[rows, choice_col]
        J = length(rows)
        sim_prob = 0.0
        for r in 1:R
            β_r = b_rnd .+ σ_rnd .* η_draws[r]
            V = zeros(J)
            for (jj, row) in enumerate(rows)
                for (fi, fv) in enumerate(fixed_vars)
                    V[jj] += β_fix[fi] * df[row, fv]
                end
                for (ri, rv) in enumerate(rand_vars)
                    V[jj] += β_r[ri] * df[row, rv]
                end
            end
            eV = exp.(V .- maximum(V))
            probs = eV ./ sum(eV)
            chosen = findfirst(y .== 1)
            if !isnothing(chosen)
                sim_prob += probs[chosen]
            end
        end
        ll += log(max(sim_prob / R, 1e-12))
    end
    return -ll
end

# Estimate on 3-alternative subset (charter dropped)
sub_mix = filter(r -> r.fishmode != "charter", df15long)
θ0_mix = zeros(5 + 1 + 1)  # 5 fixed + 1 mean + 1 log-σ for p
res_mix = Optim.optimize(
    θ -> mixlogit_nll(θ, sub_mix, :id, :d, unique(sub_mix.fishmode);
                       fixed_vars = [:q, :dpier, :dprivate, :ypier, :yprivate],
                       rand_vars  = [:p], R = 100, base_alt = "pier"),
    θ0_mix, Optim.NelderMead(),
    Optim.Options(iterations = 5000, show_trace = false))
θ_mix = Optim.minimizer(res_mix)
println("— Mixed logit (random coefficient on p, 3 alternatives) —")
println("Log-likelihood = ", round(-Optim.minimum(res_mix), digits=2))
fix_names = ["q", "dpier", "dprivate", "ypier", "yprivate"]
for (i, n) in enumerate(fix_names)
    @printf("%-12s (fixed)  %10.4f\n", n, θ_mix[i])
end
@printf("%-12s (mean)   %10.4f\n", "p", θ_mix[6])
@printf("%-12s (σ)      %10.4f\n", "p", exp(θ_mix[7]))
```

```{julia}
end
```

```{julia}
# summarize hlthstat age linc ndisease
summarize_stata(df15h, [:hlthstat, :age, :linc, :ndisease]);
```

### 15.9.2 Ordered outcomes

### 15.9.3 Ordered logit MLE

```{julia}
# Ordered logit MLE manually via Optim.jl
# P(y=j) = Λ(τ_j - x'β) - Λ(τ_{j-1} - x'β)
# with τ_0 = -∞, τ_J = ∞
function ologit_nll(params, y, X, J)
    n, k = size(X)
    β   = params[1:k]
    # Cut points: enforce τ_1 < τ_2 < ... via cumulative exp
    raw = params[(k + 1):(k + J - 1)]
    τ   = cumsum([raw[1]; exp.(raw[2:end])])
    Λ(z) = 1 / (1 + exp(-z))
    ll  = 0.0
    for i in 1:n
        η = LinearAlgebra.dot(X[i, :], β)
        if y[i] == 1
            p = Λ(τ[1] - η)
        elseif y[i] == J
            p = 1 - Λ(τ[J - 1] - η)
        else
            p = Λ(τ[Int(y[i])] - η) - Λ(τ[Int(y[i]) - 1] - η)
        end
        ll += log(max(p, eps()))
    end
    return -ll
end

df15h_c = DataFrames.dropmissing(df15h[:, [:hlthstat, :age, :linc, :ndisease]])
y15h = Float64.(df15h_c.hlthstat)
X15h = hcat(Float64.(df15h_c.age), Float64.(df15h_c.linc), Float64.(df15h_c.ndisease))
J_o  = 3

res_ol = Optim.optimize(p -> ologit_nll(p, y15h, X15h, J_o),
                          zeros(3 + (J_o - 1)),
                          Optim.LBFGS(); autodiff = :forward,
                          Optim.Options(iterations = 200))
θ_ol  = Optim.minimizer(res_ol)
β_ol  = θ_ol[1:3]
raw_τ = θ_ol[4:5]
τ_ol  = cumsum([raw_τ[1]; exp(raw_τ[2])])
# Numerical Hessian → SEs
H_ol  = Optim.hessian!(Optim.TwiceDifferentiable(
    p -> ologit_nll(p, y15h, X15h, J_o), θ_ol; autodiff=:forward), θ_ol)
se_ol = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(LinearAlgebra.inv(H_ol)), 0.0))

println("Ordered logit regression")
Printf.@printf("  N = %d   Log-likelihood = %.4f\n\n", length(y15h), -Optim.minimum(res_ol))
PrettyTables.pretty_table(
    DataFrames.DataFrame(
        variable = ["age", "linc", "ndisease", "/cut1", "/cut2"],
        Coef     = vcat(β_ol, τ_ol),
        SE       = vcat(se_ol[1:3], se_ol[4], se_ol[5] * exp(raw_τ[2])),
        z        = vcat(β_ol ./ se_ol[1:3], [NaN, NaN]));
    formatters = PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.6f", 2:4))
```

### 15.9.4 Predicted probabilities

```{julia}
# Calculate P(y=1), P(y=2), P(y=3) for each observation
function ologit_probs(β, τ, X, J)
    n = size(X, 1)
    P = zeros(n, J)
    Λ(z) = 1 / (1 + exp(-z))
    for i in 1:n
        η = LinearAlgebra.dot(X[i, :], β)
        P[i, 1] = Λ(τ[1] - η)
        for j in 2:(J - 1)
            P[i, j] = Λ(τ[j] - η) - Λ(τ[j - 1] - η)
        end
        P[i, J] = 1 - Λ(τ[J - 1] - η)
    end
    return P
end

P_ol = ologit_probs(β_ol, τ_ol, X15h, J_o)
@printf("Observed: hlthpf=%.4f hlthg=%.4f hlthe=%.4f\n",
        Statistics.mean(df15h_c.hlthstat .== 1),
        Statistics.mean(df15h_c.hlthstat .== 2),
        Statistics.mean(df15h_c.hlthstat .== 3))
@printf("Predicted: P1=%.4f P2=%.4f P3=%.4f\n",
        Statistics.mean(P_ol[:, 1]),
        Statistics.mean(P_ol[:, 2]),
        Statistics.mean(P_ol[:, 3]))
```

### 15.9.5 Marginal effects

```{julia}
# AME of each regressor on P(y=3)
function ame_ol(β, τ, X, J, j_target; col, h = 1e-4)
    Xp = copy(X); Xp[:, col] .+= h
    Xm = copy(X); Xm[:, col] .-= h
    Pp = ologit_probs(β, τ, Xp, J)
    Pm = ologit_probs(β, τ, Xm, J)
    return Statistics.mean((Pp[:, j_target] .- Pm[:, j_target]) ./ (2h))
end
println("\n— AME on P(hlthstat = 3, excellent) —")
for (i, n) in enumerate(("age", "linc", "ndisease"))
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f\n", n, ame_ol(β_ol, τ_ol, X15h, J_o, 3; col = i))
end
```

## 15.10 Multivariate outcomes

### 15.10.1 Bivariate probit

```{julia}
# Two binary dependent variables: hlthe and dmdu
df15h.dmdu_int = Int.(df15h.dmdu)
df15h.hlthe_int = Int.(df15h.hlthe)
ct = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df15h, [:hlthe_int, :dmdu_int]),
                         DataFrames.nrow => :freq)
println(ct)
@printf("ρ(hlthe, dmdu) = %.4f\n",
        Statistics.cor(df15h.hlthe, Float64.(df15h.dmdu)))
```

```{julia}
# Bivariate probit MLE: requires bivariate-normal CDF.
# Implement using Distributions.MvNormal CDF (which uses owens_t for n=2).
function bvn_cdf(a, b, ρ)
    # Bivariate normal CDF P(X ≤ a, Y ≤ b) for std normals with correlation ρ
    Distributions.cdf(Distributions.MvNormal([0.0, 0.0], [1.0 ρ; ρ 1.0]),
                       [a, b])
end

function biprobit_nll(params, y1, y2, X)
    n, k = size(X)
    β1 = params[1:k]
    β2 = params[(k + 1):(2k)]
    ρ  = tanh(params[end])  # ensure |ρ| < 1
    ll = 0.0
    for i in 1:n
        η1 = LinearAlgebra.dot(X[i, :], β1)
        η2 = LinearAlgebra.dot(X[i, :], β2)
        s1 = 2 * y1[i] - 1
        s2 = 2 * y2[i] - 1
        p  = bvn_cdf(s1 * η1, s2 * η2, s1 * s2 * ρ)
        ll += log(max(p, eps()))
    end
    return -ll
end

df15h_b = DataFrames.dropmissing(df15h[:, [:hlthe, :dmdu, :age, :linc, :ndisease]])
X_b = hcat(ones(DataFrames.nrow(df15h_b)),
           Float64.(df15h_b.age), Float64.(df15h_b.linc),
           Float64.(df15h_b.ndisease))
y1_b = Float64.(df15h_b.hlthe)
y2_b = Float64.(df15h_b.dmdu)

res_bp = Optim.optimize(p -> biprobit_nll(p, y1_b, y2_b, X_b),
                         vcat(zeros(8), 0.0),
                         Optim.NelderMead(),
                         Optim.Options(iterations = 2000))
println("Bivariate probit (NelderMead):")
println(Optim.minimizer(res_bp))
@printf("Estimated ρ = %.4f\n", tanh(Optim.minimizer(res_bp)[end]))
```

```{julia}
# Predicted probabilities (marginal and joint)
function biprobit_predict(params, X)
    k = size(X, 2)
    β1 = params[1:k]; β2 = params[(k + 1):(2k)]
    ρ  = tanh(params[end])
    n  = size(X, 1)
    p1  = zeros(n); p2  = zeros(n)
    p11 = zeros(n); p10 = zeros(n)
    p01 = zeros(n); p00 = zeros(n)
    for i in 1:n
        η1 = LinearAlgebra.dot(X[i, :], β1)
        η2 = LinearAlgebra.dot(X[i, :], β2)
        p1[i]  = Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), η1)
        p2[i]  = Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), η2)
        p11[i] = bvn_cdf(η1, η2, ρ)
        p10[i] = p1[i] - p11[i]
        p01[i] = p2[i] - p11[i]
        p00[i] = 1 - p1[i] - p2[i] + p11[i]
    end
    return p1, p2, p11, p10, p01, p00
end
p1, p2, p11, p10, p01, p00 = biprobit_predict(Optim.minimizer(res_bp), X_b)
@printf("Marginal P(hlthe=1)  = %.4f  (obs %.4f)\n",
        Statistics.mean(p1), Statistics.mean(y1_b))
@printf("Marginal P(dmdu=1)   = %.4f  (obs %.4f)\n",
        Statistics.mean(p2), Statistics.mean(y2_b))
@printf("Joint P(1,1)         = %.4f\n", Statistics.mean(p11))
```

```{julia}
# Bivariate probit with different regressor sets per equation
println("Different regressors per equation: extend params layout — see above.")
```

### 15.10.2 Nonlinear SUR

```{julia}
# NLS-SUR = stack the two nonlinear equations and minimize Σ residuals²
function nlsur_obj(params, df)
    a = params[1:4]; b = params[5:8]
    Φ(x) = Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), x)
    ξ1 = Φ.(a[1] .* df.age .+ a[2] .* df.linc .+ a[3] .* df.ndisease .+ a[4])
    ξ2 = Φ.(b[1] .* df.age .+ b[2] .* df.linc .+ b[3] .* df.ndisease .+ b[4])
    return sum((df.hlthe .- ξ1).^2 .+ (df.dmdu .- ξ2).^2)
end
df_nls = df15h_b
res_nls = Optim.optimize(p -> nlsur_obj(p, df_nls), zeros(8),
                          Optim.NelderMead(),
                          Optim.Options(iterations = 5000))
println("Nonlinear SUR (NelderMead):")
println(Optim.minimizer(res_nls))
```

```{julia}
# Separate probits for comparison
GLM.glm(GLM.@formula(hlthe ~ age + linc + ndisease),
        df15h_b, Distributions.Binomial(), GLM.ProbitLink())
```

```{julia}
GLM.glm(GLM.@formula(dmdu ~ age + linc + ndisease),
        df15h_b, Distributions.Binomial(), GLM.ProbitLink())
```

## 16.3 Tobit model example

### 16.3.1 Data summary

The data come from Deb, Munkin and Trivedi (2006), "Bayesian Analysis of
Two-Part Model with Endogeneity", *Journal of Applied Econometrics*. The
dependent variable `ambexp` is ambulatory medical expenditure (in dollars),
left-censored at zero. Regressors include insurance type, chronic diseases,
age, sex, education and ethnicity.

```{julia}
# Raw data summary
df16 = DataFrames.DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("musr/mus16data.dta"))
DataFrames.describe(df16[:, [:ambexp, :age, :female, :educ, :blhisp,
                              :totchr, :ins]])
```

```{julia}
# Detailed summary of ambexp (skewness, kurtosis, percentiles)
let x = collect(skipmissing(df16.ambexp))
    @printf("N = %d, mean = %.2f, sd = %.2f\n",
            length(x), Statistics.mean(x), Statistics.std(x))
    @printf("p25 = %.1f, p50 = %.1f, p75 = %.1f, p99 = %.1f, max = %.1f\n",
            Statistics.quantile(x, 0.25), Statistics.median(x),
            Statistics.quantile(x, 0.75), Statistics.quantile(x, 0.99),
            maximum(x))
    @printf("skew = %.4f, kurt = %.4f\n",
            StatsBase.skewness(x), StatsBase.kurtosis(x) + 3)
end
```

```{julia}
# Summary of positive expenditures only
let x = collect(skipmissing(df16.ambexp[df16.ambexp .> 0]))
    @printf("N>0 = %d, mean = %.2f, sd = %.2f, p50 = %.1f\n",
            length(x), Statistics.mean(x), Statistics.std(x),
            Statistics.median(x))
end
```

### 16.3.2 Tobit analysis

```{julia}
# Tobit MLE — manual implementation via Optim.jl
# Model: y* = x'β + ε,  y = max(0, y*),  ε ~ N(0, σ²)
function tobit_nll(params, y, X, ll = 0.0)
    n, k = size(X)
    β = params[1:k]
    σ = exp(params[end])
    Φ(z) = Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), z)
    ϕ(z) = Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), z)
    nll = 0.0
    for i in 1:n
        η = LinearAlgebra.dot(X[i, :], β)
        if y[i] > ll
            nll += -log(ϕ((y[i] - η) / σ) / σ)
        else
            nll += -log(max(Φ((ll - η) / σ), eps()))
        end
    end
    return nll
end

xlist = [:age, :female, :educ, :blhisp, :totchr, :ins]
df16c = DataFrames.dropmissing(df16[:, vcat([:ambexp], xlist)])
y16 = Float64.(df16c.ambexp)
X16 = hcat(ones(DataFrames.nrow(df16c)),
           Float64.(df16c.age),    Float64.(df16c.female),
           Float64.(df16c.educ),   Float64.(df16c.blhisp),
           Float64.(df16c.totchr), Float64.(df16c.ins))
xnms16 = ["_cons", "age", "female", "educ", "blhisp", "totchr", "ins"]

# Starting values from OLS
β_ols = (X16' * X16) \ (X16' * y16)
σ_ols = sqrt(sum((y16 .- X16 * β_ols).^2) / (length(y16) - size(X16, 2)))
res_tob = Optim.optimize(p -> tobit_nll(p, y16, X16),
                          vcat(β_ols, log(σ_ols)),
                          Optim.LBFGS(); autodiff = :forward,
                          Optim.Options(iterations = 200))
β_tob = Optim.minimizer(res_tob)[1:size(X16, 2)]
σ_tob = exp(Optim.minimizer(res_tob)[end])
println("\n— Tobit MLE (left-censoring at 0) —")
for i in 1:length(xnms16)
    @printf("%-10s %12.4f\n", xnms16[i], β_tob[i])
end
@printf("σ = %.4f\n", σ_tob)
@printf("logL = %.4f\n", -Optim.minimum(res_tob))
```

### 16.3.3 Prediction after tobit

```{julia}
# Linear prediction x'β (uncensored)
yhatlin = X16 * β_tob
@printf("yhatlin: mean = %.4f, sd = %.4f\n",
        Statistics.mean(yhatlin), Statistics.std(yhatlin))
```

### 16.3.4 Marginal effects

#### Censored / uncensored / truncated expectations

```{julia}
# E[y | x, y > 0] = x'β + σ·λ((x'β)/σ)  with λ = ϕ/Φ
# ME at means via finite differences
function ame_tobit(model_pred, X, col; h = 1e-3)
    Xp = copy(X); Xp[:, col] .+= h
    Xm = copy(X); Xm[:, col] .-= h
    return Statistics.mean((model_pred(Xp) .- model_pred(Xm)) ./ (2h))
end

Φ(z) = Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), z)
ϕ(z) = Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), z)

# (1) E[y | x, y > 0]
function pred_truncated(X)
    η = X * β_tob
    return η .+ σ_tob .* (ϕ.(η ./ σ_tob) ./ Φ.(η ./ σ_tob))
end

# (2) E[y | x] (no censoring) = x'β  (just the linear predictor)
pred_uncensored(X) = X * β_tob

# (3) E[y | x] with censoring at 0: Φ(η/σ)·η + σ·ϕ(η/σ)
function pred_censored(X)
    η = X * β_tob
    return Φ.(η ./ σ_tob) .* η .+ σ_tob .* ϕ.(η ./ σ_tob)
end

# AMEs
println("\n— Marginal effects on E[y|x, y>0] —")
for (i, n) in enumerate(xnms16[2:end])
    @printf("%-10s %12.4f\n", n, ame_tobit(pred_truncated, X16, i + 1))
end
```

```{julia}
# (2) ME on E[y|x]
println("\n— Marginal effects on E[y*|x] (uncensored) —")
for (i, n) in enumerate(xnms16[2:end])
    @printf("%-10s %12.4f\n", n, β_tob[i + 1])  # in linear model, ME = β
end
```

```{julia}
# (3) ME on E[y | 0 < y < 535] — manual finite difference using truncated mean
function pred_truncated_535(X)
    η = X * β_tob
    α = (0.0 .- η) ./ σ_tob
    βu = (535.0 .- η) ./ σ_tob
    Z  = Φ.(βu) .- Φ.(α)
    return η .+ σ_tob .* (ϕ.(α) .- ϕ.(βu)) ./ Z
end
println("\n— Marginal effects on E[y | 0 < y < 535] —")
for (i, n) in enumerate(xnms16[2:end])
    @printf("%-10s %12.4f\n", n, ame_tobit(pred_truncated_535, X16, i + 1))
end
```

#### Left-censored case computed directly

```{julia}
# Direct computation of marginal effects via the formula:
#   ∂E[y|x]/∂x_j = Φ(x'β/σ) · β_j
xbar = vec(Statistics.mean(X16, dims = 1))
mean_xb = LinearAlgebra.dot(xbar, β_tob)
PHI = Φ(mean_xb / σ_tob)
println("\n— Direct computation: dE[y|x]/dx_j = Φ(x'β/σ)·β_j —")
@printf("Φ(x̄'β/σ) = %.4f\n", PHI)
for i in 2:length(xnms16)
    @printf("%-10s %12.4f\n", xnms16[i], PHI * β_tob[i])
end
```

```{julia}
# Same as a nicely-formatted table
println("\n— Marginal effects (formatted) —")
for i in 2:length(xnms16)
    @printf("%-10s %12.4f\n", xnms16[i], PHI * β_tob[i])
end
```

#### Marginal impact on probabilities

```{julia}
# AMEs on P(5000 < ambexp < 10000)
function pred_pr_window(X; lo = 5000.0, hi = 10000.0)
    η = X * β_tob
    return Φ.((hi .- η) ./ σ_tob) .- Φ.((lo .- η) ./ σ_tob)
end

println("\n— Marginal effects on Pr(5000 < ambexp < 10000) —")
for (i, n) in enumerate(xnms16[2:end])
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f\n", n, ame_tobit(pred_pr_window, X16, i + 1))
end
```

## 16.4 Tobit for lognormal data

### 16.4.1 Data example

The dependent variable `lny` is `ln(ambexp)` if positive, with the censored
zeros mapped to a small value `γ - ε` to keep them on the same scale.

```{julia}
# Summary of lambexp (= ln(ambexp) | ambexp > 0)
let x = collect(skipmissing(df16.lambexp))
    @printf("N>0 = %d, mean = %.4f, sd = %.4f\n",
            length(x), Statistics.mean(x), Statistics.std(x))
end
```

### 16.4.2 Setting the censoring point for the log model

```{julia}
# Construct y, dy, lny as in the Stata example
df16.y   = df16.ambexp
df16.dy  = Int.(df16.ambexp .> 0)
df16.lny = ifelse.(df16.dy .== 1, log.(max.(df16.y, eps())), missing)
γ = minimum(skipmissing(df16.lny))
@printf("γ = %.4f\n", γ)
df16.lny = coalesce.(df16.lny, γ - 1e-7)
DataFrames.describe(df16[:, [:y, :lny, :dy]])
```

```{julia}
# Quick summary
DataFrames.describe(df16[:, vcat([:y, :lny, :dy], xlist)])
```

### 16.4.3 Results

```{julia}
# Tobit on lny with left-censoring at γ
df16c2 = DataFrames.dropmissing(df16[:, vcat([:lny, :dy], xlist)])
y16l = df16c2.lny
X16l = hcat(ones(DataFrames.nrow(df16c2)),
            Float64.(df16c2.age),    Float64.(df16c2.female),
            Float64.(df16c2.educ),   Float64.(df16c2.blhisp),
            Float64.(df16c2.totchr), Float64.(df16c2.ins))
β_ols2 = (X16l' * X16l) \ (X16l' * y16l)
σ_ols2 = sqrt(sum((y16l .- X16l * β_ols2).^2) / (length(y16l) - size(X16l, 2)))
res_tob2 = Optim.optimize(p -> tobit_nll(p, y16l, X16l, γ),
                           vcat(β_ols2, log(σ_ols2)),
                           Optim.LBFGS(); autodiff = :forward,
                           Optim.Options(iterations = 300))
β_tob2 = Optim.minimizer(res_tob2)[1:size(X16l, 2)]
σ_tob2 = exp(Optim.minimizer(res_tob2)[end])
println("\n— Tobit on lny (lower censoring at γ) —")
for i in 1:length(xnms16)
    @printf("%-10s %12.4f\n", xnms16[i], β_tob2[i])
end
@printf("σ = %.4f, logL = %.4f\n", σ_tob2, -Optim.minimum(res_tob2))
```

```{julia}
# OLS on lny for comparison
FixedEffectModels.reg(df16c2,
    GLM.@formula(lny ~ age + female + educ + blhisp + totchr + ins))
```

### 16.4.4 Two-limit tobit

```{julia}
# Two-limit Tobit: lower at γ, upper at log(10000)
function two_limit_tobit_nll(params, y, X, ll, ul)
    n, k = size(X)
    β = params[1:k]
    σ = exp(params[end])
    Φ(z) = Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), z)
    ϕ(z) = Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), z)
    nll = 0.0
    for i in 1:n
        η = LinearAlgebra.dot(X[i, :], β)
        if y[i] <= ll
            nll += -log(max(Φ((ll - η) / σ), eps()))
        elseif y[i] >= ul
            nll += -log(max(1 - Φ((ul - η) / σ), eps()))
        else
            nll += -log(ϕ((y[i] - η) / σ) / σ)
        end
    end
    return nll
end

upper = log(10000)
@printf("upper = %.6f\n", upper)
res_tob3 = Optim.optimize(p -> two_limit_tobit_nll(p, y16l, X16l, γ, upper),
                           vcat(β_tob2, log(σ_tob2)),
                           Optim.LBFGS(); autodiff = :forward,
                           Optim.Options(iterations = 300))
β_tob3 = Optim.minimizer(res_tob3)[1:size(X16l, 2)]
σ_tob3 = exp(Optim.minimizer(res_tob3)[end])
println("\n— Two-limit Tobit —")
for i in 1:length(xnms16)
    @printf("%-10s %12.4f\n", xnms16[i], β_tob3[i])
end
@printf("σ = %.4f, logL = %.4f\n", σ_tob3, -Optim.minimum(res_tob3))
```

### 16.4.5 Model diagnostics

### 16.4.6 Tests of normality and homoskedasticity

#### Generalized residuals

```{julia}
# Generalized residuals for the (single-limit) Tobit
xb_g = X16l * β_tob2
threshold = (γ .- xb_g) ./ σ_tob2
λ_v = ϕ.(threshold) ./ Φ.(threshold)
df16c2.threshold = threshold
df16c2.lambda    = λ_v
```

```{julia}
# First-moment generalized residuals
u = (df16c2.lny .- xb_g) ./ σ_tob2
gres1 = ifelse.(df16c2.dy .== 1, u, .-λ_v)
@printf("gres1: mean = %.4f, sd = %.4f\n",
        Statistics.mean(gres1), Statistics.std(gres1))
```

```{julia}
# Second to fourth moment generalized residuals
gres2 = ifelse.(df16c2.dy .== 1, u.^2 .- 1,        .-threshold .* λ_v)
gres3 = ifelse.(df16c2.dy .== 1, u.^3,             .-(2 .+ threshold.^2) .* λ_v)
gres4 = ifelse.(df16c2.dy .== 1, u.^4 .- 3,
                .-(3 .* threshold .+ threshold.^3) .* λ_v)
df16c2.gres1 = gres1; df16c2.gres2 = gres2
df16c2.gres3 = gres3; df16c2.gres4 = gres4
```

#### Test of normality

```{julia}
# Score-augmented test of normality: regress 1 on score components and gres3, gres4
for v in xlist
    df16c2[!, Symbol("score_$v")] = gres1 .* Float64.(df16c2[!, v])
end
df16c2.one = ones(DataFrames.nrow(df16c2))
fmla_norm = GLM.@formula(one ~ gres3 + gres4 + score_age + score_female +
                                score_educ + score_blhisp + score_totchr +
                                score_ins + gres1 + gres2 + 0)
m_norm = FixedEffectModels.reg(df16c2, fmla_norm)
NR2 = DataFrames.nrow(df16c2) * FixedEffectModels.r2(m_norm)
@printf("Normality test: NR² = %.4f, p (χ²₂) = %.4f\n",
        NR2, 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(2), NR2))
```

```{julia}
# Diagnostic summary: scores should have mean ≈ 0 under correct specification
for v in (:gres1, :gres2, :gres3, :gres4)
    @printf("%-8s mean = %12.6f\n", v, Statistics.mean(df16c2[!, v]))
end
```

```{julia}
# Same regression — already shown above
@printf("(Normality test) NR² = %.4f\n", NR2)
```

#### Test of homoskedasticity

```{julia}
# Add additional score terms based on gres2
for v in xlist
    df16c2[!, Symbol("score2_$v")] = gres2 .* Float64.(df16c2[!, v])
end
fmla_het = GLM.@formula(one ~ gres3 + gres4 + score_age + score_female +
                               score_educ + score_blhisp + score_totchr +
                               score_ins + score2_age + score2_female +
                               score2_educ + score2_blhisp + score2_totchr +
                               score2_ins + gres1 + gres2 + 0)
m_het = FixedEffectModels.reg(df16c2, fmla_het)
NR2_h = DataFrames.nrow(df16c2) * FixedEffectModels.r2(m_het)
@printf("Homoskedasticity test: NR² = %.4f, p (χ²₂) = %.4f\n",
        NR2_h, 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(2), NR2_h))
```

```{julia}
# Tobit on lny with left-censoring at 0 (the original example)
res_tob_at0 = Optim.optimize(p -> tobit_nll(p, y16l, X16l, 0.0),
                              vcat(β_tob2, log(σ_tob2)),
                              Optim.LBFGS(); autodiff = :forward,
                              Optim.Options(iterations = 200))
println("Tobit on lny with ll = 0: logL = ",
        -Optim.minimum(res_tob_at0))
```

### 16.4.7 Next step?

## 16.5 Two-part model in logs

### 16.5.1 Model structure

### 16.5.2 Part 1 specification

```{julia}
# Probit for selection (positive ambexp)
df16c3 = DataFrames.dropmissing(df16[:, vcat([:dy, :y, :lny], xlist)])
m_probit_dy = GLM.glm(GLM.@formula(dy ~ age + female + educ + blhisp + totchr + ins),
                      df16c3, Distributions.Binomial(), GLM.ProbitLink())
ll_probit = GLM.loglikelihood(m_probit_dy)
@printf("Probit logL = %.4f\n", ll_probit)
```

### 16.5.3 Part 2 of the two-part model

```{julia}
# OLS of lny on regressors among positives
df_pos = filter(r -> r.dy == 1, df16c3)
m_ols_pos = FixedEffectModels.reg(df_pos,
    GLM.@formula(lny ~ age + female + educ + blhisp + totchr + ins))
println(m_ols_pos)
ll_lognormal = -DataFrames.nrow(df_pos)/2 * (log(2π) + 1) -
               DataFrames.nrow(df_pos)/2 * log(FixedEffectModels.rss(m_ols_pos) /
                                                DataFrames.nrow(df_pos))
@printf("Log-normal part logL = %.4f\n", ll_lognormal)
```

```{julia}
# Total two-part model log-likelihood
@printf("Two-part total logL = %.4f\n", ll_probit + ll_lognormal)
```

```{julia}
# Heteroskedasticity (Breusch-Pagan) and skewness/kurtosis tests on residuals
yhat_pos = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_ols_pos, df_pos)
df_pos.uhat = df_pos.lny .- yhat_pos
df_pos.uhat2 = df_pos.uhat.^2
m_hettest = FixedEffectModels.reg(df_pos,
    GLM.@formula(uhat2 ~ age + female + educ + blhisp + totchr + ins))
LM = DataFrames.nrow(df_pos) * FixedEffectModels.r2(m_hettest)
@printf("BP heteroskedasticity LM = %.4f, p = %.4f\n",
        LM, 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(6), LM))
@printf("skewness(uhat) = %.4f, kurtosis(uhat) = %.4f\n",
        StatsBase.skewness(df_pos.uhat),
        StatsBase.kurtosis(df_pos.uhat) + 3)
```

## 16.6 Selection model

### 16.6.1 Model structure and assumptions

### 16.6.2 ML estimation

### 16.6.3 Estimation without exclusion restrictions

```{julia}
# Heckman MLE (manual implementation via Optim.jl)
# y_1 = 1{γ'z + u_1 > 0},  y_2 = β'x + u_2  (observed only when y_1 = 1)
# (u_1, u_2) ~ N(0, [[1, ρσ], [ρσ, σ²]])
function heckman_nll(params, y_obs, d, X, Z)
    k_x = size(X, 2); k_z = size(Z, 2)
    γ = params[1:k_z]
    β = params[(k_z + 1):(k_z + k_x)]
    logσ = params[end - 1]; atanh_ρ = params[end]
    σ = exp(logσ); ρ = tanh(atanh_ρ)
    Φ(z) = Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Normal(), z)
    ϕ(z) = Distributions.pdf(Distributions.Normal(), z)
    nll = 0.0
    for i in 1:length(d)
        z_i = LinearAlgebra.dot(Z[i, :], γ)
        if d[i] == 1
            x_i = LinearAlgebra.dot(X[i, :], β)
            r_i = (y_obs[i] - x_i) / σ
            arg = (z_i + ρ * r_i) / sqrt(max(1 - ρ^2, eps()))
            nll += -(log(ϕ(r_i) / σ) + log(max(Φ(arg), eps())))
        else
            nll += -log(max(1 - Φ(z_i), eps()))
        end
    end
    return nll
end

# Same regressors in both equations (no exclusion restrictions)
df16h = DataFrames.dropmissing(df16[:, vcat([:dy, :lny], xlist)])
y_h = df16h.lny  # zero-rows are not used
d_h = Int.(df16h.dy)
X_h = hcat(ones(DataFrames.nrow(df16h)),
           Float64.(df16h.age), Float64.(df16h.female),
           Float64.(df16h.educ), Float64.(df16h.blhisp),
           Float64.(df16h.totchr), Float64.(df16h.ins))
Z_h = X_h     # no exclusion restrictions

p0_h = vcat(zeros(7), zeros(7), 0.0, 0.0)
res_heck = Optim.optimize(p -> heckman_nll(p, y_h, d_h, X_h, Z_h), p0_h,
                           Optim.NelderMead(),
                           Optim.Options(iterations = 5000))
println("Heckman MLE (no exclusion restriction): NLL = ",
        Optim.minimum(res_heck))
println("ρ̂ = ", tanh(Optim.minimizer(res_heck)[end]))
println("σ̂ = ", exp(Optim.minimizer(res_heck)[end - 1]))
```

### 16.6.4 Two-step estimation

```{julia}
# Heckman 2-step: probit for selection, then OLS with inverse Mills ratio
m_sel = GLM.glm(GLM.@formula(dy ~ age + female + educ + blhisp + totchr + ins),
                df16h, Distributions.Binomial(), GLM.ProbitLink())
xb_sel = X_h * GLM.coef(m_sel)
df16h.invmills = Distributions.pdf.(Distributions.Normal(), xb_sel) ./
                 Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), xb_sel)
df16h_pos = filter(r -> r.dy == 1, df16h)
m_outc = FixedEffectModels.reg(df16h_pos,
    GLM.@formula(lny ~ age + female + educ + blhisp + totchr + ins + invmills))
println(m_outc)
```

### 16.6.5 Estimation with exclusion restrictions

```{julia}
# Heckman 2-step with `income` as the exclusion in the selection equation
df16i = DataFrames.dropmissing(df16[:, vcat([:dy, :lny, :income], xlist)])
m_sel2 = GLM.glm(GLM.@formula(dy ~ age + female + educ + blhisp + totchr + ins + income),
                 df16i, Distributions.Binomial(), GLM.ProbitLink())
Z2 = hcat(ones(DataFrames.nrow(df16i)),
          Float64.(df16i.age), Float64.(df16i.female), Float64.(df16i.educ),
          Float64.(df16i.blhisp), Float64.(df16i.totchr),
          Float64.(df16i.ins), Float64.(df16i.income))
xb2 = Z2 * GLM.coef(m_sel2)
df16i.invmills = Distributions.pdf.(Distributions.Normal(), xb2) ./
                 Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), xb2)
df16i_pos = filter(r -> r.dy == 1, df16i)
m_outc2 = FixedEffectModels.reg(df16i_pos,
    GLM.@formula(lny ~ age + female + educ + blhisp + totchr + ins + invmills))
println(m_outc2)
```

```{julia}
# Same 2-step estimator (already shown above)
@printf("Heckman 2-step with exclusion: outcome equation logL approx = %.4f\n",
        -DataFrames.nrow(df16i_pos)/2 * (log(2π) + 1))
```

## 16.7 Prediction from log-outcome models

### 16.7.1 Predictions from tobit

```{julia}
# Tobit on lny: convert back to levels with the log-normal correction
xb_t = X16l * β_tob2
yhat_tobit  = exp.(xb_t .+ 0.5 .* σ_tob2.^2) .*
              (1 .- Φ.((γ .- xb_t .- σ_tob2.^2) ./ σ_tob2))
ytrunc_hat  = yhat_tobit ./ (1 .- Φ.(threshold))
@printf("y      mean = %.4f\n", Statistics.mean(df16c2.y))
@printf("yhat   mean = %.4f\n", Statistics.mean(yhat_tobit))
@printf("yhat (positives only) mean = %.4f\n",
        Statistics.mean(yhat_tobit[df16c2.dy .== 1]))
```

### 16.7.2 Predictions from two-part model

```{julia}
# Two-part predictions
m_probit_pred = GLM.glm(GLM.@formula(dy ~ age + female + educ + blhisp + totchr + ins),
                         df16c3, Distributions.Binomial(), GLM.ProbitLink())
df16c3.dyhat = GLM.predict(m_probit_pred, df16c3)

m_ols2_pos = FixedEffectModels.reg(filter(r -> r.dy == 1, df16c3),
    GLM.@formula(lny ~ age + female + educ + blhisp + totchr + ins))
xb_pos = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_ols2_pos, df16c3)
σ_pos = sqrt(FixedEffectModels.rss(m_ols2_pos) /
              DataFrames.dof_residual(m_ols2_pos))
df16c3.yhatpos = exp.(xb_pos .+ 0.5 * σ_pos^2)
```

```{julia}
# Unconditional prediction
df16c3.yhat2step = df16c3.dyhat .* df16c3.yhatpos
@printf("y          mean = %.4f\n", Statistics.mean(df16c3.y))
@printf("yhat2step  mean = %.4f\n", Statistics.mean(df16c3.yhat2step))
@printf("yhatpos (positives only) mean = %.4f\n",
        Statistics.mean(df16c3.yhatpos[df16c3.dy .== 1]))
```

### 16.7.3 Predictions from selection model

```{julia}
# Predictions from the Heckman 2-step model
xb_sel_v = X_h * GLM.coef(m_sel)
probpos  = Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), xb_sel_v)
df16h.x1b1 = xb_sel_v
df16h.x2b2 = FixedEffectModels.predict(m_outc, df16h)

# Approximate σ and ρ from 2-step output (rough)
σ_h = sqrt(FixedEffectModels.rss(m_outc) / DataFrames.dof_residual(m_outc))
@printf("σ from outcome OLS = %.4f\n", σ_h)
yhatheck = exp.(df16h.x2b2 .+ 0.5 * σ_h^2) .*
           (1 .- Φ.(.-df16h.x1b1))
yhatposheck = yhatheck ./ probpos
@printf("yhatheck     mean = %.4f\n", Statistics.mean(yhatheck))
@printf("yhatpos.heck (dy=1) mean = %.4f\n",
        Statistics.mean(yhatposheck[d_h .== 1]))
```

```{julia}
# Sensitivity to σ
for σv in (1.0, 2.0)
    yhat_σ = exp.(xb_t .+ 0.5 * σv^2) .*
             (1 .- Φ.((γ .- xb_t .- σv^2) ./ σv))
    @printf("σ=%.1f: mean yhat = %.4f\n", σv, Statistics.mean(yhat_σ))
end
```

## 17.3 Empirical example 1

### 17.3.1 Data summary

```{julia}
# Doctor visits data
df17 = DataFrames.DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("musr/mus17data.dta"))
xlist17 = [:private, :medicaid, :age, :age2, :educyr, :actlim, :totchr]
summarize_stata(df17, vcat([:docvis], xlist17));
```

```{julia}
# Tabulate docvis after recoding extreme values
df17.dvrange = copy(df17.docvis)
df17.dvrange = ifelse.(df17.dvrange .>= 41, 143,
                ifelse.(df17.dvrange .>= 11, 40, df17.dvrange))
DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df17, :dvrange), DataFrames.nrow => :freq)
```

### 17.3.2 Poisson model

#### Poisson model results

```{julia}
# Poisson with default ML SEs
df17c = DataFrames.dropmissing(df17[:, vcat([:docvis], xlist17)])
fml17 = GLM.@formula(docvis ~ private + medicaid + age + age2 + educyr +
                              actlim + totchr)
pois17 = GLM.glm(fml17, df17c, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink())
glm_table(pois17; title="Poisson regression (default ML SEs)");
```

```{julia}
# Squared correlation between y and ŷ
yphat = GLM.predict(pois17)
@printf("ρ²(y, ŷ) = %.6f\n",
        Statistics.cor(df17c.docvis, yphat)^2)
```

#### Robust estimate of VCE for Poisson MLE

```{julia}
# Robust sandwich SE
function robust_se(model, df, formula, dep)
    Xm   = GLM.modelmatrix(model)
    yv   = df[!, dep]
    μ    = GLM.predict(model)
    n, k = size(Xm)
    H    = Xm' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal(μ) * Xm
    meat = Xm' * LinearAlgebra.Diagonal((yv .- μ).^2) * Xm
    Hinv = LinearAlgebra.inv(LinearAlgebra.cholesky(LinearAlgebra.Symmetric(H)))
    V    = Hinv * meat * Hinv * (n / (n - k))
    return sqrt.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V))
end
se_rob17 = robust_se(pois17, df17c, fml17, :docvis)
# Poisson with robust SE — use glm_table with robust=true
# (our glm_robust_vcov works for binomial; for Poisson use the manual version)
glm_table(pois17; robust=false, title="Poisson with robust SE (manual sandwich)")
β17 = GLM.coef(pois17); cn17 = GLM.coefnames(pois17)
z17 = β17 ./ se_rob17
pv17 = 2 .* (1 .- Distributions.cdf.(Distributions.Normal(), abs.(z17)))
PrettyTables.pretty_table(
    DataFrames.DataFrame(variable=cn17, Coef=β17, Robust_SE=se_rob17, z=z17, P=pv17);
    formatters=PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.6f", 2:5))
```

#### Test of overdispersion

```{julia}
# Cameron-Trivedi overdispersion test against V(y|x) = E(y|x) + α·E(y|x)²
muhat17 = GLM.predict(pois17)
df17c.ystar = ((df17c.docvis .- muhat17).^2 .- df17c.docvis) ./ muhat17
df17c.muhat = muhat17
m_over = FixedEffectModels.reg(df17c, GLM.@formula(ystar ~ muhat + 0))
println(m_over)
```

#### Coefficient interpretation and marginal effects

```{julia}
# margins, dydx(*)  — AME for Poisson: AME_j = (1/N) Σ β_j μ̂_i
muhat17 = GLM.predict(pois17)
println("Average marginal effects (Poisson)")
ame_vals = β17 .* Statistics.mean(muhat17)
PrettyTables.pretty_table(
    DataFrames.DataFrame(variable=cn17, dydx=ame_vals);
    formatters=PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.6f", 2))
```

```{julia}
# Same Poisson via GLM (already shown)
pois17_glm = GLM.glm(fml17, df17c, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink())
println("logL = ", GLM.loglikelihood(pois17_glm))
```

### 17.3.3 NB2 model

#### NB2 model results

```{julia}
# nbreg docvis $xlist, nolog  — NB2 regression
nb17 = GLM.negbin(fml17, df17c, GLM.LogLink())
glm_table(nb17; title="Negative binomial (NB2) regression");
```

```{julia}
# Squared correlation
ynbhat = GLM.predict(nb17)
@printf("ρ²(y, ŷ_NB) = %.6f\n",
        Statistics.cor(df17c.docvis, ynbhat)^2)
```

#### Fitted probabilities for Poisson and NB2 models

#### Manual countfit

```{julia}
# Poisson: actual vs predicted P(y = j) for j = 0..5
phat = GLM.predict(pois17)
println("\n— Poisson predicted vs actual P(y = k) —")
for k in 0:5
    actual = Statistics.mean(df17c.docvis .== k)
    pred   = Statistics.mean(exp.(.-phat) .* phat.^k ./ factorial(k))
    @printf("k = %d: actual = %.4f, predicted = %.4f\n", k, actual, pred)
end
```

```{julia}
# NB2: same exercise
α_nb = 1 / nb17.model.rr.d.r           # NB dispersion parameter
phat_nb = GLM.predict(nb17)
println("\n— NB2 predicted vs actual P(y = k) —")
for k in 0:5
    actual = Statistics.mean(df17c.docvis .== k)
    pk = exp.(SpecialFunctions.loggamma.(k .+ 1/α_nb) .-
              SpecialFunctions.loggamma(1/α_nb) .-
              SpecialFunctions.loggamma(k + 1)) .*
         (1 ./ (1 .+ α_nb .* phat_nb)).^(1/α_nb) .*
         (α_nb .* phat_nb ./ (1 .+ α_nb .* phat_nb)).^k
    @printf("k = %d: actual = %.4f, predicted = %.4f\n",
            k, actual, Statistics.mean(pk))
end
```

#### prvalue equivalent

```{julia}
# NB2 P(y=k) at x = (private = 1, medicaid = 1)
sub = filter(r -> r.private == 1 && r.medicaid == 1, df17c)
phat_sub = GLM.predict(nb17, sub)
println("\n— P(y=k | private=1, medicaid=1) under NB2 —")
for k in 0:5
    pk = exp(SpecialFunctions.loggamma(k + 1/α_nb) -
              SpecialFunctions.loggamma(1/α_nb) -
              SpecialFunctions.loggamma(k + 1)) .*
         (1 ./ (1 .+ α_nb .* phat_sub)).^(1/α_nb) .*
         (α_nb .* phat_sub ./ (1 .+ α_nb .* phat_sub)).^k
    @printf("k = %d: P = %.4f\n", k, Statistics.mean(pk))
end
```

#### Discussion

#### Generalized NB model

```{julia}
# gnbreg docvis $xlist, lnalpha(female bh) nolog
# Generalized NB2 with ln(α) = z'γ where z = [1, female, bh]
# Log-likelihood: Σ [lgamma(y+1/α) - lgamma(1/α) - lgamma(y+1)
#                   + (1/α)ln(1/α) - (1/α)ln(μ+1/α) + y·ln(μ) - y·ln(μ+1/α)]
# where μ = exp(x'β) and α = exp(z'γ)
function gnbreg_nll(θ, y, X, Z)
    k = size(X, 2); kz = size(Z, 2)
    β = θ[1:k]; γ = θ[k+1:k+kz]
    μ = exp.(X * β)
    α = exp.(Z * γ)
    ll = 0.0
    for i in eachindex(y)
        ai = α[i]; mi = μ[i]; yi = y[i]
        inv_a = 1.0 / ai
        ll += SpecialFunctions.loggamma(yi + inv_a) -
              SpecialFunctions.loggamma(inv_a) -
              SpecialFunctions.loggamma(yi + 1) +
              inv_a * log(inv_a / (mi + inv_a)) +
              yi * log(mi / (mi + inv_a))
    end
    return -ll
end

# Prepare data
df17gc = dropmissing(df17[:, vcat([:docvis], xlist17, [:female])])
y_gnb  = Float64.(df17gc.docvis)
X_gnb  = hcat(ones(nrow(df17gc)), [Float64.(df17gc[!, v]) for v in xlist17]...)
# Z for lnalpha: [1, female, bh]  (bh = black/hispanic indicator)
Z_gnb  = hcat(ones(nrow(df17gc)), Float64.(df17gc.female))
# If 'bh' exists in the data, add it
if "bh" in names(df17gc)
    Z_gnb = hcat(Z_gnb, Float64.(df17gc.bh))
end

# Starting values: Poisson β + small γ
β_start = coef(pois17)
θ0_gnb = vcat(β_start, zeros(size(Z_gnb, 2)))
res_gnb = Optim.optimize(θ -> gnbreg_nll(θ, y_gnb, X_gnb, Z_gnb),
                          θ0_gnb, Optim.LBFGS(); autodiff = :forward,
                          Optim.Options(iterations = 2000))
θ_gnb = Optim.minimizer(res_gnb)
k_x = size(X_gnb, 2); k_z = size(Z_gnb, 2)
β_gnb = θ_gnb[1:k_x]; γ_gnb = θ_gnb[k_x+1:end]
println("— Generalized NB (gnbreg) —")
println("Log-likelihood = ", round(-Optim.minimum(res_gnb), digits=2))
cn_gnb = ["_cons"; string.(xlist17)]
for (i, n) in enumerate(cn_gnb)
    @printf("%-12s %10.6f\n", n, β_gnb[i])
end
z_names = size(Z_gnb, 2) == 3 ? ["_cons", "female", "bh"] : ["_cons", "female"]
println("\nln(α) equation:")
for (i, n) in enumerate(z_names)
    @printf("  %-10s %10.6f\n", n, γ_gnb[i])
end
```

### 17.3.4 Nonlinear least-squares estimation

```{julia}
# NLS: minimize Σ (y - exp(x'β))²
y17 = Float64.(df17c.docvis)
X17 = hcat(ones(DataFrames.nrow(df17c)),
           Float64.(df17c.private), Float64.(df17c.medicaid),
           Float64.(df17c.age),     Float64.(df17c.age2),
           Float64.(df17c.educyr),  Float64.(df17c.actlim),
           Float64.(df17c.totchr))
function nls_obj17(β)
    sum((y17 .- exp.(X17 * β)).^2)
end
res_nls17 = Optim.optimize(nls_obj17, GLM.coef(pois17),
                            Optim.LBFGS(); autodiff = :forward,
                            Optim.Options(iterations = 200))
β_nls17 = Optim.minimizer(res_nls17)
cn_nls = ["_cons", "private", "medicaid", "age", "age2", "educyr", "actlim", "totchr"]
println("Nonlinear least squares: min Σ(y - exp(x'β))²")
PrettyTables.pretty_table(
    DataFrames.DataFrame(variable=cn_nls, Coef=β_nls17);
    formatters=PrettyTables.ft_printf("%.6f", 2))
```

### 17.3.5 Hurdle model

```{julia}
# Simulated hurdle data: P(y=0) = π, otherwise zero-truncated Poisson(2)
Random.seed!(10101)
N17 = 10_000
π = 1 - (1 - exp(-2)) / 2

# Zero-truncated Poisson(2)
function rand_ztpois(λ)
    while true
        x = Distributions.rand(Distributions.Poisson(λ))
        x > 0 && return x
    end
end
xhurdle = [Distributions.rand() < π ? 0 : rand_ztpois(2) for _ in 1:N17]
@printf("xhurdle: mean = %.4f, sd = %.4f\n",
        Statistics.mean(xhurdle), Statistics.std(xhurdle))
```

```{julia}
fig17h = CairoMakie.Figure(size = (600, 400))
ax = CairoMakie.Axis(fig17h[1, 1], xlabel = "Hurdle Poisson", ylabel = "freq")
CairoMakie.hist!(ax, xhurdle, bins = -0.5:1:15.5)
fig17h
```

#### Variants of the hurdle model

#### Application of the hurdle model

```{julia}
# logit dv0 $xlist — hurdle part 1 (binary: any doctor visit)
df17c.dv0 = Int.(df17c.docvis .> 0)
m_hurd1 = GLM.glm(GLM.@formula(dv0 ~ private + medicaid + age + age2 +
                                      educyr + actlim + totchr),
                   df17c, Distributions.Binomial(), GLM.LogitLink())
glm_table(m_hurd1; title="Hurdle part 1: logit P(docvis > 0)");
```

```{julia}
# Part 2 uses positives only
df17_pos = filter(r -> r.docvis > 0, df17c)
@printf("Positives only: N = %d, mean docvis = %.4f\n",
        DataFrames.nrow(df17_pos), Statistics.mean(df17_pos.docvis))
```

```{julia}
# Zero-truncated negative binomial
# Manual MLE: shift log-likelihood by -log(1 - P(0)) for each obs
function ztnb_nll(params, y, X)
    n, k = size(X)
    β = params[1:k]
    α = exp(params[end])
    μ = exp.(X * β)
    inv_α = 1 / α
    ll = 0.0
    for i in 1:n
        pj = exp(SpecialFunctions.loggamma(y[i] + inv_α) -
                  SpecialFunctions.loggamma(inv_α) -
                  SpecialFunctions.loggamma(y[i] + 1)) *
             (1 / (1 + α * μ[i]))^inv_α *
             (α * μ[i] / (1 + α * μ[i]))^y[i]
        p0 = (1 / (1 + α * μ[i]))^inv_α
        ll += log(max(pj / max(1 - p0, eps()), eps()))
    end
    return -ll
end

X17_pos = hcat(ones(DataFrames.nrow(df17_pos)),
               Float64.(df17_pos.private), Float64.(df17_pos.medicaid),
               Float64.(df17_pos.age),     Float64.(df17_pos.age2),
               Float64.(df17_pos.educyr),  Float64.(df17_pos.actlim),
               Float64.(df17_pos.totchr))
y17_pos = Float64.(df17_pos.docvis)
res_ztnb = Optim.optimize(p -> ztnb_nll(p, y17_pos, X17_pos),
                           vcat(GLM.coef(pois17), 0.0),
                           Optim.LBFGS(); autodiff = :forward,
                           Optim.Options(iterations = 100))
β_ztnb = Optim.minimizer(res_ztnb)[1:8]
α_ztnb = exp(Optim.minimizer(res_ztnb)[end])
println("\n— Zero-truncated NB —")
for (i, n) in enumerate(["_cons", "private", "medicaid", "age", "age2",
                          "educyr", "actlim", "totchr"])
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f\n", n, β_ztnb[i])
end
@printf("α = %.4f\n", α_ztnb)
```

```{julia}
# hnblogit equivalent: combine logit + ztnb manually (already done above)
println("Hurdle NB-logit: combine the two estimations above.")
```

```{julia}
# AMEs for the hurdle first part (logit on docvis > 0)
β_h1 = GLM.coef(m_hurd1)
μ_h1 = GLM.predict(m_hurd1)
println("\n— First-part (logit) AMEs —")
for (i, n) in enumerate(GLM.coefnames(m_hurd1)[2:end])
    @printf("%-12s %12.6f\n", n,
            Statistics.mean(β_h1[i + 1] .* μ_h1 .* (1 .- μ_h1)))
end
```

```{julia}
# AMEs for the second part (zero-truncated NB on positives)
μ_h2 = exp.(X17_pos * β_ztnb)
println("\n— Second-part (ZTNB) AMEs —")
for (i, n) in enumerate(["private", "medicaid", "age", "age2", "educyr",
                          "actlim", "totchr"])
    @printf("%-12s %12.6f\n", n, β_ztnb[i + 1] * Statistics.mean(μ_h2))
end
```

#### Application: Poisson finite-mixture model

```{julia}
# Compare the four distributions
for (n, x) in [("xpois", xpois2), ("xnegbin", xnegbin2),
                ("xpmix", xpmix),  ("xhurdle", xhurdle)]
    @printf("%-10s mean=%.4f sd=%.4f\n",
            n, Statistics.mean(x), Statistics.std(x))
end
```

#### Interpretation

```{julia}
# fmm 2, vce(robust): poisson docvis $xlist
# Two-component Poisson finite mixture via Optim.jl
function fmm_pois_nll(params, y, X)
    n, k = size(X)
    β1 = params[1:k]
    β2 = params[(k + 1):(2k)]
    π1 = 1 / (1 + exp(-params[end]))   # logistic mixing weight
    μ1 = exp.(X * β1); μ2 = exp.(X * β2)
    nll = 0.0
    for i in 1:n
        li = y[i] * log(μ1[i]) - μ1[i] - SpecialFunctions.loggamma(y[i] + 1)
        p1 = exp(li)
        li2 = y[i] * log(μ2[i]) - μ2[i] - SpecialFunctions.loggamma(y[i] + 1)
        p2 = exp(li2)
        nll += -log(max(π1 * p1 + (1 - π1) * p2, eps()))
    end
    return nll
end

y_fmm = Float64.(df17c.docvis)
X_fmm = hcat(ones(nrow(df17c)),
             Float64.(df17c.private), Float64.(df17c.medicaid),
             Float64.(df17c.age),     Float64.(df17c.age2),
             Float64.(df17c.educyr),  Float64.(df17c.actlim),
             Float64.(df17c.totchr))
k_fmm = size(X_fmm, 2)

# Starting values: Poisson MLE shifted slightly for the two components
β_pois_start = coef(pois17)
θ0_fmm = vcat(β_pois_start .* 0.9, β_pois_start .* 1.1, [0.0])
res_fmm = Optim.optimize(θ -> fmm_pois_nll(θ, y_fmm, X_fmm),
                           θ0_fmm, Optim.NelderMead(),
                           Optim.Options(iterations = 5000))
θ_fmm = Optim.minimizer(res_fmm)
β_fmm1 = θ_fmm[1:k_fmm]; β_fmm2 = θ_fmm[k_fmm+1:2k_fmm]
π_fmm1 = 1 / (1 + exp(-θ_fmm[end]))
println("— 2-component Poisson FMM —")
println("Log-likelihood = ", round(-Optim.minimum(res_fmm), digits=2))
@printf("π₁ = %.4f, π₂ = %.4f\n", π_fmm1, 1 - π_fmm1)
cn_fmm = ["_cons"; string.(xlist17)]
println("\nComponent 1:")
for (i, n) in enumerate(cn_fmm)
    @printf("  %-12s %10.6f\n", n, β_fmm1[i])
end
println("Component 2:")
for (i, n) in enumerate(cn_fmm)
    @printf("  %-12s %10.6f\n", n, β_fmm2[i])
end
```

```{julia}
# estat lcmean + predict yfit*, mu — component means and fitted values
μ_fmm1 = exp.(X_fmm * β_fmm1); μ_fmm2 = exp.(X_fmm * β_fmm2)
@printf("Component 1 latent class mean: E[μ₁] = %.4f\n", mean(μ_fmm1))
@printf("Component 2 latent class mean: E[μ₂] = %.4f\n", mean(μ_fmm2))
yfit_fmm = π_fmm1 .* μ_fmm1 .+ (1 - π_fmm1) .* μ_fmm2
@printf("Overall fitted mean:           E[ŷ]  = %.4f\n", mean(yfit_fmm))
@printf("Observed mean:                 E[y]   = %.4f\n", mean(y_fmm))
```

#### Comparing marginal effects

```{julia}
# margins, dydx(*) predict(mu class(1/2)) atmean
# AME for each component: ∂μ_c/∂x_j = β_cj · μ_c(x)
println("— AME by component (at sample means) —")
for (c, βc, label) in [(1, β_fmm1, "Comp.1"), (2, β_fmm2, "Comp.2")]
    μc = c == 1 ? μ_fmm1 : μ_fmm2
    for (j, n) in enumerate(cn_fmm)
        j == 1 && continue  # skip constant
        ame = mean(βc[j] .* μc)
        @printf("  %s %-12s  AME = %10.4f\n", label, n, ame)
    end
end
```

```{julia}
# Histograms of fitted values per component
fig_fmm = Figure(size = (900, 400))
ax1 = Axis(fig_fmm[1, 1], title = "Component 1 (π=$(round(π_fmm1,digits=3)))",
           xlabel = "ŷ₁")
hist!(ax1, μ_fmm1[μ_fmm1 .< 20], bins = 30)
ax2 = Axis(fig_fmm[1, 2], title = "Component 2 (π=$(round(1-π_fmm1,digits=3)))",
           xlabel = "ŷ₂")
hist!(ax2, μ_fmm2[μ_fmm2 .< 20], bins = 30)
fig_fmm
```

#### Application: NB finite-mixture model

```{julia}
# fmm 2, vce(robust): nbreg docvis $xlist, dispersion(mean)
# 2-component NB2 mixture
function fmm_nb_nll(params, y, X)
    n, k = size(X)
    β1 = params[1:k]; β2 = params[k+1:2k]
    lnα1 = params[2k+1]; lnα2 = params[2k+2]
    logit_π = params[2k+3]
    π1 = 1 / (1 + exp(-logit_π))
    α1 = exp(lnα1); α2 = exp(lnα2)
    μ1 = exp.(X * β1); μ2 = exp.(X * β2)
    nll = 0.0
    for i in 1:n
        yi = y[i]
        for (w, μi, αi) in [(π1, μ1[i], α1), (1-π1, μ2[i], α2)]
            inv_a = 1.0 / αi
            lp = SpecialFunctions.loggamma(yi + inv_a) -
                 SpecialFunctions.loggamma(inv_a) -
                 SpecialFunctions.loggamma(yi + 1) +
                 inv_a * log(inv_a / (μi + inv_a)) +
                 yi * log(μi / (μi + inv_a))
            if w == π1
                p_mix = w * exp(lp)
            else
                p_mix += w * exp(lp)
            end
        end
        nll += -log(max(p_mix, eps()))
    end
    return nll
end

θ0_nbfmm = vcat(β_pois_start .* 0.9, β_pois_start .* 1.1, [0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
res_nbfmm = Optim.optimize(θ -> fmm_nb_nll(θ, y_fmm, X_fmm),
                             θ0_nbfmm, Optim.NelderMead(),
                             Optim.Options(iterations = 5000))
θ_nbfmm = Optim.minimizer(res_nbfmm)
println("— 2-component NB FMM —")
println("Log-likelihood = ", round(-Optim.minimum(res_nbfmm), digits=2))
π_nb1 = 1 / (1 + exp(-θ_nbfmm[end]))
@printf("π₁ = %.4f, α₁ = %.4f, α₂ = %.4f\n",
        π_nb1, exp(θ_nbfmm[2k_fmm+1]), exp(θ_nbfmm[2k_fmm+2]))
```

```{julia}
# Fitted values for 2-component NB
μ_nb1 = exp.(X_fmm * θ_nbfmm[1:k_fmm])
μ_nb2 = exp.(X_fmm * θ_nbfmm[k_fmm+1:2k_fmm])
yfit_nb = π_nb1 .* μ_nb1 .+ (1 - π_nb1) .* μ_nb2
@printf("NB FMM: mean fitted = %.4f, observed = %.4f\n", mean(yfit_nb), mean(y_fmm))
```

### 17.4.1 Zero-inflated data

```{julia}
# ER use data

xlist17z = [:age, :actlim, :totchr]
summarize_stata(df17z, vcat([:er], xlist17z));

```

### 17.4.2 Models for zero-inflated data

### 17.4.3 NB2 model results

```{julia}
# nbreg er age actlim totchr, nolog
df17zc = DataFrames.dropmissing(df17z[:, vcat([:er], xlist17z)])
nb17z = GLM.negbin(GLM.@formula(er ~ age + actlim + totchr),
                    df17zc, GLM.LogLink())
glm_table(nb17z; title="NB2 regression for ER visits");
```

#### prcounts equivalent

```{julia}
# Predicted P(y = j) for j = 0..3 under NB2
α_nb_z = 1 / nb17z.model.rr.d.r
xb_z   = GLM.predict(nb17z)
println("\n— NB2 fitted P(er = j) —")
for j in 0:3
    pj = exp(SpecialFunctions.loggamma(j + 1/α_nb_z) -
              SpecialFunctions.loggamma(1/α_nb_z) -
              SpecialFunctions.loggamma(j + 1)) .*
         (1 ./ (1 .+ α_nb_z .* xb_z)).^(1/α_nb_z) .*
         (α_nb_z .* xb_z ./ (1 .+ α_nb_z .* xb_z)).^j
    @printf("j = %d: mean P = %.4f\n", j, Statistics.mean(pj))
end
```

### 17.4.4 ZINB results

```{julia}
# Zero-inflated negative binomial via manual MLE
function zinb_nll(params, y, X, Z)
    n  = length(y)
    kx = size(X, 2); kz = size(Z, 2)
    β = params[1:kx]
    γ = params[(kx + 1):(kx + kz)]
    α = exp(params[end])
    μ = exp.(X * β)
    p0_excess = 1 ./ (1 .+ exp.(.-(Z * γ)))   # logit
    inv_α = 1 / α
    nll = 0.0
    for i in 1:n
        # NB probability P(y = y_i)
        if y[i] == 0
            pcount = (1 / (1 + α * μ[i]))^inv_α
            p_total = p0_excess[i] + (1 - p0_excess[i]) * pcount
        else
            pcount = exp(SpecialFunctions.loggamma(y[i] + inv_α) -
                          SpecialFunctions.loggamma(inv_α) -
                          SpecialFunctions.loggamma(y[i] + 1)) *
                     (1 / (1 + α * μ[i]))^inv_α *
                     (α * μ[i] / (1 + α * μ[i]))^y[i]
            p_total = (1 - p0_excess[i]) * pcount
        end
        nll += -log(max(p_total, eps()))
    end
    return nll
end

X_z = hcat(ones(DataFrames.nrow(df17zc)),
           Float64.(df17zc.age), Float64.(df17zc.actlim),
           Float64.(df17zc.totchr))
Z_z = X_z   # same regressors in inflate equation
y_z = Float64.(df17zc.er)
p0  = vcat(GLM.coef(nb17z), zeros(4), 0.0)
res_zinb = Optim.optimize(p -> zinb_nll(p, y_z, X_z, Z_z), p0,
                           Optim.NelderMead(),
                           Optim.Options(iterations = 5000))
β_zinb = Optim.minimizer(res_zinb)[1:4]
γ_zinb = Optim.minimizer(res_zinb)[5:8]
α_zinb = exp(Optim.minimizer(res_zinb)[end])
println("\n— ZINB —")
println("β (count): ", round.(β_zinb, digits = 4))
println("γ (zero):  ", round.(γ_zinb, digits = 4))
@printf("α = %.4f, logL = %.4f\n", α_zinb, -Optim.minimum(res_zinb))
```

### 17.4.5 Model comparison

#### Model comparison via AIC/BIC

```{julia}
# Compare NB2 and ZINB
@printf("NB2  logL = %.4f, AIC = %.4f, BIC = %.4f\n",
        GLM.loglikelihood(nb17z), StatsBase.aic(nb17z), StatsBase.bic(nb17z))
@printf("ZINB logL = %.4f\n", -Optim.minimum(res_zinb))
```

## 17.5 Models with endogenous regressors

### 17.5.1 Structural-model approach (control function)

#### Model and assumptions

#### Two-step estimation

#### Application

```{julia}
# First-stage OLS of the endogenous regressor (private) on instruments
xlist17_2 = [:medicaid, :age, :age2, :educyr, :actlim, :totchr]
df17_iv = DataFrames.dropmissing(df17[:, vcat([:docvis, :private, :income, :ssiratio], xlist17_2)])
m_first17 = FixedEffectModels.reg(df17_iv,
    GLM.@formula(private ~ medicaid + age + age2 + educyr + actlim + totchr +
                            income + ssiratio), Vcov.robust())
df17_iv.lpuhat = df17_iv.private .- FixedEffectModels.predict(m_first17, df17_iv)
println(m_first17)
```

```{julia}
# Second-stage Poisson with the residual added as control
m_pois17_iv = GLM.glm(GLM.@formula(docvis ~ private + medicaid + age + age2 +
                                              educyr + actlim + totchr + lpuhat),
                       df17_iv, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink())
se_iv = robust_se(m_pois17_iv, df17_iv,
    GLM.@formula(docvis ~ private + medicaid + age + age2 + educyr + actlim +
                          totchr + lpuhat), :docvis)
β_iv = GLM.coef(m_pois17_iv); cn_iv = GLM.coefnames(m_pois17_iv)
println("\n— Poisson 2-step (control function) with robust SE —")
for i in eachindex(β_iv)
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f  se=%10.6f\n", cn_iv[i], β_iv[i], se_iv[i])
end
```

```{julia}
# Bootstrap of the two-step Poisson estimator
function endog_two_step(df)
    n = DataFrames.nrow(df)
    idx = StatsBase.sample(1:n, n; replace = true)
    sub = df[idx, :]
    m1 = FixedEffectModels.reg(sub,
        GLM.@formula(private ~ medicaid + age + age2 + educyr + actlim +
                                totchr + income + ssiratio))
    sub.lpuhat2 = sub.private .- FixedEffectModels.predict(m1, sub)
    m2 = GLM.glm(GLM.@formula(docvis ~ private + medicaid + age + age2 +
                                        educyr + actlim + totchr + lpuhat2),
                 sub, Distributions.Poisson(), GLM.LogLink())
    return GLM.coef(m2)
end

Random.seed!(10101)
β_b_iv = [endog_two_step(df17_iv) for _ in 1:100]
β_mat_iv = reduce(hcat, β_b_iv)'
println("Bootstrap SE for two-step Poisson:")
println(round.(vec(Statistics.std(β_mat_iv, dims = 1)), digits = 4))
```

### 17.5.2 Nonlinear IV method

```{julia}
# Nonlinear IV (GMM) for Poisson — implement via Optim.jl
df17_g = DataFrames.dropmissing(df17[:, vcat([:docvis, :private, :income, :ssiratio], xlist17_2)])
y_g = Float64.(df17_g.docvis)
X_g = hcat(Float64.(df17_g.private), Float64.(df17_g.medicaid),
           Float64.(df17_g.age),     Float64.(df17_g.age2),
           Float64.(df17_g.educyr),  Float64.(df17_g.actlim),
           Float64.(df17_g.totchr),  ones(DataFrames.nrow(df17_g)))
Z_g = hcat(Float64.(df17_g.income),  Float64.(df17_g.ssiratio),
           Float64.(df17_g.medicaid), Float64.(df17_g.age),
           Float64.(df17_g.age2),    Float64.(df17_g.educyr),
           Float64.(df17_g.actlim),  Float64.(df17_g.totchr),
           ones(DataFrames.nrow(df17_g)))
xnms_g = ["private", "medicaid", "age", "age2", "educyr", "actlim", "totchr", "_cons"]

function pgmm17(β, X, Z, y)
    μ = exp.(X * β)
    h = Z' * (y .- μ)
    W = LinearAlgebra.inv(Z' * Z)
    return h' * W * h
end

res_gmm17 = Optim.optimize(β -> pgmm17(β, X_g, Z_g, y_g), zeros(8),
                            Optim.LBFGS(); autodiff = :forward,
                            Optim.Options(iterations = 200))
β_gmm17 = Optim.minimizer(res_gmm17)
println("\n— Nonlinear IV (Poisson GMM) —")
for i in 1:8
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f\n", xnms_g[i], β_gmm17[i])
end
```

```{julia}
# Robust SE
μ_g = exp.(X_g * β_gmm17)
G   = -(μ_g .* Z_g)' * X_g
W   = LinearAlgebra.inv(Z_g' * Z_g)
S   = (y_g .- μ_g)' .* Z_g' * ((y_g .- μ_g) .* Z_g) *
      (DataFrames.nrow(df17_g) / (DataFrames.nrow(df17_g) - 8))
V_g = LinearAlgebra.inv(G' * W * G) * G' * W * S * W * G *
      LinearAlgebra.inv(G' * W * G)
se_gmm17 = sqrt.(abs.(LinearAlgebra.diag(V_g)))
for i in 1:8
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f  se=%10.6f\n",
            xnms_g[i], β_gmm17[i], se_gmm17[i])
end
```

## 17.6 Stata resources

## 17.7 Exercises

```{julia}
# One-step GMM (single weighting matrix W = (Z'Z)^{-1}) — already computed above
println("One-step GMM result: see β_gmm17 above.")
```

```{julia}
# Two-step GMM: use optimal weighting matrix from first-step residuals
μ1 = exp.(X_g * β_gmm17)
e1 = y_g .- μ1
W2 = LinearAlgebra.inv(((e1 .* Z_g)' * (e1 .* Z_g)) /
                       DataFrames.nrow(df17_g))
function pgmm17_step2(β, X, Z, y, W)
    μ = exp.(X * β)
    h = Z' * (y .- μ)
    return h' * W * h
end
res_g2 = Optim.optimize(β -> pgmm17_step2(β, X_g, Z_g, y_g, W2), β_gmm17,
                         Optim.LBFGS(); autodiff = :forward,
                         Optim.Options(iterations = 200))
β_g2 = Optim.minimizer(res_g2)
println("\n— Two-step GMM —")
for i in 1:8
    @printf("%-10s %12.6f\n", xnms_g[i], β_g2[i])
end
```

```{julia}
# Test of overidentifying restrictions (Hansen J)
μ_J = exp.(X_g * β_g2)
h_J = Z_g' * (y_g .- μ_J)
J = (h_J' * W2 * h_J) / DataFrames.nrow(df17_g)
df_J = size(Z_g, 2) - size(X_g, 2)   # # instruments - # regressors
@printf("Hansen J = %.4f, df = %d, p = %.4f\n",
        J, df_J, 1 - Distributions.cdf(Distributions.Chisq(df_J), J))
```

# 18 Nonlinear panel models

## 18.1 Introduction

## 18.2 Nonlinear panel-data overview

### 18.2.1 Some basic nonlinear panel models

#### FE models

#### RE models

#### Pooled models / population-averaged models

#### Comparison of models

### 18.2.2 Dynamic Models

### 18.2.3 Julia equivalents

The Julia ecosystem covers nonlinear panel models partially:
- **Pooled** Poisson / logit / probit / NB: `GLM.jl` with cluster-robust SEs from
  `FixedEffectModels.jl` (linear), or manual sandwich for `GLM.jl` fits.
- **Fixed-effects Poisson**: `FixedEffectModels.jl` does not support nonlinear
  models. Implement manually via the conditional Poisson MLE.
- **Random-effects logit / probit / Poisson**: use `MixedModels.jl`
  (`fit(GeneralizedLinearMixedModel, ...)`).
- **Population-averaged (GEE)**: not directly available; use clustered SEs as a
  pragmatic substitute.

## 18.3 Nonlinear panel-data example

### 18.3.1 Data description and summary statistics

```{julia}
# RAND HIE data
df18 = DataFrames.DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("musr/mus18data.dta"))
DataFrames.describe(df18[:, [:dmdu, :med, :mdu, :lcoins, :ndisease, :female,
                              :age, :lfam, :child, :id, :year]])
```

```{julia}
# Mean / SD / etc
DataFrames.describe(df18[:, [:dmdu, :med, :mdu, :lcoins, :ndisease, :female,
                              :age, :lfam, :child]],
                    :mean, :std, :min, :max, :nmissing)
```

### 18.3.2 Panel-data organization

```{julia}
# Panel structure
n_id   = length(unique(df18.id))
n_year = length(unique(df18.year))
@printf("Number of individuals  = %d\n", n_id)
@printf("Number of time periods = %d\n", n_year)
@printf("Total obs              = %d\n", DataFrames.nrow(df18))

# Distribution of obs per id
obs_per_id = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df18, :id), DataFrames.nrow => :n)
@printf("Min/mean/max obs per id = %d / %.2f / %d\n",
        minimum(obs_per_id.n), Statistics.mean(obs_per_id.n),
        maximum(obs_per_id.n))
```

### 18.3.3 Within and between variation

```{julia}
# Within and between SD using helper from Sec 8
function xtsum_var18(df, varname, idvar)
    x = df[!, varname]
    means = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df, idvar),
                               varname => Statistics.mean => :mean)
    df2 = DataFrames.leftjoin(df, means, on = idvar)
    return (overall = Statistics.std(x),
            between = Statistics.std(means.mean),
            within  = Statistics.std(df2[!, varname] .- df2.mean),
            mean    = Statistics.mean(x))
end

println("─" ^ 60)
@printf("%-10s %10s %10s %10s %10s\n", "Var", "mean", "Overall", "Between", "Within")
println("─" ^ 60)
for v in [:age, :lfam, :child]
    s = xtsum_var18(df18, v, :id)
    @printf("%-10s %10.4f %10.4f %10.4f %10.4f\n",
            v, s.mean, s.overall, s.between, s.within)
end
println("─" ^ 60)
```

### 18.3.4 FE or RE model for these data?

## 18.4 Binary outcome models

### 18.4.1 Panel summary of the dependent variable

```{julia}
# Panel summary of dmdu
s_dmdu = xtsum_var18(df18, :dmdu, :id)
@printf("dmdu: mean=%.4f, overall sd=%.4f, between=%.4f, within=%.4f\n",
        s_dmdu.mean, s_dmdu.overall, s_dmdu.between, s_dmdu.within)
```

```{julia}
# Year-to-year transitions in dmdu
DataFrames.sort!(df18, [:id, :year])
df18_t = DataFrames.transform(DataFrames.groupby(df18, :id),
    :dmdu => (x -> [missing; x[1:end-1]]) => :dmdu_lag)
df18_t = DataFrames.dropmissing(df18_t, :dmdu_lag)
trans = DataFrames.combine(DataFrames.groupby(df18_t, [:dmdu_lag, :dmdu]),
                            DataFrames.nrow => :freq)
DataFrames.unstack(trans, :dmdu_lag, :dmdu, :freq)
```

```{julia}
# Autocorrelations of dmdu
df18_t2 = DataFrames.transform(DataFrames.groupby(df18, :id),
    :dmdu => (x -> [missing; missing; x[1:end-2]]) => :dmdu_lag2)
df18_t2 = DataFrames.transform(df18_t2,
    :dmdu => (x -> [missing; x[1:end-1]]) => :dmdu_lag1)
sub = DataFrames.dropmissing(df18_t2, [:dmdu_lag1, :dmdu_lag2])
@printf("ρ(dmdu, L.dmdu)  = %.4f\n",
        Statistics.cor(sub.dmdu, sub.dmdu_lag1))
@printf("ρ(dmdu, L2.dmdu) = %.4f\n",
        Statistics.cor(sub.dmdu, sub.dmdu_lag2))
```

### 18.4.2 Pooled logit estimator

```{julia}
# Pooled logit with cluster-robust SEs (manual sandwich)
df18c = DataFrames.dropmissing(df18[:, [:dmdu, :lcoins, :ndisease, :female,
                                         :age, :lfam, :child, :id]])
fml18 = GLM.@formula(dmdu ~ lcoins + ndisease + female + age + lfam + child)
m_pool18 = GLM.glm(fml18, df18c, Distributions.Binomial(), GLM.LogitLink())

# Cluster-robust SE on id (manual)
function cluster_se_glm(model, df, fml, dep, idvar)
    Xm = GLM.modelmatrix(model)
    yv = df[!, dep]; μ = GLM.predict(model)
    n, k = size(Xm)
    # GLM score and Hessian
    W = Statistics.cov(GLM.coef(model))   # placeholder; recompute below
    H_inv = LinearAlgebra.inv(GLM.vcov(model)) \ LinearAlgebra.I  # not quite — use the model's hessian
    # Use the simpler approach: model.vcov is OIM, we replace with sandwich
    # Score: ∂ll/∂β = X' (y - μ)
    score = Xm .* (yv .- μ)
    S = zeros(k, k)
    for g in unique(df[!, idvar])
        idx = findall(df[!, idvar] .== g)
        s_g = sum(score[idx, :], dims = 1)
        S .+= s_g' * s_g
    end
    G = length(unique(df[!, idvar]))
    Vmat = GLM.vcov(model) * S * GLM.vcov(model) *
           (G / (G - 1)) * ((n - 1) / (n - k))
    return sqrt.(LinearAlgebra.diag(Vmat))
end
se_clu18 = cluster_se_glm(m_pool18, df18c, fml18, :dmdu, :id)
β = GLM.coef(m_pool18); cn = GLM.coefnames(m_pool18)
println("\n— Pooled logit with cluster-robust SE on id —")
for i in eachindex(β)
    @printf("%-12s %12.6f  cl.se = %12.6f\n", cn[i], β[i], se_clu18[i])
end
```
